Summary: sldvruntest?errors out on loading analysis results from later releases for export function models
Description: Simulink Design Verifier supports loading analysis results from later releases (example: R2021b analysis can be loaded in R2017b) to collect coverage results. For export function models, sldvruntest  throws the below mentioned error.Error message:? Error using sldvruntest (line 157)  Unable to invoke sldvruntest because the model <modelName> has 2 Inport blocks but the input data specifies test cases for 1 inputs.
Workaround: Follow the below steps in the UI:Navigate to Analysis > Design Verifier > Results > Load.Choose the data file.Click? on Simulate tests and produce a model coverage report

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier Analysis finishes quickly without any progress
Description: When div-by-zero check is selected and user performs active logic analysis, in some cases, analysis may abruptly end reporting most objectives as undecided.

Summary: Large CAD assemblies imported from cloud storage might affect simulation performance
Description: Models containing large assemblies that are stored on the cloud (such as, in OneDrive) might have degraded performance during simulations subsequent to the first simulation. The degraded performance is a slowdown in the simulation compared to runtimes when files are stored on a local or network directory. The slowdown occurs even if the files have not been changed since last being read. Normally, when models with CAD assemblies are loaded into a Simulink session for the first time, the operation takes some time. Subsequent simulations of the model during the same session run faster because they do not require reimporting the files unless they have changed since last being read.?
Workaround: Move the CAD files to a local or network directory.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Creating an anonymous function might result in a memory leak
Description: When the handle to an anonymous function is no longer in use, MATLAB might not free up all the memory allocated to the function handle.

Summary: Analysis may terminate with an?Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end?error message.
Description: Analysis might terminate without producing the results due to floating-point computations in the model that produce very large values. When you run the analysis with a user interface, it displays the following error message: Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end.  When you run the analysis in the?Test Generation ?mode, the error message displayed at the MATLAB Command Window is: Test generation produced errors  In releases beginning in R2018a, the below stack trace is produced: src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:475 err=data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double    src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:110 err=data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double    src/tempo/tempo_safe_induction.c:1343 tempo_error=tempo_err_unknown  When this issue is hit, the stack trace will contain data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB code with System objects stored in cell arrays generates HDL code with only one instance for all objects in the cell array
Description: When System objects are stored in cell arrays and step method is called by dynamically selecting one element from the cell array, the generated HDL code does not create instances for all System objects. In generated HDL code, one instance gets reused for all the elements in the cell array. For the following example MATLAB code only one RAM is created. function y = fcn(u, c)    persistent objs  if isempty(objs)  ? ? objs = cell(3,1);  ? ? for ii=1:3  ? ? ? ? objs{ii} = hdl.RAM;  ? ? end  end    y = step(objs{c}, u, uint8(1), true);
Workaround: Store System objects in individual variables and not in cell arrays. Do not assign to System object variables conditionally.function y = fcn_cell_workaround(u, c)persistent obj1 obj2 obj3if isempty(obj1)? ? obj1 = hdl.RAM;? ? obj2 = hdl.RAM;? ? obj3 = hdl.RAM;endy = coder.nullcopy(uint8(0));if c == 1? ? y = step(obj1, u, uint8(1), true);endif c == 2? ? y = step(obj2, u, uint8(1), true);endif c == 3? ? y = step(obj3, u, uint8(1), true);end
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: The command window password prompt for Matlab Job Scheduler does not correctly wait for input
Description: When using a Matlab Job Scheduler configured with security level greater than zero, certain operations require the user to interact with a password prompt to confirm their identity. If the MATLAB client is running in nodisplay mode, this prompt switches from an interactive window to a running in the command window. For some operations, this command window prompt does not correctly wait for the user input. This means the prompt cannot be correctly completed, and instead the user input is executed as a command in the MATLAB console.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Code generation assertion when using matrix multiply operation in MATLAB Function Block with variable sized matrices
Description: Code generation asserts when the model has:MATLAB Function Block with variable sized matrix of structs passed as arguments. MATLAB Function block uses matrix multiply operator on the function arguments which are created as variable sized matrices.
Workaround: MATLAB Function Block with functions accepting matrix arguments, should use for-loop to apply matrix multiply operation on individual elements of the matrix.

Summary: The Capacity vector and Head vector?parameters?in the Centrifugal Pump (TL) block cannot be set to Run-time
Description: In the Centrifugal Pump (TL) block, the?Capacity vector ?and?Head vector? parameters?do not currently support run-time capabilities. However, the block dialog incorrectly provides the option to choose between the?Compile-time ?or?Run-time ?settings. Selecting?Run-time ?and simulating the model results in a compilation error: Function 'fluids_functions. polynomial_fit' must be an unprotected MATLAB function. ?In fluids. thermal_liquid. pumps_motors. centrifugal_pump
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might produce incorrect results for AUTOSAR model containing For Each subsystem
Description: Generated code might produce incorrect results for an AUTOSAR model that meets all of these conditions: The AUTOSAR model uses export-function modeling, represented by a function-call subsystem at the root level of the model. The function-call subsystem contains a For Each subsystem, which is directly connected to the inport of the function-call subsystem. The inport of the function-call subsystem also connects to another branch. The AUTOSAR data access mode of the root-level inport is set to ExplicitReceive .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2020b Update 4, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when querying the input elements of a Bus Selector block with?get_param
Description: Querying the input elements of a Bus Selector block with get_param  might cause MATLAB to crash when all of these conditions apply: One of the elements of the input bus for the Bus Selector block originates from a Function Caller block. Any blocks between the Function Caller block and Bus Selector block are virtual blocks.  The query might look like: get_param('model/Bus Selector','InputSignals')
Workaround: If the output of the Function Caller block is not a bus, connect a Gain block with Gain set to 1?between the Function Caller block and the Bus Selector block.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: If you set the Vehicle Body 3DOF Dual Track block Axle force?parameter to?External forces, the block incorrectly implements the lateral force signal input to the front right tire
Description: If you set the Vehicle Body 3DOF Dual Track block?Axle force? parameter to?External forces , the block incorrectly implements the signal array connected to the input port?FwF . Specifically, the block does not correctly use the array element for lateral force on the right front tire. The issue does not affect the Vehicle Body 3DOF Single Track block.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Uncompilable code generated due to coincident naming in a Simulink Function and in an Outport block
Description: When the name of a Simulink Function's variable is also used as the label of an Outport block, the generated model.h  file will contain an incorrect declaration of that variable by appending '_i ' to its name.
Workaround: Rename the label of the Outport block to something else.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: sum(x,dim) might return incorrect answers for?single?and?half?inputs?in generated code that uses row-major layout
Description: Code generated for the function  sum(x,dim) , where?dim ?is the dimension to operate along,?might return incorrect answers if all  the following conditions are true: class(x)  is 'single ' or 'half ' The generated code stores  x ?in row-major order x  is not a vector x  is not empty The dim  argument is supplied size(x,dim)  is greater than 1024  size(x,k)~=1 ?for some?k < dim   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valve might incorrectly calculate the Pressure Compensator valve and Variable Orifice areas
Description: The Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valve incorrectly calculates the Variable Orifice opening fornonzero Initial opening  values and might use an incorrect value for the Pressure Compensator maximum passage area.
Workaround: If the block has a nonzero value for Initial Opening, you can remedy this with the following steps:Set the?InitialOpening?parameter to 0 m.Add a PS Add block and connect its output port to the Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valveblock port?C.?Connect a PS Constant block to one of the PS Add block input ports. Set the Constant parameter equal to the desired?Initial Opening?value.?Connect the control signal originally connected to port?C?to the other PS Add block input port.Additionally, if the Valve parameterization setting is?By area vs. opening table, set theOrifice maximum area?equal to the lastelement of Tabulated orifice area. Whilethe?Orifice maximum area?is not used in the?By area vs. opening table setting, the parameter is used to set the maximum passage area of the Pressure Compensator valve.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Shared utility code folder error for modified Vehicle Dynamics Blockset and Powertrain Blockset example
Description: Simulating or updating the diagram of a Powertrain Blockset or Vehicle Dynamics Blockset example model might produce an unexpected error if you have edited a model in the model hierarchy. The error might look like: ? Error while bringing model references of model SiCiPtReferenceApplication up to date: Error????building model 'SiEngineController'.  The current model configuration differs from the model????configuration used to generate the shared utility code folder, ????'<myPath>\ slprj\ sim\ _sharedutils'.
Workaround: Delete the slprj folder and .slxc files, then try again.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unexpected result or error when using a tabulated data parameterization of the Pressure Relief Valve
Description: In the Pressure Relief Valve block, when the Opening area parameterization  is set to Tabulated data - Area vs. pressure , and the pressure drop between port A  and port B  exceeds the last element of the?Pressure differential vector , one of the following may occur: The orifice area may incorrectly change to an unexpected value. The block may erroneously generate the following message: "Nonlinear solver: failed to converge, ?residual norm too large."
Workaround: To avoid these errors, either:Replace the Pressure Relief Valve with a Pressure Compensator block.Set the?Opening area parameterization?to?Tabulated data - Area vs. pressure.Connect the pressure sensor port X to port B. Connect the sensor port Y to port A.Reverse the order of the elements in the?Opening area vector. For example, if the existing vector is [0.25, 0.8, 1.3], adjust it to [1.3, 0.8, 0.25]. This can be achieved by setting it to?fliplr([0.25, 0.8, 1.3]).Reverse?the order of the elements in the Pressure differential vector and flip the element signs. For example, if the existing vector is [-10, 5, 13], adjust it to [-13, -5, 10]. This can be achieved by setting it to?-fliplr([-10, 5, 13]).All other parameters and port connections should match the original Pressure Relief Valve block settings.?In the Pressure Relief Valve block, use a linear parameterization by setting the?Opening area parameterization?to Linear area-pressure relationship.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: The Pressure Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve may default to an incorrect value for the maximum orifice area.
Description: In the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve block, when?Orifice parameterization ?is set to By orifice area vs. orifice opening table , and the sum of the signal at port C  and the Orifice initial opening  ?exceeds a certain threshold, the orifice area may equal a value that is unrelated to the values in?Tabulated orifice area .   In R2018b and earlier, this threshold is the value of the?Orifice maximum opening , which is visible when the?Orifice parameterization  is By maximum opening and area .?In R2019a and later, this threshold is 0.005 m, the default value of?Orifice maximum opening .
Workaround: To avoid these errors, either:Replace the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve with a Variable Orifice block and Pressure Relief Valve block.Connect the Variable Orifice S port to the node that was previously connected to the original block C port.Connect the Variable Orifice port A and Pressure Relief Valve A to the node that was previously connected to the original block A port.Connect the Variable Orifice port B to the node that was previously connected to the original block B port.Connected the Pressure Relief Valve B to the node that was previously connected to the original block R port.Set the Variable Orifice?Model parameterization?to?By area vs. opening table.Leave the Variable Orifice Orifice orientation?as?Opens in positive direction.Set all other Variable Orifice parameters to match the parameters on the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve?block Variable orifice tab.Leave the Pressure Relief Valve Opening area parameterization??as Linear area-pressure relationship.?Set all other?Pressure Relief Valve parameters to match the parameters on the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve?block Pressure compensator tab.?In the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve block, use a linear parameterization by setting the?Orifice parameterization?to?By maximum opening and area.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect carrier damping loss calculation in Limited Slip Differential and Open Differential blocks
Description: The carrier damping loss calculation, set using the parameter Carrier damping, bd , is incorrect by a factor of one quarter of the carrier to driveshaft ratio, Ndiff/4. The affected blocks are Limited Slip Differential and Open Differential.  For typically small values of Carrier damping, bd ?or in applications with a?Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff? of approximately 4, there might not be any noticeable effect.
Workaround: Apply a quarter factor of the Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff to the nominal value used for the parameter Carrier damping, bd.For example, if Carrier damping, bd is nominally 1e-3 and the Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff is 3.7, then the corrected entry for the parameter Carrier damping, bd is 1e-3*3.7/4.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating models with non-complementary motion actuation and at least one implicit 6-DOF tree joint
Description: If a Simscape Multibody model has non-complementary motion actuation and at least one implicit 6-DOF tree joint, MATLAB might crash during a normal simulation. The crash occurs when an insufficient computed torque error occurs, and the joint with the largest magnitude error is an implicit 6-DOF joint.  When generating code for such a model (rapid accelerator, etc), MATLAB will always crash.
Workaround: Simscape Statistics Viewer displays the number of implicit 6-DOF joints in a model; try to eliminate all implicit 6-DOF tree joints by adding explicit joint blocks, such as the 6-DOF Joint block, from the Simscape Multibody library.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The Cartridge Valve Insert block may implement an incorrect value for the poppet stroke.
Description: In the Cartridge Valve Insert block, when Orifice specification  is set to either?Area vs. opening table  or Pressure-flow characteristic , the poppet stroke remains at its default value of 0.005 m, irrespective of the?Tabulated orifice opening   parameter value.
Workaround: To avoid this error, replace the Cartridge Valve Insert block with a Hydraulic Cartridge Valve Actuator block and Variable Orifice block as shown on the block?Documentation page:Connect the Hydraulic Cartridge Valve Actuator port A to the Variable Orifice port A.Connect the Hydraulic Cartridge Valve Actuator port B to the Variable Orifice port B.In the Cartridge Valve Actuator block,?Set the?Poppet stroke equal to the last element of the Tabulated orifice opening, minus the first element of the?Tabulated orifice opening, from the Cartridge Valve Insert block.Set the?Poppet-seat Initial gap?equal to the Cartridge Valve Insert Initial opening setting.Ensure that the Actuator Orientation?remains at its default setting of Acts in positive direction.All other parameters should match the parameter values in the Cartridge Valve Insert block.In the Variable Orifice block,?Ensure that the Orifice?Orientation?remains at its default setting of Opens?in positive direction.Ensure that the Initial opening?parameter remains at its default value of 0 m.All other parameters should match the parameter values in the Cartridge Valve Insert block. The Cartridge Valve Insert block parameters correspond to the following Variable Orifice block parameters:Orifice specification to?Model parameterizationTabulated orifice opening to?Orifice opening vectorTabulated orifice area to Orifice area vectorTabulated pressure differential to?Pressure differential vectorTabulated flow rate to?Volumetric flow rate table
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit Conversion block returns incorrect generated code for conversions with offsets and fixed-point data types with bias
Description: The Unit Conversion block might return incorrect answers for accelerator mode, rapid accelerator mode simulation, or code generation when the following conditions are true:The Output data type  parameter is set to Inherit: Inherit via back propagation , The input or output data type is a fixed-point data type with a bias, The unit conversion has an offset, for example, ˇăC  to ˇăF  or ˇăC  to K .
Workaround: Set the Output data type parameter of the Unit Conversion block to Inherit: Inherit via internal rule and insert a Data Type Conversion block after the Unit Conversion block.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB crashes at or around startup
Description: MATLAB might crash at or around startup with an?Access violation in?the following function call at the top of the stack trace:   MathWorks::lxe::Convertor::FoldMatlabStringLiteral

Summary: Simulink PLC Coder does not support the Simulink.LookupTable, Simulink.Breakpoint, and Simulink.DualScaledParameter objects for code generation
Description: The Simulink.Lookuptable , Simulink.Breakpoint , and Simulink.DualScaled  parameter objects use the coderInfo  property for tunability purposes, that might result in the generated code containing variables linked to these objects having no initial values.? For this reason, Simulink PLC coder does not support these objects for code generation.?

Description: Adding a Function-Call Subsystem as a slice component using  addSliceComponent? API throws an error which says  Component is a function call and cannot be sliced
Products: Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage, Requirements Toolbox

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated for?a model containing a For Iterator Subsystem
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for a model that meets the following conditions: There is an Assignment block connected to a Unit Delay block in a For Iterator subsystem. The?Initial Condition ?of the Unit Delay block initialization may or may not be zero.
Workaround: For releases prior to R2018b, unzip the attachment and save the fix_2178873.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run this script in each MATLAB session before opening your model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit Conversion block gives incorrect answer when converting data types during Normal mode simulation
Description: The Unit Conversion block might return incorrect answers for Normal mode simulation when the following conditions are true: The?Output data type ?parameter is set to Inherit: Inherit via back propagation , The input or output data type is a double or single, The output data type and input data type are different.  or The?Output data type ?parameter is set to?Inherit: Inherit via back propagation , The input or output data type is integer or fixed-point, The input signal is nonscalar.
Workaround: Set the Output data type parameter of the Unit Conversion block to?Inherit: Inherit via internal rule and?insert a Data Type Conversion block after the Unit Conversion block.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a

Summary: Error when starting a real-time model on system that uses Windows 10 May 2020 Update
Description: When starting a real-time model on a system that uses the Windows 10 May 2020 Update  operating system, the model does not start. MATLAB reports the error: Hardware timer cannot be allocated. Real-time kernel cannot run.

Description: String data with any of the following custom storage classes might generate code that does not compile: Volatile  ExportToFile  ImportFromFile  FileScope  Localizable  Struct  Reusable      The noncompilable code contains an incomplete character literal, such as the following: MyTestStruct.A[7] = '  For R2018b, only Update 5 and later are affected by this issue. Summary: Missing requirement links for Stateflow objects in library after resolve-push
Description: If you have a Stateflow chart in a referenced library, and objects inside the chart have requirements traceability links, these links might break after resolving the link to this library and using the "push" option to brings updates from model to library. The Function  parameter is set to mod ?or  rem  Block input ports 1 (a) and 2 (b) are of floating-point data types    The quotient of a/b  overflows to?Inf ?or?-Inf   The result in simulation is correct (zero ), but the result in generated code is incorrect (non-zero ). ?Note: This bug also affects MATLAB Coder (Bug report ).

Summary: Generated code for referenced models does not initialize shared local data stores to zero
