Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Relay and Backlash blocks might produce incorrect results when block is in frame-based mode and model uses row-major array layout
Description: The Relay and Backlash blocks might produce incorrect results in generated code when all of the following conditions are true:? The?Input Processing ?parameter of the Relay or Backlash block is set to?Columns as channels (frame based)  The model configuration parameter?Code Generation? >?Interface? >?Array Layout?  is set to? Row-Major   The input signal is a 2-D matrix
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Simulink Web View unable to export to file path that contains non-ASCII characters
Description: If you export a Simulink model to a Web View and provide a file path that contains non-ASCII characters, files in the support  folder can be corrupt and have a file size of zero.
Workaround: Specify a file path that contains only ASCII characters and then copy the files to the final destination.

Summary: datetime time zones do not reflect recent change to Brazil's time zone
Description: In April 2019, the government of Brazil announced that the country no longer observes Daylight Saving Time. The time zone data in MATLAB does not reflect this recent change. In MATLAB, this change manifests itself as datetime  arrays in what used to be the DST period (for example, 17 November 2019 to 16 February 2020) with Brazilian time zones (for example, 'America/Sao_Paulo' ) being treated as UTC-2 rather than UTC-3. The Brazilian time zones impacted by this change are listed here. 'America/Sao_Paulo'  'America/Campo_Grande'  'America/Cuiaba'
Workaround: If working only with recent datetime values (since 17 February 2019), use the fixed time zone offset. For each time zone listed below, change the time zone to the corresponding fixed time zone offset.?'America/Sao_Paulo' -> 'Etc/GMT+3''America/Campo_Grande' -> 'Etc/GMT+4''America/Cuiaba' -> 'Etc/GMT+4'For example, for datetime values associated with S?o Paulo, change the TimeZone property to 'Etc/GMT+3'.if all(d > datetime(2019,2,17))    d.TimeZone = 'Etc/GMT+3'end For datetime values associated with Campo Grande or Cuiab¨˘, use 'Etc/GMT+4'.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Starting MATLAB Job Scheduler on Windows can fail if earlier versions of MATLAB exist on the system path
Description: When you try to start the MATLAB Job Scheduler on Windows, the following commands can fail with different errors when you run them: ?mjs start  An error has occurred. The procedure entry point? Initialize @ i18n @ fl @@ YAX_N @ Z was not found from the dynamic link library (MATLAB installation directory) \ ?toolbox \ ?parallel \ ?bin \ ?win64 \ ?setMATLABExecutable.exe.   An error has occurred, ?but mjs has been started.     startjobmanager     Exception in thread "main" java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\nativei18n.dll: The specified procedure could not be found       startworker       matlabExecutable must be of the form '<release string>=<path to executable>;<release string>=<path to executable>;'. Actual string: %MATLAB_EXECUTABLE%          These errors occur because dynamic libraries from previous versions of MATLAB are being loaded and cause the commands to fail to run correctly.
Workaround: To prevent this issue from occurring, edit your System Path variable and remove any previous versions of MATLAB from the Path.Warning: Incorrectly editing the System Path can cause your system to become unusable. Make a backup of your Path so that you can restore it in the case of an error.?Go to Control Panel -> System and Security -> System -> Advanced System Settings.Click the button labeled "Environment Variables" at the bottom of the dialog box.Under System variables select the "Path" variable and click Edit.Select any previous version of MATLAB and click the "Delete" button.Click OK.Exit and restart any command prompts before starting MATLAB Job Scheduler.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a model containing C action language Stateflow Chart with shift operation applied to custom storage class
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains C action language Stateflow chart. The Stateflow chart applies a shift operation (<< or >>) to a signal or parameter that resolves to a custom storage class. ?The Casting modes  model configuration parameter is set to Standards Compliant .  For an example of this issue, please see the attached issue_example_2133775.slx within the attached file issue_2133775.zip

Summary: Figure reporter incorrectly scales the snapshot
Description: The Report API Figure  reporter might incorrectly scale the figure snapshot to fit a PDF report page in either of the following cases: The report output type is not specified. The report output type is specified in upper case as PDF .?   The?Figure  reporter might also cause the caption to overflow the page boundaries.
Workaround: Specify the report output type in lower case as pdf.If the figure caption overflows the page boundaries, set the Scaling property to? 'custom' and set the Height and Width properties.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MAT-files might contain incorrect data when Format?model configuration parameter is set to?Array when data alignment is used
Description: The code generator might log incorrect data in MAT-files, when?all of the following conditions?are true:In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Format ?parameter is set to Array .? The code generator uses a code replacement library to replace an operation or function with a custom function. The corresponding?code replacement?entry specifies an alignment requirement with the?AlignmentBoundary? property.? Code replacement occurs in the generated code.  If a logged variable requires data alignment, an incorrect result might be logged to the generated MAT- file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Use of Bus containing enum element with a non-zero default value might lead to incorrect code when zero initialization is disabled
Description: Incorrect code might be generated if all these conditions are true:? Model uses Bus object. The Bus contains an element of enum  type. The enum  type has non-zero default value. When the Bus is used in root-level I/O, model configuration parameter Remove root level I/O zero initialization ?is selected or when the the Bus is used in internal data, model configuration parameter?Remove internal data zero initialization  is selected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Probe block generates incorrect HDL Code
Description: HDL Coder incorrectly generates code for the Probe block as a wire instead of throwing an unsupported block error. The Probe block is supported R2018b onwards.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table n-D block or prelookup table block with non-power of 2 spacing generates incorrect HDL code
Description: Lookup Table n-D block or prelookup block might generate incorrect HDL code when the following conditions are true: The breakpoint is spaced with non-power of 2 values Inputs to the block are signed fixed point or integer type Saturation lower limit is a negative number.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code might be generated for a model referencing another model?containing Stateflow charts with similar graphical functions
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model references another model. The referenced model contains a Stateflow chart containing two graphical functions that have the same operations but reference different variables. At least one of the variables referenced within the graphical function is not used as the input or the output of the graphical function and is not defined within the local scope of the function.  When the above conditions are met, the generated code might have a graphical function call incorrectly replaced by a call to another graphical function. For the attached example g2121509.slx , the code generator generates the incorrect code in the function g2121509Ref  in g2121509Ref.c : *rty_Out1 = g2121509Ref_Fun1(tmp, rtu_Input1) + g2121509Ref_Fun1(tmp, rtu_Input1); ? ?The expected correct code is:? *rty_Out1 = g2121509Ref_Fun1(tmp, rtu_Input1) + g2121509Ref_Fun2(tmp, rtu_Input2);??
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: C/C++ compiler throws an overflow warning when compiling an S-function
Description: When using GCC 9 or newer, the C/C++ compiler might throw an overflow error when compiling an S-function because the error message is too large for the buffer. The error message is similar to: ?simulink.c:101:5: warning: '</arg></arguments></diag></d...' directive writing 37 bytes into a region of size 14 [-Wformat-overflow=]
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: AUTOSAR code compilation fails when inter-runnable variable code contains application data types rather than implementation data types
Description: An AUTOSAR model build might generate uncompilable code when all of the following conditions exist: The model uses function-call subsystems to model AUTOSAR runnables. A connection between subsystems is mapped to an AUTOSAR inter-runnable variable (IRV). The IRV application data type is mapped to an implementation data type with a different name. For example, AppType is mapped to ImpType.  In certain modeling conditions, the generated code refers to the application data type (AppType) rather than the corresponding implementation data type (ImpType).
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019b Update 8, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: webcamlist and webcam cannot detect USB webcams in certain Linux operating systems
Description: USB webcams connected to your system might not be detected by the webcamlist  and webcam  functions in the following Linux operating systems: Ubuntu 19.04 Ubuntu 18.04 LTS Debian 10 Debian 9
Workaround: For the following MATLAB releases, a fix is provided as an update to the "MATLAB Support Package for USB Webcams".To install the updated version, start Add-On Explorer:On the MATLAB Home tab, in the Resources section, click Add-Ons > Get Hardware Support Packages , orExecute supportPackageInstaller command in MATLAB command window.MATLAB R2019b,Open Support Package Installer and update the "MATLAB Support Package for USB Webcams" to version 19.2.2 by uninstalling the support package and reinstalling it.MATLAB R2019a,Open Support Package Installer and update the "MATLAB Support Package for USB Webcams" to version 19.1.3 by uninstalling the support package and reinstalling it.MATLAB R2018b,Open Support Package Installer and update the "MATLAB Support Package for USB Webcams" to version 18.2.2 by uninstalling the support package and reinstalling it.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When?input exceeds?last breakpoint, under certain conditions, Prelookup or n-D Lookup table blocks?might fail to return warning or error message
Description: Prelookup or n-D Lookup table blocks?might not return warning or error message under these conditions: Prelookup or n-D Lookup Table block is present in the model Diagnostic for out-of-range input? parameter is set to Warning  or  Error  Index search method? parameter is set to?Evenly spaced points ?or?Breakpoints specification? parameter is set to?Even spacing  Data type of Input or Breakpoints are integer or fixed-point data types Input value exceeds the last breakpoint value The difference between the input value and the last breakpoint value is small.  Note: The issue only occurs if the difference between the input and the last breakpoint is small. For larger differences, the warning or error is correctly returned.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assignment block with Y0 port might produce incorrect results when inside nested iterator subsystem
Description: Simulation and code generation for models involving Assignment blocks inside nested iterator subsystems might produce incorrect results when all the following conditions are met: The Assignment block is contained in a For Each subsystem, which is directly or indirectly contained by a For or While Iterator subsystem. The initialization port (Y0 port) of the Assignment block is enabled.

Summary: Code generation error for AUTOSAR model in which Simulink Function sends message to root outport
Description: Code generation fails for an AUTOSAR model if both  of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root outport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to QueuedExplicitSend . The model contains a Simulink Function that sends queued data messages to the root outport.  Under these conditions, AUTOSAR code generation fails with an error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing a Power block or for a MATLAB code with a power function and Standard Math Library as C99 (ISO)
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for MATLAB code containing a power  function or for a model with a Power block if the following conditions are met:The input datatype to the power? function or Power block is single . If generating C code from a model, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box the Standard math library ?parameter is set to C99 (ISO).  If generating C code from MATLAB code using the MATLAB Coder app, on the? Custom Code tab,? ?the Standard math library ?parameter is set to?C99 (ISO).  The input to the Power block or power  function is either 1  and NaN  or NaN  and 0 . The generated code contains powf  functions.
Workaround: Select C89/C90(ANSI) as the Standard Math Library when using power function in MATLAB or when using Power block in Simulink
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results for S-Function block with conditional function call output
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a user written S-Function block with a function call output. The output function call is called conditionally. The function call signal is connected to a reusable subsystem block.  The generated code might incorrectly contain local variables for the reusable subsystem output, instead of global variables. Thus, the reusable subsystem output does not hold its value between calls.
Workaround: Change the code generation option of the reusable subsystem to inlined or non-reusable.

Summary: wvd?function produces incorrect time-vector output when 'NumTimePoints' is specified
Description: When the wvd  function computes the smoothed pseudo Wigner-Ville distribution with the 'NumTimePoints'  name value-pair, the time vector output contains a time range that depends on the value specified in 'NumTimePoints' . The time vector should span the whole signal duration.  For example,  wvd(sin(2*pi*(0:100)/4),100,'SmoothedPseudo','NumTimePoints',100)??% Displays half the correct signal duration of 1 second
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Unsupported data types in custom C code triggers assertion
Description: If there are any unsupported data types in the C code to be imported into Simulink to use in C Caller, MATLAB Function, MATLAB System blocks and in Stateflow chart, it will trigger an internal assertion.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Small memory leak in generated C++ shared libraries when accessed via MATLAB Data API
Description: C++ applications may leak a few bytes of memory on every evaluation of compiled MATLAB functions, if all of the following conditions are true: The compiled MATLAB functions were compiled using Visual Studio Compiler The compiled?MATLAB functions were called using MATLAB Data API  The leaked memory will not be released until the program finishes execution.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results for coding pattern involving While loop with empty body
Description: For MATLAB code that satisfies the following conditions: There exists a while  loop which does not contain any statements inside its body.?? The condition of the while  loop is a compound logical expression involving logical and '&& ' At least one of the logical subexpressions is a function-call with a side effect.  Simulation using MATLAB Function block, MATLAB System block, or MATLAB Function in Stateflow might produce incorrect results. In this situation, the simulation will behave like the while loop never executes or executes once. For an example please see 'issue_model_2093701.slx' in attached issue_2093701.zip. See also Bug Report ID 2067108 ?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for Bus Assignment block in a model with the Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks configuration parameter selected
Description: Generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a Bus Assignment block. The model configuration parameter Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks  is selected. ? The input signal to be assigned by the Bus Assignment block feeds into a subsystem or a Stateflow chart. In the subsystem or stateflow block parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  tab, the Function packaging  parameter is set to Auto  or Nonreusable function .
Workaround: Clear the Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks parameter.

Summary: Run-time parameters in mechanisms with kinematic loops might cause memory corruption and crashes for code-generation targets?
Description: MATLAB might crash and corrupt memory or produce an error when generating code or performing a rapid accelerator mode simulation for a model that meets these conditions:The model must have a least one run-time parameter specified in a Simscape Multibody block. The run-time parameters that are specified in the Simscape Multibody block must also be in a kinematic loop.??  When a model meets the above conditions, this bug typically occurs when there are high-degree-freedom joints in the kinematic loop that have state targets, or for R2019b, when the low degree-of-freedom joints have the?Mode ?parameter set to?Provided by Input . When this bug produces an error, this is the message you get: Error occurred while executing External Mode MEX-file 'ext_comm':ExtTargetPktPending() call failed while checking??for target pktError occurred while executing External Mode MEX-file 'ext_comm':ExtSetTargetPkt() call failed on CLOSE.Ensure target is still running

Summary: Using CTRL+C while saving an object of a MATLAB class might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you press CTRL+C while saving a MAT-File that contains an object of a MATLAB class, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: Incorrect ARXML generated for export function model when Inport feeds both IRT Function and another runnable
Description: An AUTOSAR model generates incorrect ARXML when the following conditions are true: The model uses the export function modeling style. An Inport signal branches, and one branch of the Inport feeds an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate (IRT) Function. Another branch of the Inport signal is connected to a Function-Call Subsystem or another IRT Function.  The ARXML description generated for the runnable mapped to the IRT Function does not describe the variable access corresponding to the branched Inport. For AUTOSAR implicit data access modes, an RTE generator will not generate the macro required by the generated code. This also causes Simulink SIL or PIL mode to fail for implicit data access with an error message similar to the following: modelName.obj : error LNK2019: unresolved external symbol Rte_IRead_Runnable_Init_In1_In1 referenced in function Runnable_Init .\modelName.exe : fatal error LNK1120: 1 unresolved externals

Summary: Function-call does not execute inside a Simulink based state in a Stateflow chart in Accelerator mode
Description: When you run your model in Accelerator mode, Stateflow does not execute the function-call output signal of a block that is placed inside a Simulink based state.
Workaround: Choose any simulation mode other than Accelerator mode.

Summary: MATLAB might crash while loading an AUTOSAR model that has mapped signals
Description: MATLAB might crash while loading an AUTOSAR model if both of the following conditions exist: The model was saved using MATLAB R2018b, R2019a or R2019b. The model maps signals to AUTOSAR variables.  One example is when the model has AUTOSAR mapping for signals associated with library blocks, and the model is saved before the library models have been loaded.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point numeric type bias resets to 0 on update diagram if used in an evenly spaced breakpoint of lookup table object
Description: Simulink resets the bias value of a Simulink.NumericType object to zero on update diagram when the following conditions are true: The Simulink.NumericType  object or a Simulink.AliasType  object referencing the Simulink.NumericType  object is used as the data type for the breakpoints of a Simulink.LookupTable  object The Simulink.LookupTable  object?Breakpoints specification ?is set to?Even spacing  The? Simulink.NumericType  object has a bias value that is nonzero  This bug impacts anything in the model that references the Simulink.NumericType  object, since the object itself is changed.
Workaround: Replace the Simulink.NumericType object with a Simulink.AliasType object where the data type is an explicitly specified fixed-point type instead of a reference to a numeric type. For example:>>mytype = Simulink.AliasType('fixdt(1,16,2^0,1)');?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Model reference start time consistency check uses incorrect values given nonzero start times
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy might produce an error that uses incorrect values when all of the following conditions apply: A referenced model specifies a nonzero value for its?Start time ?configuration parameter. The parent model specifies the same nonzero value for its?Start time ?configuration parameter. The specified nonzero start time is not a multiple of the specified step size. The referenced model simulates in accelerator mode.  The simulation start time of models with a specified nonzero start time is adjusted to fall on a sample hit, which is a multiple of the step size. The consistency check incorrectly compares the top model Start time ?value against the simulation start time of the referenced model. The resulting error is similar to this one: Error in Model block 'top/Model': the 'Start time' must match between the parent model 'top' and the referenced model 'bot'.? The parent model has a value of '2.0', while the referenced model has a value of '10.0'.? To change this parameter, go to the 'Solver' page of the Configuration Parameters dialog.? Note that the 'Start time' that is used for a model is adjusted to fall exactly on a sample hit.? Set the parameter 'Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Automatic solver parameter selection' to either 'warning' or 'error' to be notified when Simulink adjusts the 'Start time'.

Summary: DOM? API HTML and HTMLFile objects ignore the CSS height property for table rows
Description:
The DOM API HTML and HTMLFile objects ignore the CSS height property for table rows. For example, the height property in the following script is ignored and generates a warning at the MATLAB command line: import mlreportgen.dom.*htmlTable = '
hello
';domTable = HTML(htmlTable);
Workaround:
Use row entry to specify row height, for example:import mlreportgen.dom.*htmlTable = '
hello
';domTable = HTML(htmlTable);
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results when assigning a single element of a bus array to the output of a function
Description: You might get incorrect results from a model containing a Stateflow chart when the following conditions are met: The chart uses an output or Data Store Memory variable which has a Simulink bus type and is non-scalar. The chart has an assignment from a function to a single element of this variable. For instance: busarray[0] = fcn(); Here fcn  is an internally defined Stateflow function or an external Simulink function.  In such scenarios, other elements of the bus array might be over-written with uninitialized values.
Workaround: Instead of a direct assignment to an element of an array, first assign the output of the function to a temporary variable and then assign the bus element to that temporary variable.tmp = fcn();busarray[i] = tmp;Here tmp is a Stateflow local variable whose type is the same as that of busarray, but which is a scalar.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation result might be incorrect for a Bus Creator block inside a Model block
Description: Simulation result?might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a referenced model. The referenced model contains a?Bus Creator?block. The simulation mode of the referenced model is set to Accelerator mode.

Summary: MATLAB might crash after running a model with Simulink Editor side panes open
Description: MATLAB might crash sporadically after simulation when at least one Simulink Editor side pane is open. For example: Open a model with In Bus Element or Out Bus Element blocks at its top level. Open the Model Data Editor pane. Run the model.  The stack trace for the crash mentions?ctx_getBlockByName . The crash is not limited to the above combination, however, it is the most common form reported.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR generated code for signal might not honor mapped AUTOSAR variable type
Description: The generated code for an AUTOSAR model might incorrectly represent the AUTOSAR mapping of a signal if all of the following conditions exist: A subsystem output signal is mapped for AUTOSAR, as displayed in the Code Mappings editor, Signals/States  tab. The underlying signal within the subsystem, which is connected to the outport of the subsystem output signal, is also mapped for AUTOSAR. One of the two signals is mapped to Auto , while the other is mapped to AUTOSAR variable type ArTypedPerInstanceMemory  or StaticMemory .  Generated code for the signal mapped to an AUTOSAR variable type might not reflect the configured type.
Workaround: Remove the AUTOSAR mapping for the signal that is mapped to Auto.

Summary: sim3d.Engine class errors when you use 3D simulation blocks after updating Windows?? ??
Description: After you install a Windows update that includes .Net 4.8 (528049) , Simulink models that include 3D simulation blocks that interface with the Unreal Engine? display this error at simulation start:? Invalid default value for property 'engine' in class 'sim3d.Engine': No method 'Start' with matching signature found for class 'System.Diagnostics.Process'.   Vehicle Dynamics Blockset??blocks that interface with the Unreal Engine are in the Vehicle Scenarios > Sim3D Simulink block library.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Passing single precision arrays to extrinsic functions when using GPU Coder may generate wrong answer
Description: When passing single precision arrays to a function declared as extrinsic, the generated code may produce incorrect results. For example, consider this MATLAB code: function out = foo()    coder.extrinsic('sqrt');    out = sin(single([4 4]));end In MATLAB, foo  returns the correct value of [2 2] .? In the generated code,??foo ?returns the incorrect value of?[0 0] .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Range Collection Mode as Derived ranges in Fixed-point Tool fails for export function models
Description: Range collection in Fixed-point Tool with Range Collection Mode ?selected as Derived ranges  fails for Export-function models. The diagnostic viewer shows the error:?Ranges could not be derived for the model.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Simulink Web View?displays as blank pane in code generation report
Description: In a code generation report, the Web View displays as a blank pane if the web?browser is missing a function required by the browser detection JavaScript code.?
Workaround: Open the code generation report using a different web browser, such as the MATLAB web browser, Firefox, or Google Chrome.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Code generator might crash if the MATLAB code passes a vector input to a method that returns multiple outputs
Description: The code generator might crash if a call to a method in your MATLAB code satisfies both of these conditions: The call passes a vector (for example, 1:n  or a:b:c ) to the method. The method returns multiple outputs to the caller function.  The crash report contains an assertion?at CallExpr.hpp line 16 . For example, suppose that you define a MATLAB class A_class :  classdef A_Class    methods        function [a,b] = a_method(obj, input)            a = 0;            b = input;        end    endend If you attempt to generate code for this function, the code generator might crash: function for_codegen(n)obj = A_Class;[a,b] = obj.a_method(1:n);end
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, save the vector input into a temporary variable and pass this temporary variable to the method.For example, modify the for_codegen?function in the previous section by introducing a temporary variable temp.?function for_codegen(n)obj = A_Class;temp = 1:n;[a,b] = obj.a_method(temp);end
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB functions performing subtractions with signed integers typecasted as unsigned integers might generate incorrect HDL code
Description: Incorrect HDL code can be generated from MATLAB functions that performs subtractions with signed integers are typecasted as unsigned integers. For example: % in32 is of type int32?  function y_uint32?= foo (in32)  ????uint32_extreme = uint32([0, 4294967295]);  ? ? y_uint32 = uint32_extreme??- uint32(in32);??  end??   The generated code in such cases does not perform saturation of negative values of the signed integer to zero, which results in testbench simulation mismatches.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: While loop with empty body incorrectly omitted in generated code
Description: For MATLAB code that satisfies the following conditions:? There exists a while  loop which does not contain any statements inside its body.?? The condition of the while  loop is a compound logical expression involving logical and '&& '. At least one of the logical subexpressions is a function-call with a side effect.  The code generator might incorrectly omit the while  loop in generated code.? For an example, see the file issue_example_2067108.m in the attached issue_2067108.zip.? function out = issue_example_2067108(idx)    global globalState;    globalState = 0;    while(globalState < idx  && ...          updateGlobalState())    end     out = globalState;end function out = updateGlobalState()    coder.inline('never');    global globalState;    globalState = globalState + 1;    if(globalState > 100)        out = false;        return;    end    out = true;end  The call to updateGlobalState  in the while  loop condition has a side-effect of incrementing globalState . In the generated code, the while  loop and the call to this function are incorrectly omitted. >> codegen issue_example_2067108 -args {0} -globals {'globalState',0}real_T issue_example_2067108(const emlrtStack *sp, real_T idx){  (void)sp;  (void)idx;  globalState = 0.0;  globalState_dirty |= 1U;  return globalState;} Instead the code generator should have produced real_T issue_example_2067108(const emlrtStack *sp, real_T idx){  boolean_T exitg1;  globalState = 0.0;  globalState_dirty |= 1U;  exitg1 = false;  while ((!exitg1) && (globalState < idx)) {    if (*emlrtBreakCheckR2012bFlagVar != 0) {      emlrtBreakCheckR2012b(sp);    }    if (!updateGlobalState()) {      exitg1 = true;    }  }  return globalState;} This issue can affect MATLAB Coder only code generation Simulink Coder code generation of models containing a MATLAB Function block, a MATLAB System block, or a MATLAB function in Stateflow?  See also Bug Report ID 2093701
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Error or crash loading Embedded Coder Dictionary created with Japanese encodings
Description: When a coder dictionary is created in a Simulink Data Dictionary on a machine with Japanese encodings, opening it in the Embedded Coder Dictionary dialog box in a new MATLAB session causes MATLAB to crash in R2018b.? In R2019a, this causes an error and the Embedded Coder Dictionary dialog box is not loaded.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code is generated when State Reader block reads from a Delay block in a model with row-major array layout
Description: Incorrect code is generated when all of the following conditions are true in a Simulink model: Delay block's output port is connected to State Reader block's input port. Delay block's Delay length  value is greater than 1. Model configuration parameter Array layout  is set to Row-major .  The generated code for State Reader block reading Delay block's state value is incorrect as it does not consider row-major array layout of the Delay block's state value. For example: Delay Block's delay length = 6? Delay Block's Input port or Initial Condition dimension = 2 * 5? ?? Incorrect code where the row-major array layout was not taken into consideration: ? ? ? ?memcpy(&stateReader.TerminateInnerOut[0], &stateReader.Delay_DSTATE[0],?(uint32_T)(60U * sizeof(real_T))); ? Correct code to read Delay block's state value by a State Reader block, where the row-major layout is considered:     int32_T i;    int32_T i_0;    int32_T i_1 = 0;    for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {          for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < 6; i_0++) {            stateReader.TerminateInnerOut[i_1] = stateReader.Delay_DSTATE[(int32_T)((int32_T)(i_0 * 10) + i)];            i_1++;         }? ? ? ? ?}

Summary: MATLAB crashes when creating an atomic subchart around an existing atomic subchart with input events
Description: MATLAB crashes when you create an atomic subchart that contains an existing linked atomic subchart if the linked subchart has input events.?

Summary: Invalid error for Data Store Memory block specifying symbolic dimensions
Description: An invalid error message might be reported when simulating or generating code for a model containing a Data Store Memory block when all of the following conditions are true: The Data Store Memory block resolves to a Simuink.Signal  object. The?Simuink.Signal?object specifies symbolic dimensions. The configuration parameter Allow symbolic dimension specification ?is cleared.  The error message is similar to: The 'Dimensions' parameter of the Data Store Memory block 'model/Data Store Memory' must match the symbolic dimensions of the object 'MySignal' that it resolves to. To disable symbolic dimension propagation, search for 'Allow symbolic dimension specification' in the Configuration Parameters dialog box and clear the checkbox.  The error message is invalid because?Allow symbolic dimension specification ?is already cleared.
Workaround: Set the dimensions of the?Simuink.Signal?object to be their numeric, non-symbolic equivalent.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with a Data Store Memory block that is written to from inside a subsystem and read from outside the subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: A?Data Store Write block?is in a subsystem and writes to a Data Store Memory block. A Data Store Read block reads from the same Data Store Memory block and connects to an inport of the subsystem. The Data Store Read block is used and evaluated after the subsystem is evaluated.? In the subsystem block parameters dialog box, the?Function Packaging ?parameter is set to?Reusable function .  In the generated code the Data Store Memory value is incorrectly updated after the subsystem function call.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 7, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with a nested?For Each?subsystem block
Description: Generated code might be incorrect for a modelthat meets the following conditions: The model contains a For Each subsystem block nested inside another For Each subsystem block. The?Function Packaging ? parameter of theinner For Each?subsystem block is set to Reusable . In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Pass reusable subsystem outputs as ?parameter is set to? Structure reference .

Summary: BridgeMode property cannot be set to 'Half' or 'Full'
Description: Setting a bridge channel BridgeMode  property value to 'Half'  or 'Full'  silently fails, and its value remains at the default value of 'Quarter' , even though the display reflects the requested change. The issue affects the following example: s = daq.createSession('ni');ch = addAnalogInputChannel(s, "cDAQ1Mod2","ai0","Bridge")ch =Data acquisition analog input channel 'ai0' on device 'cDAQ1Mod4':             BridgeMode: Unknown       ExcitationSource: Internal      ExcitationVoltage: 2.5NominalBridgeResistance: 'Unknown'                  Range: -0.025 to +0.025 VoltsPerVolt                   Name: ''                     ID: 'ai0'                 Device: [1x1 daq.ni.CompactDAQModule]        MeasurementType: 'Bridge'          ADCTimingMode: '' s.Channels(1).BridgeMode = 'Half'ch =Data acquisition analog input channel 'ai0' on device 'cDAQ1Mod4':             BridgeMode: Half       ExcitationSource: Internal      ExcitationVoltage: 2.5NominalBridgeResistance: 'Unknown'                  Range: -0.025 to +0.025 VoltsPerVolt                   Name: ''                     ID: 'ai0'                 Device: [1x1 daq.ni.CompactDAQModule]        MeasurementType: 'Bridge'          ADCTimingMode: ''  Despite the fact that the BridgeMode  property value is reported as 'Half' , its actual value is 'Quarter' .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 5, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Chapter reporter ignores page number format from custom template
Description: For chapters other than the first chapter of a PDF report, the Report API Chapter  reporter ignores the page number format from a custom template.
Workaround: Customize the page number format for each chapter by using the?Chapter?reporter's?Layout property. For example:ch = mlreportgen.report.Chapter("MyChapter"); ch.Layout.PageNumberFormat = "A";

Summary: Vehicle Body 3DOF block in dual track mode neglects track width in wheel force calculation
Description: If you set the Vehicle Body 3DOF block parameter Vehicle track  to Dual , the block neglects the track width in the wheel force, FzF  and FzR , calculations. If you set?Vehicle track ?to?Dual ,?the block correctly calculates the wheel forces.

Summary: Design error detection analysis may run out of memory or report an internal error
Description: When analyzing a model for design errors, memory usage may increase after each processed design error check. If the model is large and has many design error checks, Simulink Design Verifier may run out of memory or report an internal error. The error message reported in the summary window is as follows:? Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 5, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: strip removes only '' in the generated code
Description: In generated code, strip  removes only ''. ? It does not remove other white space characters, such as tab and new line.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model containing Enabled Synchronous Subsystem or Resettable Synchronous Subsystem and?Single output/update function?parameter set for referenced model with states might generate incorrect C code
Description: Embedded Coder might generate numerically incorrect code when a model meets all the following conditions: In the referenced model, Model configuration parameter Single output/update function ?parameter is selected. The model contains synchronous semantics. The model has states.  AND  In the top model, Model configuration parameter Single output/update function ?parameter is selected. The referenced model is inside an Enabled Synchronous Subsystem or a Resettable Synchronous Subsystem.  The results obtained from running this C code might differ from simulation results.??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR generated code might write uninitialized value if IRV feeds back to runnable
Description: AUTOSAR generated code might write an uninitialized value to an IRV if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains a subsystem that is mapped to an AUTOSAR runnable. The subsystem writes to a signal that is mapped to an AUTOSAR IRV. The subsystem reads from the IRV signal that it writes.  Under these conditions, the generated code might write an uninitialized variable.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing function calls for variable-size signals propagated from root input to root output ports of model in reference hierarchy that specifies SIL or PIL
Description: Consider a model A , which contains a Model block with block parameter Simulation mode  set to  Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or  Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) . The Model block references model  B , which in turn references model  C . The generated code for model  B ? can differ from the code that is generated if the Model block parameter Simulation mode  is set to Normal  or Accelerator  and all of these conditions apply to model C :  There are root input or output ports that have variable dimensions.  The model configuration parameter Propagate sizes of variable-size signals  is set to Infer from blocks in model .  The variable dimensions of at least one root output port are propagated from the variable dimensions of a root input port.   When the Model block parameter Simulation mode  is set to  Normal  or Accelerator , these function calls might be present in the generated code for model B :    modelC_SetDims(...)   modelC_FinalizeDims(...)   When the Model block parameter Simulation mode  is set to Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or  Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) , the function calls are not present in the generated code for model B . You might observe incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: For each referenced model below the model referenced by the Model block with parameter Simulation mode set to? Software-in-the-loop (SIL) or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL), set the model configuration parameter?Propagate sizes of variable-size signals? to During Execution.Alternatively:Delete the code that was generated with the Model block configured for SIL or PIL.For each model in the model reference hierarchy that references the model with variable-size signal propagation, set the simulation mode to normal or acceleration.Regenerate code.

Summary: Inadvertentchanges to properties of a copied reporter, when the property is a reporter orDOM object
Description: Some Report API reporters have properties that are set to reporter or DOM object values. When a reporter is copied using the copy method, only the references to the reporter or DOM objects in these properties are copied. Any modifications to the reporter or DOM object properties of one reporter also affect the other reporter because the properties of both reporters reference the same reporter or DOM object. The consequences of this issue vary based on the reporter. For example, mlreportgen.report.MATLABVariable  has a property, ParagraphFormatter , that is a DOM Paragraph object used to format the value of the MATLAB variable. In the following code, both MATLABVariable  reporters display paragraphs in italics, even though only the copied reporter's ParagraphFormatter  property is modified: import mlreportgen.report.* mlVar1 = MATLABVariable('x'); mlVar2 = copy(mlVar1); mlVar2.Variable = 'y'; mlVar2.ParagraphFormatter.Italic = true;     Other reporters such as slreportgen.report.Diagram  and slreportgen.report.SimulinkObjectProperties  add content directly to properties that are reporter objects. Adding copies of these reporters to a report results in the same content being added to the report for each reporter, despite any modifications to copied reporters. For example, the following code creates a report with two identical tables that both report the properties of the block? vdp/Mu :  import slreportgen.report.* load_system('vdp'); rpt = Report('exampleReport','pdf'); obj1 = SimulinkObjectProperties('vdp/Mu'); obj2 = copy(obj1); obj2.Object = 'vdp/x1'; add(rpt, obj1); add(rpt, obj2); close(rpt); rptview(rpt);
Workaround: After copying a reporter, use the clone method to copy DOM object properties:chTitle = mlreportgen.dom.Text('example title');?rptr1 = mlreportgen.report.Chapter(chTitle);?rptr1Copy = copy(rptr1);?rptr1Copy.Title = clone(rptr1.Title);?Use the?copy?method to copy reporter object properties:load_system('vdp');?rptr2 = slreportgen.report.SimulinkObjectProperties('vdp');?rptr2Copy = copy(rptr2);?rptr2Copy.PropertyTable = copy(rptr2.PropertyTable);??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing a?Unit Delay block inside a?For Each Subsystem block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Unit Delay block inside a For Each subsystem block. In the For Each Subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, the Function packaging  parameter is set to  Inline .  In the For Each Subsystem Block parameters dialog box, the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences ?parameter is selected .  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Single output/update function ?parameter is selected .   In the generated code, the input buffer of the Unit Delay block may be incorrectly localized into a single scalar type variable without considering the slice instances.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 7, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: DOM API ignores VAlign format for formal table header entries in PDF output
Description: For PDF reports, report generation ignores the VAlign  format for header table entries of a formal table represented by an mlreportgen.dom.FormalTable  object. For example, in the table generated from this script, content is not vertically aligned to the middle of header entries.  import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('output','pdf');open(d);  t = FormalTable({'Col1','Col2'}, {'entry1','entry2'});t.Border = 'solid';t.RowSep = 'solid';t.ColSep = 'solid';  t.Header.TableEntriesStyle = { ...??? Height('1in'), ...??? VAlign('middle') };append(d,t);  close(d); rptview(d.OutputPath);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing an?output port of a reusable MATLAB Function block connected to a Terminator block
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block. The MATLAB Function block has the block parameter Treat as atomic unit ?enabled and Function packaging ?parameter is set to Reusable function . The MATLAB Function block has more than one input and output ports.? The same variable is used for both an input and an output of the MATLAB Function block and the output port is connected to a Terminator block.??  In the generated code the input port data is not passed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect numerical results might occur for a model containing a referenced model with at least two outputs feeding a Concatenation block
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that contains a referenced model with at least two outputs feeding the same Concatenation block.
Workaround: Move the Concatenation block inside the source referenced model or inside a new reusable subsystem. Both of these strategies will avoid the generated code containing a call to a model reference function with an array (with offsets) as arguments.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect row-major code generation for Product of Elements block in All Dimensions mode
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect row-major code for a model when all of these conditions are true: Model configuration parameter Array layout  is set to Row-major . Model configuration parameter Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout  is not selected. Model contains Product of Elements block with Multiply over  parameter set to All dimensions . For the Product of Elements block, Accumulator data type  is different than input data type.
Workaround: Don't use row vectors or leading 1's when generating row-major code when Multiply over parameter set to All dimensions and Accumulator data type is different than input type. For example:Instead of feeding 1x2 matrix to Product of Elements block, use an array of size 2.Instead of feeding 1x3x4 matrix to Product of Elements block, use a matrix of 3x4.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing a?Unit Delay block inside a?For Each Subsystem block
Description: The generated code contains an incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The Unit Delay block is inside a For Each subsystem. The Initial Condition  of the Unit Delay block initialization is specified by a parameter instead of a fixed value. In the For Each Subsystem block parameters dialog box, the?Parameter Partition ?is selected .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 2, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Complex to Real-Imag block with constant input and test-pointed output generates uninitialized code
Description: The code generated for a Complex to Real-Imag in your Simulink model is uninitialized if the following conditions are met: One of the output ports of the Complex to Real-Imag block is marked as a test point. The input to the Complex to Real-Imag block?is fed from a Constant block.?
Workaround: The workaround would be to remove the test-point.

Summary: Invalid parameters in a real-time test case cause MATLAB crash when refreshing parameters
Description: In a real-time test case, if some parameters are not valid real-time parameters, clicking the refresh parameter button crashes MATLAB. For example, invalid real-time parameters might be in the parameter override table after converting a test case to a real-time test case.

Summary: Simulink Web View displays a blank screen when using Firefox 68+
Description: Starting in Firefox 68+, Firefox no longer allows local files to access other files in the same directory.? This change prevents Simulink Web View from loading the required files.
Workaround: ?Simulink Web View cannot be viewed in Firefox 68+ because of local file security restrictions. To allow local file access:Type about:config into the Firefox location bar.Search for privacy.file_unique_origin and set value to false.

Summary: Incorrect Variant condition propagation with operation on a BusElement and a normal signal
Description: Variant condition propagation fails when all the below conditions are satisfied  An operation (example: lookup table or Add block) with two inputs.  One input being the output signal of a Bus Selector and the other being a non-bus capable input.  The output of the operation is fed to a Variant Source block.

Summary: Commented out Probe block?might crash MATLAB
Description: Commented out Probe block?might crash MATLAB if the following are true: Probe sample time  check box is selected. Probe block is commented out after update diagram of the model.

Summary: Models containing Entity Unbatcher block might return an error?about invalid data type
Description: When a model with Entity Unbatcher block is compiled, a compilation error like the following might be generated: Error Attempt to use invalid data type id -10
Workaround: To change the block compilation order, try renaming any SimEvents block.?

Summary: Rebuilding a test harness might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you manually modify the interface to the conversion subsystems in the test harness before rebuilding it, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Streaming logged data directly to SDI with selective logging may produce wrong results?if Simscape model contains variables with ExternalAccess set to none
Description: Simscape variables with (ExternalAccess = none)  should not be displayed in simlog  and in the Simulation Data Inspector. If a Simscape model contains such variables,?then enabling selective logging and choosing to record data in Simulation Data Inspector may produce wrong results.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do one of the following:?In the Model Configuration settings, disable Record data in Simulation Data Inspector when Log simulation data is set to Use Local Settings.When Record data in Simulation Data Inspector is selected, set?Log simulation data?to All.If you have control over the Simscape file, set the ExternalAccess attribute of the variables?to observe instead of none.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for modelling pattern consisting of matrix accesses that use logical indexing
Description: The simulation and code generation of Simulink model with MATLAB Function block can produce incorrect results when following conditions are satisfied: The code contains a loop in which one matrix (A ) subscripts and grabs elements from another matrix (B ). The loop contains writes to all elements of A .  Consider the mdl_example.slx  model in the attached file. The model contains a MATLAB Function block with the following MATLAB code: function B = example( in )    B = zeros(1,400);    A = (4:13);    for i=1:10        B(A) = in;        A(i) = 8;    endend Simulating the model produces incorrect output. Executing the generated code from the Simulink model also produces incorrect output.?The incorrect generated C code is below. In this code, the write to 'A ' and the indexing operation using 'A ' inside the loop are missing.? void example(const double in[10], double B[400]){  int i;  memset(&B[0], 0, 400U * sizeof(double));  for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {    B[7] = in[i];  }} See also Bug Report ID 2006614.

Summary: Simulink might crash when string data type is used in multi-instance export-function submodel in accelerator mode
Description: Simulink might crash if a referenced model: Has a Simulink string signal Is a multi-instance export-function model Runs in accelerator mode?
Workaround: Run the submodel in normal mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In Restricted mode, blocks with conditional visibility of ports or parameters might produce wrong answers
Description: When a model is in Restricted mode, ?opening a block dialog (or accessing the Property Inspector for a block) triggers the block to ignore the actual values of its control parameters and use?the default values from the Simscape file instead.?As a result, themodel loads and simulates correctly in Restricted mode, but opening a blockdialog box or accessing the Property Inspector can cause the block to use thewrong parameter settings.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Interpolation Using Prelookup block might have previously unused?Valid index input may reach last index check box incorrectly selected when upgrading models from releases prior to R2014b
Description: The Interpolation Using Prelookup block might have a previously hidden and unused?Valid index input may reach last index   check box incorrectly selected?when upgrading models under these conditions: Models being upgraded from version R2014a or earlier Interpolation Method  parameter set to Flat . Valid index input may reach last index? parameter selected (even though it is hidden). For example,?you might have done this by: Programatically?selecting the check box using set_param(gcb, 'ValidIndexMayReachLast', 'on') . Setting the Interpolation Method  parameter to Linear , selecting the visible?Valid index input may reach last index  check box, and setting the??Interpolation Method  parameter back to Flat .   Under these conditions, upgraded version models output results assuming the check box is selected. Previous version models output results assuming the check box is cleared.
Workaround: After the upgrade, clear the Valid index input may reach last check box. Programatically clear the check box?using?set_param(gcb, 'ValidIndexMayReachLast', 'off').
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 5, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Compiler that supports only the C89 standard does not compile deployed Simscape C code
Description: If the deployed Simscape C codes are not pre-compiled for a C compiler that supports only the C89 standard, the compiler will fail to compile the deployed Simscape C codes during code generation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated for multiple similar Prelookup or n-D Lookup Table blocks?when?inputs, breakpoints, or fractions contain same fixed-point data types with different word lengths
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for models with multiple Prelookup or n-D Lookup Table blocks?when one of these sets of conditions are true: The blocks?have fixed-point data types for both inputs and breakpoints. Each block input and breakpoint?has different fixed-point data types. The blocks?have the same configuration except that: The data?types of the inputs differ only by word length but have the same?container word length . For example: Block 1 data type of?input: fixdt(1,12,3)  Block 2 data type of input:?fixdt(1,14,3)  The data? types of the breakpoints?differ only by word length but?have the same?container word length . For example: Block 1 data type of?breakpoint: fixdt(1,15,3)  Block 2 data type of breakpoint:?fixdt(1,16,3)    ?Or The blocks?have fixed-point data types for fractions. The blocks?have the same configuration except that: The data?types of the fractions differ only by word length but have the same?container word length . For example: Block 1 data type of?fraction: fixdt(1,12,3)  Block 2 data type of fraction:?fixdt(1,14,3)    ?

Summary: Generated code for top model that references model that uses zero-crossing detection is different when Model block is in SIL or PIL mode
Description: Consider a model that c ontains a Model block that is configured for SIL or PIL mode simulation. In addition, the referenced model  uses zero-crossing detection.  The code you generate for the model can differ from the code that you generate when the Model block is configured for normal mode simulation.  When the Model block parameter Simulation mode  is set to Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) , this line?is not present in the model .h  file:  #include "zero_crossing_types.h

Summary: Validation of AUTOSAR mapping might error out unnecessarily with mapped signals
Description: The mapping validation for an AUTOSAR model might unnecessarily error out if all of the following conditions exist: A subsystem output signal is mapped for AUTOSAR, as displayed in the Code Mappings editor,?Signals/States ?tab. The underlying signal within the subsystem, which is connected to the outport of the subsystem output signal, is also mapped for AUTOSAR. One of the two signals is mapped to?Auto , while the other is mapped to AUTOSAR variable type?ArTypedPerInstanceMemory ?or?StaticMemory .  The error message reports a signal configuration conflict. For example: Conflicting signal configuration. The code generation properties in code perspective for signal on output port 1 of 'autosar_swc/SS1/Sum' conflicts with signal on output port 1 of 'autosar_swc/SS1' since both outputs map to the same signal.
Workaround: Remove the AUTOSAR mapping for the signal that is mapped to?Auto.

Summary: Slow model compilation for large array of buses inside nonvirtual bus
Description: Updating the diagram, simulating, and generating code for a model might be slow when a nonvirtual bus contains a large array of buses.

Summary: svd?might fail to converge when operating on complex-valued matrices
Description: The three-output syntax of svd  might fail to converge when the input matrix is complex: ?>> [U,S,V] = svd(A)  Error using svdSVD did not converge.

Summary: Comparing two large LinkSet files can result in an error
Description: Simulink Requirements may throw following error when you compare two large LinkSet files:  com.mathworks.toolbox.rptgenxmlcomp.exception.XMLComparisonException: Error occurred in server thread; nested exception is: java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.PostMatlabRunnable(JZLjava/lang/Runnable;)

Summary: Temporary variable assignment before declaration with Variant Sinks and Variant Subsystem
Description: When a non-virtual block connected to a Variant Subsystem's inport has same condition as the inport of an active choice, local variables are assigned before they are declared as shown below:  void try2_step(void)  {? ?  #if Z == 1 || Z == 2  rtb_VariantMerge_For_Variant_So  = try2_U.In2;  {  ? ? real_T rtb_VariantMerge_For_Variant_So ;  ? ? #if Z == 2  ? ? rtb_VariantMerge_For_Variant_So = 1.0;  ? ? #endif  ? ? try2_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut3 = rtb_VariantMerge_For_Variant_So;  ? ? }  ? ? try2_Y.Out2 = try2_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut3;  ? ? #endif  }

Summary: Performance regression for large FIS creation and ANFIS tuning
Description: Performing the following operations on large fuzzy systems (number of rules greater than or equal to 250) takes significantly longer than in previous releases of MATLAB: Tuning fuzzy inference systems using the anfis  function Tuning fuzzy inference systems using?the Neuro-Fuzzy Designer  app Creating fuzzy inference systems using the?genfis ?function

Summary: Function Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct might return incorrect value when a virtual bus contains a nonvirtual bus
Description: Function Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct  might return an incorrect value for a virtual bus when all of these conditions apply: The virtual bus contains only nonvirtual buses. The data types of the nonvirtual buses are the same.

Summary: Building AUTOSAR model that contains root inport with enumerated data type and QueuedExplicitReceive data access mode crashes MATLAB
Description: Building an AUTOSAR model crashes MATLAB if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root inport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to QueuedExplicitReceive . The model reads an enumerated type value at the root inport. An enumeration class defines the enumerated data type. The root inport connects to a Stateflow chart that is configured to receive a message.  Under these conditions, building the model crashes the MATLAB session.

Summary: Model Advisor Check Issues metrics incorrectly counts passed model advisor configuration parameter checks as issues?
Description: The metric engine counts Model Advisor checks of model configuration parameter settings as issues even if they pass. in addition, the metric engine does not count as issues Model Advisor checks that warn or fail and do not contains links to blocks in the model. This bug pertains to these two metrics:??mathworks.metrics.ModelAdvisorCheckIssues.hisl_do178  ?mathworks.metrics.ModelAdvisorCheckIssues.maab   The Metrics Dashboard displays the incorrect number of issues.
Products: Simulink Design Verifier, Simulink Check, Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage

Summary: MATLAB crashes during property proving analysis on model with Assertion blocks
Description: If the Simulink Design Verifier option DVAssertions  is set to EnableAll or DisableAll and the model contains Assertion blocks, property proving analysis might result in a MATLAB crash.
Workaround: Set the DVAssertions option to UseLocalSettings and use the Enabled block parameter of Assertion blocks to enable or disable individual blocks. Then, perform property proving analysis.?

Summary: Actor trajectory creation in driving scenarios is slow
Description: When specifying a large number of waypoints in the trajectory function (or the path function in previous releases), the function executes slowly. The slowdown is most evident in scenarios with a large number of actors.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing nested reusable subsystems
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains? nested reusable subsystems. In the subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, the?Function packaging ?parameter is set to  Reusable function .  The signal that leaves the input port of the subsystem has a  Reusable  custom storage class.  The subsystem contains a child subsystem with the??Function packaging parameter  is?set to  Reusable Function.  The signal that enters the output port of the child subsystem has the same  Reusable  custom storage class.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 5, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant comparison in MATLAB code can generate incorrect HDL code
Description: In the following cases, the generated HDL code might incorrectly return true or false: 0 < u?  0 >= u?  MAX_CONSTANT <= u?  MAX_CONSTANT > u   ?where MAX_CONSTANT  is the maximum value that can be represented using the data type specified for u .?
Workaround: Rewrite the relational expression by moving the constant in the left hand side to the right hand side. For example, rewrite:? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?0 < u? ? ? ? ? ?as ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? u > 0

Summary: MATLAB might crash when virtual bus crosses atomic subsystem boundary
Description: MATLAB might crash when all of these conditions apply: A virtual bus contains a nonvirtual bus. This virtual bus crosses an atomic subsystem boundary. A Signal Conversion block converts this virtual bus to a virtual bus. A Bus to Vector block converts this virtual bus to a vector signal. This vector signal connects to another atomic subsystem.

Summary: Reporting on a vector of objects can cause an error
Description: If you use the mlreportgen.report.MATLABVariable  reporter to report on a variable, report generation ends with an error when both of these conditions are true: A property of the variable or of a descendant of the variable in a hierarchy is set to a vector of objects. The DepthLimit  property value of the reporter causes the reporter to report the vector of objects as inline text instead of expanding it.  In the following example, an mlreportgen.report.MATLABVariable  reporter reports on a variable ax  that is an Axes  object. This example causes an error because the Children  property of ax  is set to a vector of Line  objects and the DepthLimit  property of the reporter is set to 0.plot(rand(5))ax = gca;rpt = mlreportgen.report.Report("test");rptr = mlreportgen.report.MATLABVariable(ax);rptr.DepthLimit = 0;add(rpt,rptr);close(rpt);rptview(rpt);

Summary: Assertion during code generation when Stateflow chart and MATLAB Function block call the same external function
Description: An assertion error occurs during code generation when all the following conditions are met: The model contains a Stateflow chart with C as the action language. The model contains a MATLAB Function block. The model has the setting Import custom code  enabled. The Stateflow chart and the MATLAB Function block both call the same external function. The Stateflow chart is from a library model with the setting Use local custom code settings (do not inherit from main model)  disabled.  The assertion is similar to the following:? ?failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\rtwcg\rtwcg_utils\managedcustomcodescope.cpp at line 243:?  ? ? In getSpecialScopeImpl: Assertion failed: scope->parent() == fCCScope?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Anonymous entity with fixed-point data type at control port of Entity Gate block causes?MATLAB crash
Description: If the control port of the Entity Gate block receives an anonymous entity with fixed-point data type, MATLAB crashes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unexpected behavior of model rebuild when using structure array with enumeration
Description: When a model uses a structure array with enumeration, the following unexpected rebuild behavior can occur:? For a structure array with multiple elements using the same enumeration value, switching between a serial build and a parallel build can unexpectedly trigger the model to rebuild, even though the structure array has not been modified. For a structure array with multiple elements using different enumeration values, changing the enumeration value of an array element to a value already used by another array element does not trigger a rebuild as expected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB action language Stateflow charts that perform floating point operations may generate incorrect HDL Code
Description: Stateflow charts may generate incorrect HDL code when the following conditions are met:? The chart is a MATLAB action language chart Variables or parameters used in the chart are of single or double types Floating Point IP Library  is set to  Native Floating Point   ?Note? that floating point types specified at the inputs are correctly detected and reported.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing a?Unit Delay block connected to a?reusable subsystem output
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a subsystem. In the subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, the?Function packaging ?parameter is set to? Reusable function .  The subsystem contains a Unit Delay block which directly connects to a subsystem output port.  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to  Individual Arguments .  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Optimize global data access ?parameter is set to  Use global to hold temporary results ?or? Minimize global data access .

Summary: Simulink.Signal object property InitialValue on root outport of referenced model is ignored in certain cases
Description: Simulink can produce incorrect numeric results when each of the following conditions are true: Simulink.Signal  object is resolved for signal feeding root outport of referenced model. Simulink.Signal  object has property InitialValue  and a custom storage class specified. Referenced model is conditionally executed. For example, the model is triggered or enabled.? Top model is set to accelerator or rapid accelerator simulation mode, or the parent model is a referenced model that uses an accelerated simulation mode.
Workaround: There are 2 ways to work around this problem:Specify the?InitialOutput property?on the outport block of the referenced model instead of specifying the InitialValue property on the?signal object.Simulate the top model and referenced models in normal simulation mode.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch between simulation and code generation results might occur?for CORDIC atan2 function in Trigonometric Function block?when block input is negative minimal value of its data type
Description: Mismatch between simulation and code generation results might occur for a Trigonometric Function block under these conditions: Function ?parameter is set to atan2.  Approximation method  parameter is set to CORDIC. Data type of both inputs is signed integer or signed fixed-point. Value of either input is the negative minimal value of its data type. For example, the negative minimal value for int8 is?-128 .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 5, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Code generation may fail with a For-Iterator,Variant subsystem and a Merge block
Description: Code generation errors out when all below conditions are met :  ?A variant subsystem?with GeneratePreprocessorConditionals  on, contains a For-Iterator subsystem inside one of the choices  The For-Iterator subsystem drives the output of the variant subsystem, which is connected to a Merge block   An example error message:?? The signal from 'model/block1/blocksys_two/If Action Subsystem1/Variant Subsystem/VARIANT_ONE/Subsystem/Assignment' output port 1 is required to be persistent, hence this signal cannot be connected to a Merge block?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for Identify Matrix block
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets both of these conditions:  An Identity Matrix block (from the DSP System Toolbox block set) directly feeds into the Root Outport block. The signal at the output of the Identity Matrix?block has a non-Auto custom storage class (for example, ExportToFile ).

Summary: svd can return invalid NaN values in the singular vectors of some rare matrices.
Description: For a few rare matrices, [U,S,V] = svd(A) ?returns the?singular vectors in the columns of U  and V  with incorrect NaN  entries. >> [U,S,V] = svd(A);>> any(isnan(V(:))) | any(isnan(U(:)))  ans =??  ? ? ? 1?

Summary: Application does not synchronize PTP (IEEE 1588) clock to grand master PTP device
Description: A Simulink Real-Time application does not synchronize its Precision Time Protocol (PTP) clock with other parties when the following conditions are true: The application includes an IEEE 1588 Real-Time UDP  block.  The block parameter Destination IP address of transmit packets  is set to Standard PTP multicast .   If the block parameter Slave Only  is set, the protocol remains in the Initializing  state (value 4) instead of the Slave  state (value 9). If the block parameter Slave Only  is not set, the protocol enters the Master  state (value 6) instead of the Slave  state (value 9), even when a third party device (for example, Grand Master Clock) is in Master  state.
Workaround: Set the block parameter "Destination IP address of transmit packets" to "Specify" and use the address "255.255.255.255". Note that third party PTP applications in the network must be configured in the same way.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 3, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Scale to fit fails for tall images
Description: The DOM API ScaleToFit format fails to scale images that are too tall to fit between the top and bottom margins of a page. This issue occurs in both Microsoft Word and PDF output. The following script illustrates the issue. import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('repro_pdf_portrait', 'docx');open(d);plo = d.CurrentPageLayout;plo.PageMargins.Top = '4in';plo.PageMargins.Bottom = '4in';plo.PageMargins.Left = '0.25in';plo.PageMargins.Right = '0.25in';% b747 ~= 5x6 inchesimg = Image(which('b747.jpg'));img.Style = {ScaleToFit};append(d, img);close(d);rptview(d);
Workaround: Scale the image in your MATLAB report generation program:import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('repro_pdf_portrait', 'docx');open(d);plo = d.CurrentPageLayout;plo.PageMargins.Top = '4in';plo.PageMargins.Bottom = '4in';plo.PageMargins.Left = '0.25in';plo.PageMargins.Right = '0.25in';% b747 ~= 5x6 inchesimg = Image(which('b747.jpg'));%img.Style = {ScaleToFit};scaletofit(img, '8in', '3in');append(d, img);close(d);rptview(d);function scaletofit(img, maxWidth, maxHeight)    maxWidth = mlreportgen.utils.units.toPixels(maxWidth);    maxHeight = mlreportgen.utils.units.toPixels(maxHeight);    imgWidth = mlreportgen.utils.units.toPixels(img.Width);    imgHeight = mlreportgen.utils.units.toPixels(img.Height);    imgAspectRatio = imgHeight/imgWidth;    if imgHeight > maxHeight        imgHeight = maxHeight;        imgWidth = imgHeight / imgAspectRatio;    end    if imgWidth > maxWidth        imgWidth = maxWidth;        imgHeight = imgHeight * imgAspectRatio;    end    img.Width = sprintf('%4.1fpx', imgWidth);    img.Height = sprintf('%4.1fpx', imgHeight);end

Summary: Toolbox project files sometimes fail to open
Description: Toolbox project files (.prj ) created while packaging a toolbox might fail to open. When this occurs, MATLAB displays the error:?Not a valid project .? Restarting MATLAB usually resolves the issue.? This error does not prevent packaging new toolboxes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect casts when using max or min with arguments of different types
Description: If you call?max  or min ?from a Stateflow chart, generated code might contain incorrect casts if all of the following conditions are true: The chart uses C action language The first argument in the function call is a numeric literal  For example, the expression? ? ? ?b = max(0, myVar)?  where b and myVar are of type single, results in the following incorrect casts in the generated code: ? ?if (0 > (int8_T)myVar) {? ? ?b = 0.0F;? ?} else {? ? ?b = (int8_T)myVar;? ?}
Workaround: Specify the first argument as a type that can hold the value of the second argument. For the example above, you could consider using:max(single(0), myVar)max(0.0, myVar)max(Zero, myVar) (where Zero is a Stateflow constant with value 0 of type single).max(myVar,0)?

Summary: Setting a format in the Style property of a PowerPoint (PPT) API object causes MATLAB to crash
Description: Setting a format in the Style  property of a PPT API object causes?MATLAB to crash if there is a corresponding format property. For example, the following code causes MATLAB to crash because the Style  property is used instead of the Bold   property. para = mlreportgen.ppt.Paragraph();  para.Style = {mlreportgen.ppt.Bold(true)};?
Workaround: Do not specify the format in the Style property when there is a corresponding format property. For example, to specify that the text of a paragraph is bold, set the Bold property instead of specifying the Bold format in the Style property.para = mlreportgen.ppt.Paragraph();para.Bold = true;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized variable passed to AUTOSAR?Rte_Write call when subsystem signal branches to root outport and another subsystem
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for an AUTOSAR model when all of the following conditions are true: An output signal from a subsystem branches. One branch of the signal feeds a root outport. The AUTOSAR data access mode of the root outport is ExplicitSend . The other signal branch feeds another subsystem.  In the generated code, an uninitialized stack variable is passed as an argument to the Rte_Write  function.
Workaround: Place a Signal Conversion block on either branch of the branched subsystem output signal.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for Switch block whose signal source is a Constant block inside an enabled subsystem
Description: The code generated from a model may be incorrect if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a Switch block whose signal source is a Constant block that is inside an Enabled Subsystem block.?
