In the Constant block parameters dialog box, the Sample time  parameter is set to Inf . The model configuration parameter Default parameter behavior  is set to Inlined .
Workaround: Set the sample time of the Constant block that is inside the Enabled Subsystem block to -1, or clear the model configuration parameter Block reduction.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Shift operations incorrectly cast to unsigned type
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for shift operations matching the following situation:? The Casting mode  is set to Standards Compliant . The result of the bitshift operation is assigned to a floating point variable or casted to a floating point type. The value of the bitshift operation is a negative value.  In the incorrect generated code, the result of the bitshift is cast to an unsigned type and then cast to the floating point data type. An additional cast to a signed type is required to properly represent negative values. Note that the cast to unsigned is required to comply with MISRA. For example, when generating code for the following function with standards casting mode enabled?    function issue_example_2008845(x)       global out      out = double(bitshift(int64(1), int64(x)));  end The bitshift operation generated is?   out = (double)(unsigned long)((unsigned long)1L << (unsigned long)(long)x); Instead an additional cast to a signed integer type is needed prior to casting to double  out = (double)(long)(unsigned long)((unsigned long)1L << (unsigned long)x);

Summary: Simulink might crash when extracting signals from an element of a nonvirtual bus that a virtual bus contains
Description: Simulink crashes when all of these conditions apply: A virtual bus has multiple levels of hierarchy and contains a nonvirtual bus. A Bus Selector block selects a multidimensional signal from this nonvirtual bus. A Demux block extracts signals from this multidimensional signal.
Workaround: For R2018b, unzip and run the attached script every time you start Simulink. The script enables Simulink to convert a nonvirtual bus into a virtual bus when the nonvirtual bus is contained in a virtual bus.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 4, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with a?Bus Assignment block and?an?Assignment block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a?Bus Assignment block block and an?Assignment?block.  An?Assignment ?block connects to the?assignment input ports?of the?Bus Assignment ?block. The?Assignment ?block and?Bus Assignment block are inside ?the same?For-Iterator ?or?While-Iterator ?subsystem.
Workaround: Clear the model configuration parameter Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks.

Summary: Accessing property of signal object in model workspace can crash MATLAB
Description: If you create signal objects in the model workspace and access properties of these signal objects (such as Min  or Max ) in block parameter expressions, this can cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: To avoid this crash, use one of these options:Do not access properties of signal objects in block parameter expressions.Define signal objects in the base workspace or in Simulink data dictionaries.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for MATLAB code consisting of matrix accesses that use logical indexing
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for MATLAB code consisting of the following elements: The code contains a loop in which one matrix (A) subscripts and grabs elements from another matrix (B). The loop contains writes to all elements of A.  For example, incorrect generated code occurs for the following MATLAB code:? function B = example( in )    B = zeros(1,400);    A = (4:13);    for i=1:10        B(A) = in;        A(i) = 8;    endend The incorrect generated C code is below. Notice the write to 'A ' and the indexing operation using 'A ' inside the loop are incorrectly removed.? void example(const double in[10], double B[400]){  int i;  memset(&B[0], 0, 400U * sizeof(double));  for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {    B[7] = in[i];  }} See also Bug Report ID?2041681.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer when assigning to a variable of Complex type while its being read on RHS with an aliased handle class object
Description: Incorrect code generation might occur for MATLAB code when the following conditions are true: A MATLAB function creates at least one handle class object and its alias. A class property is a complex  array. The MATLAB function contains an assignment from the aliased object to the handle class variable in a loop (the variables from condition 1).  Consider the following example??where the function?createMCOSAlias ?returns an alias (or reference) to the input variable:??  function [cv] = example_2005746(ca)  persistent handle_ca  if isempty(handle_ca)  ? ? handle_ca = handleVar;  ? ? handle_ca.value =? ca;  end    handle_alias_ca = createMCOSAlias( handle_ca);  global P1  for k1 = 12:43  ? ? for k2 = 1:22  ? ? ? ? handle_ca.value( k2 + 1, k1 - 7 ) =...  ? ? ? ? ? ? cast( handle_alias_ca.value( 6, 33 ).* P1( 1, 1 ), 'double' );  ? ? end  end    ca = handle_alias_ca.value;  end  In this?MATLAB code, the handle class object,?handle_ca? and?its alias,? ?handle_alias_ca ?satisfy condition 1. The input to the function is a complex array (ca ) which is assigned to the variable of handle class object, which satisfies condition 2. Finally, the assignment inside the loop from?handle_alias_ca  to?handle_ca  satisfies condition 3. The??generated C code shown below is incorrectly?reading the value of??handle_alias_ca->value[869].re  which was written in the statement above it. Instead it should have used the original before write in previous statement.? handle_alias_ca = createMCOSAlias(&handle_ca);??d = P1.re;??d1 = P1.im;??for (k1 = 0; k1 < 32; k1++) {? ??for (k2 = 0; k2 < 22; k2++) {? ? ? i = (k2 + 27 * (k1 + 4)) + 1;? ? ? handle_ca.value[i].re = handle_alias_ca->value[869].re * d -? ? ? ? handle_alias_ca->value[869].im * d1;? ? ? handle_ca.value[i].im = handle_alias_ca->value[869].re * d1 +? ? ? ? handle_alias_ca->value[869].im * d;? ? ? if (*emlrtBreakCheckR2012bFlagVar != 0) {? ? ? ? emlrtBreakCheckR2012b(sp);? ? ? }? ? }  The generated C code for the above function should be the following where the writes to a real and imaginary part are stored in the temporary variables 're' and 'im' before the reads occur as is shown below: handle_alias_ca = createMCOSAlias(&handle_ca);  ? d = P1.re;  ? d1 = P1.im;  ? for (k1 = 0; k1 < 32; k1++) {  ? ? for (k2 = 0; k2 < 22; k2++) {  ? ? ? re  = handle_alias_ca->value[869].re * d - handle_alias_ca->value[869].im * d1;  ? ? ? im  = handle_alias_ca->value[869].re * d1 + handle_alias_ca->value[869].im * d;?  ? ? ? i = (k2 + 27 * (k1 + 4)) + 1;  ? ? ? handle_ca.value[i].re = re;  ? ? ? handle_ca.value[i].im = im;  ? ? ? if (*emlrtBreakCheckR2012bFlagVar != 0) {  ? ? ? ? emlrtBreakCheckR2012b(sp);  ? ? ? }  ? ? }  To reproduce this bug, see the example in the attached zip file  'example_2005746.zip' .

Summary: Merging data dictionary files within a GitHub project sets property HasAccessToBaseWorkspace to true
Description: The property HasAccessToBaseWorkspace? for a Simulink data dictionary ?is automatically set to true ?when the following conditions are true: You are working in a Windows environment. You use GitHub to manage your Simulink data dictionaries (.sldd  files). You merge .sldd  files from different GitHub branches within MATLAB.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation results may vary between Rapid Accelerator mode and Normal mode for?a model containing a For Iterator Subsystem
Description: Simulation in the Rapid Accelerator mode may not match the Normal mode?for a model that meets the following conditions: There is an Assignment blockconnected to a Unit Delay block in a For Iteratorsubsystem. The?Initial Condition ?of the Unit Delay block initialization may or may not be zero.
Workaround: For releases prior to R2018b, unzip the attachment and save the fix_2002452.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run this script in each MATLAB session before opening your model.

Summary: Code generation errors out with variants and data generation to a shared location
Description: Code generation may error out when all the following conditions are met:?  Data is generated to a shared location i.e. the parameter UtilityFuncGeneration  is set to Shared   Data is reused in more than one location in the model, with atleast one usage inside a Variant Subsystem choice.  The Variant Subsystem is configured for generating preprocessor conditionals(GeneratePreprocessorConditionals  turned on   Example error message:? Error:? Error occurred when generating shared data declaration: The contents of the following objects do not match those in the current slprj directory:  Content 'extern real_T G;' for G does not match content '#if NONLINEAR? extern real_T G;? #endif /* NONLINEAR */' in the shared header file a.h   To continue, either manually remove the slprj folder, or rename these objects Please note that removing the slprj folder does not fix it.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with a For Each subsystem block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a For Each  subsystem. The signal driving the partitioned?input port of a For Each Subsystem?also drives an Outport  block. The Outport  block is inside a reusable subsystem, or it is ?a root-level Outport block? of a referenced model? block.  ?

Summary: Configuration parameters Array layout?and Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout?are ignored
Description: Simulink Design Verifier considers?Array layout  to be 'Column-major'  and?Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout ?to be?'Off' , irrespective of their actual values. This may result in incorrect analysis results. ?

Summary: add_block?and replace_block functions cause port placement and connectivity issues forEntity Store, Resource Acquirer, and Resource Releaser blocks
Description: Entity Store, Resource Acquirer and Resource Releaser blocks have the following issues when added to a model.? Using the?add_block  function causes?the blockoutput port?to appear on top of the block instead of on the right side ofthe block. Using?the replace_block  function causes?the block portsto be?unconnected.
Workaround: Open the block dialog. In?the Statistics tab, enable any statistic.Click Apply.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for multiple calls to functions writing to global data inside of an if statement within at least two level loop nests
Description: Incorrect generated code might occur for MATLAB code that satisfies all of the following the conditions:? The MATLAB function contains at-least one for  loop.? The for -loop contains a call to a function which writes to some global data.? The for -loop contains at least one if  statement. The if  statement contains a while loop.? The if  statement contains another call to a function which writes to the same global data.  In the generated code, the order of the calls inside the for loop is incorrectly changed which might cause incorrect results.  For example, in the below MATLAB function: function [IA1, IA2, IA3, IA4] = funToCall(IA1, IA2, IA3, IA4)?????for P3 = 8:9?????????for P4 = 5:7?? ? ? ? ? ? funOnPath2( IA3( 1:1:1, ?2:1:2 ) );?????????????if cast( IA2( 6 ).f1, ?'logical' )?????????????????P1 = 3;?????????????????while P1<=5?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? funOnPath4();?????????????????????P1 = P1 + 1;?????????????????end?????????????end?????????end?????end? end  function funOnPath4    coder.inline( 'never' );    global P12    P12( P23, 1 ) = [ cast( P22( P21, P23 ), 'uint64' ) ]; end ? function funOnPath2(IA3_1_1_1_2_1_2_)    coder.inline('never');    global P12    P12( 1, 1 ) = IA3_1_1_1_2_1_2_( 1 ).f2( 1:1:1, 1:1:1 );end funOnPath2  and funOnPath4  write to the global variable P12.  In the incorrect generated code, the writes to the global data in  funOnPath4  executes before the writes to the global data in  funOnPath2 . Following ?is the incorrectly generated C code. void funToCall(const unsigned short IA1[40], const struct0_T IA2[6],  const struct1_T IA3[2], const struct2_T IA4[5]){  int P3;  (void)IA1;  (void)IA4;  if (!isInitialized_funToCall) {    funToCall_initialize();  }  for (P3 = 0; P3 < 2; P3++) {    if (IA2[5].f1 != 0UL) {      funOnPath4();      funOnPath4();      funOnPath4();    }    funOnPath2(IA3[1].f1, IA3[1].f2);    funOnPath2(IA3[1].f1, IA3[1].f2);    funOnPath2(IA3[1].f1, IA3[1].f2);  }}

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code for parameter partitions in For Each subsystem
Description: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code under the following scenario:The model contains a masked For Each subsystem block. The For Each block configures a parameter partition for a multi-dimensional mask parameter. The parameter partition is used in a Lookup, Prelookup or any other block that would use the starting address of the parameter partition.  Simulink Coder will use the base address of the mask parameter instead of the parameter partition as the starting address.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a

Summary: mlreportgen.report.Figure?reporter might produce images that are cropped or not centered in PDF documents
Description: The mlreportgen.report.Figure  reporter produces images that are cropped or not centered when both of these conditions are true: The snapshot format is SVG. The screen resolution is not 96 pixels per inch.
Workaround: Try printing the figure to an image file and then inserting the image file into the report by using an mlreportgen.dom.Image object.% Print the figure, where fig is the handle to your figure print(fig,'-dsvg','my_snapshot.svg'); % Create image reporter and set scale-to-fit to true imgr = mlreportgen.dom.Image('my_snapshot.svg'); imgr.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.ScaleToFit(true)};

Summary: Nominal friction scaling factor, mu?is not visible in the Vehicle Body 3DOF block mask
Description: In the Vehicle Body 3DOF block mask, the?Nominal friction scaling factor, mu ?parameter is not visible when you do both of these: Set the Axle forces  parameter to External longitudinal forces  or External longitudinal velocity.  On the Input signals  pane, clear External friction.
Workaround: You can do either of the following:?Use set_param?command to set the?Mu?block parameter, orOn the?Input signals?pane, select?External friction to create the input port Mu. Connect the input port Mu?to a Constant block with the friction value.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect?results are possible for a model containing?Switch and Data Store Memory blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions:? The model contains Switch, Data Store Memory, Data Store Read, and Data Store Write blocks. The Switch block's control port connects to a Constant block.  The relative execution order of the Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks might be different in simulation than in the generated code.
Workaround: Choose either of these workarounds:Specify the priority of Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks to enforce their relative execution order.Disable the elimination of the Switch block by deselecting the?Block reduction?model configuration parameter.?

Summary: Exceptions when comparing Simulink models with changes in both MATLAB function block code and annotation text
Description: If there are changes in bothMATLAB function block code and annotation text in the Simulinkmodel comparison, you see a Java exception in the MATLAB command windowafter you select both of these sets of changes in the comparison report.You cannot see the details of both the annotation changes and theMATLAB function block changes. To see the 'other' set of detailed changes, you must close and rerun the comparison.

Summary: FormalImage reporter incorrectly scales an image
Description: The FormalImage reporter might incorrectly scale an image to fit a report page when the following conditions are true: The report program adds a FormalImage object to a Chapter object.   The layout specified by the Chapter object is different from the layout specified by the Report object.  For example: import mlreportgen.report.*import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Report('bug1','pdf');img = FormalImage('Image',which('ngc6543a.jpg'));img.ScaleToFit = true;img.Caption = 'Cat''s Eye Nebula';ch = Chapter('Title','Image Bug');ch.Layout.Landscape = true;add(ch,PageBreak);add(ch,img);add(rpt,ch);close(rpt);rptview(rpt);
Workaround: A report program can scale the image instead of relying on the FormalImage reporter to do the scaling:import mlreportgen.report.*import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Report('bug1', 'pdf');img = FormalImage('Image',which('ngc6543a.jpg'));img.Caption = 'NGC6543a';img.ScaleToFit = false;scale(img, 9, 6);ch = Chapter('Title','Image Bug');ch.Layout.Landscape = true;add(ch, PageBreak);add(ch, img);add(rpt, ch);close(rpt);rptview(rpt);function scale(formalImg,toWidth,toHeight)    import mlreportgen.dom.*    import mlreportgen.utils.*        domImg = Image(formalImg.Image);        domImgHeight = units.toInches(domImg.Height);    domImgWidth = units.toInches(domImg.Width);        imgAspectRatio = domImgHeight/domImgWidth;    if domImgHeight > toHeight        domImgHeight = toHeight;        domImgWidth = domImgHeight / imgAspectRatio;    end    if domImgWidth > toWidth        domImgWidth = toWidth;        domImgHeight = domImgHeight * imgAspectRatio;    end    formalImg.Width = sprintf('%0.2fin',domImgWidth);    formalImg.Height = sprintf('%0.2fin',domImgHeight);    end

Summary: Hilbert spectrum output of the Hilbert-Huang transform (HHT) is incorrect
Description: The Hilbert spectrum output, P ,  of hht ?is incorrect. The plot of the Hilbert spectrum obtained when hht ?is called with no output arguments is correct, but returning the Hilbert spectrum results in the incorrect output. ?

Summary: Models with Pipe (2P) or 3-Zone Pipe (2P) block might cause simulation to stall or fail
Description: Simulation might stall or fail for models with a Pipe (2P) block from the Simscape Foundation library or a 3-Zone Pipe (2P) block from the Simscape Fluids library. This occurs because?the Gnielinski correlation  equation, which is?used to calculate the heat transfer, may become singular under some conditions, such as low flow rate.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with a?Bus Assignment block?or an?Assignment block
Description: The results of an Accelerator mode simulation might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a?Bus Assignment block?or?an?Assignment?block.  The input to the assignment input port of the Bus Assignment block is one or more signals from a bus.? The signal that connects to the assignment input ports of the?Bus Assignment block  or the U-input data port of the?Assignment block? drives a root-level output port.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, deselect the Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks parameter.

Summary: U suffix on integer literals might be missing from the generated code causing a??MISRA 10.6?violation
Description: The generated code might contain a MISRA 10.6 violation for a model that contains a signal with an unsigned integer data type that is smaller than int16  and contains a custom storage class.For this modeling pattern, MISRA compliant code should contain a U  suffix in the assignment to the signal variable with the custom storage class. The MISRA violation occurs because the integer literal in the assignment statement is missing the U  suffix.

Summary: MATLAB does not honor the MATLAB proxy settings for a connection to dws.mathworks.com during startup
Description: MATLAB does not honor the MATLAB proxy settings for a connection to dws.mathworks.com . This connection occurs on MATLAB startup to perform a check for product updates.  This bug does not affect system proxy settings outside of MATLAB that are configured.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initialization of state for Unit Delay block that reuses the same variable for its state and input signal in generated code
Description: The generated code contains an incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The input to the Unit Delay block is not a conditionally-executed subsystem.? The input to the Unit Delay block is a constant value which does not equal the value of the Initial condition  parameter of the Unit Delay block. In the generated code, reuse occurs between the Unit Delay block state and its input signal.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with a?Bus Assignment block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a?Bus Assignment block.?  The input to the assignment input port of the Bus Assignment block is one or more signals from a bus. An Assignment  block also connects to the?assignment input ports?of the Bus Assignment  block. The Assignment  block has a Y0-input initialization?signal .  The Assignment  block and Bus Assignment block are inside  the same For-Iterator  or While-Iterator  subsystem.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, deselect the?Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment?blocks parameter.

Summary: Code generation error when model has Model block in SIL/PIL simulation mode connected to?Merge block
Description: A code generation error occurs under these conditions:? A top model or a model in a reference hierarchy contains a Model block with?Simulation mode ?set to?Software-in-the-loop (SIL) ?or?Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) ?mode. the Model block is connected to a Merge block.  The error is:  You cannot connect the signal from Model Reference block?'<model>/ <block>' output port <port number> to a Merge block when the Model block is in Software-in-the-Loop (SIL) or Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL) simulation?mode. To workaround this limitation, ?first make sure that no errors occur when?the Model block is in Accelerator simulation mode. Next, ?restore the Model?block simulation mode to its previous setting. Then, ?if the Model block is?inside a conditionally executed subsystem that is connected to the Merge block, add a Signal Conversion block (Signal copy) between the Model block and the?Outport of the conditionally executed subsystem. Alternatively, ?if the Model?block is connected directly to the Merge block, ?add an Enabled Subsystem?between the Model block and the Merge block that passes the signal straight?through. Configure the Enable Port of the new Enabled Subsystem so that only?one Merge block input is active at a time.

Summary: Indirect assignments for Variant Subsystem choice output connected to ground block
Description: A Variant Subsystem output connected to a constant parameter in one choice?and to a ground block in another choice, may generate the below code with indirect assignments.    For example :    void In1(void)?  {?  #if a == 1?      ? mdl1_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut1 = 1.0;?      ? mdl 1_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut2 = 1.0;?      ? mdl 1_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut3 = 1.0;?    #endif?      ? mdl1_Y.Out1 = test1_18b_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut1;?    ? mdl1_Y.Out2 = test1_18b_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut3;?    ? mdl1_Y.Out3 = test1_18b_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut2;?    }    Where, the expected code is :  ?  void In1(void)?  {?    #if a == 1?    ? mdl1_B.Out1 = 1.0;?    ? mdl1_B.Out3 = 1.0;?    ? mdl1_B.Out2 = 1.0;?    #endif? ??  }

Summary: Simulink Coder build process fails on Linux for models containing UDP Send or UDP Receive?blocks when certain GCC C/C++ version compilers are used.
Description: When Simulink Coder is used with models containing UDP Send or UDP Receive blocks, and compiled using some of the recent versions of GCC C/C++ compilers such as GCC 4.9.x on Ubuntu 16.04.6 LTS, the build process fails. These versions of GCC C/C++ compiler require the link flags -lm -ldl to appear last in the build command line. The gcc command line did not have these link flags listed last, and the link phase of the build fails. Errors will indicate undefined references to functions such as 'dlsym' and 'dlcose', and that the build fails to generate all binary outputs.
Workaround: Manually call the gcc build command in a Linux shell with the link flags?-lm -ldl only appearing at the very end of the gcc build command line.

Summary: Potential incorrect results for models containing Data Store Memory block resolving to Simulink.Signal
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may provide incorrect results for a model when the following conditions are met:  The model contains model references One of these referenced models contains a data store which resolves to a Simulink.Signal object (call this data store 'A').  In addition to the above condition one of the following 2 conditions should be met:  There is a separate Data Store Memory block in a parent model with the same name, which does not resolve to a Simulink.Signal object  'A' is used inside of a MATLAB Function Block or Stateflow chart   If the above mentioned conditions are satisfied for a model, the replacement model that Simulink Design Verifier creates may produce incorrect results.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C Caller blocks with Mux blocks as inputs generate incorrect code
Description: C Caller block receiving an input signal from a Mux block generates incorrect code. The expected generated code should combine the input signals to the Mux block into one vector signal and call the C Caller block's function once with the combined vector signal. The incorrect generated code creates a call to the C Caller block's function for each input signal to the Mux block.
Workaround: Replace Mux blocks with Vector Concatenate blocks as inputs to C Caller blocks.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 4, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Generating code for a model that contains a Stateflow Chart produces an error that mentions tlc
Description: Generating code for a model that meets the following conditions results in a  tlc  error:  The model contains a Stateflow Chart that uses the C action language with string inputs.  The model configuration parameter Import Custom Code  is selected.    Generating code results in a build failure and a message similar to the following:  Main program:==> matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenRateGroupedCodeFromModule(5237)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenerateCodeFromModule(5119)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcn(256)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodyFcnCache(501)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcnEntry(608)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc:FcnGenBodySysCache(1594)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc:SLibGenBodyCache(5940)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\formatwide.tlc:<NONE>(55) ?

Summary: Simulink Test Manager figure capture error when IntegerHandle property is off
Description: In Simulink Test Manager, an error occurs when Store MATLAB figures  is on and a test creates a figure with the IntegerHandle  property off . The test fails with this error message:? Invalid input for argument 1 (rhs1):  Value must be a scalar.?

Summary: Polyspace compilation might fail with message: error: identifier "va_start" is undefined
Description: If?a header file contains functions that call?va_start()  and va_end() , Polyspace Bug Finder might fail to compile if the header file is used with the option -include . For example, if a header file my_header.h  contains this code: #include void foo(int n,? ...){  va_list vl;  va_start(vl,?n);  va_end(vl);}  You can see the following error message during the compilation of my_file.cpp   if you use the analysis option?-include?my_header.h .   Verifying my_file.cpp (X/Y)    <pathto>\myfile.h,? line XX: error: identifier "va_start" is undefined  my_file.cpp: warning: Failed compilation./<pathto>

Summary: Report Explorer Simulink Data Dictionary component with access to base workspace might cause report generation error
Description: In Report Explorer, report generation of a report with a Simulink Data Dictionary component might end with an error when both of these conditions are true: The data dictionary is in the?Dictionaries in list that you specified in the    Simulink Data Dictionary Loop component.       The data dictionary has access to the base workspace.?    The error message is similar to :  In component Simulink Data Dictionary: The specified entry is not present in the dictionary"  ?"Error while evaluating TimerFcn for timer 'timer-1'?         ?

Summary: polyspace-configure does not correctly handle relative paths to preincludes in a build command
Description: If you use a preinclude option with your build command, for instance the -include  option of gcc , and the path to the include file is relative, polyspace-configure  does not handle the build option correctly. For example, if you call polyspace-configure  this way: polyspace-configure gcc -include limits.h main.c?  it produces this warning:? WARNING: Could not find pre-include file limits.h?  And the generated options file or project file contains an incorrect path to?limits.h .

Summary: Signals cannot be mapped to ArTypedPerInstanceMemory when signal is?inside Variant or For Each Subsystems
Description: In an AUTOSAR Software Component model, signal lines cannot be mapped to ArTypedPerInstanceMemory inside Variant Subsystems. The following error occurs during code generation: The model [model name]?has a subsystem block [block name] which contains a block () that has a '_PIM' storage class registered with it. The storage class is unstructured. Data inside of 'Variant Subsystem' or 'For Each Subsystem' can not be unstructured.
Workaround: Use a Data Store Memory block to represent the ArTypedPerInstanceMemory instead of mapping the signal line directly.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: An export-function model with merged buses generate unconditional code for Variant Subsystem outputs
Description: A Variant subsystem containing buses may generate unconditional code for its conditional outports when all of the following conditions are satisfied:  The Simulink model is an export-function model containing variant subsystem.  The parameter Propagate conditions outside of variant subsystem  is turned on.  The Variant subsystem has flexible interfaces.?  Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  parameter is turned on.  The inactive variant choice contains a Merge block.  The Merge block inside the inactive choice merges input bus signals.   You may notice that some?of the outports are shown as conditional in the model, may appear unconditional?in the generated code.

Summary: Simulink cache file might become corrupted
Description: After you run or build a model reference hierarchy with automatic parallel building enabled (using Parallel Computing Toolbox), Simulink might throw an error on subsequent runs or builds: The Simulink cache?<model_path>\mymodel.slxc?has been corrupted. To use this Simulink cache, you need to recreate it.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: At model?compilation, Simulink.LookupTable object Min and Max values are ignored when set via command line
Description: At model?compilation, Simulink.LookupTable  object Min  and Max  values are ignored when the object properties are set and applied to the lookup table via the command line. These Simulink.LookupTable  object properties are affected: Table  Breakpoints   When the object is applied via the lookup table block dialog,?these object properties are honored.

Summary: Model Advisor?edit-time violations are reported for a model when saved in Code Perspective view
Description: If you save a model while the Code Perspective view is active, edit-time checking for all supported Model Advisor checks stays enabled even after exiting Code Perspective.?
Workaround: You can turn off the edit-time checks by selecting?Analysis?>?Model Advisor >?Display Advisor Checks in?Editor and re-saving the model.

Summary: MATLAB crash when running Model Advisor check for dry hydraulic nodes on subsystem with unconnected Physical Modeling connector port
Description: MATLAB crashes when running the Model Advisor check for dry hydraulic nodes on a Simulink model which contains a specific block connection topology. The crash occurs when a Simulink model contains a subsystem with a Physical Modeling connector port that is both: Connected outside the subsystem? Unconnected inside the subsystem
Workaround: There are two different workaround strategies: Connect the Physical Modeling connector port inside the subsystem to another block with a Physical Modeling?port.Remove Physical Modeling connector ports which are unconnected inside subsystems.

Summary: AUTOSAR model update reverts library block signal mapping from ArTypedPerInstanceMemory or StaticMemory to Auto
Description: Updating an AUTOSAR model, or saving and reopening the model, incorrectly reverts the AUTOSAR mapping of a signal if both of the following conditions exist: The signal is mapped to ArTypedPerInstanceMemory  or StaticMemory . The source of the signal is one of the following: A library block. A block inside of a library block.   Under these conditions, the mapping of the signal reverts to Auto . Effectively, the described signals cannot be configured as ArTypedPerInstanceMemory  or StaticMemory .

Summary: Block reduction leads to unconditional assignment of signal with non-Auto storage class
Description: When a signal in a model has a non-Auto storage class, and that signal is the output of a block inside a conditionally executed subsystem, the expectation is that the code generated for the model will conditionally assign the variable corresponding to that signal. However, if that block is reduced, the signal is then computed by its source block, which may be a block that is outside the conditionally executed subsystem. In that case, the variable in the code will be assigned unconditionally, which is unexpected behavior.
Workaround: Disable block reduction for the model, by clearing the model configuration parameter Simulation Target > Block reduction.

Summary: slreq.refreshLinkDependencies API fails with an incorrect error message
Description: Updating the link dependency information for Simulink Requirements link sets using  slreq.refreshLinkDependencies  API command results in an incorrect error message like below:  Error using char? Conversion of element 1 from <missing> to character vector is not supported.?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/registerOpenArtifacts?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/updateMetadataFromLinkset?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/buildProjectMetadataForLinksetFile?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/buildProjectMetadata?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/reIndexProjectMetadata?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/reIndexMetadata?  Error in slreq.refreshLinkDependencies?

Summary: Error when using rapid accelerator mode with parameter AdvancedOptControl?set to value -SLCI
Description: Simulink might produce an error while simulating a model in rapid accelerator mode when a model?contains a MATLAB Function block and has the parameter AdvancedOptControl ?set to the value?-SLCI .  The error is due to an Inf or NaN initializer element that is not constant and is similar to the following:? example_raccel_model.c(42): error C2099: initializer is not a constant ?
Workaround: Remove '-SLCI' from the 'AdvancedOptControl' parameter.? For example >> set_param(gcs, 'AdvancedOptControl', '');

Summary: Axes toolbar is slow to appear or does not appear on direct hover
Description: The axes toolbar is slow to appear over the set of axes.  In some cases, if you do not hover over the set of axes before hovering over the axes toolbar, then the axes toolbar does not appear at all.

Summary: Using Outgoing Links Editor dialog may result in breaking existing links to requirements
Description: When you use the legacy Outgoing Links Editor to modify existing links in Simulink Requirements, some links may appear broken after making changes to other links.? For example, if you open the Outgoing Links Editor for a Simulink block that has two outgoing links to?requirements?in .slreqx, and delete the 1st link, the second link may be highlighted as unresolved (yellow icon) in the Links View.? This does not happen every time, but may happen under certain conditions that may not be immediately clear.? For example, this may happen when the remaining link stores a relative path to the local file, while the current user preference for document file path reference is NONE (short name only) or ABSOLUTE (full path to file).
Workaround: Do not use the Outgoing Links Editor to modifying existing links.? You can still use it to add new links where source object does not yet have links.If you need to delete an existing link, you can use the Delete action in the Links View in Simulink Requirements.?If you need to modify the description label for the link, you can edit it in Simulink Requirements?Editor or using the newer (slreq.*) command-line API.? Note that it is not possible to modify the type of the link in Outgoing Links Editor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect raw-to-physical and physical-to-raw conversion errors in A2L files
Description: When using A2L files with LINEAR and COMPU_VTAB characteristics, the COMPU_METHODs for converting between physical and raw data might compute incorrect results.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code for a model with flexible bodies and certain types of output signals
Description: MATLAB crashes when generating C code for a Simscape Multibody model containing both of the following: 1) At least one General Flexible Beam block 2) At least one of the following modeling patterns: Any block from the "Joints" sub-library, when sensing accelerations, actuation forces or torques, and when enabling any of the outputs from the "Composite Force/Torque Sensing" section The Transform Sensor block from the "Frames and Transforms" sub-library, sensing any of the outputs from the "Angular Acceleration" and "Acceleration" sections Any block from the "Constraints" sub-library, sensing any forces and torques    This can occur during Rapid Accelerator simulation or when generating C code using Simulink Coder or Embedded Coder, for example.

Summary: Running certain web apps causes MATLAB Web App Server performance degradation?
Description: Some MATLAB apps created using App Designer use 100% of the CPU when running for a long time. These apps, when deployed as web apps, slow down the response of the MATLAB Web App Server and cause the performance of the server to degrade.
Workaround: Restart the MATLAB Web App Server service. The name of the service depends on the operating system you are using:Windows Service Name:? MATLAB Web Apps (R2018b) or MATLAB Web Apps (R2019a)Linux and macOS Service Name: mw-webapps-R2018b or mw-webapps-R2019a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data Type Conversion block with Nearest Integer rounding mode might sometimes generate HDL incorrect code
Description: Conversion of a floating-point value to a fixed-point value might generate incorrect HDL code that differs from Simulink behavior when: The floating-point value is outside the representable range of the data type such as?single(-0.024180471897125)  The conversion is performed using a Data Type Conversion block with Integer rounding mode  set to Nearest ?

Summary: Simulink Diagram reporter can generate illegible diagrams
Description: The Simulink Diagram reporter can generate illegible diagrams. This occurs when a complex diagram contains fine detail. For example, the operators on arithmetic blocks are illegible in complex block diagrams.
Workaround: Set the Scaling, MaxHeight, and MaxWidth properties of the slreportgen.report.Diagram reporter as follows:    diagReporter.Scaling = "zoom";    diagReporter.MaxHeight = "100in";    diagReporter.MaxWidth = "100in";This script uses these settings to generate fully legible diagrams:import mlreportgen.dom.*import mlreportgen.report.*import slreportgen.report.*import slreportgen.finder.*model_name = 'f14';load_system(model_name);rpt = slreportgen.report.Report('myreport', 'pdf');rpt.CompileModelBeforeReporting = false;finder = DiagramFinder(model_name);results = find(finder);for result = results    chapter = Chapter('Title', result.Name);    diagReporter = getReporter(result);    diagReporter.Snapshot.ScaleToFit = true;    diagReporter.Scaling = "zoom";    diagReporter.MaxHeight = "100in";    diagReporter.MaxWidth = "100in";    add(chapter, diagReporter);    add(rpt, chapter);endclose(rpt);close_system(model_name);rptview(rpt);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect?results are possible for a model containing?floating-point to fixed-point data type conversion when the Code replacement library is?AUTOSAR 4.0
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a floating-point to fixed-point data type conversion (cast) operation, performed by a Data Type Conversion block or another block such as an Addition block. In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Code replacement library  parameter? is set to AUTOSAR 4.0 ? In the generated code, the data type conversion operation is replaced with AUTOSAR's? data type conversion routine Mfl_Cvrt_f32_outtype  . The fixed-point data type of the data type conversion operation output has a non-unit slope (that is, a value other than 1).  The generated value for the second input argument for Mfl_Cvrt_f32_outtype   incorrectly uses the fixed exponent  of the output data type rather than the expected value of?-1* fixed exponent.

Summary: Detailed description of dead logic found in C/C++ custom code is not reported
Description: When you perform dead logic analysis on a model that contains C/C++ custom code and if dead logic is found in the code, Simulink Design Verifier does not report the detailed description of the dead logic.    For example, if there is dead logic in C/C++ code associated with S-Function or C Caller block, then the presence of dead logic is reported. But, the details such as C/C++ file name and the line number that results in dead logic are not reported.

Summary: Passing arrays of fixed-point values to the complex function can cause a size mismatch error during code generation
Description: This issue occurs when all of the following conditions are true:? In your MATLAB code, an input to the complex ?function is a multi-dimensional array of fixed-point values. The code generator can determine the sizes of some of the dimensions of this array. The code generator cannot determine the sizes of the other dimensions of this array. These dimensions are variable-size.  In such situations, the code generator can treat all dimensions of the array that the?complex ?function returns as variable-size. This behavior can cause a size mismatch in a subsequent assignment or expression. Here is an example MATLAB code that is affected by this issue. function D = test(n) % B is a vector. B = repmat(fi(1,1,16,10), n, 1); % C is treated as a matrix during code generation. Both dimensions of C are% treated as variable-size because the value of the fixed size of the% second dimension is lost when complex() is called. C = complex(B,B); % D is a vector. D = repmat(fi(complex(1),1,16,10), n, 1); % Size mismatch:% the left hand side is 1x1% the right hand side is 1x? D(1,:) = C(1,:); This issue affects MATLAB Coder and the use of MATLAB in Simulink via MATLAB Function blocks, MATLAB System blocks or MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when BLAS functions are called in a nested context
Description: MATLAB might crash when following conditions are met: BLAS functions are called in nested functions The sub-function contains coder.inline('never')   For example, the following code pattern causes MATLAB to crash: function a = simple_blas(b, c, d) ? ? t = simple_mul(b, c);??? ? a = t * d;??end?  function t = simple_mul(a, b)?? ?coder.inline('never');?? ?t = a * b;?end?  You may experience similar issues with FFT  and LAPACK  functions.?
Workaround: Avoid using?coder.inline('never') when BLAS functions are called in a nested context.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink ignores the runtime value of a model argument when used as a Model Workspace data object's value
Description: For a model workspace data object, if you specify value with an expression that includes a model argument, definition value of the model argument is used during simulation. Simulink does not use the model argument's updated value from parent model during simulation.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: EtherCAT Async SSC/SoE Upload and Download blocks stop functioning after first execution
Description: EtherCAT Async SSC/SoE Upload and Download blocks work correctly when executed in a model the first time only, but not after that. These asynchronous SoE blocks read or write only the first time each block is enabled during model execution. You can use the write block for one-time initialization of parameters on a drive.

Summary: slreq.importViewSettings command does not work if the Requirements Editor or requirements perspective is used in the current session.
Description: slreq.importViewSettings  function works only if the Requirements Editor has not been opened in the current MATLAB session, and the requirements perspective has not been opened in the current MATLAB session.  slreq.exportViewSettings  function works only when the Requirements Editor is closed.

Summary: Verification stuck in the C to Intermediate Language phase
Description: When translating a procedure that uses a large, complex array, the verification can appear to be stuck in the "C to Intermediate Language" phase, with an indication like the following:? ?- translating procedure foo1 (92 / 103)  ?- translating procedure foo2 (93 / 103)

Summary: Index exceeds the number of array elements error when importing requirements from Excel
Description: When importing requirements from Microsoft Excel, if the unique ID column is the last column in the spreadsheet, the import operation can result in an error:? Index exceeds the number of array elements?

Summary: Polyspace-configure might miss some build information on Bazel when using Linux containers
Description: The polyspace-configure  command might miss some build information on Bazel when using Linux containers. The command can crash as a result and display the message Segmentation fault .

Summary: 'makima'?interpolation produces incorrect results with permuted?grid?coordinates and values.
Description: The 'makima' interpolation method produces incorrect results when operating on a permutedversion of the grid coordinates and grid values in 2-D, 3-D, and N-Dinterpolation with griddedInterpolant ,interp2 , interp3 , and interpn . The fix for this issue adjusts the underlying 'makima' formula, which changes the results in any number of dimensions, including 1-D.For 1-D 'makima'  interpolationwith griddedInterpolant  or interp1 , the results in R2019b andlater releases are slightly different, but equally valid.? As an example of the issue,  'makima'? interpolation on a 2-D grid (x,y,V)  does not produce the same results as interpolation on the 2-D grid (y,x,V.')  obtained by swapping the x  and y  coordinates and transposing the grid values V .? Namely, in the following example, the interpolation results Vq1  and Vq2  should be the same (up to a small numerical round-off difference), but instead differ by a large amount: ? ? % Grid coordinates (x,y)? ??x = 1:10;? ??y = 1:10;? ??% Grid values: V(i,j) represents the value at grid node (x(i),y(j))?? ??V = peaks(10);? ??% Query coordinates: new points where we want to interpolate? ??xq = linspace(1, 10, 100);? ??yq = xq;?? ??% Interpolate the original 2-D grid (x,y,V)? ??G1 = griddedInterpolant({x,y},V, 'makima');? ??Vq1 = G1({xq, yq});? ??% Interpolate the transposed grid (y,x,V.') and transpose back? ??V_swap_xy = V.';? ??G2 = griddedInterpolant({y,x},V_swap_xy, 'makima'); ? ??Vq2 = G2({yq,xq});? ??Vq2 = Vq2.';? ??% The difference between the two interpolation results?Vq1?and?Vq2?should be zero? ? % (or around 1e-15 to account for different numerical round-off between the two).? ? % But this is not the case:? ??max(max(abs(Vq1-Vq2)))? ??ans =? ??? ? 0.0130

Summary: LibGetNumRuntimeExportedTasks always return 1
Description: LibGetNumRuntimeExportedTasks  always return 1 which may result in incorrect answer.

Summary: Aerospace Blockset block World Magnetic Model 2015 block and Aerospace Toolbox wrldmagm function do not support WMM2015v2 model
Description: Aerospace Blockset block World Magnetic Model 2015  block and Aerospace Toolbox wrldmagm  function do not support the WMM2015v2 model, released by NOAA in February, 2019. This new model addresses degraded performance of WMM2015(v1) in the Arctic region and supersedes it. For more information about the WMM2015v2 release, see?https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/news/world-magnetic-model-out-cycle-release .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer when generating code for a model with joint state targets and flexible bodies or floating mechanisms
Description: Wrong answers are possible when executing generated C code for a Simscape Multibody model containing the following: 1) Joint state targets (whether position or velocity) 2) At least one of the following modeling patterns: Floating mechanisms (systems of components that are not explicitly connected to a World Frame block via a joint block) General Flexible Beam blocks  Under these circumstances the joint state targets might be ignored, leading to incorrect initialization of the model and subsequently erroneous results when simulating the model using the generated code. This can occur during Rapid Accelerator simulation or when generating and executing C code using Simulink Coder or Embedded Coder, for example.
Workaround: For the specific case of rigid multibody systems with implicit 6-DOF joints, i.e., floating mechanisms not containing any flexible body blocks, explicitly add World Frame blocks and 6-DOF Joint blocks to the model, removing the need for Simscape Multibody to implicitly add the joints.

Summary: "File does not exist" error when updating results of previous custom import
Description: You might see an error about a missing MAT file ("File XYZ.mat does not exist" ) when you try to perform the Update action under these conditions: You have an updated version of the external requirements document with a different requirement module or top-level container ID due to a configuration or version number change. You rely on slreq.ReqSet.updateSrcFileLocation()  API to reconfigure your previously imported content to match the updated ID of the source document.

Summary: Polyspace plugin in Eclipse cannot run on source files in virtual folders
Description: When you try to run Polyspace on a source file in a virtual folder, this message appears: The operation is not applicable to the current selection. Select a source file in the Project Explorer and retry.

Summary: Powertrain Blockset incorrectly checks out an Aerospace Blockset license when running models saved in R2017b, R2017a, or R2016b
Description: If you run a model saved in R2017b, R2017a, or R2016b that contains vehicle body blocks, Powertrain Blockset can incorrectly check out an Aerospace Blockset license. Specifically, models that contain these blocks can incorrectly check out a license:  Vehicle Body Total Road Load Vehicle Body 1DOF Longitudinal Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal?
Workaround: In the model, replace these vehicle body blocks with the same blocks from the Simulink library browser:?Powertrain Blockset > Vehicle Dynamics > Vehicle Body 1DOF LongitudinalPowertrain Blockset > Vehicle Dynamics > Vehicle Body 3DOF LongitudinalPowertrain Blockset > Vehicle Dynamics > Vehicle Body Total Road LoadAfter you replace the blocks, make sure that you update the parameters with values that were in the original model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Bus connected to atomic subsystem might produce silent wrong answer or make MATLAB unresponsive
Description: Simulink might produce a silent wrong answer when all of these conditions apply: You select a nested nonvirtual bus from a nonvirtual bus. The selected nonvirtual bus becomes an input to a virtual bus. The virtual bus connects to an atomic subsystem. In the atomic subsystem, you select a signal from the nested nonvirtual bus.  MATLAB might become unresponsive during update diagram when all of these conditions apply:? A virtual bus contains a nonvirtual bus. The virtual bus connects to an atomic subsystem.
Workaround: Unzip and run the attached script every time you start Simulink. The script enables Simulink to convert a nonvirtual bus into a virtual bus when the nonvirtual bus is contained in a virtual bus.

Summary: Analysis returns error in log file No OIL files found in OSEK multitasking mode
Description: Polyspace analysis can finish with a completed status on the server and the following message in the log file: Error: No OIL files found in automatic multitasking mode. In 'custom' mode,? the '-osek-multitasking' option looks for OIL files in all provided directories. Make sure that they contain at least one file with .oil extension. This can happen systematically with an analysis sent to the server using the option -osek-multitasking  and batch mode option -batch .

Summary: MATLAB crashes when saving a Simulink model with custom code
Description: If a Simulink model has custom code specified in the Configuration Parameters  > Simulation Target , saving this model might trigger access violation with stack trace indicating: SLCC::CCScopeData::getScopeDataAsMxArray

Summary: MATLAB might crash with inactive Simulink Function in an Export-Function model
Description: MATLAB might crash during update diagram when a Simulink model contains all of the following:  Variant inactive Simulink Function  Variant inactive Function-Call Subsystem (Export-Function model)   The stack trace includes the following lines:  matlab/ bin/ glnxa64/ libmwsl_engine_classes.so  matlab/ bin/ glnxa64/ libmwsl_compile.so  matlab/ bin/ glnxa64/ libmwsimulink.so

Summary: Output modules not synchronized with NI 9215 (BNC)
Description: Output modules and the NI 9215 (BNC) are not automatically synchronized when they are in the same CompactDAQ chassis. ?For example, the following code shows that an input can be significantly out of phase with the output. s = daq.createSession('ni');% NI cDAQ-9179 (cDAQ2)aiDev = 'cDAQ2Mod8'; % NI 9215 (BNC)aoDev = 'cDAQ2Mod6'; % NI 9263addAnalogOutputChannel(s, aoDev, 0, 'Voltage');addAnalogInputChannel(s, aiDev, 0, 'Voltage');s.Rate = 25000; f = 1000;A = 1;t = linspace(0,1,round(s.Rate))';Vout = A*sin(2*pi*f*t);%%queueOutputData(s, repmat(Vout,1,numChannels))[Vin, timestamps] = startForeground(s);figureplot(t, Vin(:,1),'o',timestamps,Vout,'x')xlim([0 0.005])xlabel('Time (s)')ylabel('Voltage (V)')

Summary: Incorrect coverage for first assignment statement in Stateflow state when "on" state action is present
Description: When analyzing coverage for a Stateflow state in a C Chart, the Condition and MCDC results for the first logical assignment statement in the state might be incorrect if this state also contains an "on..." state action. For example, consider a state with entry  and on  actions. State1  en:?  ? a = w && x;  ? b = y && z;  on every(1, tick):  ? c = x || y;   MCDC and Condition coverage results for the logical expression (w && x)  found in the first assignment statement might be incorrect.? Note that this issue only affects coverage for this first assignment in the state.

Summary: MEX command not work outside of MATLAB on Windows
Description: mex  command does not work outside of MATLAB on Windows in R2018b.
Workaround: From the system operating prompt, type:<matlabroot>\bin\win64\mexwhere?<matlabroot> is the value returned by the matlabroot command.

Summary: EtherCAT Sync SSC/SoE Download block handles the IDN value incorrectly
Description: The EtherCAT Sync SSC/SoE Download block handles the IDN (identification number) value incorrectly, passing the wrong value to the S-function for the IDN. This issue results in incorrect EtherCAT device configuration. Depending on the EtherCAT device, this can result in incorrect answers or an error message when running the model on the target.?
Workaround: Adapt your model to use the EtherCAT Async SSC/SoE Download block rather than the EtherCAT Sync SSC/SoE Download block.

Summary: Coverage not recorded for assignments under "on" state actions in C Charts
Description: When analyzing coverage for Stateflow states in C Charts, coverage is recorded for logical expressions in assignment statements only when these statements are found under the entry , during , or exit  state actions.? Assignment statements under "on " state actions (such as "on event_name " or "on every(1, tick) ") are not analyzed for coverage.
Products: Simulink Design Verifier, Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage, Requirements Toolbox

Summary: If the axle torque input is zero, the Longitudinal Wheel and Combined Slip Wheel 2DOF blocks do not use the initial angular velocity block parameters
Description: If the axle torque input port AxlTrq ?is zero, the Longitudinal Wheel and Combined Slip Wheel 2DOF blocks do not use the initial angular velocity block parameters. Specifically, theCombined Split Wheel 2DOF block does not use the Initial rotational velocity, omegao  parameter Longitudinal Wheel block does not use the?Wheel initial angular velocity, omegao ?parameter
Workaround: Do not provide the axle torque?AxlTrq? input port a signal that is exactly zero. Instead, you can use a signal that is near zero, for example 2*eps N*m, where eps is a very small number approaching zero. To achieve this, consider either approach:??Use a Sum block to add the axle torque signal to a Constant block with a value set to 2*eps?Use an IC block to set the initial condition of the axle torque?AxlTrq?input signal to??2*eps

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: While-loop used to increment or decrement a single variable omitted from the generated code
Description: Incorrect results are possible for a MATLAB function containing a while-loop with the following properties: The expression for the while-loop condition is a simple relational comparison between a variable and a constant (for example, k < 5 or k >= 10). In the following text, this variable is referred to as the loop induction variable. The body of the loop contains a statement in which the loop induction variable is incremented or decremented by a constant (for example, k = k + 1 or k = k - 5). The body of the while loop contains no other statements. The loop induction variable is assigned to a persistent variable after the while-loop.  The code generator might incorrectly omit code for the while-loop. For an example see the file issue_example_1950912.m in the attached issue_1950912.zip:  persistent P6;?  [SNIP]?  P31 = 1;  while P31<=6     P31 = P31 + 1;  end  P6( 1, 1 ) = P31( 1, 1 ); In the generated code? for issue_example_1950912, there is no loop corresponding to the while-loop above. This can be observed by comparing the numeric result of the generated Mex file against MATLAB:?  >> cfg = coder.config('mex');>> cfg.IntegrityChecks = 0;>> cfg.ResponsivenessChecks = 0;>> codegen -config cfg issue_example_1950912>> [~,~,~,ml_out] = issue_example_1950912();>> [~,~,~,cg_out] = issue_example_1950912_mex();>> disp(ml_out - cg_out)   56
Workaround: Replace the while-loop with an assignment to the final value of the loop induction variable. For an example see issue_workaround_1950912.m:  persistent P6;?  [SNIP]?  P31 = 7;  P6( 1, 1 ) = P31( 1, 1 );>> cfg = coder.config('mex');>> cfg.IntegrityChecks = 0;>> cfg.ResponsivenessChecks = 0;>> codegen -config cfg issue_workaround_1950912>> [~,~,~,ml_out] = issue_workaround_1950912();>> [~,~,~,cg_out] = issue_workaround_1950912_mex();>> disp(ml_out - cg_out)?   0

Summary: Variable Editor cannot be launched from the Model Explorer Dialog pane for custom attribute properties of data objects
Description: In the Model Explorer Dialog pane, when you select?Open Variable Editor  from the menu near the value for a custom attribute property of a data object, the Variable Editor does not launch.
Workaround: Launch the dialog from the custom attribute property in the Model Explorer?Contents?pane.?

Summary: Error when opening App Designer app saved over a later release app
Description: If you save an app, created in R2017b or earlier, over an existing app saved in App Designer release R2018a or later, the saved app file is corrupted. The error that displays when attempting to open the app in releases R2018a, R2018b, and R2019a is?Reference to non-existent field 'code' . The saved app opens without error in releases R2017b and earlier.
Workaround: When saving an app in App Designer in releases older than R2017b, either:* Do not choose an existing file saved in R2018a, R2018b, or R2019a* Save the app to a new file, not an existing one.?

Summary: Simulink can crash during upgrade of models using the Upgrade Advisor
Description: When upgrading models to a new release of Simulink using the Upgrade Advisor, Simulink may crash.? This occurs after there has been an error saving one of the models.?
Workaround: Change settings to ensure that no errors will occur when saving the models.? Two such settings are "Block diagram contains disabled library links" and "Block diagram contains parameterized library links" in the "Diagnostics" section of the Configuration Parameters window.? Alternatively:? set_param(modelname,'SaveWithDisabledLinksMsg','warning')? set_param(modelname,'SaveWithParameterizedLinksMsg','warning')?

Summary: C Caller Block displays the same error message when issue with custom code is fixed
Description: The C Caller Block displays an error if a header file is missing or the file name is wrong. When you fix the issue and rerun the model,? the same error message is displayed.
Workaround: Rename the header file, insert an empty line in the source code C file, and rerun the model.

Summary: Slower MCDC analysis of Test Harness model compared to regular model
Description: Simulation of a Simulink test harness takes a longer time to complete when model coverage analysis is enabled and the structural coverage level is set to?Modified Condition Decision Coverage (MCDC) .  By comparison, MCDC analysis of an equivalent model that is not configured as a test harness takes less time to complete.??
Workaround: To work around the issue, do one of the following:Disable the MCDC metric if it is not necessary for your coverage analysis. Changing the structural coverage level to Condition Decision should improve performance.? This setting can be adjusted via the Coverage pane of the Configuration Parameters or by manually setting the value of the corresponding parameter?CovMetricStructuralLevel.If MCDC analysis is necessary, then avoid running this analysis on test harnesses. Instead, detach and export the test harness, then perform the coverage simulation on the resulting model. To export an individual test harness, from?the test harness menu, select?Analysis?>?Test Harness?>?Detach and Export Harness, as described in the documentation page?Manage Test Harnesses.
Products: Simulink Design Verifier, Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage

Summary: Assertion error when generating code for model : 'In computeAndSetType: Type checker is unable to infer the type of Node 'hcat'. Please provide the type explicitly.'?
Description: Simulink might error unexpectedly when generating code for a model with the message: CGIR assertion 'In computeAndSetType: Type checker is unable to infer the type of Node 'hcat'. Please provide the type explicitly.'?  This error might occur when the model uses one or more Simulink.AliasType? objects ?and one or more of the following conditions is true: The model contains concatenation of a?Simulink.AliasType ?signal with a signal that has the same data type as the BaseType  of the Simulink.AliasType .  The model contains concatenation of different Simulink.AliasType ?signals that have the same specified BaseType . The model contains concatenation of a Simulink.AliasType ?signal with a signal driven by a Constant block.  For examples of this issue see the models within issue_1948052.zip: issue_model_1948052_signal_mismatch and issue_model_1948052_parameter_mismatch. In issue_model_1948052_signal_mismatch there are Mux blocks that concatenate a signal of?single_alias ?type and?single ?type. In issue_model_1948052_parameter_mismatch there is a Mux block driven by a signal of single_alias  type and a Constant block .
Workaround: Adjust the model so that signals of the exact same data type drive the inports of blocks that perform concatenation (for example, Mux and Vector Concatenate). This may involve:Replacing the use of a Simulink.AliasType with its specified BaseType.?Replacing the uses of two or more?Simulink.AliasTypes of the same BaseType with a single?Simulink.AliasType.Inserting a Data Type Conversion block to unify the types of signals feeding a block performing concatenation.?A workaround for issue_model_1948052_signal_mismatch can be seen in the attached model issue_workaround_1948052_signal_mismatch. All inputs in the model have been adjusted to have a single_alias type. This unified all signals driving mux blocks to have the type single_alias.If a constant block is driving a concatenating block like issue_model_1948052_parameter_misatch, use a Simulink.Parameter to specify the value of the Constant block driving the concatenating block with properties:A?DataType?matching the Simulink.AliasType driving the other inports of the concatenating blockA storage?class other than AutoA workaround for issue_model_1948052_parameter_mismatch can be see in the attached model issue_workaround_1948052_parameter_mismatch. The value of the Constant block is now specified as single_alias_ONE which resolves to a Simulink.Parameter with DataType single_alias and a Const storage class specification.

Summary: Check data types for blocks with index signals incorrectly flags an index that contains a math operation
Description: The Model Advisor check Check data types for blocks with index signals ?(Check ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0022 ) might incorrectly flag blocks that use arithmetic operators (+/-)? to calculate index variables.

Summary: TFL callback?error during model build
Description: The following error message is thrown during code generation: Simulink Coder Fatal: TFL callback is attempting to create file "UserDefinedFIleName" however, ?this file was already created by Simulink.  The error is thrown under these conditions: The model is associated with custom TLC code that executes file packaging operations. For example: creating a new file.? The model uses custom storage classes that write code to a separate file. For example: ExportToFile  .?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing multiple?Reusable custom storage classes
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: A pair of root-level inport and outport signals contain the same Reusable  custom storage class specification. There are two blocks in between the pair of root-level inport and outport blocks. There is another Reusable  custom storage class specified between the blocks and it drives a separate root-level outport.

Summary: Compilation might fail with error message: segmentation fault occurred in "ps_cxx_fe"
Description: If?a source code file contains some constant initialization of structurevariables inside a C++ class declaration, Polyspace Bug Finder might fail to compile this file.? For example, if you have this class declaration in a file?my_file.cpp : Class myClass {private:struct myStruct { int field1; bool field2, ... };const myStruct var = {.field1 = 1; .field2 = false; ...}; }; You see this error message during the compilation phase of my_file.cpp :?     Verifying my_file.cpp (X/Y)      Error: A segmentation fault occurred in "ps_cxx_fe.exe".    my_file.cpp: warning: Failed compilation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: ?Variable Integer Delay block outputs wrong answer when Upper Limit overflows data type of Delay length input
Description: The Variable Integer Delay block may generate incorrect code when both of these conditions are true: The Delay length  source is Input?port  The delay?length Upper Limit  value?does not fit in the range of the?input delay length data type (causing an?overflow)  For example, if the?Delay length ?input type is?'int16' ?and the delay length?UpperLimit? parameter is set?to?65535 ,?the type of?'int16' ?cannot represent?UpperLimit ?because?cast(65535, 'int16') ?returns?32767? in MATLAB.? The incorrect code causes the input signal?to be delayed by some value other than the specified delay length. This causes?a mismatch in behavior between normal mode simulation and all code generation targets (accelerator mode, rapid accelerator mode, standalone code generation, and so forth).
Workaround: To convert the Delay length input to a type that will fit the value of Upper Limit, connect a Data Type Conversion block to input d of?the Variable Integer Delay block.

Summary: Upgrading a project leaves Simulink models with disabled library links
Description: Running the Project Upgrade can incorrectly disable library links in Simulink models if they do not fail any Simulink Upgrade Advisor checks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation and code?generation results for Shift Arithmetic block when sum of Bits to shift and Binary points to shift exceeds 32-bit integer maximum value
Description: Incorrect simulation and code?generation results occur for Shift Arithmetic block under these conditions: Source of Bits to shift  parameter is set to Input port  Data type of the Bits to shift  input port (s) signal is double  Total shift due to the addition/subtraction of the? Bits to shift ?input port signal value and Binary points to shift  parameter value exceeds the 32-bit integer maximum value

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing Reusable?custom storage classes
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: A root-level inport signal to a block has a?Reusable  custom storage class. A non root-level outport signal from that block has the same?Reusable ?custom storage class. A second block that connects to this non root-level outport signal and has a different sample time from the first block. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Treat each discrete rate as a separate task ?parameter is selected.
Workaround: Remove the Reusable custom storage class from the non root-level inport or outport signal.

Summary: Models containing Ideal Airspeed Correction block do not pass Underspecified data types configuration parameter diagnostic check
Description: Models containing the Ideal Airspeed Correction block do not pass the Underspecified data types  (UnderSpecifiedDataTypeMsg ) configuration parameter diagnostic check.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using Dataflow subsystems in referenced models may cause build failure
Description: In a Simulink model hierarchy, when both the top-level and referenced model contain Dataflow subsystems and the model names are longer than 9 characters, building the top-level model fails.
Workaround: Change model names to be less than 9 characters in length.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a?Model?block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model references another model. The referenced model's output port connects to a root-level?Outport?block. The signal connecting to the root-level Outport block contains a storage class. A non-zero constant signal drives the referenced model output port that connects to the root-level Outport block.
Workaround: Remove the storage class specification on the Model?block's output port and use function prototype control to achieve the customization of root-level Outport . For more information, see?Optimize Buffering for Arguments.

Summary: Using parsim to simulate a model inside a referenced project closes the parent project
Description: When simulating a model inside a referenced project using parsim, the parent project will be closed if it's open. The referenced project with the model will then be loaded before simulating the model.

Summary: Some linear algebra operations with sparse, singular inputs?produce memory leaks in the generated code
Description: With sparse, singular inputs, calling the following functions results in generated code which contains memory leaks: mldivide? (or the '\ ' operator) if the first input is a sparse, singular matrix inv

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect array indexing when using S-function blocks
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for an AUTOSAR model when all of the following conditions are true: A root Inport signal feeds to a S-function block. The root Inport?signal has matrix as its data type. The AUTOSAR data access mode of the root Inport is ImplicitReceive .  In the generated code, return value from Rte_read  call might be treated as a pointer to scalar element, rather than pointer to a vector/matrix. This leads to incorrect array indexing.

Summary: Unexpected error with instrhwinfo('vxipnp', <drivername>)
Description: The instrhwinfo  command, when used with?vxipnp  argument and a driver name, incorrectly? returns the following error: >> driverInfo = instrhwinfo ('vxipnp','niFgen'); ? Undefined variable "VXIPnPLoader" or class "VXIPnPLoader.toBasicMatlabDriverModel".? Error in instrhwinfo>localGetVXIPnPDriverInfo (line 833)?model = VXIPnPLoader.toBasicMatlabDriverModel([fullDriverName '.fp']);? Error in instrhwinfo (line 445)?out = localGetVXIPnPDriverInfo(adaptor);??

Summary: Some Simulink Coverage model coverage reports in the DO Qualification Kit include incorrect coverage data
Description: The DO Qualification Kit provides baseline Simulink Coverage model coverage reports to use for comparison with coverage reports that are generated in a users' environment. The following baseline model coverage reports do not reflect the Simulink Coverage product changes and will not match the user-generated reports:  mSFSatOnOverflow_CoverageReport.html hexternalmfilemcdc_cov.html mSFRelCov_Fxp_CoverageReport.html mModelRefSubsys_cov.html mmodelblock_cov.html mmodelblock_multilevel_cov.html

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Global data store read/write blocks in top model might share data with shared local data stores in referenced models
Description: Data store read/write blocks in the top model access the data for shared local data stores in referenced models under the following conditions: A data store read/write block in the top model and a shared local data store have the same data store name. The data store read/write block is intended to access a global data store memory of that name, so there is not a data store memory block with that name in the top model.?  ?This can result in silent wrong answers during normal mode simulation.
Workaround: Change the name of shared local data stores in referenced models so that they do not collide with global data stores used by the top model.

Summary: Error when using setWidths?method to customize Metrics Dashboard
Description: Using the setWidths  method to specify widths when customizing the Metrics Dashboard results in this error: Undefined variable "slmetric" or class "slmetric.dashboard.Width.Medium".?Error in DisplayMetricDashBoard (line 65)? The setWidths  method is applicable for the slmetric.dashboard.Container , slmetric.dashboard.CustomWidget , slmetric.dashboard.Group , and slmetric.dashboard.Widget  classes. Here is an example of how you apply the method:? newWidget.setWidths(slmetric.dashboard.Width.Medium);
Workaround: Theissue is due to a missing 'p file' in the final shipping version of MATLAB.Theworkaround is to create a folder named ˇ°+dashboardˇ± at this location :C:\ProgramFiles\MATLAB\R2018b\toolbox\slcheck\slmetrics_mmt\+slmetricPleasemake sure to replace ˇ°C:\Program Files\ˇ± in the above path to your installationlocation. Aftercreating the folder, please unzip the attached file and copy the "Width.p" file to thenewly created ˇ°+dashboardˇ± folder.?Thenexecute the following command:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: MATLAB and compiled MATLAB applications may fail to start in some Linux container environments
Description: MATLAB and compiled MATLAB applications may encounter problems determining the number and type of CPUs when run inside some Linux container environments, causing MATLAB or the application to crash with the following message:  Fatal Internal Error: std :: exception: CPU inspection returned inconsistent values

Summary: Enumeration values that are contiguous do not appear in generated code causing a MISRA 2004 Rule 9.3 violation
Description: The generated code for an enumeration might be missing contiguous values for a model or MATLAB code that uses an enumeration type consisting of contiguous values such as 0, 1, 2. For example, the following enumeration contains both contiguous and noncontiguous values: ? ? ? ? Red(0), ?? ? Blue(1), ?? ? Orange(2), ?? ? Yellow(8)? end?  The generated code contains only the first value and the noncontiguous value as follows: ??typedef enum {?  ? ?Red?= 0, ??????????????????/ * Default value */ ?? ?Blue, ?? ?Orange, ?? ?Yellow = 8????} Colors;?  The above code is noncompliant with MISRA 2004 Rule 9.3. Note, that the R2018b and R2019a product documentation contains a release note titled "Enumerated Types: Optimizations in Generated Code" stating that these inferred enumerated values might not be in the generated code. With this bug fix, that part of the release note is no longer valid.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: ComposeString or ScanString blocks with extended ASCII characters in their Format parameters generate code might produce?wrong answers
Description: ComposeString or ScanString blocks with extended ASCII characters in their Format  parameters generate code that might produce wrong answers when the Format  parameter contains extended ASCII characters followed by characters 0 -9 , A -F , or a -f . For example, a Format  parameter of "ˇăa %d"  will become "\xb0a %d"  in the generated code. This string contains?four characters in the C programming language: '\xb0a'  (character 2826), ' ' , '%' , and 'd' . The escape sequence \xb0  has extended to consume the character 'a' .?

Summary: Incorrect size of structure from sizeof with Polyspace options -compiler diab and -target tricore
