Description: Polyspace calculates the size in bytes of some structures incorrectly when you use analysis options?-compiler diab ?and?-target tricore.  For example, on this structure with a mix of basic type of different sizes: typedef struct {  unsigned int field1;  unsigned long long field2;  char field3;  char field4;} MyStruct; Polyspace calculates the size as 24 bytes (8 bytes alignment). However, the size of the structure with the selected compiler and target should be reported as 16 bytes (4 bytes alignment).

Summary: Incorrect enums size when using polyspace-configure on diab toolchain with option -Xenum-is-best
Description: When you run polyspace-configure  on a build system that uses the diab compiler with the option -Xenum-is-best , the resulting options file does not take into account the alignment size of enums.?A Polyspace Bug Finder analysis using this options file might result in a false negative or a compilation error due to the enum size. For instance, the size of MyStructEnum  below should be 2U: typedef enum {A0,? B1,? C2,? D3} Enum1;typedef enum {AA0,? BB1,? CC2,? DD3,?              AAA0,? BBB1,? CCC2,? DDD3,?              AAAA0,? BBBB1,? CCCC2,? DDDD3,?              AAAAA0,? BBBBB1,? CCCCC2,? DDDDD3} Enum2;typedef struct {  Enum1 a1;  Enum2 a2;} MyStructEnum; However, when you run polyspace-configure  and use the resulting options file with Polyspace Bug Finder, the analysis calculates the size of?MyStructEnum  to be 4U.
Workaround: After you run polyspace-configure, open the resulting options file and add option -enum-type-definition with value 'auto-signed-first' or 'auto-unsigned-first'.

Summary: In Simscape simulation data?logging, logged values that depend on conditional expressions?may be incorrect
Description: In Simscape logging, wrong values may be recorded and displayed in the log, for values that depend on conditional expressions. Note that this issue affects Simscape data?logging only. It does not affect Simscape Variable Viewer.
Workaround: You can use sensors placed in the model to view the quantities of interest?(for example, by putting a voltage sensor in parallel with component x and looking at its output, rather than looking at x.V in the log). Also, for starting values only, you can use the Variable Viewer.?

Summary: Restoring disabled library link with "push to library" option may take longer time than before.
Description: When your Simulink model has library reference blocks, and when you "disable" the library link to make local changes inside the referenced subsystem, and then "restore" the library link to "push" your changes to library, Simulink Requirements may need to update related data, in case requirements links for library objects where modified locally while the link was disabled.? The process of synchronizing requirements links data from reference context to library may take longer time than it used to.

Summary: Redrawing images or surfaces with texture mapping might slow down or crash your system
Description: Redrawing the figure when it is displaying an image or a surface that uses texture mapping might cause MATLAB?  to run out of memory. As a result, your system might slow down or crash. MATLAB  redraws the figure under different circumstances. Here are a few situations that might cause the problem: You call the drawnow  function You change the contents of the figure You add additional graphics objects to the figure You minimize and then restore the figure

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Design Verifier may sometimes miss to report impact of approximation on Design Range Check or Out of Bound Array Access Errors
Description: When Simulink Design Verifier performs analysis using approximations such as rational approximation, it reports impact of the approximation in the analysis result. But it may sometimes miss to report approximation impact on Design Range Check or Out of Bound Array Access Errors for Stateflow or MATLAB variables or Simulink signals when the corresponding variable or signal has multiple assignments.

Summary: Unexpected error message displayed regarding 5000 simultaneous events per block
Description: When simulating a model that contains SimEvents blocks, the following error message is unexpectedly displayed: The '[BLOCK_NAME]' block has exceeded 5000 simultaneous events at time 0. This likely indicates a livelock condition. Please inspect any loops involving this block that might cause entities to cycle indefinitely.  This error is displayed incorrectly for blocks that output multiple entities at the same time such as Entity Store, Entity Selector, Entity Server, and Entity Replicator blocks. This unexpected error may be observed when one or more of these blocks store a large number of entities, while the next block is blocking or preventing their acceptance. In this case, the error message is incorrectly displayed although there is no livelock condition.

Summary: Extended ASCII characters in String Constant block?might produce wrong results in referenced models executing in accelerator mode
Description: Running a submodel in a referenced model that executes in?accelerator mode?might produce wrong simulation results when all of the following conditions are true: String Constant block specifies a string value that contains extended ASCII characters (char(128) to char(255)) String Constant block Is used in the submodel  For example,?the output of the String Constant block might contain unexpected additional characters.

Summary: Using single-scan operations with multiple sessions can generate an error
Description: While using two sessions to interact with National Instruments hardware, calling outputSingleScan  or inputSingleScan  successively on each session might result in an error. For instance, using a simulated NI USB-6351, this code might cause the error: s1 = daq.createSession('ni');s2 = daq.createSession('ni');addDigitalChannel(s2,'Dev1','port0/line1:4','OutputOnly');addDigitalChannel(s1,'Dev1','port0/line0','OutputOnly');outputSingleScan(s1, 1)outputSingleScan(s2, [1 1 1 1])outputSingleScan(s1, 1)
Workaround: To avoid this issue, call clearSingleScanCache on the next session between successive calls to outputSingleScan or inputSingleScan. For example:s1 = daq.createSession('ni');s2 = daq.createSession('ni');addDigitalChannel(s2,'Dev1','port0/line1:4','OutputOnly');addDigitalChannel(s1,'Dev1','port0/line0','OutputOnly');outputSingleScan(s1, 1)clearSingleScanCache(s2)outputSingleScan(s2, [1 1 1 1])clearSingleScanCache(s1)outputSingleScan(s1, 1)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect diagnostic message with conditionally executed system containing Variant Sink
Description: Incorrect diagnostic error message, example?'testMdl/ss/Merge' is driven by the function-call root-level Inport block(s). Therefore, it is not allowed to branch the output signal from output port 1 of this block to feed multiple root Outport blocks 'testMdl/x' and? 'testMdl/y'  may be shown when all of the below conditions are met:  Variant sink block present inside a conditionally executed system connects to outport blocks.  Analyze all choices during update-diagram and generate pre-processor conditionals ?is selected.  Outport blocks connected to Variant sink inherit initial output values from input signals.   The diagnostic error message is not shown when a signal conversion block with?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization  is inserted in any one of the branched output signal of the variant sink. However, you may notice missing? preprocessor condition for the branched signal in the generated code.
Workaround: Deselect Analyze all choices during update-diagram and generate pre-processor conditionals for the Variant sink block within the conditionally executed system.Explicitly specify initial condition for Outport blocks inside conditionally executed systems.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling a row-major C function through C Caller block inside of Dataflow Subsystem?generates wrong simulation result
Description: ?A column-major model that has a C Callerblock calling a row-major array layout?function in a Dataflow Subsystem gives wrong simulation results.

Summary: Model block SIL or PIL simulation produces error for AUTOSAR software component with model workspace parameters mapped to SharedParameter
Description: If you run a Model block software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation for an AUTOSAR software component that has model workspace parameters mapped to AUTOSAR parameters of type?SharedParameter , the simulation produces an error. For example:  SIL or PIL component "mymodel" references AUTOSAR calibration parameter "Model workspace parameter 'myparam'" which cannot be initialized. The parameter is not initialized in the generated code and is not supported for SIL/PIL dynamic parameter initialization. To avoid this error, only use parameters that SIL/PIL can dynamically initialize (see SIL and PIL tunable parameter support documentation).
Workaround: To test the generated code for the AUTOSAR software component, you can run a top-model SIL or PIL simulation, which does not produce the error.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 6, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulating a model containing a String Length block might crash when the configuration parameter Hardware Implementation setting for int is 16
Description: Simulating a model containing a String Length block might crash when the configuration?parameter Hardware Implementation > Device details > Number of bits > int ?is set to 16.

Summary: Some linear algebra operations with sparse matrices produce memory leaks in the generated code
Description: With sparse inputs, calling the following functions results in generated code which contains memory leaks: mldivide? (or the '\ ' operator) if the first input is a sparse matrix inv   For a similar issue related to sparse, singular inputs, see?Bug Report ID 1941277 .?

Summary: Functions that access the Internet may display web content incorrectly
Description: Functions that access the Internet may not honor the proxy settings defined in the MATLAB web preferences, causing the web content to display incorrectly. For example, the doc ?function?displays content incorrectly when viewing the documentation on the web. The web ?and webmap  functions display content incorrectly when opening external sites.
Workaround: If you are experiencing this problem using the doc?function, change the MATLAB help preferences:Go to the Home tab and in the Environment section, click?Preferences.Select MATLAB > Help.In the?Documentation Location?section, select Installed locally.

Summary: Simulink model with simulation target custom code reports compiler warnings as errors during model update or when refreshing C Caller block
Description: If a Simulink model is associated with custom code and the custom code contains issues that can throw compiler warnings and errors, warnings report as errors during model update or when you click "Refresh". The warnings appear on top of errors in the diagnostic viewer. It is hard to distinguish warnings from real errors in the diagnostic viewer. The very last error in the diagnostic viewer is the true error.
Workaround: The very last error in the diagnostic viewer is the true error.?Fix the errors in custom code and do a model update or refresh C Caller block.

Summary: slreq.structToObj command fails to return object for requirement, Simulink Test, and Simulink Data Dictionary
Description: Using the slreq.structToObj command can return an error if you query one of the following objects:  A requirement used as a source of a link  A test case, test suite, or test file in Simulink Test A Simulink data dictionary?  For example: Reference to non-existent field 'reqSet'. Error in slreq.structToObj (line 84)????????????reqSet = slreq.utils.getReqSet(dataStruct.reqSet);

Summary: Potential incorrect results for models containing Data Store Memory blocks with ShareAcrossModelInstances enabled
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may provide incorrect results for a model when the following conditions are met:  The model contains model references  One of those model references contains a Data Store Memory block with ShareAcrossModelInstances  enabled  There are at least two distinct instances of that model reference in the model hierarchy   If the above mentioned conditions are satisfied for a model, the replacement model that Simulink Design Verifier creates may produce incorrect results.
Workaround: Replace each Data Store Memory block that has ShareAcrossModelInstances enabled with a distinct global data store.

Summary: Crash or data corruption when using MATLAB System block in interpreted mode with global variables.
Description: Global variables can be used in System objects by declaring them using a?global ? keyword in MATLAB. When a System object is used in MATLAB System block, the global variables cannot change their dimensions, type or complexity between calls to stepImpl   method. If the global variables are type struct , all fields of the struct cannot change their type, dimensions, or complexity between calls to stepImpl  method. Changing the type, dimensions, or complexity may cause a data corruption or a crash during simulation.
Workaround: When you use global variables with System objects and the System object is used in a MATLAB System block, avoid changing type, size, or complexity of global variables and fields of global variable struct types in your code.

Summary: When applying Simulink.exportToTemplate to a Simulink Project object, 'Group' argument has no effect
Description: Using Simulink.exportToTemplate  on a Simulink Project with the Name-Value pair argument for 'Group'  has no impact on the value of the Group property. The template is always generated with the 'My Templates' group.?

Summary: parsim fails to load project using referenced projects on a non-local cluster
Description: When running simulations using parsim  on a non-local cluster, if a model is part of a project using referenced projects, the referenced projects are not copied to the parallel workers. This causes loading of the main project to fail on parallel workers. This can lead to errors related to unresolved files or functions during simulation.?

Summary: Order of arguments reversed in function generated for Simulink Function
Description: When generating code for a model containing a Simulink Function, the prototype of the function is generated as: void foo(u4, u3, u2, u1)   If the Configure C/ C++ Function Interface  dialog on the Simulink Function is now opened, ?it displays the argument order as u1, ?u2, ?u3, ?u4 .  If the dialog is closed by hitting OK  without making changes and code is regenerated, the function is now generated as: void foo(u1, u2, u3, u4)

Summary: Rebuild on externally-saved test harness may discard changes
Description: Rebuilding an externally-saved test harness when the test harness is closed may cause changes in the test harness to be discarded.?

Summary: Enabling Saturation on Synchronous Machine pu Standard block gives unstable results
Description: When you select Simulate saturation  in the Parameters settings for the Synchronous Machine pu Standard block under Simscape > Electrical >Specialized Power Systems > Fundamental Blocks > Machines  sub-library, the simulation results are unstable.

Summary: Mask Initialization of S-Function block may affect model performance
Description: Mask Initialization commands of S-Function block may run multiple times?when the following conditions are met:   The masked S-Function connected to a bus selector block.  The user uses  get_param/set_param ?on the bus selector block to retrieve the signal properties.

Summary: Entity Selector block sometimes selects and outputs more than one matching entity from a matching stream
Description: Normally, for each key entity, Entity Selector block selects and outputsonly one matching entity from each matching stream. However, if an entity ismatched from a storage and simultaneously another higher priority matchingentity arrives at the storage, then both entities are erroneously selected bythe Entity Selector block.

Summary: Incorrect compiled properties on the output of Merge?block with?inactive?input
Description: Incorrect compiled properties on the output port of the Merge block when all the following are satisfied: Input port of Merge block is inactive and Merge block is active Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditional  is unselected on the Variant block The?Variant block propagates condition to?the Merge block  For Example:if 'Model/Merge' block is a Merge block with 2 inports and 1 outport then after simulation   >>pH =?get_param('Model/Merge' , 'PortHandles'); %Port handles for merge block>>outport_handle = pH.Outport;>>get_param(outport_handle,? 'CompiledBusType'); % Incorrect

Summary: DO Qualification Kit test cases for Simulink Check that require code generation can fail to execute
Description: When executing the DO Qualification Kit for Simulink Check, test cases that require code generation can fail to execute because of conflicting shared utilities folders. For such test cases, the summary qualification report will include error message: Cannot complete the configuration parameters for Model blocks because build failed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect numerical results when a Constant block with a tunable parameter branches its output to drive multiple export functions
Description: A Constant block is allowed at the root level of an export-function model when its 'SampleTime' parameter is set to 'Inf'. However, when such a Constant block has a tunable parameter and its output is branched to drive multiple export functions, the generated code is incorrect and leads to incorrect numerical results.?
Workaround: Make copies of the Constant block, and rewire the connections so that each Constant block only drives one export function at a time.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Shift Arithmetic block might output wrong?answer for normal mode simulation when Bits to Shift set to?input port
Description: The Shift Arithmetic block might output incorrect results for normal mode simulation when the following conditions are true: Bits to shift  set to?input port.  Check for out-of-range 'Bits to shift' in generated code  check box is cleared. The run-time value of "Bits to shift"  does not fit in the range?of the data type of?int16 . For example,?a run-time value less than -65536 or greater than 65535 triggers this issue.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for Prelookup or Interpolation Using Prelookup blocks within For Each subsystems when Breakpoints/Table inputs are structure elements
Description: Code is incorrectly?generated from a model that contains a Prelookup or Interpolation Using Prelookup block inside a For Each subsystem with the following configurations: Prelookup block configured as follows Breakpoints specification  set to Explicit values  Breakpoints Data?Source  set to Input port  Index search method ?set to Linear  or Binary  Value of Breakpoints Data  is an element of a MATLAB structure variable   Or Interpolation Using Prelookup configured as follows: Table specification  set to Explicit values  Interpolation method  set to Linear point-slope  or Linear Lagrange  Table Source  set to Input port  Value of Table  is an element of a MATLAB structure variable

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulating a Stateflow chart containing a bus with string elements can produce a wrong answer
Description: Assigning a bus variable to another bus variable containing string elements can cause future string operations on either bus to produce incorrect results in Stateflow charts with C action language. For example, consider the following Stateflow chart code. The variables basis_bus  and altered_bus  share a bus type with a string element str : basis_bus.str = "hi";  altered_bus = basis;  // the following evaluates to true?  strcmp(altered_bus.str, "hi") == 0??  basis_bus.str = "hello";?  // now the following evaluates to true, but the value of altered_bus.str?should still be "hi"  strcmp(altered_bus.str, "hello") == 0?

Summary: Deployment of XCP scripts fails with A2L file error
Description: When attempting to deploy a MATLAB script that uses the xcpA2L  function of Vehicle Network Toolbox, the following error occurs:   Couldn't open file e:\?MATLAB\?R2018a\?mcr\?toolbox\?shared\?xcp\?+xcp\?private\?a2l_def_1_31.a2ml for reading

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL mismatch for LTE Turbo Decoder with some data block sizes
Description: The HDL code generated from the LTE Turbo Decoder block may not match the Simulink simulation when decoding the following block sizes: {56,112,120,168,280,560}? This mismatch occurs regardless of if the block size comes from a port or a property.? This mismatch can cause the generated HDL to incorrectly decode a waveform that was successfully recovered in Simulink simulation. Running co-simulation between a HDL simulator and Simulink shows the mismatch.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Truth table functions created in previous releases with whitespace-separated actions might auto-generate content incorrectly
Description: For truth table functions in previous releases, multiple actions could be specified in a single decision column as a whitespace-separated list. If you open a model with a whitespace-separated list in one of the affected releases, the truth table function might auto-generate content incorrectly when you: Edit the the action row in the decision table Click "View Auto-generated Content"  The incorrect content is truncated compared to the expected result, but can still simulate without throwing an error. This can lead to wrong answers in simulation and generated code.
Workaround: Follow these steps to rebuild the content:?Change all whitespace-separated lists to comma-separated lists.Click "View Auto-generated Content".?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Truth table functions with a final action defined might auto-generate content incorrectly
Description: Truth tables functions that use C as the action language and have a final action (using the label FINAL) already defined might auto-generate content incorrectly when you: Edit the the action row in the decision table Click "View Auto-generated Content"  The incorrect content is truncated compared to the expected result, but can still simulate without throwing an error. This can lead to wrong answers in simulation and generated code.
Workaround: Unzip the attached P-file, workaroundForBugReport1927402.p. Place the P-file in your working directory or somewhere else on the MATLAB path.For every new MATLAB session, in the Command Window, enter the following command before opening Simulink.>> workaroundForBugReport1927402Note that after running this script, the auto-generated content for all Truth Tables (both functions and stand-alone blocks, whether using C or MATLAB as the action language) will regenerate only when you?save the model, run the model, generate code, or update the diagram. To update the diagram, select Simulation > Update Diagram or press Ctrl+D.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation with an assertion error
Description: During code generation, MATLAB might crash with the following error: Assertion at dom_core/ analysis/ loopAnalysis/ RegularSectionBuilder.cpp line 874: In weakUnionComponent: Assertion failed: aComp1->type() == aComp2->type()
Workaround: For MEX mode, setting the coder configuration object (cfg) as?cfg.ResponsivenessChecks = 1;?might prevent the issue from occurring.

Summary: Waveform Generator block behaves unexpectedly or crashes MATLAB when names in the full block path (gcb)?contain forward slashes
Description: Waveform Generator block behaves unexpectedly in the following ways when one or more block names in the?full block path to the Waveform Generator (gcb ) contain a forward slash: Block outputs 0 regardless of waveform setting. If block mask is changed, mask callbacks do not work correctly. Simulation of model containing Waveform Generator block might crash MATLAB.
Workaround: Remove forward slashes from names in the full block path (gcb).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for referenced models
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:  The model contains referenced models with Boolean  input     and/or output signals  The model configuration parameter Pack Boolean data into Boolean bit fields  is enabled. The model configuration parameter Reuse global block outputs  is enabled or the Optimize global dataaccess  parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results .  The incorrect generated code might contain a read from an uninitialized variable or a write to a variable that never gets use.

Summary: Block parameter modification using mask initialization may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash with a Simulink mask block in the below scenario:  The mask initialization code generates an error message following the selection of a specific option in the mask dialog.  After the error message, the mask initialization code modifies a block parameter (example : block position) using  set_param .   Stack trace includes the below lines:  [ 0] 0x00000001b692292a bin\win64\m3i.dll+00665898 M3I::Deviant::commitTransaction+00000474?[ 1] 0x00000001b6b5da17 bin\win64\m3i.dll+03004951 M3I::NSInterface::ModelIntf::commitTransaction+00000023?[ 2] 0x00000001e9876631 bin\win64\glue2.dll+00812593 GLUE2::Command::commitTransactionsAndHandlePostDo+00000113
Workaround: Add a new function example?delayedParamSet,as below :function delayedParamSet(blk, value)t = timer;t.StartDelay = 0.01;t.TimerFcn = @(~,~) set_param(blk, <SOME_PARAM>, value);start(t);endThen inside the mask initialization callback, change the existing?set_param(gcb, <SOME_PARAM>, <VALUE>)?to?delayedParamSet(gcb, value)The introduced timer causes the parameter setting to occur just after the mask initialization callback completes, thus avoiding the crash.

Summary: Error when trying to push changes to a project that was added to GitHub using the "share to GitHub" option
Description: If you use the the project's "Share to GitHub" option, and then try to push or pull changes, you see an error. The problem is caused by a conflict between a ReadMe.md file in your local repository and a ReadMe.md file that the project creates on the remote GitHub repository. This conflict prevents a push between the local and remote repository. This issue only affects GitHub repositories created using the project's "Share to GitHub" functionality.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when updating a model containing an Outport block that inherits its initial value from a Simulink.Signal object.
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when it meets the following conditions: Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Validity > Advanced parameters > Underspecified initialization detection  is set to 'simplified'  A Simulink.Signal  object is specified on a signal that drives an Outport block of a conditionally executed subsystem, such as Enabled Subsystem, Triggered Subsystem, etc. The field InitialValue  of the Simulink.Signal  object is not empty. The Outport block has one of the following configurations (which are equivalent to each other): Source of initial output value  is set to 'Dialog'  and Initial output  is set to '[]'  (empty matrix). Source of initial output value ?is set to?'Input signal'.   The Outport block of the conditionally executed subsystem is driving a root-level Outport block. The port number of the conditionally executed subsystem Outport block is greater than the total number of the root-level Outport blocks in the model.  When the above conditions are met, updating the model causes MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Root Inport Mapper fails for non built-in integer type enumerations (Simulink.IntEnumType)
Description: Root Inport Mapper fails for non built-in integer type enumerations (for example, Simulink.IntEnumType). This regression can also prevent previously linked signals of these enumerated types from linking?in the Root Inport Mapper.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Functions imported from Simulink into a Stateflow Chart may not be called if the Chart contains a comment with certain Unicode characters
Description: Simulink Functions called from Stateflow might not be called properly when all of the following conditions are true: The chart uses C Action Language The function?is defined in a Simulink Function block at the Simulink model level.  The State actions or Transition labels which call the function also have code comments containing at least one Unicode character that does not have an ASCII character equivalent. The function call?is?after the Unicode comment.??  Any side-effects of the function call will not happen and the function will always return 0. No warning or notification is given to the user. This affects both simulation and code generation.
Workaround: You can use either of the following to work around this issue:Remove comments containing Unicode characters from State actions and Transition labels.Move the Simulink Function Block inside the Stateflow Chart.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a?Referenced?Model with a conditionally executed subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a?Referenced ?Model. Inside the?Referenced ?Model?block, there is a conditionally executed subsystem like a Function Call or a Trigger subsystem. The output of the conditionally executed subsystem connects to the root level output of the?Referenced ?Model?block.

Summary: Test harness creation errors for name conflict when no conflict exists
Description: Test harness creation unexpectedly aborts with the following error message:  'Could not insert the stub Simulink Function for the function 'FUNCTION' because the scope name 'SUBSYSTEM_NAME' matches the name of the component under test. ' This error should occur only when the test harness construction needs to create a scoped subsystem with the same name as the component under test. Two components at the same level cannot have same name.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code can incorrectly call handle class destructor when nested loops are present
Description: Given a handle class with a destructor or function delete method, the code generator can insert the destructor call prematurely in the generated code. This insertion occurs under the condition that the MATLAB code uses the handle class in the presence of nested loops.? For example, consider this class definition: classdef A < handle?  properties  ? ? IsClean  end?  methods??  ? ? function this = A?  ? ? ? ? this.IsClean = true;?  ? ? end?  ? ? function mymethod(this)?  ? ? ? ? assert(this.IsClean);?  ? ? end?  ? ? function delete(this)?  ? ? ? ? this.IsClean = false;?  ? ?end?  end?  end?  Consider this function that uses the class definition: function main(n)?  assert(isa(n, 'double'));?  while (n > 0)?  ? ? a = A;?  ? ? for k = 1:10?  ? ? end?  ? ? out = a.mymethod();?  ? ? n = n - 1;?  end  Generate code for the function.? >> codegen main?  >> main_mex?  When you execute the MEX function, MATLAB returns an error.? >> main_mex(10)  Assertion failed.  Error in A/mymethod (line 10)  ? ? ? ? assert(this.IsClean);  Error in main (line 7)  ? ? a.mymethod();  The assertion fails because the generated code erroneously calls the destructor after the inner for-loop (for k = 1:10 ) exits.

Summary: Support Software Installer fails while installing third-party content on machines with certain specific region and locale settings
Description: The Support Software Installer downloads and extracts third-party content. On some specific region and locale settings  that do not follow the Gregorian calendar, the third-party extraction fails with a "Negative time" exception. (This only affects other regions that do not follow the Gregorian calendar; for example, a Thai region following a Buddhist calendar.) MATLAB displays an error that begins with: Failed to download the third-party software??  Refer to?this MATLAB Answers post ?to check for a "Negative time" exception in your MATLAB installation and Activation log file.

Summary: Closing a large PDF report fails
Description: Closing a large PDF report can fail with the following error message: Error using slreportgen.report.internal.Document/closeError closing document package: Could not commit changes:Could not remove the original package:Directory not empty This error can occur on Microsoft Windows if another program, such as a virus checker or cloud drive, accesses the report directory during report generation. This situation can prevent the Report API from deleting and renaming files when closing a report.?
Workaround: If possible, generate the report in a directory that is not being accessed by other programs. Otherwise, try to set the hidden DOM document property, FORollback, to  false. For example:import mlreportgen.report.*rpt = Report('myreport', 'pdf');open(rpt);rpt.Document.FORollback = false;add(rpt, 'Hello');close(rpt);Setting this property to false reduces the chance that another process accesses files that the Report API is trying to delete or rename when closing the report.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Coder generated code is incorrect for models with constant values propagated to Add blocks
Description: HDL Coder can generate incorrect code for a model if the following conditions are met: There is an Add block used in any of the following modes: ++, +-, -+ or --. A constant value is propagated to all inputs of the Add block.  This issue is more pronounced for fixed-point computations. The incorrect HDL code may be detected when you generate the validation model and then simulate the validation model with an exhaustive test bench.?
Workaround: If your design is affected by this issue, run the attached P-coded file to disable the constant optimization. The syntax for running the P-coded function is as follows:?>> turnOffConstantOptimization('<modelName>');

Summary: Linker error occurs for model build when model contains EtherCAT blocks and Speedgoat IO modules
Description: The linker reports a compiler mismatch error (LNK2038) for a model build when the model contains EtherCAT blocks and certain Speedgoat IO modules. The error message indicates a Microsoft Visual C++ (MSVC) version mismatch. The linker error could occur when: The compiler for Simulink Real-Time is MSVC 2017. The Simulink-Real Time model contains EtherCAT library blocks. The Simulink-Real Time model includes certain Speedgoat IO library blocks.  This combination of compiler, Speedgoat IO modules, and EtherCAT blocks causes a mismatch when linking Simulink Real-Time generated code against Speedgoat library components.

Summary: Reparenting a component in App Designer apps might cause the component to be duplicated
Description: When reparenting a component by moving it from one container to another, the component could be duplicated in the target container if the component is moved quickly. This can occur for a single reparented component or a grouping of reparented components. When the component is duplicated, the app is corrupted in the following ways: The app cannot run because the generated code has duplicate component names. ?The users have no way to fix the app to remove the duplicate component.

Summary: MATLAB might crash if a Simulink model uses a Simulink.LookupTable object within a model reference hierarchy that uses a large amount of memory
Description: MATLAB might crash under these conditions:? A model reference hierarchy using a large amount of memory The computer in which code is being generated has relatively low memory (e.g., 16 Gigabyte RAM instead of 64, numbers will vary depending on the memory size of the model reference hierarchy) Deep within the model reference hierarchy one or more models use a Simulink.LookupTable  object.?
Workaround: Increase the RAM memory of the computer (e.g., from 16 Gigabytes to 64).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with For Each subsystem block and AUTOSAR target
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a For Each Subsystem. The root Inport block connects to the input port of a For Each Subsystem. In the For Each blockparameters dialog box, on the Input Partition  tab, for the input portthat connects to the root output port, the Partition  parameter isselected. In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the System target file ?parameter is set to autosar.tlc .
Workaround: Place a Signal Conversion block between the root Inport block and the partitioned input port of the For Each subsystem block. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select?Exclude this block from "Block Reduction' optimization parameter.

Summary: C-style hexadecimal constant for EtherCAT SDO?index or subindex crashes MATLAB during model build
Description: When index or subindex block parameter value for EtherCAT SDO blocks is provided as a hexadecimal constant in C language format (for example, 0x6060 ), MATLAB crashes during model build. This impacts the EtherCAT Sync SDO Upload, EtherCAT Sync SDO Download, EtherCAT Async SDO Upload, and EtherCAT Async SDO Download blocks.
Workaround: You can enter hexadecimal values in the edit box with the hex2dec conversion. For example, input hex2dec('6060') for the index instead of 0x6060.

Summary: Analysis may delete model blocks that are inactive variant choices in the design model
Description: Simulink Design Verifier analysis may delete model blocks that are inactive choices within variant subsystems in referenced models.

Summary: Test objectives for Stateflow library block in the referenced model are reported as Undecided with testcase
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may report test objectives for a Stateflow library block within a referenced model as  Undecided with testcase.    Additionally, the MATLAB command line will display diagnostic messages of the form:  Invalid Simulink object name: mg1915146/Subsystem/RefModel/ChartWithGraphicalFcnSubsystem/With Graphical Functions1  where,  RefModel  is the name of the referenced model containing the Stateflow library block.
Workaround: The error is generated when Simulink Design Verifier attempts to validate the generated test-cases. Even though the validation fails with the above error, the test-cases themselves are available in the Simulink Design Verifier data file. These test cases can be simulated to check if they achieve coverage of the test-objectives of the Stateflow library block using sldvrun, or by clicking on the Simulate tests and produce a model coverage report link in the Result Summary window.

Summary: Incorrect usage of RTE call when AUTOSAR calibration parameters passed by reference to a function
Description: The code generator produces incorrect code for a model when all of the following conditions exist: Model configuration parameter System target file  is set to?autosar.tlc . The model uses AUTOSAR?calibration parameters that are configured with storage class CalPrm  or InternalCalPrm . The calibration parameters are scalars. The calibration parameters are passed to a function that takes their addresses as function arguments.  For example, assume that function foo  accepts pointer arguments: void foo(uint8_t* arg1, uint8_t* arg2)  Also assume that AUTOSAR calibration parameters param1  and param2  are configured with storage classes CalPrm  and InternalCalPrm , respectively. If the calibration parameters are passed by pointer to function foo , the code generator produces incorrect code that looks like this:  foo(Rte_Prm_port_param1(), Rte_CData_param2());  However, the correct code is: uint8_t signalConversion1 =?Rte_Prm_port_param1();uint8_t signalConversion2 =?Rte_CData_param2();foo(&signalConversion1, &signalConversion2);  The incorrect code causes compiler errors or warnings, depending on the compiler in use. For example:? warning: passing argument 1 of ˇ®fooˇŻ makes pointer from integer without a cast[-Wint-conversion] [2 similar]?  One known modeling pattern that can expose this bug involves calibration parameters fed as inputs to a Stateflow chart that generates functions for its states rather than inlining them.

Summary: Source code annotation on a static function inside a header file is ignored
Description: Source code annotation is ignored when you justify an unproven check by annotating a static function located inside a header file. When you review Polyspace Code Prover results, the annotation for the check is not taken into account in the Result Details pane.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect handling of matrix concatenation when using multiple handle classes that reference the same object
Description: Incorrect code generation might occur when using matrix concatenation with handle class objects under the following conditions: The code contains at least two handle class variables which reference (alias) the same object. Vertical matrix concatenation is used on column vectors on the right-hand side of an assignment. One of the handle class variable's properties is written to on the left-hand side of the assignment from condition 2. The same property from a different aliased handle class variable is indexed inside the matrix concatenation from condition 2 such that one or more elements are extracted.  For example, consider the following MATLAB code: function y1 = reproEx(inVec)  ? ? h1 = handleVar();?  ? ? --SNIP--? ?  ? ? h1_alias = createMCOSAlias(h1);  ? ??root_vcat_pointer_aliased(h1,h1_alias);?  end  ?function root_vcat_pointer_aliased(h1, h1_alias)  ? ? h1_alias.value = [111; 222; 333; h1.value(3); 555; 666; 777; 888];  end ?? Condition 1 is satisfied? by: h1_alias = createMCOSAlias(h1); . The variables h1_alias  and h1  are handle classes and the function createMCOSAlias  assigns its input to its output (creating an alias). Condition 2 is satisfied by the expression [111; 222; 333; h1.value(3); 555; 666; 777; 888];  appearing on the right-hand side of an assignment. This expression is doing vertical concatenation of 1x1 column vectors. Condition 3 is satisfied by? h1_alias.value  appearing on the left-hand side of the assignment from Condition 2. Condition 4 is satisfied by? h1.value(3)  appearing inside the matrix concatenation from Condition 2. The generated code for this example is as follows:? void reproEx(const real_T inVec[8], real_T b_y1[8])  {  ? handleVar h1;  ? handleVar *h1_alias;  ? --SNIP--?  ? h1_alias = createMCOSAlias(&h1);  ? root_vcat_pointer_aliased(&h1, h1_alias);  ? --SNIP--?  }   ? handleVar h1;  ? handleVar *h1_alias;  ? --SNIP--?  ? h1_alias = createMCOSAlias(&h1);  ? root_vcat_pointer_aliased(&h1, h1_alias);  ? --SNIP--?  }??  static void root_vcat_pointer_aliased(const handleVar *h1, handleVar *h1_alias)  {??  ? h1_alias->value[0] = 111.0;  ? h1_alias->value[1] = 222.0;  ? h1_alias->value[2] = 333.0;  ? h1_alias->value[3] = h1->value[2];  ? h1_alias->value[4] = 555.0;  ? h1_alias->value[5] = 666.0;  ? h1_alias->value[6] = 777.0;  ? h1_alias->value[7] = 888.0;  }  The  read  of??h1->value[2]  incorrectly appears after the write to h1_alias->value[2] . The correct code is as follows: void reproEx(const real_T inVec[8], real_T b_y1[8])  {  ? handleVar h1;  ? handleVar *h1_alias;  ? --SNIP--?  ? h1_alias = createMCOSAlias(&h1);  ? root_vcat_pointer_aliased(&h1, h1_alias);  ? --SNIP--?  }??  static void root_vcat_pointer_aliased(const handleVar *h1, handleVar *h1_alias)  {  ? real_T d;  ? d = h1->value[2];  ? h1_alias->value[0] = 111.0;  ? h1_alias->value[1] = 222.0;  ? h1_alias->value[2] = 333.0;  ? h1_alias->value[3] = d;  ? h1_alias->value[4] = 555.0;  ? h1_alias->value[5] = 666.0;  ? h1_alias->value[6] = 777.0;  ? h1_alias->value[7] = 888.0;  }  The read of h1->value[2]   correctly appears before the write to? h1_alias->value[2] .

Summary: asbFlightControlAnalysis function does not accept inputs of type string
Description: Inputs to?asbFlightControlAnalysis  function must be of type char.  Inputs of type  string  generate?an error message.
Workaround: Specify inputs of type char?for function asbFlightControlAnalysis.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Dataflow execution domains using non-zero latency and a single thread might produce incorrect results
Description: Simulation and code generation of dataflow execution domains might produce incorrect results when the following conditions are true: Latency parameter of the dataflow execution domain is non-zero. The dataflow execution domain is partitioned into a single thread as indicated in the Property Inspector. The dataflow execution domain contains blocks with non-zero initial conditions.
Workaround: Set the latency parameter of the dataflow execution domain to zero.

Summary: Simulink Coder might fail to generate code for a model
Description: Simulink Coder might fail to generate code for a model displaying the following error message: tlc/ lib/ blkiolib.tlc Line: 3502 Column: 50? Unable to find VarGroupIdx within the GlobalBlockOutput scope

Summary: Remove root level I/O zero initialization code does not come into effect when zero valued enums initialize root inports
Description: The code generator might not remove initialization code for root-level inports for a model that meets these conditions:Enum  values initialize one or more root-level input ports.? These enum  values resolve to 0.?? The model configuration parameter Remove root level I/O zero initialization  is selected.

Summary: Large delay encountered when starting accelerator simulation for models with S-function blocks containing large number of PWork vectors
Description: If you are referencing a model from a Model block, Simulink can take a long time to build the accelerator simulation target. This long build time can occur when the model you are referencing contains an S-Function block that declares a large number of PWork vectors. For example, to check the number of PWork vectors for a C-MEX S-Function block, look for the statement: ssSetNumPWork(S, n);   where n  is large in the source code.

Summary: Scalar symbolic dimensions?propagating through multiple input single output blocks in Simulink cause dimension mismatch errors
Description: When propagating scalar symbolic dimensions through a multiple input, single output block, the symbolic dimension is?propagated to all other scalar inputs and outputs of the block when they have a matching width.  This can cause downstream symbolic dimension mismatch errors where the signal is scalar expanded at that input of a multiple input, single output block, or if the downstream block does not support symbolic dimensions.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect value propagates through a virtual bus that contains a nonvirtual bus
Description: If a signal selected from a virtual bus has the same name as a different signal in a nonvirtual bus that the virtual bus contains, the selected signal might have an incorrect value.
Workaround: Unzip and run the attached script every time you start Simulink. The script enables Simulink to convert a nonvirtual bus into a virtual bus when the nonvirtual bus is contained in a virtual bus.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Specified Initial output value of a conditional system may be overwritten with ground values
Description: Conditionally executed subsystems (example:Enabled, Triggered or Function-call subsystems) show incorrect simulation result in the first?time step when all the below conditions are TRUE:  The block output of the conditional subsystem explicitly specifies an Initial output  value.  The block outport of the conditional subsystem is unconditionally available.  The block outport of the conditional subsystem is connected to a non-virtual block.?  The non virtual block is made inactive using a Variant block, e.g:  Variant Sink/Variant Source.  Variant Subsystem with Propagate conditions outside variant subsystem? selected.    In the above mentioned scenario, the Initial conditions specified in the block output are overwritten by ground values.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model?with a Bus Assignment block
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Bus Assignment block. In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks? parameter is selected. At least one of the signals of the bus object that the Bus Assignment block assigns a new value contains a non-Auto  storage class.
Workaround: Clear the?Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks?parameter.

Summary: Compatibility check fails with generated saturate on overflow code
Description: If the code generated from your Simulink model contain statements that saturate on integer overflow, Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check might fail for test generation. For more information on the list of blocks that generate saturation code on integer overflow, search for?SaturateOnIntegerOverflow  in?this page in the documentation . Note that the code generation process in some cases can detect when overflow is not possible. When such cases are detected, the code generation process does not produce saturation code.

Summary: MATLABVariable reporter incorrectly reports a numeric variable
Description: The Report API MATLABVariable  reporter may incorrectly report a numeric variable if the variable value is same as a valid Graphics or Simulink handle. For example, in the below code, the?MATLABVariable ?reporter?reports a numeric variable as a Graphics object. num = 2;  fig = figure(num);  mlVarReporter = mlreportgen.report.MATLABVariable(num);?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect?results are possible when applying an?Intel SSE/AVX or ARM Neon library to code that contains a matrix variable definition in between two operations
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for MATLAB code that meets the following conditions: The?Code Replacement Library ? parameter, on the Custom Code tab of the Generate Code?pane?in?the MATLAB Coder app,?is set to Intel SSE (Linux) , Intel SSE (Windows) , Intel AVX (Linux) , Intel AVX (Windows),  or Inlined ARM NEON Intrinsics.  Two element wise arithmetic operations use the same single or double matrix variable. The matrix variable definition occurs in between the two?element wise arithmetic operations.?  When the above conditions are met, the generated code might incorrectly use stale values of a variable instead of using the updated value due to the definition.?? For the attached example, the code generator?generates the following incorrect code in which second call to _mm_mul_pd  uses stale input values stored in r0  :? void vectorOperations(double IA1[14]) {  ? ? int idx;  ? ? __m128d r0;   ? ? __m128d r1;  ? ? ? for (idx = 0; idx <= 12; idx += 2) {  ? ? ? ? ? r0 = _mm_loadu_pd(*(double (*)[2])&IA1[idx]);  ? ? ? ? ? r1 = _mm_mul_pd(r0, r0);  ? ? ? ? ? _mm_storeu_pd(&IA1[idx], r1);  ? ? ? ? ? r1 = _mm_mul_pd(r0, r0);  ? ? ? ? ? _mm_storeu_pd(&IA1[idx], r1);  ? ? ? ?}  }   After?following the steps?in the?workaround section, the code?generator generates the following code where updated IA1  is loaded into r0 , r1  before being used?in second call to _mm_mul_pd:   void vectorOperations(double IA1[14]) {???? int idx;???? __m128d r0;???? __m128d r1;???? __m128d r2;?????for (idx = 0; idx <= 12; idx += 2) {????????r0 = _mm_loadu_pd(*(double (*)[2])&IA1[idx]);??????? r1 = _mm_loadu_pd(*(double (*)[2])&IA1[idx]);??????? r2 = _mm_mul_pd(r0, r1);??????? _mm_storeu_pd(&IA1[idx], r2);??????? r0 = _mm_loadu_pd(*(double (*)[2])&IA1[idx]);?????? ?r1 = _mm_loadu_pd(*(double (*)[2])&IA1[idx]);??????? r2 = _mm_mul_pd(r0, r1);??????? _mm_storeu_pd(&IA1[idx], r2);??? }}       ?
Workaround: Unzip the attached P-file,?workaroundForBugReport1907910.p.Insert the P-file on the MATLAB file path.For every new MATLAB session, in the Command Window, enter the following command:run workaroundForBugReport1907910.p?

Summary: Referenced models are re-compiled unnecessarily when adding parameters to a parameter set in Test Manager
Description: Referenced models in a top model may be repeatedly compiled each time a parameter is added to a parameter set in a Test Manager test case.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model?with a reset state inside For iterator or While Iterator Subsystem block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a For Iterator or While Iterator Subsystem block. Inside the For Iterator or While Iterator Subsystem block, there is at least one Unit Delay block. The input or output port of the Unit Delay block drives the For Iterator or While Iterator?Subsystem's output port. The parameter?States when starting  ?of the For Iterator or While Iterator block inside the For Iterator or While Iterator subsystem is set to reset.  The For Iterator or While Iterator subsystem is conditional executed. For example, the subsystem is inside a Triggered subsystem, or its loop bound is variant and the loop doesn't implement until the passage of a few simulation time steps.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generating code for a model hierarchy that contains referenced models in Normal mode with zero-crossing signals may take a long time to generate code and detect incorrect zero-crossings in RSim target
Description: When generating code from a model containing referenced models in normal-mode which have zero-crossing signals at any level of the hierarchy, the intermediate model .rtw  file may be excessively large in size and cause a slow-down in the code generation process. In addition, if the code is generated for the RSim target, the solver may detect zero-crossings in incorrect directions, or may not detect the zero-crossings that should be detected.??
Workaround: Set the Simulation Mode parameter of any Model blocks to Accelerator if they are referencing directly or indirectly a model that contains zero-crossing signals.

Summary: Graphic EQ produces error with certain gain settings
Description: The Graphic EQ block can generate an error when using very low gains (<1e-6) or when its? EQ Order  parameter is set to the default value of 2.

Summary: quat2angle, dcm2angle, and rod2angle functions might return complex results
Description: Some singularity inputs to quat2angle , dcm2angle , and rod2angle  functions might return complex numbers with?small imaginary parts.

Summary: spi function returns an error on Windows
Description: When trying to create an spi  object with 'aardvark' or 'ni845x' adaptors on Windows, MATLAB returns the following error: >> s = spi('aardvark', 0, 0)   Error using spi (line 66)?  Undefined function or variable 'adaptor'.?

Summary: Long FMU parameter names may cause Simulink failure
Description: Simulink fails to load an FMU with more than 100 parameters if the first 60 or more characters of the parameter names are identical.
Workaround: Users must change parameter names to avoid the limitation.

Summary: Deploying an application using the databaseDatastore function on an MPS instance fails with an error
Description: When using an MPS instance, the?databaseDatastore ? function fails with the following error:? [err] [worker:72] Error using databaseDatastore (line 71) [err] [worker:72] SQLite file does not exist.
Workaround: On an MPS instance, access a MATLAB installation file by using this command instead:?toolboxdir('__product__')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with Reusable custom storage class and Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: A root Inport block and a root Outport block connects to an Initialize Function block, a Reset Function block, or Terminate Function block. The same Reusable custom storage class is specified at both root Inport and root Outport blocks.
Workaround: Remove the Reusable custom storage class from the root Inport block and root Outport block.

Summary: "Access denied" error during model protection
Description: When you create or modify a protected model on Windows machines, you might receive an "Access Denied" error.?

Summary: Running large numbers of tests in?parallel mode using the Test Manager causes warnings
Description: When you run test cases in parallel mode using the Test Manager and Parallel Computing Toolbox, the messages that are passed between host and workers can cause conflicts if the model that is being simulated on a worker is being closed or compiled on the host.  Test Manager automates the scheduling and simulations on the workers, therefore the warnings may appear sporadically for a very large number of simulations when the above condition is met.
Workaround: Start a?worker pool manually?using parpool?and execute the following command.Simulink.sdi.enablePCTSupport('manual');?Now, execute the?tests from Test Manager in?parallel mode.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calls to coder.ceval with GPU arguments can produce CPU arguments in the generated code
Description: For a coder.ceval  call, the generated code may incorrectly pass CPU pointer for a GPU argument if the argument is an unbounded variable. For example, consider a dummy function: function y = mysamplefunc(x)   ? ?y = coder.nullcopy(0);  ? ?coder.ceval('a_c_function', coder.rref(x,'gpu'), coder.wref(y));  end   Generating code with an unbounded input as shown below will result in incorrect code.? codegen -config coder.gpuConfig -args {coder.typeof(0, [1, inf])} mysamplefunc?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Absolute-time temporal logic operators execute incorrectly in Stateflow charts resumed from a saved simulation state
Description: When you save and restore the simulation state for your model, an incorrect simulation time might be saved when all of the following conditions are true: Your model contains a Stateflow chart that uses absolute-time temporal logic operators in state and transition actions. You use quotes with the time unit keyword in the temporal logic operator, for example:?after(1000,'sec') .  If the simulation time is not saved correctly, then temporal logic operators may return an incorrect value. It is possible for transitions guarded by temporal logic operators to evaluate as true at unexpected times.
Workaround: Remove any quotes surrounding the time unit keyword?sec, msec, and?usec, for example:?after(1000,sec)?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when closing a model after an undo operation
Description: MATLAB might crash when you perform these steps in the Simulink Editor: Right-click a Subsystem block. Select Subsystem & Model Reference >?Expand Subsystem . Undo the subsystem expansion. Close the window without saving the model.

Summary: Descriptor State-Space (DeSS) block uses full matrix format instead of sparse matrix format for its solver Jacobian
Description: Descriptor State-Space (DeSS) block should support sparse solver Jacobian but it didn't in R2018b. This may have negative impact on simulation performance for systems with large number of continuous states.?

Summary: executecql and partitionRead functions?throw a Java exception for bigint, smallint, counter columns with more than 5000 rows
Description: The executecql  and partitionRead  functions?can throw a Java exception if both of the following conditions are true: The result contains a column defined as a bigint , smallint , or counter  on the Cassandra database. The result has more than 5000 rows.  The error resembles the following: Error using cassandra/readCassandraResultSet (line 1084)Java exception occurred:java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: 5000at com.mathworks.toolbox.database.utilities.longArrayList.add(longArrayList.java:35)at com.mathworks.toolbox.cassandra.CassandraResultSet.readData(CassandraResultSet.java:136)Error in cassandra/executecql (line 457)            resultsTable = this.readCassandraResultSet(results);Error in cassandra/partitionRead (line 533)                resultsTable = executecql(this,"SELECT * FROM " + keyspace + "." + tableName                +";","ConsistencyLevel","ONE");

Summary: Support Software Installer fails if the proper SSL certificates are not available
Description: The Support Software Installer uses SSL certificates to establish secure internet connections when downloading third-party content. When? the expected SSL certificates are invalid or are not available on your machine, the third-party content fails to download. MATLAB displays an error message that begins with:? Failed to download the third-party software?

Summary: Stateflow editor enters text edit mode when trying to move objects
Description: If you try to reposition an object on the Stateflow canvas by clicking and dragging on the object's label or text area, the Stateflow editor enters text edit mode instead of moving the object with the mouse cursor.
Workaround: Click and drag on another part of the object besides the label or text area.?

Summary: When RebuildOnOpen is true for an externally saved test harness, running a test case in the Test Manager may silently resave the harness.
Description: When RebuildOnOpen  is true  for an externally saved test harness, running a test case in the Test Manager may silently resave the harness.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, set the Test Harness RebuildOnOpen flag to false before running the test.

Summary: The trainFasterRCNNObjectDetector function produces a CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS error
Description: The?trainFasterRCNNObjectDetector  issues an error during training. The error, CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS, occurs when regions of interest are near the image boundary.?

Summary: Variable-step simulation of a Simulink model containing one or more?FIR Decimation?blocks?may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when you run a model containing a FIR Decimation block, and all of the following conditions are true:  The model uses a variable-step solver The?Rate options ?parameter on the?block is set to?Allow multirate processing; ?the sample time of the output port of the block is?K ?times slower than that of the input port where K  is the integer value specified for the Decimation factor  parameter The greatest common denominator of all the discrete sample times in the model is a small number relative to the magnitude of the individual sample times.  To help determine if the greatest common denominator of all the discrete sample times in the model is a relatively small number, switch the model to a fixed-step solver and then press Run. If the condition is present, Simulink throws an error similar to:? The computed fixed step size (...) is 1000000.0 times smaller than all the discrete sample times in the model... You can fix this error either by switching to a variable step solver...

Summary: fetchOutputs errors on jobs submitted from a R2016a MATLAB client to a R2018b MATLAB Job Scheduler.
Description: A job submitted from a R2016a MATLAB client to a R2018b MATLAB Job Scheduler will finish successfully, but will throw an error when you try to fetch the results. The same error is thrown when you try to access the 'Error' or '"Warnings' properties of the job's tasks.  >> fetchOutputs(job);  Error using parallel.task.MJSTask/hGetProperty (line 196)  An error that is unknown to this version of MATLAB has occurred on the server. Original message:  unrecognized method hash: method not supported by remote object  Error in parallel.Task/get.Warnings (line 105)  ? ? ? ? ? ? diagnosticWarnings = task.hGetProperty('DiagnosticWarnings');  Error in parallel.Task/hWarnAboutWarnings (line 564)  ? ? ? ? ? ? warnings = task.Warnings;  Error in parallel.Job/fetchOutputs (line 986)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? thisTask.hWarnAboutWarnings('parallel:job:WarningsDuringTaskExecution', {thisTaskID});?

Summary: Error when selecting variables for model that has an active configuration reference
Description: In the following Simulink Design Optimization UIs, an error is thrown when you try to specify variables to tune for a model with an active configuration reference. Response Optimization tool, when creating a new Design Variables Set or Uncertain Variables Set Parameter Estimation tool, when clicking Select Parameters Sensitivity Analysis tool, when clicking Select Parameters  The error message is similar to: The variable 'configSet' cannot be resolved by the model 'mdl'. Make sure that the variable is defined in the model, base workspace, or data dictionary.

Summary: Index exceeds the number of array elements (0) error when editing or simulating Test Sequence block
Description: Opening a Test Sequence block or simulating a model containing a Test Sequence block can cause the error?Index exceeds the number of array elements (0) .

Summary: Dangling expression might be in generated code for a model with S-Function block
Description: Simulink Coder might generate a dangling expression while expression folding for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains an S-Function block.? The block has a TLC implementation that outputs an expression in TLC. The S-Function block uses signals with custom data types.  For example, consider the lines of code below:? y=a+b;   z=y+c;   The code generator?generates the dangling expression?a+b ?after expression folding:? a+b;?  z=a+b+c; ?

Summary: Export-function model fails to update when two function-call root-level Inport blocks drive a common sample time independent Model block
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when it meets the following conditions: The model is an export-function model. The model contains a Function-Call Subsystem block that includes a Model block. The referenced model for the Model block has the configuration parameter Periodic sample time constraint  set to 'Ensure sample time independent' . The Function-Call Subsystem block is driven by two function-call root-level Inport blocks through a Mux block.  When updating the model, Simulink throws an error message similar to the one below.? Model block 'testMdl/Chart/a/Model' is driven by an asynchronous function call at inport 1. An asynchronous function call that directly or indirectly involves multiple asynchronous function calls is not allowed to drive a model block input port.

Summary: Parallel simulations might produce incorrect simulation results
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy in parallel might produce incorrect simulation results when all of the following conditions are true:The top model has the?Enable parallel model reference builds ?configuration parameter selected. The top model?Rebuild ?configuration parameter is set?to?Never . The top model?Never rebuild diagnostic? is set to?none . The Simulation cache folder contains only Simulink cache files. The model hierarchy contains a Model block set to simulate in accelerator mode. The referenced model contains another Model block set to simulate in accelerator mode. The model referenced by the second Model block was modified before the previous simulation. The previous simulation used a parallel pool of workers. The previous simulation rebuilt simulation targets according to the top model?Rebuild ?configuration parameter setting of?Always ,?If any changes detected , or?If any changes in known dependencies detected .

Summary: Error when using Multiply-Accumulate block? in a model
Description: If you add the Multiply-Accumulate block to a model or if you double click on this block when inside a model, you get this error message: Error evaluating 'MaskDialog' callback of Multiply-Accumulate block(mask( '<modelname>/Multiply-Accumulate'. Callback string is 'hdlblkmask_streaming_number_of_samples_cb(gcb);'   Undefined function 'hdlblkmask_streaming_number_of_samples_cb' for input arguments of type 'char'.?

Summary: Documentation search hangs on Chinese Windows systems when configured to search the installed Chinese documentation
Description: On Chinese Windows systems, when the help preferences are set to use the locally installed Chinese documentation, MATLAB hangs when you use the search.
Workaround: To search the documentation, change the help preferences to use the Chinese documentation on the web or the locally installed English documentation.?

Summary: Running Simulink Test test files with MATLAB Unit Test may result in multiple coverage reports for the same model
Description: Multiple Simulink Coverage reports for the same model may be generated under these conditions: A Simulink Test test file is configured to collect coverage using Simulink Coverage. The Simulink Test test file is run using the MATLAB Unit Test Runner. There are three or more test cases in a test suite, and each test case uses the same model as the system under test (SUT).  The Simulink Coverage reports are shown on the result set, and paths are printed to the MATLAB Command line when using the sltest.plugins.ModelCoveragePlugin .
Workaround: The last coverage report in the Result Set or printed to the command line will have the total aggregated results.

Summary: Accessing Cloud Center clusters without a valid online license displays an incorrect error message
Description: When you attempt to use a Cloud Center cluster and you do not have an online license for MATLAB Parallel Server, you can get one of the following error messages: You are not signed up to use MATLAB Parallel Cloud. To sign up, click here . You are not signed up to use MATLAB Parallel Cloud. To sign up, run parcluster('MATLAB Parallel Cloud')   The message should say: Your MathWorks account is not associated with a MATLAB Parallel Server license that is managed online.   If you want to use Cloud Center clusters, sign up for an online license for MATLAB Parallel Server. On the MATLAB Home  tab, in the Environment  section, click Parallel  > Create and Manage Clusters , then click the Create Cloud Cluster  button in the toolstrip. If you do not want to create a cluster at this point, then close the dialog once you have reached the Create Cluster  stage.

Summary: Build process fails when?TargetHWDeviceType is set to RTW.HWDeviceRegistry object that has no Alias value
Description: You can register a custom hardware device through a?RTW.HWDeviceRegistry  object. If you do not specify a value for the Alias  property of the object and specify the device for the model configuration parameter? 'TargetHWDeviceType' , then building the model produces this error: Error using mf.zero.PrimitiveSequence/ at?Invalid index.?Error in Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderSpecificationTokens/ resolveTargetEnvironemntToken (line 138)?????????????????value = device.AliasList.at(1);?Error in Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderSpecificationTokens (line 63)?????????????this.registerToken('TARGETENVIRONMENT', Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderSpecificationTokens.resolveTargetEnvironemntToken(hardwareDevice, ?stf));?Error in Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderConfiguration (line 227)?????????????this.Tokens = Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderSpecificationTokens(this.ModelName, ?isProtected, resolveLoadDependentTokens, ?stf);?Error in slbuild_private (line 459)?????folders = Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderConfiguration(mdl);?Error in sl (line 15)?[varargout{1:nargout}]=feval(varargin{:});?Error in slbuild (line 83)?????sl('slbuild_private', ?mdl, ?target, ?varargin{2:end});?Error in rtwbuild (line 234)???????????????slbuild(sys, ?'StandaloneRTWTarget', ?...
Workaround: To specify an Alias property value for the RTW.HWDeviceRegistry object, use the device registration procedure:function sl_customization(cm)  cm.registerTargetInfo(@loc_register_device);endfunction thisDev = loc_register_device  thisDev = RTW.HWDeviceRegistry;  thisDev.Vendor = 'myDevVendor';  thisDev.Type = 'myDevType';  thisDev.Alias = {'VendorTypeAlias'};  thisDev.Platform = {'Prod', 'Target'};  thisDev.setWordSizes([8 16 32 32 32]);  ...end

Summary: Intensity value lost when using pcdownsample, pcdenoise, and pcmerge functions
Description: The pcdownsample , pcdenoise , and pcmerge  functions return a pointCloud  object with an empty value for the Intensity  property.?

Summary: cd?command fails for NTFS paths/network drive letter paths having junctions
Description: On Windows, the MATLAB?cd?command fails in the below scenarios :    NTFS network path has directory junctions either to directories in different hard disk partitions, or physical drives on a remote machine.   The directory junction, or symbolic link, is created using the Windows? mklink? command.  For example, junction1?is a directory junction to folder?Z:\folder1?on the Z: drive on a remote machine. Diagnostic error shown is as below:   cd \\RemoteMachine\ShareFolder\junction1?   Error using cd    Cannot CD to \\RemoteMachine\ShareFolder\junction1 (Name is nonexistent or not a directory).   NTFS network path is mounted as a drive letter path and has a directory junction to directories on different hard disk partitions or physical drives on a remote machine.    For example,?junction1?is a directory junction to folder?Z:\folder1?on the Z: drive on a remote machine and?the shared network path is mounted to drive letter Y: such that Y:\ is equivalent to \\RemoteMachine\ShareFolder      Diagnostic error shown is as below:               cd? Y:\junction1               Error using cd                Cannot CD to??Y:\junction1? (Name is nonexistent or not a directory).?                  Note: Make sure Z:\ drive/partition does not exist on your local machine

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model?with an?Assignment block
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: Configuration Parameters > System target file ?parameter is set to 'ert.tlc' . Configuration Parameters >?Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks  parameter is selected. The Input data port and at least one index signal port of an Assignment block share the same source.  This may result in an uninitialized local variable in the generated code.
Workaround: Clear the Configuration Parameters >?Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks?parameter.

Summary: MATLAB will crash when loading a figure with pinned data tips
Description: If you save a figure with pinned data tips and then attempt to load the figure, MATLAB will crash.

Summary: niqe?function has dependency on Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox or Financial Toolbox
Description: If you call the niqe  function and you do not have Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox or Financial Toolbox installed, you get the following error: 'nanmean' requires one of the following:  ? Financial Toolbox  ? Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox    Error in niqeModel/calculateNIQEscore (line 173)  ? ? ? ? ? ? mu_testparam? ? ?= nanmean(full_NSSfeatures);   Error in niqe (line 89)  score = model.calculateNIQEscore(im);? ?

Summary: PowerPoint template background image missing from PPT API presentation
Description: The? background image that is specified in the slide layout of a PowerPoint template might not appear in the output presentation that is generated by using the PPT API.

Summary: Exporting label data with scene labels throws an error in ImageLabeler App
Description: When more images are added to an ImageLabeler app session with scene labels, exporting the labels results in the following command line error. Error using vision.internal.labeler.validation.checkLabelData (line 90) Invalid entry in label data table for Scene labelType. Table entries for column 'scenelabelname' must be logical scalars.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initialization value for State Writer block that writes to the state of fixed-point data type
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that satisfies the following conditions: Model contains State Writer block that writes to a state of fixed-point data type. State Writer block writes a fixed-point value where the real world value and stored integer (SI) value do not match.  In such cases, the generated code for the state writer might write an incorrect value to the state variable.
Workaround: Use the Initial condition?block parameter of the corresponding block rather than the State Writer inside an Initialize Function. If the initial condition is not a constant or if the State Writer is not inside an Initialize Function, convert the state to an integer type of the same word length and signedness as the fixed point type and work directly with the stored integer value, if possible. For example, if the State Writer is writing the state of a Delay block, convert the Delay to an integer type and use data type conversion blocks at the delay input and output to convert the fixed point type to its stored integer value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit conversion factor may be wrong between frequency and angular_frequency physical quantities
Description: Unit conversion factor?between frequency and angular_frequency physical quantities?may?be wrong. For example, the conversion?between 1/s@frequency  and rad/s@angular_frequency  may?output a wrong numeric?result. This error may?occur in the Unit Conversion block and in implicit conversions.
Workaround: Use Gain block and specify Gain as the conversion factor, instead of using the Unit Conversion block. Avoid implicit conversion between frequency and angular frequency.

Summary: Vehicle wheel in 3D environment wobbles when you simultaneously vary the wheel camber and rotation angles
Description: In the 3D visualization environment, if you simultaneously vary the camber (roll) and rotation (pitch) angles on the vehicle wheel, the wheel rotates about the wrong axis.? The wheel appears to wobble.

Summary: XCP Configuration block generates error when A2L file has extended flag for CAN_ID_MASTER or CAN_ID_SLAVE
Description: In a Simulink model with a XCP Configuration block, if an A2L file is specified that includes an extended CAN_ID_MASTER  or CAN_ID_SLAVE  parameter, the following error occurs.   Error evaluating 'InitFcn' callback of XCP Configuration block

Summary: Simulation of an S-Function with a large number of elements in DWork fails with "Memory Allocation Error"
Description: When you run a model that contains an S-Function with a large number of elements in DWork, Simulink might throw a "Memory Allocation Error". In rare situations where the number of elements in DWork is extremely large, MATLAB will crash.

Summary: Flight Control Analysis Tools dialog windows appear partially offscreen on low-resolution monitors
Description: Flight Control Analysis Tools dialog windows, used for analysis workflow input selection, appear partially offscreen on low-resolution monitors.

Summary: DPI component generation errors if the model contains Simscape blocks
Description: Code generation errors when generating a DPI component from a Simulink model that contains Simscape blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation with Function Packaging configured for a conditional non-virtual Subsystem
