Description: Incorrect code may be generated when a Variant Source is connected directly to an Inport of a non-virtual Subsystem Below are the scenarios where the incorrect code generation may happen:? Non-virtual Subsystem contains more than one input port. A multiple input Variant Source is directly connected to Inport of a non-virtual Subsystem. Condition on the input port is not same as the subsystem's condition. The non-virtual Subsystem has Function Packaging  parameter set to?Reusable function/Nonreusable  function.  ?Example:Consider a Variant source inside the non-virtual subsystem. The subsystem block's condition is (for example: A==1||A==2). However, Inport1 directly connected to Variant source within has condition A==1. Inport2 connected to Variant source has condition A==2.

Summary: Incorrect confusionchart data display with sorted classes
Description: If you create a chart with the?confusionchart  function, and change the order of its classes  with the  sortClasses  function, then the chart does  not display correctly in the Live Editor and does not save and load correctly  at the command line.  If you save and then reload the chart, then the order of data in the chart is permuted with respect to the order of class labels, so the chart does not show the correct data. The  confusionchart  function is  available in both Deep Learning Toolbox and Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox.
Workaround: To work around this problem outside Live Editor, before saving the chart, use the sortClasses function to return the order of classes to that set on chart creation. The chart will then save and load correctly.When using Live Editor, after using the sortClasses function, open the chart in a figure window.

Summary: Running?test case with MATLAB Unit Test framework?and the ModelCoveragePlugin results in an error for models containing a Model Reference block with a test harness
Description: When using the sltest.plugins.ModelCoveragePlugin  class?to generate coverage reports for a model which includes a referenced model which has a test harness, the following error can occur: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.    Error in sltest.internal.sources.ModelSource (line 18)  obj.TopLevelModel = managerCoverageResults.TopLevelModel;    Error in sltest.internal.coverage.returnModelCoverageResults (line 20)  theSources(end+1) = ModelSource(coverageResults(coverageResultsIndex),resultSetIDs(resulSetIndex));    Error in sltest.plugins.ModelCoveragePlugin/runTestSuite (line 222)  [modelCoverageResults,modelSources] = returnModelCoverageResults(plugin.ResultSetIDs);    Error in matlab.unittest.plugins.TestRunnerPlugin/invokeTestContentOperatorMethod_ (line 686)  [varargout{1:nargout}] = method(plugin, pluginData);    Error in matlab.unittest.plugins.TestRunnerPlugin/runTestSuite (line 226)  iter.CurrentOperator.invokeTestContentOperatorMethod_(@runTestSuite, pluginData, iter);    Error in sltest.plugins.TestManagerResultsPlugin/runTestSuite (line 59)  plugin.runTestSuite@matlab.unittest.plugins.TestRunnerPlugin(pluginData);    Error in matlab.unittest.plugins.TestRunnerPlugin/invokeTestContentOperatorMethod_ (line 686)  [varargout{1:nargout}] = method(plugin, pluginData);    Error in matlab.unittest.TestRunner/evaluateMethodOnPlugins (line 756)  [varargout{1:nargout}] = iter.CurrentOperator.invokeTestContentOperatorMethod_(method, pluginData, iter);    Error in matlab.unittest.TestRunner/doRun (line 411)  runner.evaluateMethodOnPlugins(@runTestSuite, pluginData);    Error in matlab.unittest.TestRunner/run (line 290)  result = doRun(runner);    Error in TestModelCoveragePlugin (line 41)  testResult = runner.run(ts);
Workaround: Leverage the coverage results after adding the sltest.plugins.TestManagerResultsPlugin object to create a Model Coverage report using the cvhtml function.runner = matlab.unittest.TestRunner.WithTextOutput();runner.addPlugin(sltest.plugins.TestManagerResultsPlugin);suite = testsuite('SimulinkTestFile.mldatx');results = runner.run(suite);?cvhtml('/path/to/coveragePage.html',results.Details.SimulinkTestManagerResults.CoverageResults);

Summary: Installing latest update to Visual Studio 2017 can cause target computer to report real-time application download error
Description: Installing the latest update to Visual Studio 2017 (15.6 or later) and building a model can cause an error when you download the real-time application to the target computer. The error that appears on the target computer is: Unable to load DLL dependency: KERNEL32.dll.GetFileSizeEx  Because the Visual Studio 2017 update modifies the Windows 10 SDK on the development computer, this update causes the same issue (error on real-time application download) to occur for models built after the update with Visual Studio 2015 on that development computer.? For more information about this update, see?Update Visual Studio 2017 to the most recent release .
Workaround: For MATLAB releases from R2016b through R2018b update 1, use these steps to rerun the Visual Studio 2017 installer and change the version of the Windows 10 SDK that is installed.Rerun the Visual Studio 2017 installer.With the installer running, push the 'modify' button.Choose 'Individual components' in the top menu bar.A long list of components that are installed is shown.Scroll down to the 'SDKs, libraries, and frameworks' section.Continue down to the Windows 10 SDK choices.Deselect 'Windows 10 SDK (10.0.17763.0)' and make sure 'Windows 10 SDK (10.17134.0)' is selected.Click the 'Modify' button in the lower right corner to complete the change.Now, both Visual Studio 2015 and Visual Studio 2017 will compile models that can be loaded on the target computer.

Summary: Warnings are not displayed in the Simulation Manager
Description: When using the Simulation Manager  to monitor the status of Simulink simulations, warnings generated during the simulation are not shown in the Diagnostics  tab when there are multiple similar warnings. The status of the simulation correctly indicates "Completed with Warnings'. For example, if there are two or more warnings about unconnected lines, the Diagnostics  tab will show up blank. Multiple different warnings or a single warning message will be shown as expected.?
Workaround: The warnings can be?accessed?from?SimulationMetadata?on the?SimulationOutput?object.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with?OptimizeBlockOrder?model configuration parameter set to Improved code execution speed
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains more than one Function Caller block. In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Optimize block operation order in the generated code? parameter is set to Improved code execution speed.
Workaround: Set?the?Optimize block operation order in the generated code??model configuration parameter to None.

Summary: Crash when generating code for model that uses Simulink Function and Function Caller blocks and Stateflow
Description: Generating code for a model causes MATLAB to crash if all of these conditions are true: Model uses an ERT-based system target file. Model includes Simulink Function or Function Caller blocks and is using Stateflow. Model data and function interfaces are configured with code mappings. For example, you can configure code mappings by using the Code Mappings editor.  When the crash occurs, the stack trace contains text which is similar to the following text: [ 2] 0x00007ff641e05479 bin/glnxa64/libmwcodermapping.so+01885305 _ZN15coderdictionary23SimulinkFunctionMapping27getCodeInputOutputArguments EPK8SLRootBDRSt6vectorINSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIcSt11char_traitsIcESaIcEEESaISA_EESD_+00000233?

Summary: Figure shown in parsim example is incorrect
Description: The figure shown in parsim example "Rapid Accelerator Simulations Using Parsim" is incorrect. Creating the plot using the output from the simulations gives a different plot than the one shown in the documentation. Specifically, the correct plot should show eight different curves corresponding to each of the simulations.

Summary: Deleting a linked block from a library might crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB crashes or produces an error when the following conditions occur: A Simulink library links to blocks inside the same library. The library is unlocked for modification. Another window or tab contains a linked block. The source block is deleted from the library.  If you select the linked block inside the library after the source block has been deleted, MATLAB crashes. If source block deletion fails, the MATLAB command window displays this error: Assertion failed: Deviant '43' :?object of type 'SLM3I.Block' [id = -1 ] is not present at?b:\ matlab\ src\ m3i\ kernel\ deviant\ deviant.cpp:72:?

Summary: A saved RF Blockset model containing an RF Filter block reopens with default RF Filter block settings and not with the saved settings.
Description: If you open a saved model, it will run the model with the default RF filter block settings even though the mask contains the correct saved settings.? This is a silent wrong answer.

Summary: Unable to open or delete Spectrum Analyzer Block
Description: You might be unable to open or delete Spectrum Analyzer block if both of the following conditions are true:? Model saved with Channel Measurements enabled. Inherited or specified sample rate is greater than 10kHz.

Summary: When closing a window MATLAB sometimes crashes with an abnormal termination message
Description: MATLAB might occasionally crash when you close some windows, such as Add-On Explorer, Add-On Manager, App Designer, UIFigure windows, or the Simulink Template Gallery. The crash report includes the line:   "std::terminate() detected"  And the report lists the method:   webwindow_extprocess:BrowserAppProcess::~BrowserAppProcess

Summary: Simulink uses old values of data dictionary entries when dictionary is closed or recreated while in use
Description: If a Simulink model uses a data dictionary and you manually recreate the dictionary at the command-line (by deleting the original dictionary and creating or copying in a new dictionary), the model continues to use the data from the original dictionary. The same problem occurs if you manually close the dictionary (by calling Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll ) and then open and modify the dictionary by using the command-line dictionary API.
Workaround: Avoid deleting then copying or recreating data dictionaries. Instead, use the command-line dictionary API to modify the contents of the dictionary. Do not close the dictionary prior to making changes.Alternatively, close all models and dictionaries that use a dictionary before deleting then copying or recreating it.

Summary: Incorrect promotion of LookupTableObject parameter of lookup table block to mask
Description: Promotion of the LookupTableObject  parameter of a Lookup Table block to its mask may result in the following error: Reference to non-existence field 'moveTo'   Older release models that have the LookupTableObject  parameter promoted to the block, may lose this promoted parameter when the model is upgraded to R2018b release version.

Summary: Simulink might crash at startup on Linux
Description: On some Linux systems or Linux remote desktop environments with the X11 RANDR extension enabled, Simulink might crash at startup. The stack trace references QXcbScreen::updateRefreshRate .

Summary: Site Viewer terrain data contains geographical offset
Description: The terrain data used by Site Viewer and RF Propagation functions contains a geographical offset. The offset amount is location-dependent and may be up to 125 meters. The offset may be visible for some locations in Site Viewer and reduces the accuracy of signal strength estimates by the following functions:?coverage , sinr , sigstrength , and link .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code is missing necessary data copy when using multiple handle classes that reference the same object
Description: Incorrect code generation might occur when creating references (aliases) with handle classes under the following conditions: The code contains at least two handle class variables that reference the same object. A property of one of the handle class objects is passed as an argument to a function which writes to its corresponding input argument. Following the function call from condition 2, the same property on one of the other handle class objects is read. For example, it could be read by passing it to another function which reads the value.  ?For example, consider the following MATLAB code:? function [y11,y12,y13,y14,y15,y16] = issue_1884256(zero,vec,mat,idxVec)  ? ? --SNIP--  ? ? handle_alias_mat = createMCOSAlias( handle_mat );  ? ? --SNIP--  ? ? [y13,y14] = fcn_1( handle_alias_mat.value, handle_alias_zero.value - 1, handle_alias_idxVec.value );  ? ? --SNIP--  ? ? [handle_alias_y15.value,y16] = fcn_2( handle_y15.value, handle_mat.value, handle_zero.value - 15, handle_idxVec.value );  end?  Condition 1 is satisfied? by: handle_alias_mat = createMCOSAlias( handle_mat ); . The function createMCOSAlias  assigns its input to its output (creating an alias). Condition 2 is satisfied by:? [y13,y14] =?fcn_1( handle_alias_mat.value, handle_alias_zero.value - 1, handle_alias_idxVec.value ); . The function?fcn_1?writes to its first input argument (which corresponds to the property of one of the handle classes passed as the first argument). Condition 3 is satisfied by:? [handle_alias_y15.value,y16] =?fcn_2( handle_y15.value, handle_mat.value, handle_zero.value - 15, handle_idxVec.value );  The function fcn_2  reads its second input argument (which corresponds to the property of the other handle class passed as the second argument). The generated code for this example might be as follows: void?issue_1884256(double zero, const double vec[12], const double mat[99],  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? const double idxVec[20], double *y11, double y12[99], double  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? *y13, double y14[99], double y15[12], double y16[99])  {  ? --SNIP--?  ? handle_alias_mat = createMCOSAlias(&handle_mat);  ? --SNIP--?  ?? fcn_1(handle_alias_mat->value, idxVec, y14);  ? --SNIP--  ? fcn_2(handle_mat.value, y16);  }  However, the correct generated code should be (notice the extra necessary data copy):? void? issue_1884256(double zero, const double vec[12], const double mat[99],  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? const double idxVec[20], double *y11, double y12[99], double  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? *y13, double y14[99], double y15[12], double y16[99])  {  ? --SNIP--  ? handle_alias_mat = createMCOSAlias(&handle_mat);  ? --SNIP--  ? for (i = 0; i < 99; i++) {  ? ? b_handle_mat[i] = handle_alias_mat->value[i];  ? }  ? fcn_1(b_handle_mat, idxVec, y14);  ? --SNIP--  ? fcn_2(handle_mat.value, y16);  }?
Workaround: For MATLAB code that uses this modeling pattern, a workaround is to avoid using handle classes which reference the same object.

Summary: Code generation in parallel build fails with error:?Model is already in a compiled state as a referenced model
Description: If you use parallel building for your Simulink model, Simulink Coder can produce this error: Model is already in a compiled state as a referenced model  This error can occur if a previous parallel build of the model failed with a different error, or was terminated prematurely.
Workaround: Before you start a parallel build, run this command:spmd bdclose('all'); endThe command closes (without saving) open models on the parallel build workers.

Summary: Subcompartments of a model are not visible in the SimBiology diagram
Description: When you open a model in the SimBiology desktop, some subcompartments might not be visible in the diagram. The model contains the subcompartments, but you are not able manipulate the model using the diagram anymore.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Execution order of disp function call and nested loop containing run-time error not correct in generated code
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a MATLAB function that meets these conditions: The function contains a for -loop with a disp ?statement followed by a nested for -loop The data used in the nested for -loop is invariant of the data used in the parent loop. The numeric output of the function does not change if the nested for -loop moves before its parent loop. A run-time error occurs in the nested loop code.  When these conditions are met, the generated code might not preserve the order of the disp ?function call and the nested loop because an optimization in the code generator moves the nested loop out of its parent loop. An example MATLAB function is below: function y = issue_1883778(inData,idx)    y = zeros(100,1);    for offset = 1:10       disp('Entered Loop');       disp(offset);       for k = 1:10           y(k) = inData(idx + k);       end    endend If this function executes with a value of idx  that attempts to access an out-of-bounds value of the? inData  array, ?there is a run-time error in the nested loop. Note that the disp? statements execute before the runtime error.>> issue_1883778(zeros(20,1),21)Entered Loop     1Index exceeds the number of array elements (20).Index exceeds array dimensions.  Index value 22 exceeds valid range [1-20] of array inData.Error in issue_1883778 (line 11)           y(k) = inData(idx + k);Error in issue_1883778 (line 11)           y(k) = inData(idx + k); When you generate a mex function for this function, the run-time error instead happens prior to the execution of the disp  statements>> cfg = coder.config('mex');>> cfg.ResponsivenessChecks = false;>> codegen -config cfg issue_1883778 -args {zeros(20,1),21}>> issue_1883778_mex(zeros(20,1),21)Index exceeds array dimensions.  Index value 22 exceeds valid range [1-20] of array inData.Error in issue_1883778 (line 11)           y(k) = inData(idx + k);

Summary: Error when simulating or generating code from model that contains multiple cross-release blocks
Description: For a model that contains multiple cross-release SIL/PIL blocks, simulation or code generation produces an error when all  of the following conditions apply:?? The model contains two or more cross-release blocks. The cross-release blocks reference code generated using the non-reusable top model code interface or the single-instance model reference code interface. The cross-release blocks all reference code from?different ?build folders. The model has been saved, closed, and then re-opened.  You see this error: Cross-release imported code 'pathToSourceFile ' does not support multiple instances, but multiple instances of the cross-release blocks were found:  firstBlock   secondBlock   ...?
Workaround: For each cross-release SIL/PIL block that is affected, disable the single-instance validation:set_param(block, 'XRelIsReusable', 'on')

Summary: Incorrect Simulation range checking warnings or errors for fixed-point type data
Description: W hen evaluating the Simulation range checking  diagnostic, Stateflow incorrectly compares the quantized integer of a fixed-point type data instead of its real-world value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect reuse of?Function-Call Subsystem?containing State Writer block in generated code
Description: The reusable function generated?from a?Function-Call Subsystem containing a State Writer block might be incorrect if all of the following conditions are true:? A?model contains a Stateflow block or a non-virtual subsystem with a function-call output to a Function-Call Subsystem that contains a State Writer block. The parameter Function packaging  for the?Function-Call Subsystem?is set to Auto?  or using the?command ?set_param , the?parameter?  RTWSystemCode ?is set to 'Auto' . There are at least two identical instances of?the model with the?Function-Call Subsystem and a state owner block. After generating code for the top model, the code generation report shows that a reusable function has been generated for the?Function-Call Subsystem. To verify this problem, open the code generation report. In the left pane under Contents, select Subsystem Report. In the right pane?under the Code Mapping section, check the column Reuse Outcome for the?Function-Call Subsystem. If the reuse outcome is listed as?Reusable Function, your model is subject to this bug.  The code is incorrect because the reusable function only writes to the state of one particular instance of the state owner block. The intended behavior is that the state of the corresponding instance of the state owner block is passed into the reusable function to be accessed by the State Writer block.
Workaround: Set Function packaging to Inline or Nonreusable function for all?Function-Call Subsystems blocks?containing State Writer blocks.

Summary: External mode Connect error when building Simulink project model with custom code generation folder
Description: Suppose you create an XCP-based external mode target application by building a Simulink project model with a custom code generation folder. When you try to connect Simulink to the target application, you might see this error:  External Mode Open Protocol Connect command failed    Additionally,?an error?message dialog box shows this error message:   Executing the 'Connect' command produced the following error:  DWARF Parser internal error: Parser::initialize failed to open file   The error occurs because the internal DWARF parser is incorrectly trying to open the signal and parameter symbols file from the current folder instead of the custom code generation folder.

Summary: Simulink might crash when generating code for some System objects
Description: Simulink might crash?during code generation?for a model containing MATLAB System block when a System object implements the releaseImpl  method, or uses other System objects that implement the releaseImpl  method.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incremental rebuild ignored with normal MATLAB variant controls
Description: Incremental rebuild is ignored when a Variant block uses normal MATLAB variant control variables.  The incorrect behavior may cause numerical mismatch in code generation,f or example:  >> rtwdemo_preprocessor_subsys; >> VSSMODE = 1; >> rtwbuild(bdroot); >> VSSMODE = 0; //Change the Variant control value in the Base Workspace >> rtwbuild(bdroot); // Notice that code corresponding to the new value is not regenerated even though the value of variant control is changed.??  The incorrect behavior happens when all the below conditions are met: The Simulink model uses an ERT? based code generation target Variant block has Analyze all choices and generate pre-processor conditionals  selected Variant control variable is a normal MATLAB variant control and not a?Simulink.Parameter
Workaround: Use Simulink.Parameter instead of normal MATLAB variables. (OR)Remove slprj folder

Summary: An mdce service started in MATLAB R2018b using an mdce_def file copied from an earlier release cannot start workers
Description: The WORKER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY  property was added to the default mdce_def  file in MATLAB R2018b. If you start the mdce service using an mdce_def  file which is missing this new property, then the following error is issued when you start workers: Problem starting the MATLAB worker.?  The cause of the problem is:?============================================================================????timeout creating child process?????????This is causing:?????????Problem starting the worker group process.?????????This is causing:?????????Problem starting the worker group process.?============================================================================
Workaround: To resolve the problem, copy the missing line from the R2018b mdce_def file to the mdce_def file copied from a previous release.For a unix platform, add the following line to your mdce_def.sh file:WORKER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY="1024m"For a windows platform, add the following line to your mdce_def.bat file:?set WORKER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY=1024m?

Summary: Missing CONCRT140.dll error
Description: On Windows, if the Visual Studio 2017 redistributable runtime is not installed, Image Processing Toolbox throws an error when calling any function which depends on CONCRT140.dll (a Visual Studio 2017 C Runtime DLL). For example, >> strel('disk',10, 8);  Error using images.internal.morphmexInvalid MEX-file 'C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\toolbox\images\images\+images\+internal\morphmex.mexw64': Missing dependent shared libraries:'CONCRT140.dll' required by 'C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b/bin/win64\opencv_core340.dll->C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b/bin/win64\libmwmorphop_ocv.dll->C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\toolbox\images\images\+images\+internal\morphmex.mexw64'.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from a model containing Model blocks both with and without Initialize/Reset/Terminate Functions may fail to compile
Description: Code generated from a model may fail to compile due to mismatched signatures when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains one or more Model blocks. The top model or one of the submodels referenced by its Model blocks contains an Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block. At least one of the submodels referenced by a Model block does not contain an Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block. The submodel without?an Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block?uses only constant or triggered sample times.
Workaround: Place an empty Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block in the referenced model that does not have one.

Summary: Altitude and Julian date inputs to the WGS84 Gravity Model block are?limited to between 0?and 20,000?meters (altitude) and?before 2451545 (Jan 1, 2000)?(Julian date)
Description: Altitude and Julian date inputs to the WGS84 Gravity Model  block are?limited to between 0?and 20,000?meters (altitude) and?before 2451545  (Jan 1, 2000)?(Julian date), regardless of the Action for out-of-range input  setting.

Summary: Incorrect error on valid header file specified for imported Simulink Bus in data dictionary
Description: When?a model containing Stateflow and MATLAB Function blocks has a Simulink bus object in model associated data dictionary, Simulink may throw a false error on a Simulink Bus, claiming the imported bus doesn't have a valid 'HeaderFile'  specified. For example:  Custom code settings contain a structure type with the name 'A_BUS'. The bus type 'A_BUS' needs to have its 'HeaderFile' field specified with the header file name.
Workaround: In the?Configuration Parameters > Simulation Target pane, clear the Import custom code parameter.

Summary: Cannot start remote server from Polyspace Metrics and Remote Analysis Server Settings interface
Description: When an mdce service is started with the Start Server  button in the Polyspace Metrics and Remote Analysis Server Settings interface (polyspace-server-settings.exe ), the interface freezes and displays a timeout message:     Script startworker unable to complete successfully - exiting    The mdce service on the host     returned the following error:    Problem starting the MATLAB worker.    The cause of the problem is:    ===================================================       timeout creating child process     This is causing:       Problem starting the worker group process.    This is causing:       Problem starting the worker group process.    ===================================================
Workaround: Edit the file MATLAB_INSTALL_DIR\toolbox\polyspace\distcomp\bin\polyspace_mdce_def.bat (or .sh on Linux/MAC) and add the following line:WORKER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY=1024m

Summary: Assertion failure in rtwcgtlc.hpp:421 during code generation for Stateflow chart
Description: MATLAB might throw an error when generating code for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a Stateflow chart that defines and uses a graphical function. In the State Properties dialog box, the?Function Inline Option ?parameter is set to?Auto  or Function .  The assertion will produce the following error message followed by a stack trace:  Code generation assertion '!varInfo->flags.isObjPtr2 || (RTW_CG_INPORT_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPORT_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_INPORT_NONAUTOSIMPLE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_ROOT_INPORT_SIZE_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_ROOT_OUTPORT_SIZE_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_INPORT_CSC == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPORT_CSC == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_DWORK_HSTRUCT_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_DWORK_CSC == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_DWORK_NONAUTOSIMPLE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_DWORK_LOCAL_SCRATCH == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPUT_CSC == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPUT_NONAUTOSIMPLE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_CONSTANT_OUTPUT_HSTRUCT_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPUT_LOCAL_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_OUTPUT_HSTRUCT_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_CSTATE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_CSTATE_DISABLED_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_DERIVATIVE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_CSTATE_ABSOLUTE_TOLERANCE_CGVAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_NSZC_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_ZCE_VAR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_INPUT_EXPR == varInfo->varType || RTW_CG_AGGREGATE_VAR == varInfo->varType)' failed in 'b:\ matlab\ src\ rtwcg\ export\ include\ rtwcg\ rtwcgtlc.hpp:421'   The file path at the end will change depending on the system, but will include 'matlab\src\rtwcg\export\include\rtwcg\rtwcgtlc.hpp:421'.
Workaround: There is no way to precisely?determine which Stateflow functions cause the assertion failure except through trial and error. A possible work around this issue is to modify the?Function Inline Option?of all Stateflow functions to Inline.?

Summary: GPU memory leak caused by generated CUDA code when MATLAB code contains FFT functions
Description: GPU Coder allocates workspace memory on the GPU for calls to the FFT function in your design if the following conditions are met: coder.GpuConfig.EnableCUFFT  is set to true . Output size of the FFT call is greater than the input size.??  In some cases, the workspace memory might not be cleared after the call to the cuFFT function, which could result in a memory leak. Multiple calls to the function might result in the GPU running out of memory, such as when the FFT functions are called inside a loop with large number of iterations.? For example, see this MATLAB code: cfg = coder.gpuConfig('mex');input = complex(rand(1024, 1024));codegen -config cfg -args {input, coder.Constant(2048), coder.Constant(1)} fft

Summary: Reading certain MP4/MOV files using?VideoReader causes MATLAB? to crash
Description: On Linux systems, using?VideoReader  to read frames from MP4/MOV files can cause MATLAB to crash.  For example, the following code can cause a crash:   vidObj = VideoReader('myVideoFile.mp4');

Summary: Initialize/Reset/Terminate subsystems errors when fixed-in-minor time step signal feeds into IRT ports on model ref blocks
Description: When fixed-in-minor time step signals feed into the initialize/reset/terminate ports on the model reference blocks, unexpected error messages occur, such as Event input port of Model block cannot be driven by signals with continuous sample time or fixed-in-minor-step sample time

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Cast that changes the value of its input might be incorrectly removed if used on a bitwise operator
Description: During normal mode simulation, for MATLAB Function and MATLAB System blocks, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow, Simulink might incorrectly remove the cast to?type  ?for the following MATLAB code pattern: out = cast(bitwiseOperator (in1,in2),'type ')?relationalOperator ?someExpression ;?  out = cast(cast(bitwiseOperator (in1,in2),'type '),'logical');   This issue occurs when all of the following conditions?are true:? ? ? 1.?bitwiseOperator  ?is either?bitand ,?bitor , or?bitxor . ? ? 2.??type  ?is any type that results in the cast changing the value of its input. For example, if?in1 ?and?in2 ?are?int64 ?and?type  ?is?uint32 , then this is a value changing cast. ? ? 3.??relationalOperator  ?is either?==, ~=,?<, <=, >,? or ?>= .? ? ? 4.?someExpression  ?is any expression. For code generation, this issue can affect any combination of blocks that?satisfies the above conditions.  For example, see 'model_example_1876146.slx' within the attached 'issue_example_1876146.zip'.?In simulation,?the MATLAB Function  block?produces an incorrect?output and the Relational Operator  block?produces the correct output. During code generation, both modelling styles produce code that contains the incorrectly removed cast: rtb_Sum = model_example_1876146_U.In1 - 1;rtb_Sum1 = model_example_1876146_U.In2 - 100;model_example_1876146_Y.Out1 = ((rtb_Sum | rtb_Sum1) != 0);model_example_1876146_Y.Out2 = ((rtb_Sum | rtb_Sum1) != 0); The?correct generated code is shown below.?This code contains?an?additional if  statement that implements the saturating behavior of the cast to uint32 , along with the actual cast to uint32 , before the comparison with 0. tmp = (model_example_1876146_U.In1 - 1) | (model_example_1876146_U.In2 - 100);if(tmp < 0) {  tmp = 0;}model_example_1876146_Y.Out1 = ((uint32)tmp != 0);model_example_1876146_Y.Out2 = ((uint32)tmp != 0);

Summary: Parameters and input/output ports in S-function builder may be shuffled when S-function builder block dialog reopens
Description: Upon opening S-function builder blockdialog, the block?tries to find the previously generated <sfcn_name>.c file where <sfcn_name>?is the nameof the S-function as it appears on the block icon. If the file exists, S-functionbuilder reads and parses the C code, then?applies the parsed data into theblock dialog. In R2018b, a bug in the parser causes the order of parameters being shuffled when the block dialog is reopened, making unintended parameter value assignments in the Ports and Parameters Properties table in the S-function builder dialog. Then, if the user builds their S-function, the parameters or input/output ports may take shuffled values and?causing?signals to be routed to wrong ports.
Workaround: Delete the <sfcn_name>.c files on path before opening an S-function builder dialog.Before building or saving an S-function builder, verify that the orders of parameters and ports are assigned as intended, to avoid any previouslyshuffled order being saved to the model.

Summary: Accelerator simulation mode fails for a model with a Simulink Function block that is called only from inside an Initialize Function or a Terminate Function block (but not both)
Description: A model with a Simulink Function block that is called only from inside an Initialize Function or a Terminate Function block (but not both) can throw an error similar to the one below when simulated in accelerator mode.? ? ? Error using accelbuild_private>LocalAccelbuildprivate (line 261)?  ? ? /private/var/folders/zz/zyxvpxvq6csfxvn_n0000jbr0004_y/T/tpdb54ee86_sjha-maci/slprj/accel/mSLFcnCalledOnlyInsideTerminate/mSLFcnCalledOnlyInsideTerminate_acc.c:230:7:?  ? ? error: use of undeclared identifier?  ? ? 'mSLFcnCalledOnlyInsideTerminate_SettheDSMValueFNI'?  ? ? ? ? ? mSLFcnCalledOnlyInsideTerminate_SettheDSMValueFNI;?  ? ? ? ? ? ^?  ? ? 1 error generated. - Show complete stack trace

Summary: Crash when loading or updating AUTOSAR model that uses Basic Software blocks or library-linked Function Callers or Rate Transitions
Description: Loading a model or updating the diagram causes MATLAB to crash if all of these conditions are true: The model is configured as an AUTOSAR component. Function Caller blocks defined in a library (for example, the Basic Software block library) or Rate Transition blocks defined in a library were added to the model. The AUTOSAR mapping was updated to include the added blocks, for example, by clicking the Code Mappings editor?Update ?button. Later, one or more of the added blocks was removed from the model, but was not removed using delete_block . The model was saved without updating the AUTOSAR mapping for the block removals.

Summary: Data logging in buffered mode does not transfer more than one GB of signal data into Simulation Data Inspector
Description: When data logging in buffered mode, only the first one GB of signal data from the real-time application is transferred to the Simulation Data Inspector (SDI) when the application stops.
Workaround: Use the following command to stop the logging service, transfer the signal data to the Simulation Data Inspector, and restart the service (without stopping the real-time application). The goal is to use this command before reaching the one GB data limit. The command to direct the target computer (tg) to stop logging, send data, and restart logging is:>> tg.importLogDataA side effect of using this command is that there is a small gap in the data that occurs between the stop and restart of the data logging service.

Summary: Data logging of signals from target computer displays corrupt or inaccurate data in SDI?immediate mode
Description: A real-time application that streams signal data in immediate mode may display corrupt or inaccurate data in Simulink Data Inspector. The signal data issue occurs most frequently when the application streams many signals at a time and often appears as large data spikes in the data displayed in SDI.
Workaround: Stream the signals in buffered mode instead of immediate mode. For more information, see?Minimize Data Loss with Simulation Data Inspector Buffer Mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model?with a Unit Delay block?and custom code definitions
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:? In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the System target file   is set to ert.tlc . The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem block such as an Enabled, Triggered, Enabled and Triggered, Enable, or Trigger block. The conditionally executed subsystem connects to model-level root output port. Inside the conditionally executed subsystem, a Unit Delay block connects to the output port?of the subsystem that drives model-level root output port. The model uses an Embedded Coder Dictionary in which the step function prototype is customized.
Workaround: Remove the step function prototype specification from the model.

Summary: ginput causes errors when used for axes inside a tab or panel
Description: The ginput  data exploration function does not work when used on Axes? objects ? inside of a Tab  or Panel  object . For example, the following code generates an error:   f = figure;   p = uipanel(f);   a = axes(p);   [x,y] = ginput;  The error also occurs for those functions that depend on ginput , such as gname  from Statistics Toolbox.?

Summary: Assertion?error when generating code for a model containing symbolic dimensions
Description: When generating code for a model containing symbolic dimensions, propagation inconsistencies between port symbolic and numeric dimensions might cause an assertion. The following text is from the assertion: ?fromType.isMatrix() == toType.isMatrix() || (!fromType.isMatrix() && toType.isMatrix() && toType.isDynamic() && toType.getMatrixSize() == 1)?  The propagation inconsistencies generally occur when a symbolic dimension propagates to a port that is configured with a scalar numeric dimension (that is a value of 1).
Workaround: Inserting a Signal Specification block in which you explicitly set with the Dimensions parameter helps resolves the inconsistency. Run the attached MATLAB function to inspect a model and identify cases of symbolic and scalar numeric dimension inconsistencies.

Summary: Error when running model with supercritical fluid properties
Description: Fluids with pseudocritical lines near the maximum valid specific internal energy for the model can produce calculation errors at compile time. The errors cause the fluid density derivatives calculated from the fluid property tables to become NaN. Model compilation fails and simulation does not run. Fluid properties, including the pseudocritical line, are specified on the Two-Phase Fluid Properties (2P) block. The pseudocritical line is an extension of the liquid and vapor saturation lines above the critical pressure. The maximum valid specific internal energy is also specified on the Two-Phase Fluid Properties (2P) block.

Summary: Error indicating a missing makefile during code generation of a model hierarchy that includes a protected model
Description: When generating code for a model reference hierarchy that includes a protected model, you see an error such as the following: Cannot find makefile 'Array_DatatypesRef.mk' in the build directory '.\ slprj\ target\ Array_DatatypesRef' which is needed to make the application ?? The error can occur when all of the following conditions apply: The unprotected source of the protected model was originally configured to use the Template Makefile (TMF) infrastructure for compiling code. The unprotected source of the protected model is configured with the configuration parameters?'GenerateMakefile' ?and 'GenCodeOnly'  set to 'on' . The resulting protected model is used in a top model which is configured with the configuration parameter?'GenCodeOnly'  set to 'off' .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Initialization code might be incorrect for n-D Lookup Table and Interpolation Using Prelookup blocks
Description: The generated code might contain incorrect initialization code for n-D Lookup Table and?Interpolation Using Prelookup blocks when all of these conditions are true: ?The number of table dimensions is greater than or equal to 2. ?The n-D Lookup Table or Interpolation Using Prelookup block operates?at a?constant sample time. ?In the model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Default?parameter?behavior ?parameter is set to?Inlined . ?In the model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Array Layout ?parameter is set to?Row-major . ?A Simulink.Signal  object is assigned to the n-D Lookup Table or Interpolation Using Prelookup block?output.?  This incorrect code might lead to a mismatch between the simulation and generated code results.
Workaround: Remove the?Simulink.Signal object that is assigned to the block output.

Summary: Error during parallel build of model hierarchy
Description: MATLAB throws an error during simulation or code generation when all of the following conditions are true: Configuration parameter Enable parallel model reference rebuilds  is selected for the top model. The model hierarchy has at least three levels, including the top model. Simulation or code generation builds simulation targets for all referenced models in the hierarchy. The slprj folder and MEX files are removed after the build. The original simulation cache folder is moved, deleted, or renamed. The new simulation cache folder is missing a Simulink cache file for a referenced model and contains the Simulink cache file for its parent model. The Simulink cache file of the parent model was created by a parallel build.  The subsequent build aborts with this error:     Error using RTW/transformPaths>locFindmissingPathAndthrowException    The path 'C:\myWorkingFolder\slprj\sim\MidModel' does not exist. - Show complete stack trace        Error using RTW/transformPaths        The path does not exist - Show complete stack trace?    Error while bringing model references of model TopModel up to date:  see errors reported above for details.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Loop unrolling incorrectly applied to while-loop with loop induction variable written using a = foo(a) style function call
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a MATLAB while-loop with the following properties: ? The expression for the?while-loop condition is a simple relational comparison between a variable and a constant?(for example,?k < 5  or k >= 10) . In the following?text, this variable is referred to as the loop induction variable. The body of the loop contains a statement?in which?the loop induction variable is written using an a = foo(a)  style function call. The body of the loop contains a statement in which?the loop induction variable is incremented or decremented by a constant (for example,?k = k + 1  or k = k - 5)   When this issue?occurs, automatic loop unrolling is incorrectly applied to the while-loop. This results in generated code that does not?correctly account for the update to the loop induction variable by the a = foo(a)  style function call.  ?For example, consider?the MATLAB function issue_example_1871917.  function [nloops,kValues] = issue_example_1871917()    nloops = 0;    kValues = zeros(4,1);    k = 1;    while (k <= 4)        nloops = nloops + 1;        kValues(nloops) = k;        k = update(k);        k = k + 1;    endendfunction k = update(k)    coder.inline('never');    if(k < 3)        k = k + 100;    endend The while-loop in this example executes for a single iteration?because of?the large increment applied by the call to update . On the other hand, the code generated for this loop is an unrolled loop with four iterations.void issue_example_1871917(double *nloops, double first[4], double last[4]){  double k;  last[0] = 0.0;  last[1] = 0.0;  last[2] = 0.0;  last[3] = 0.0;  first[0] = 1.0;  update(&k);  first[1] = 2.0;  update(&k);  first[2] = 3.0;  update(&k);  *nloops = 4.0;  first[3] = 4.0;  update(&k);} The correct generated code should look like this. This code contains a for-loop?that checks the value of the loop induction variable after it is written by the call to update. void issue_example_1871917(double *nloops, double kValues[4]){  double k;  *nloops = 0.0;  kValues[0] = 0.0;  kValues[1] = 0.0;  kValues[2] = 0.0;  kValues[3] = 0.0;  for (k = 1.0; k <= 4.0; k++) {    (*nloops)++;    kValues[(int)*nloops - 1] = k;    update(&k);  }} This issue affects the following code generation workflows: Code generation using MATLAB Coder only Code generation from a Simulink model containing MATLAB Function blocks or?MATLAB System blocks Code generation from Stateflow charts containing MATLAB Functions

Summary: Command line error when using a model mask dialog to set values to mask parameters or when using the Property Inspector to edit a signal name
Description: When editing fields of mask parameters on the model mask dialog or when editing the signal name for a line in the Property Inspector, the following command-line error is reported:? ?Caught unexpected exception of unknown type  ?The value entered in the field is not affected, but typing into the field displays the error on the command line.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: preExit method provided by the simevents.SimulationObserver?is not called?when entities depart from the MATLAB Discrete-Event System block
Description: preExit ?method provided by the simevents.SimulationObserver ?object is not invoked if you set the Simulate using  parameter of the MATLAB Discrete-Event System block to  Code generation .

Summary: Assertion error when opening stateflow chart containing function calls with argument expressions having multiple operators
Description: When you open a model with a Stateflow chart that contains function calls, and those function calls have argument expressions with multiple operators, MATLAB might throw the following assertion error:? failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\src\stateflow\utils\object.cpp at line 2318: id < pool_capacity [? 0] 0x00000004274215b1? ? ? bin\win64\stateflow.dll+09901489 SF::sf_register_local_messages+00683537 [? 1] 0x0000000426e193d7? ? ? bin\win64\stateflow.dll+03576791 SF::StateflowJITEngine::setTimeVarAccessed+00019687 [? 2] 0x0000000426e1c595? ? ? bin\win64\stateflow.dll+03589525 SF::StateflowJITEngine::setTimeVarAccessed+00032421   Example expression:? y = min(a*0.1*b, c);?
Workaround: Use temporary variables to eliminate function calls with argument expressions having multiple operators.?For example:?y = min(a*0.1*b, c);??can be re-written as??temp = a*0.1*b;? y = min(temp, c);??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Compiling generated code can throw a macro re-definition warning
Description: Embedded Coder throws a "macro re-defined"  warning when compiling the generated code of a model with all these properties: CustomSymbolStrMacro  set to a non-default value with length equal to MaxIdLength - 7  InlinedPrmAccess  set to Macro   The model may also contain blocks like Data Store Memory and Discrete Integrator.
Fixed In: R2016b Update 7, R2017a Update 4, R2018b Update 1, R2019a

Summary: Bus propagation fails for subsystem that graphically modifies itself during update
Description: Updating a model might produce an error if all of the following conditions apply: The model contains a subsystem that graphically modifies itself during update. A bus propagates across the subsystem.  The error message looks like: Cannot propagate non-bus signal from output port 1 of <model_name/subsystem_name> to input port 1 of <model_name/Out1> because the destination block requires a bus signal.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 4, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Link error during simulation of referenced model that uses inlined S-function with a library dependency
Description: The simulation of a top model that contains a referenced model fails when these conditions apply:? The referenced model runs in accelerator mode. The referenced model has an inlined S-function, that is, the S-function sets SS_OPTION_USE_TLC_WITH_ACCELERATOR . The S-function uses rtwmakecfg  to specify a library dependency. The specified library is not already built and rtwmakecfg  specifies the source files that are required to build the library.  You see a compiler linker error, for example,?Linker error (LNK1181) .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for export-function model with non-Auto?storage class specified on root Outport block and signal line driving root output
Description: Incorrectly generated code is possible for export-function model that has a non-Auto  storage class on both the root Outport block and the signal line driving the root output.
Workaround: Specify storage class on only the root Outport block or the driving signal line.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing two root-level Outport blocks with Ensure outport is virtual parameter on
Description: Incorrect results are possible for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains two root-level Outport blocks, both of which have the parameter Ensure outport is virtual  set to on. One of the Outport blocks connects to a root-level Inport block . The other Outport block connects to the same root-level Inport block but through a block that does not change model semantics (for example, a Gain block of value 1, or a Rate Transition block in a model with only a single rate, or a Data Type Conversion block between two blocks of the same data type).

Summary: Dashboard block replacement does not work for internally saved Simulink Test harnesses.
Description: Dashboard block replacement does not work for test harnesses in Simulink Test that are saved internally, as part of the main model's SLX file.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code for model containing State Writer block writing to?Outport block?of?reusable conditionally executed subsystem
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when it meets the following criteria:The model contains a State Writer block. The State Writer block is configured to write to an Outport block of a conditionally executed subsystem, such as Enabled Subsystem, Function-Call Subsystem, etc. The parameter?Function packaging ?for the?conditionally executed subsystem is set to?Reusable function?  or using the?command ?set_param , the?parameter?? RTWSystemCode ?is set to?'Reusable function' . The conditionally executed subsystem is configured to a?generate reusable function.  Generating code from such model will either result in an error similar to the one shown below or a MATLAB crash: Error: File: C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2018b\ rtw\ c\ tlc\ mw\ opaquelib.tlc Line: 3478 Column: 36?Unable to find CanonicalOutputArgDef within the Interface scope?Main program:?==> [00] C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2018b\ rtw\ c\ tlc\ mw\ opaquelib.tlc:SLibCG_TrackOArg(3478)?????[01] C:\ Work\mBuildError_ert_rtw\ tlc\ s4_b0_ag2co_Outputs.tlc:OutputsForTID(13)?????[02] C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2018b\ rtw\ c\ tlc\ mw\ opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenRateGroupedCodeFromModule(5364)?????[03] C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2018b\ rtw\ c\ tlc\ mw\ opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenerateCodeFromModule(5128)?????[04] C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2018b\ rtw\ c\ tlc\ mw\ opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcn(256)?????[05] C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2018b\ rtw\ c\ tlc\ mw\ opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodyFcnCache(504)?????[06] C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2018b\ rtw\ c\ tlc\ mw\ opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcnEntry(611)?????[07] C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2018b\ rtw\ c\ tlc\ mw\ commonbodlib.tlc:FcnGenBodySysCache(1623)?????[08] C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2018b\ rtw\ c\ tlc\ mw\ commonbodlib.tlc:SLibGenBodyCache(6060)?? ??[09] C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2018b\ rtw\ c\ tlc\ mw\ formatwide.tlc:<NONE>(55)??

Summary: System Design Description report shows incorrect value for ERTMultiwordLength
Description: When you generate the System Design Description report?from a model, the value shown for ERTMultiwordLength  does not match the value shown in the Model Configuration Parameters or returned by get_param .

Summary: Model InitFcn callback evaluated twice in Accelerator mode simulation
Description: Model InitFcn callback of Simulink models might get evaluated twice when all these conditions are true: Model has an inlined S-Function block. SS_OPTION_USE_TLC_WITH_ACCELERATOR option is used in S-Function code. Model is simulated in Accelerator mode.

Summary: asbhl20?example?(NASA HL-20with Optional FlightGear Interface) does not automatically install required FlightGear scenery during simulation
Description: The?asbhl20 ? example?(NASA HL-20 with Optional FlightGear Interface) does not automatically install required FlightGear scenery during simulation because the ?Generate Run Scriptblock  has the?Install FlightGear scenery during Simulation  check box set to off .? If the San Francisco area scenery is not already?downloaded to your FlightGear installation folder,  asbhl20  does not display terrain during FlightGear simulation.
Workaround: Select the Install FlightGear scenery during Simulation?check box?in the?asbhl20/Viewer and Feedback/FlightGear/Generate Run Script block.?

Summary: The 'NR PDSCH Throughput' simulation example does not weight the decoded LLR softbits by the CSI
Description: The 'NR PDSCH Throughput'  simulation?example does not weight the?LLR softbits?rxSymbols , returned  by nrPDSCHDecode , by the soft channel state information?csi , returned by nrEqualizeMMSE . As a result, the?simulation throughput may be less than expected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated AUTOSAR C code uses uninitialized local variables for explicit writes
Description: An AUTOSAR top model generates RTE write function calls that use uninitialized local variables if all of the following conditions are true: The model writes outputs of a referenced model to root outports. The root outports are configured for AUTOSAR data access mode ExplicitSend . Model initialization for the referenced model does not initialize outputs.  Under these conditions, the generated initialization code for the top model contains uninitialized stack variables, which RTE write function calls incorrectly write to root outports.
Workaround: Insert Signal Conversion blocks between the referenced model outputs and the root outports. In each Signal Conversion block, set Output to Signal copy (the default) and select the option Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: Out of memory error caused by a large number of simulation outputs in the test
Description: Generating a report with a large number of test simulation outputs can cause an out of memory error. The error can occur even if simulation outputs are not selected for plotting.

Summary: Repeated, failed target computer connection attempts by development computer crashes MATLAB
Description: MATLAB might crash after repeated, failed target computer connection attempts by the development computer. For example, repeatedly pressing the connect button in Simulink Real-Time Explorer might cause MATLAB to crash if the connection fails.

Summary: Updating a diagram that contains buses and multiple loops might crash MATLAB
Description: Updating the diagram might crash MATLAB when a signal in a virtual bus is in two or more loops. The stack trace contains these messages: WalkThroughConnectionBlock(SLBlock const*, int, int, int, int*, int, SLBlock const**, int*, int*, int*, int*)  SLEngineClasses::SimulinkEngineClassesContext::ctxMinimalWalkThroughConnectionBlock(SLBlock const*, int, int, int, int*, int, SLBlock const**, int*, int*, int*, int*)?  GetSrcRegionForOutputPort(unsigned int, SleCompilerDataMem*, SLBlock*, int, int, int, std::function<bool (SLBlock const*, int)>, SLBlock**, int*, int*, int*, int*, int, int*)?

Summary: Potential incorrect results when custom code global variables are modified outside custom code functions
Description: When analyzing Stateflow charts, Simulink Design Verifier might return incorrect results when all of the following conditions are true: The Stateflow chart uses custom code The custom code reads global variables within custom functions Those global variables are directly written to from the Stateflow chart  For instance, if the custom code is defining a global variable "ccVariable", and a function "use_variable", a Stateflow chart containing the following two transitions may lead to incorrect results: ? { ccVariable = value; }   ? { result = use_variable(); }?   Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly ignores the modifications to the global variables when analyzing the custom code functions.
Workaround: Modify the models and custom code so that the global variables are not written directly in the Stateflow chart, but are written through accessor functions.That is, instead of using:? { variable = value; }define and use an accessor function set_value?:void set_value(double v) {? variable = value;}which is called from Stateflow as follows: ?? {? set_value(value); }

Summary: Changing the interface of a block that contains an externally-saved test harness may cause a crash
Description: If a block has an externally-saved test harness and the test harness file is not on the path, changing the interface of the block may cause a crash.
Workaround: To work around this issue, add the externally-saved test harness file to the MATLAB path.

Summary: Running MATLAB Unit tests in parallel using the XMLPlugin.producingJUnitFormat?plugin omits test results from the final XML file.
Description: If you add the?XMLPlugin.producingJUnitFormat ?plugin to a MATLAB Unit test runner and run the tests in parallel, the resulting XML file might not contain all test results. However, all tests are run and evaluated and all results are contained in the "result" output variable.

Summary: Mapping Simulink port with bus object data type to AUTOSAR port might crash MATLAB?
Description: Using the Code Mappings editor to configure an AUTOSAR model might crash MATLAB when all of these conditions apply: You attempt to map a Simulink port to an AUTOSAR port. The Simulink port specifies a bus object as its data type.

Summary: Configuration Parameters dialog displays error message: Undefined variable "coder"
Description: The Configuration Parameters dialog box fails to load and displays the following error message: An unexpected error occurred while creating the Configuration Parameters Dialog. This might be caused by functions in your MATLAB path that shadow built-in MATLAB functions.    Undefined variable "coder" or class "coder.dictionary.internal.StepFunctionMapping.isStepFcnPrototypePresent".  file: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019a\toolbox\shared\configset\+configset\+internal\+custom\modelRefRootInputs_status.p  name: modelRefRootInputs_status  line: 0     This occurs when the model is saved with a code mapping environment and you do not have Embedded Coder installed.?

Summary: DOM container objects ignore programmatic specification of font size for a PDF report?
Description: For DOM API/Report API reports, a DOM container object, such as a Table, List, or Container object, always uses the default 11-point font for a PDF report. The container object ignores the font size that you specify by using the API.? For example, even though the following code specifies a table that has a 20-point font, in the resulting PDF report, the table has an 11-point font.? import mlreportgen.dom.* import mlreportgen.report.* d = Report('myreport','pdf'); mTable = Table({'test'}); mTable.Style = {FontSize('20pt')}; add(d,mTable); close(d);?? rptview(d.OutputPath);
Workaround: Create a custom Report API/ DOM API PDF template.?Unzip the template.?In the root.css file, remove the default font size for a paragraph.?Zip the template.Use the updated custom template when creating a DOM Document object or Report API Report object.For information about creating and updating a custom template, see Create an HTML or PDF Template and Modify Styles in PDF Templates.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code does not contain initialization code for Root Outport block that is driven by reusable subsystem in an export-function model
Description: This bug might occur for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model is an export-function model. A function-call root Inport block is connected to a Function-Call Split block that is driving two identical Function-Call Subsystem blocks. One instance of the Function-Call Subsystem connects to a root Outport block and the other is not connected. The instance that does not connect to a root Outport block executes first in the Function-Call Split block order. The parameter?Function packaging ?for each of the Function-Call Subsystem?is set to?Auto?  or  Reusable function .  After generating code, the code generation report shows that a reusable function has been generated for the above Function-Call Subsystems. To verify this problem, open the code generation report. In the left pane under Contents, select Subsystem Report. In the right pane?under the Code Mapping section, check the column Reuse Outcome for the?Function-Call Subsystems.?  The generated code is incorrect because it does not contain the initialization code for the root Outport block that connects to one of the instances of the reusable Function-Call Subsystem.

Summary: Error in model with custom code when there is no supported compiler installed
Description: An error is reported?when Simulink model has custom code specified and there is no supported compiler installed on the platform. When you update diagram in the model,?the MATLAB command window displays this error:  No mex compiler configured for the 'cxx' language. To specify a compiler, run the command "mex -setup".
Workaround: On the Configuration Parameters > Simulation Target pane, clear the Import custom code parameter.

Summary: String data type on input or output ports combined with other data types on other ports of the FMU Import Block may cause Simulink to crash
Description: String data type at an input or output port of an FMU Import block might cause Simulink to crash if there are other input or output ports with other data types. This happens when the imported FMU has multiple input or output signals, and the signals on either the input or output side include other data types in addition to string data types. ?The crash log may indicate the following:? [? 0] 0x000000000ef1ed8f bin\win64\sl_types.dll+00322959 SLSymbolicDimsErrorChecker::reportNonScalarValue+00006271  [? 1] 0x000000000ef1fe02 bin\win64\sl_types.dll+00327170 SLTypeSys::StringDataType::getUStringScalarFromVoidPtrInput+00000194  [? 2] 0x0000000010d09a93 bin\win64\SimulinkBlock.dll+00694931 slSimBlock_tag::getUStringScalarInput+00000035  [? 3] 0x000000047fd94fd1 bin\win64\fmubase.dll+00872401 FMUBase::validateParameterDataType+00630225

Summary: openExample returns an error?when userpath?folder contains extended ASCII characters
Description: If the userpath ?folder contains extended ASCII characters such as ¨ą , ? , ? , etc.,?openExample  returns this error: Error using exampleUtils.getExamplesDir (line 13)Unable to create a working directory at the location returned by the userpath function.Error in exampleUtils.getWorkDir (line 2)    ed = exampleUtils.getExamplesDir();Error in setupExample (line 11)    workDir = exampleUtils.getWorkDir(metadata);Error in openExample (line 29)    workDir = setupExample(id, workDir);
Workaround: To work around this issue, specify the 'workDir' option with openExample to open the example in a different working folder.For example, this statement opens the example?'matlab/ConvertNumbersToStringArraysExample'?in the folder?'C:\Temp': openExample('matlab/ConvertNumbersToStringArraysExample','workDir','C:\Temp');

Summary: Polyspace Bug Finder automatically using the unqualified -to verification level option
Description: Verification level option -to  is set automatically when launching an analysis in Bug Finder, and is included in the options list in generated reports. Although this option appears in the report, it is not qualified.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Row-major code not generated in HDL Coder
Description: When configuration parameter Array layout  is set to 'Row-major' , column-major HDL code is generated. This results in invalid numerical outputs for HDL code that is integrated with row-major code.
Workaround: Set configuration parameter Array layout to 'Column-major'.

Summary: ODBC interface does not correctly support TEXT data-type in Microsoft SQL Server database
Description: If you are working with a Microsoft SQL Server database and have a column in your database table with TEXT  type, then you might see one of these behaviors when importing data into MATLAB from the database using an ODBC database connection: The size of column data, defined as TEXT  type, is truncated to 8000 characters. This error message appears: Out of memory. Type "help memory" for your options. ?

Summary: Incorrect licence checkout when you run the Lane Keeping Assist with Lane Detection example?
Description: When you run the Lane Keeping Assist with Lane Detection  example, the Automated Driving System Toolbox??will incorrectly checkout an available Aerospace Blockset??license.

Summary: Incorrect simulation mode in results for iterations for Block Diagram and Model Reference harnesses
Description: When you run iteration tests on?Block Diagram and Model Reference harnesses, the simulation mode shown in the results for the second, third, etc. iterations is the simulation mode of the harness rather than the component under test.

Summary: Using readMobileSensorData always prompts for Support Package installation, even when not necessary
Description: The readMobileSensorData  function comes as part of the MATLAB Support Package for Android Sensors or the MATLAB Support Package for Apple iOS Sensors. The function converts a .zip file with mobile device sensor data to a structure of MATLAB tables. Using this function always invokes a "stub" function, which prompts you to install the Support Package even when already installed.
Workaround: To work around this issue, add the sharedmobilesensor folder to your MATLAB path with the following command:  addpath('<SupportPackageRoot>/toolbox/matlab/hardware/supportpackages/sharedmobilesensor')where <SupportPackageRoot> is the folder for your support package installations.

Summary: Anonymous function can leak memory
Description: MATLAB might have a memory leak if you repeatedly create and destroy a function handle for an anonymous function.? For example, if a function defines a function handle as a local variable and this function is called repeatedly?in a?loop, the cumulative effect of the memory leak becomes apparent. Using the clear all  command does not reclaim the memory.
Workaround: Convert the anonymous?functions to local functions.?

Summary: Solver Profiler does not show all solver reset sources when model contains blocks with multi-dimensional zero crossing signals
Description: If a model contains multidimensional zero crossing signals and these signals trigger solver resets, Solver Profiler does not count resets from some of the reset sources. This causes an incorrect number of solver resets to show up in Solver Profiler.

Summary: Help links to documentation?for blocks under Simscape/Electrical/Electronics & Mechatronics libraries not working
Description: Blocks in the Simscape  -> Electrical  -> Electronics & Mechatronics  libraries do not have access to block documentation via the Help  button in the block dialog or via the Help  option in the right-click context menu.
Workaround: Block documentation for all these blocks is still available. Open the Documentation Center and navigate to Physical Modeling -> Simscape Electrical -> Electronics & Mechatronics. Then, click the Blocks button at the top of the?page to see a listing of links to all the block reference pages in the Electronics & Mechatronics libraries.

Summary: Clicking Send to send a crash report to MathWorks does nothing
Description: If you installed MATLAB with the option to "Improve MATLAB by sending user experience information to MathWorks" turned off, and a crash occurs while using MATLAB, clicking Send  to send a crash report to MathWorks does nothing.
Workaround: To send the crash report to MathWorks for further guidance, locate the crash log file in your home folder, and attach it to a new service request you can create directly at https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model block with constant output driving a conditionally executed subsystem outport block produces incorrect code in simplified initialization mode
Description: Incorrect code is generated for a model when all of the following conditions are true:? The configuration parameter Underspecified initialization detection  is set to 'Simplified'.  A Model block is in a conditionally-executed subsystem such as Enabled Subsystem, Function-Call Subsystem, etc. An output port of the Model block is driving an outport block of the conditionally executed subsystem. In the submodel referenced by the above Model block, the following conditions applies: A Constant block is directly driving the root outport corresponding to the output port of the Model block in the top model.? In the Constant block parameters dialog box, the Sample time  parameter is set to 'Inf' . The submodel contains blocks with sample time other than 'Inf '.   The output of the Model block is constant.The intended behavior is that the generated code initializes the memory for this output using an initial value, writes to the output once with the Constant block value, and then leaves the memory untouched for the remaining of the execution. However, when all of the above conditions are true, Simulink Coder incorrectly writes the initial value over the Constant block value.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Model block and the Outport block of the conditionally executed subsystem to make a signal copy.

Summary: S-functions with non-numeric type dialog parameters are not supported
Description: Simulink Design Verifier does not support S-functions that have non-numeric type dialog parameters such as string. The software does not provide any warning or error for this type of S-functions. The S-function behaves like it is not compiled with the '-sldv ' option of slcovmex , or with supportCoverageAndDesignVerifier  option for the Legacy Code Tool.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect result for Stateflow Chart with consecutive statements that write to the same local variable
Description: A Stateflow chart might produce incorrect results when it contains the following modeling pattern: A statement writing to a Stateflow local variable A second statement writing to the same local variable The right-hand-side of the second statement calls a function that reads the same local variable  The first write statement is incorrectly skipped in both simulation and code generation of Simulink models containing Stateflow charts.

Summary: Link error when generating code for deep learning networks with MKL-DNN
Description: Generating C++ code for prediction from a deep learning network, results in a compiler linker error (LNK1181). This failure happens because of the missing library file mwmklnnet.lib . This file is not part of the standard Intel MKL-DNN library package but is used during the code generation process.?  One such build error looks similar to the following:  LINK : fatal error LNK1181: cannot open input file 'C:\matlab\coredemo\Common\Applications\GPU_Implementation\GPUCoder\MKL-DNN\mkl-dnn\lib\mwmklnnet.lib'?  gmake: *** [resnet_predict_mex.mexw64] Error 1181?    ------------------------------------------------------------------------?  ??? Build error: C++ compiler produced errors. See the Build Log for further details.?    Code generation failed: View Error Report?  Error using codegen?

Summary: MATLAB may crash during code generation when deleting blocks in the 'before_tlc' stage of the STF_make_rtw_hook
Description: If the selected target file has an associated STF_make_rtw_hook.m  file which deletes a block from the model in the 'before_tlc ' hook stage, MATLAB may crash during code generation.

Summary: MATLAB? might crash when creating and deleting axes in a loop on Linux?
Description: MATLAB might crash when working with graphics on Linux systems that have an NVIDIA?  driver between versions 367.18 and 396.24. These crashes most frequently occur when you create and delete axes in a loop.
Workaround: Start MATLAB with the -softwareopengl startup option:matlab -softwareopengl

Summary: Iterations cannot be executed in a real-time test case when using input overrides and stop simulation at last point?option
Description: Running a real-time test case with iterations using input overrides and the stop simulation at last point ?option fails and the following error is returned: Undefined function or variable 'tMaxString'?

Summary: groundTruth objects with pixel labels saved in R2018a throw warnings while loading in R2018b, and do not include any pixel label data loaded
Description: When attempting to load a groundTruth  object saved in R2018a in R2018b, this warning is thrown: Warning: While loading an object of class 'groundTruth':?Invalid entry in label data table for PixelLabel labelType. Table entries for column 'PixelLabelData' must be an image file name specified as a character vector.?   All the pixel label data entries in the LabelData  table in the groundTruth  object is empty.?

Summary: Error when generating model reference code from model that contains cross-release SIL/PIL block
Description: Generating code from a model that contains a cross-release SIL/PIL block produces an error when all  of these conditions apply: An input port of the cross-release block is implemented in the imported code by a global variable. The input port of the cross-release block is directly connected to a root input port in the model. The model reference Simulink Coder target is built for the model.  You see this error: Unable to find GrSrc within the ExternalInput scope
Workaround: Insert a block between the root input port of the model and the input port of the cross-release SIL/PIL block. For example, use the Signal Conversion block with the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter selected.

Summary: MATLAB might crash with signal label propagation enabled
Description: MATLAB crashes when signal label propagation is enabled?and either of these conditions apply: The model has disconnected lines. You delete an Inport or Outport block in a subsystem.

Summary: Error while drawing lines in an App Designer app, when running it either in MATLAB Online or in a Web browser (Web Apps)
Description: While running a Web App or an App Designer app in MATLAB Online on a high DPI monitor, an error is intermittently thrown when drawing or working with lines: Warning: An error occurred while drawing the scene: Error inweb draw?traversal: TypeError: Cannot read property 'length' ofundefined?
Workaround: Use a non-high DPI monitor or a monitor without any scaling while running the app.

Summary: Fonts differ in reports generated by compiled and uncompiled report programs
Description: Reports generated by MATLAB use Noto fonts, whereas reports generated by a compiled MATLAB program use Helvetica fonts.
Workaround: 1. Go to the MATLAB Runtime installation folder.? On Windows this folder is usually c:\Program Files\MATLAB Runtime\v95.2. Go to the Report Generator resources folder:??matlab\toolbox\shared\mlreportgen\dom\resources.?3. In the resources folder, copy the fonts folder to the?matlab\mcr\toolbox\shared\mlreportgen\dom\resources?folder.?

Summary: Polyspace Bug Finder is missing test cases to qualify gnu4.7 compiler
Description: Polyspace Bug Finder test cases do not allow you to qualify the gnu4.7 compiler.

Summary: Incorrect list of unsupported blocks for row-major code generation

Summary: Incorrect error message on templates: error: expected a ">"
Description: With C++ applications, when template definitions contain the characters >> , such as the following: ?template <typename f> struct j { bool k = h<i, a::e<f>> ; };  Polyspace incorrectly generates the following error:? ?error: expected a ">"  Other errors can follow.

Summary: Using wlanMACFrame generates the error: bitsll requires Fixed-point Designer
Description: When using wlanMACFrame , the following error might occur:   bitsll requires Fixed-Point Designer.
