Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening the Bird's Eye Scope for the Simulink Test example model LaneFollowingTestBenchExample
Description: Opening the Bird's Eye Scope from the example model LaneFollowingTestBenchExample , following a simulation, might cause MATLAB to crash. The model is part of the Simulink Test example titled 'Testing a Lane-Following Controller with Simulink Test '.

Summary: When a coverage filter is specified, function and functional call coverage reported in Simulink Test may not reflect that the coverage filter was used
Description: If you specify a coverage filter for coverage collection, and simulate a model with Simulink Test, function and function call coverage results in the Test Manager may not reflect the use of a coverage filter.

Summary: Compilation phase halts with the error: Internal error: read_memory_region: not all expected entries were read
Description: With Polyspace Code Prover in the context of a C++ application, Polyspace can halt during the compilation phase with the following error message:?   IL entry write-read difference: region number   1      entry kind = 13 (expr-node), written = 595, read = 594         missing entry = 1   Internal error: read_memory_region: not all expected entries were read  (values can be different).?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code can provide a wrong answer when specifying a numeric literal that uses the 'D' or 'd' character with a base 10 exponent
Description: In MATLAB, you can write floating point literals in scientific notation by suffixing the literal with an 'E', 'e', 'D' or 'd' character followed by an exponent. For example, the following numbers all evaluate to 314: 3.14e2, 3.14E2, 3.14d2, 3.14D2.?This syntax worked for code generation on Windows in versions R2017a and earlier. However, the exponent is ignored when using the 'D' or 'd' character in all releases on non-Windows platforms, and on Windows for releases R2017b, R2018a, and R2018b.?
Workaround: Replace all occurrences of 'D' and 'd' in numeric literals with 'E' or 'e'.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table (n-D) blocks incorrectly share same lookupnd function?for some heterogeneous fixed-point inputs and floating-point breakpoints
Description: Lookup Table (n-D) blocks might incorrectly share the?same lookupnd  function?for some heterogeneous fixed-point inputs and floating-point breakpoints?when the following conditions are true for two or more Lookup Table?(n-D) blocks in a model: The breakpoints are double or single floating-point data types. The inputs are fixed-point data types with same?container word length, different?fixed exponent.   The incorrectly shared lookupnd  function might lead to wrong results?from the generated code in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes, and in all production targets.
Workaround: Cast the fixed-point input?of Lookup n-D blocks to have the same floating-point data type as breakpoint, i.e., insert a data type conversion block before the?input?of Lookup n-D block that generates wrong result.

Summary: When simulating in model reference accelerator mode, Data Store Memory blocks with buses that contain string elements can crash MATLAB
Description: When simulating in model reference accelerator mode, models with Data Store Memory blocks containing buses that contain string elements can crash MATLAB if the following is true: The model contains a Model block referencing a submodel that contains a Data Store Memory block. The Data Store Memory block contains a bus with a string element. Either the top model or the submodel runs in accelerator mode.

Summary: Incorrect MCDC results for logic block cascade with differing sample times
Description: When the root block of a cascade of Logical Operator blocks has a sample time that is faster than one or more of the upstream logic blocks in the cascade, then the MCDC results reported for the cascade might be incorrect.? For example, consider a cascade of Logical Operator blocks where an upstream block in the cascade inherits a constant sample time , but the most downstream block in the cascade is set to run with a specific?discrete sample time .? The upstream block with constant sample time might only execute once, and therefore effectively would have a slower sample time than the downstream block.? As such, Coverage analysis might incorrectly report that some MCDC objective outcomes associated with the cascade are satisfied even if they were not actually executed during simulation.
Workaround: This issue can be avoided by ensuring that all blocks in a Logical Operator cascade are explicitly set to have the same sample time.
Products: Simulink Design Verifier, Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage, Requirements Toolbox

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer in simulation and code generation for Product block?with fixed-point output data types
Description: The Product block produces wrong answers when the output data type is a fixed point type with a slope that is very close to a power of two,?for example,?fixdt(0, 16, 1/128.0005, 0 ), and one of the following is true for the Product block: The block has more than two inputs The Number of inputs  parameter? is /* .

Summary: MATLAB?might crash during accelerator mode simulation when output of referenced model or subsystem contains strings
Description: MATLAB might crash when the following conditions are satisfied: A model simulates in accelerator mode. The model contains referenced model or subsystem that generates a function of its own during accelerator mode simulation. The referenced model or subsystem has an output that is a string signal or a bus that contain strings. The output does not directly connect to a?root output port.?

Summary: On Windows, audioDeviceReader errors when recording from certain sound cards using WASAPI driver
Description: On Windows, using the audioDeviceReader  to record audio from certain sound cards using the WASAPI driver results in an error. The code below attempts to record sound from a device whose default format is 2 channel, 24 bits per sample and 48000 sample rate: ar = audioDeviceReader;ar.Driver = 'WASAPI';ar.Device = 'Microphone (STM32 AUDIO Streaming in HS Mode)';ar.SampleRate = 48000;ar.NumChannels = 2;ar.BitDepth = '24-bit integer';acquiredAudio = ar();  The following error is thrown: Error using audioDeviceReader/setupPortAudio Error: Invalid sample rateError in audioDeviceReader/setupImpl (line 350)? ? ? ? ? ? setup(obj.pInterface);?  The same issue occurs in MATLAB when using audioDeviceWriter? to play back audio using certain sound cards using WASAPI. The same issue occurs in Simulink when using the Audio Device Writer or Audio Device Reader blocks for certain sounds cards using WASAPI.

IA6->f1 = tmp;??????? }??? }}    ?2. In? the second case in an if -statement that contains a for -loop as the body: function [IA6,IA4] = example(IA6, IA4)??? coder.inline( 'never' ); ??? if (IA6(1).f2(1, 1))==(IA6(1).f1(1, 1)) ??????? for i = 5:8??????????? IA6(1).f1 = IA4(i - 1, 1);??????? end??? endend  In the following incorrect generated code, the if -statement and the for -loop are swapped. static void example(IA6_busObject *IA6, const uint8_T IA4[10]) { ??? int32_T P9; ??? int32_T P10; ??? uint8_T tmp; ??? for (i = 0; i < 4; P9++) { ??????? if (IA6->f2 == IA6->f1) { ??????????? tmp = IA4[i + 3]; ??????????? IA6->f1 = tmp; ??????? } ??? } }

Summary: Simple cases of dead logic in C code are not reported
Description: If your model consists of custom code or generated code with trivial cases of dead logic in C code, Simulink Design Verifier report may not include these cases of dead logic. For example, the software ignores the trivially unreachable conditions such as if(0) . For test generation analysis, no test objective associated with the dead logic in C code is generated.

Summary: Performance regression when updating diagram that contains multiple normal-mode referenced model instances
Description: Updating the diagram of a model that contains multiple instances of a referenced model in normal mode might cause a performance regression when this referenced model contains a large model hierarchy. Updating the diagram might result in an out-of-memory error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when using element-wise multiplication (.*) that mixes double and int64 or uint64 inputs
Description: The generated code can produce a wrong answer if your MATLAB code contains an element-wise multiplication?(.*) ?that mixes?double ?and?int64 ?or?uint64 ?inputs. This issue occurs?when all of the following conditions are true:? One input is?double ?and the other is?either?int64 ?or?uint64.  The?double ?input has absolute value greater than or equal to 2^52.  The? int64 ?or?uint64  input has absolute value less than  2^12.   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow. See also Bug Report ID 1794275 .

Summary: JAR files belonging to installed add-ons are not added to the Java class path in subsequent logins
Description: If an add-on containing a JAR is enabled when you log out of a MATLAB Online session, the expected behavior is for the JAR file to be added to Java class path when you log back in the next time. However the JAR is not added to the Java class path in the new session.?
Workaround: When you log back into a new MATLAB Online session, run the following command to explicitly enable the add-on in the new session. This registers the JAR file with the Java class path.matlab.addons.enableAddon

Summary: MATLAB crashes and memory leaks occur when entity data size is larger than 2 MB
Description: When entity data size is larger than 2 MB: In R2018b, runninga model with SimEvents blocks may crash MATLAB. In R2018a, running amodel with a MATLAB Discrete-Event System block may cause memory leaks and MATLABcrashes when the memory is full after a long simulation.

Summary: graph and digraph plots in an app do not display node or edge labels
Description: If a graph or digraph  is plotted inside an App Designer application or a UIFigure , itsnode and edge labels are not displayed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MISRA-compliant Boolean to integer conversions may cause incorrect code
Description: In rare cases, incorrect code might occur for a Simulink model?or MATLAB code with the CastingMode  parameter or property set to Standards.  The problem occurs in MATLAB expressions or Simulink models that mix unsigned and Boolean data in subtraction. The incorrect code occurs?due to a?MISRA compliant Boolean-to-integer type conversion.? The incorrect code uses unsigned literals in the conversion causing the C promotion rules to performthe larger expression as C 'unsigned int' instead of the desired 'signed int'. Here is an example of the incorrect code:? uint16_T u16_a, u16_b;  bool_T b;u16_a <? (b ? 1U : 0U) - u16_b;?  Assume a 32-bit target with a built-in 16-bit type. The above expression might result in a negative value in a run-time evaluation of the right hand side of the comparison. However, the value will be interpreted aspositive in the comparison due to the promotion of unsigned int  from the conditional expressions.? Note that the variables of type uint16_T  promote to signed int  and literals promote to signed int  in absence of a suffix.  One example of correct code is as follows:? u16_a <? (b ? 1 : 0) - u16_b;  The above code is not MISRA compliant because the operands of the subtraction are signed and unsigned respectively.? The code below is both correct and MISRA compliant: u16_a <? (b ? (uint16_T)1 : (uint16_T)0) - u16_b;
Workaround: Avoid combining these operands in Simulink or MATLAB subtraction operations:1) An unsigned? signal or variable that fits into a built-in type smaller than the target word size.? For example, a Simulink signal of size uint16 type will typically fit in a C short on common targets.?? 2) A Boolean signal or variable or logical expression.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling a custom code function with coder.eval() in Simulink generates incorrect result in code generation
Description: coder.ceval()  in Simulink may give incorrect result in code generation. This?might occur if the following conditions are true: The custom code function called with coder.ceval()  has function array layout settings set to Row-major via the Default function array layout  configuration parameter or the Specify by function  dialog box. Configuration parameter Import custom code  is on.

Summary: Simulation stop button does not work from .NET GUIs
Description: If the MATLAB process loads a .NET assembly that also defined a GUI, and the GUI was used to control an active simulation, the button on the GUI to stop simulation would fail to take effect immediately. Simulation stops much later, based on how the callback for the button to stop simulation was written.

Summary: Help browser fails to display results when searching the locally installed Chinese documentation
Description: When searching the locally installed Chinese documentation, the Help browser fails to display any results.?
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can search the Chinese documentation on the web at https://ww2.mathworks.cn/help/, or search the locally installed English documentation in the Help browser.?

Summary: Sensitivity Analysis model evaluation errors when trying to vary parameters in base MATLAB workspace
Description: When using the Sensitivity Analysis tool, evaluation of the Simulink model errors when trying to vary parameters in the base MATLAB workspace.? The following error is displayed at the command prompt: Warning: Evaluation of function '@(simin)lModelFcn(fcn,sampSetParameters,simin)' failed  with the following errors. Using empty SimulationOutput object as return value.?  Warning: Invalid "PV" argument for the "setPVec" command.?   A similar error may also occur when using sensitivity analysis in the command line, when using the sdo.evaluate command to evaluate the model.? The error occurs if: the sdo.evaluate command is passed the optional options argument, which is an sdo.EvaluateOptions object, and the EvaluatedModel field of the options is set to the sdo.SimulationTest object  The error is:Warning: Evaluation of function '@(s)sdo.internal.simulationManagerExecuteFcn(s,fcn,p)'  failed with the following errors. Using empty SimulationOutput object as return value.?  Warning: Invalid "PV" argument for the "setPVec" command.  Unable to perform assignment because the size of the left side is 1-by-1 and the size  of the right side is 0-by-0.   Error in sdo.evaluate (line 204)  ? ? ? ? ? ? dData(ct,:) = data;
Workaround: If using Sensitivity Analysis tool, store the parameters in the model workspace or a data dictionary instead of the base MATLAB workspace.? If using sensitivity analysis in the command line, use the same workaround just mentioned for Sensitivity Analysis tool.? Or, make sure the options (the sdo.EvaluateOptions object) has its EvaluatedModel field set to the model name, rather than the sdo.SimulationTest object.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when connecting to an NI-VISA serial resource on macOS.
Description: Connecting to an NI-VISA serial resource on macOS using the visa  interface might result in a MATLAB crash. This happens when working with both the Test & Measurement Tool (tmtool ) and command-line workflows.

Summary: On?non-English platform launching?the Excel Wizard may cause Test Manager to hang
Description: On?non-English platform launching?the Excel Wizard may cause Test Manager to hang.?

Summary: Unresponsive uifigure window?when Airspeed Indicator or EGT Indicator?Limits property not increasing, or Climb Indicator MaximumRate property set to 0
Description: The?uifigure ?window becomes unresponsive when?one of the following is true: Proper ty?Limits  of AirspeedIndicator  (uiaeroairspeed ) or EGTIndicator  (uiaeroegt )?is not a 1-by-2 array of real, finite, increasing?numbers  Property?MaximumRate? of ClimbIndicator  (uiaeroclimb ) set to?0 ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using lookup table or breakpoint variables with non-auto storage within protected models may cause incorrect results for both simulation and code generation
Description: When you protect a model that contains a Simulink.LookupTable  or Simulink.Breakpoint with non-auto storage class, the value read from any of the global tunable variables in the model may be incorrect. This will lead to incorrect results for both simulation and code generation of the protected model.?

Summary: matlab::engine::setVariable crashes when transferring a large variable
Description: When transferring a variable larger than approximately 2 GB in size, matlab::engine::setVaraible  crashes.

Summary: Feeding data of type Simulink.AliasType into Compose String or Substring block can crash MATLAB
Description: If numeric inputs into the Compose?String or Substring block?have data type?Simulink.AliasType , simulating the model?can cause?MATLAB to?crash.

Summary: Generated code for reusable subsystems might contain MISRA C:2012 Rule 10.4 violation
Description: The generated code might contain a MISRA C:2012 Rule 10.4 violation for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a subsystem with the Function packaging  parameter set to Reusable function  or Auto  and a reuse opportunity is available. The subsystem has a boolean output signal.  The?Casting Modes ?model configuration parameter setting is Standards Compliant.   ?The code? might contain an unnecessary comparison operation that violates MISRA C:2012 Rule 10.4??as shown in this example: ???????? ExtU.Y? = ReusedSystem_Outputs(u) != false  ?where  ExtU.Y  has  type bool_T  and  ReusedSystem_Outputs  returns the same type .   The violation occurs because the function call returns bool_T  and the definition of false is 0U .? Boolean and unsigned int  belong to different type categories.

Summary: Error when exporting results?containing more than 998 verify statements from Test Manager
Description: If a results set contains more than 998 verify ? statement results, exporting results from the Test Manager can fail.

Summary: Serial I/O blocks can cause the target computer to crash sporadically.
Description: Real-time applications that contain any of the serial I/O blocks can cause the target computer to crash sporadically. To determine whether this issue occurred, inspect the crash info to check whether the crash occurred in fiforead.c . If so, apply the workaround.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.   Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.  Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change Log:? ?? attachment_1845756_17a_2018_08_28.zip: initial version

Summary: Adding or removing an NI DAQ device channel might result in an error
Description: When using National Instruments hardware, adding or removing channels from a data acquisition session might result in one of the following errors: Failed to load device plug-in: 'mwnidaqmxai'. Check that the DAQ Toolbox and NIDAQmx drivers are installed properly.  Failed to load device plug-in: 'mwnidaqmxdi'. Check that the DAQ Toolbox and NIDAQmx drivers are installed properly.  Failed to load device plug-in: 'mwnidaqmxci'. Check that the DAQ Toolbox and NIDAQmx drivers are installed properly.
Workaround: The error occurs if a significant amount of RAM is in use when calling addAnalogInputChannel, addDigitalChannel, addCounterInputChannel, or removeChannel.As a workaround, make sure as little memory as possible is in use before calling any of these functions. For example, you might want to create any variables that use a significant amount of memory after all data acquisition session channels are already configured.To check the amount of available and used memory, use the MATLAB memory function:>> memoryMaximum possible array:       63346 MB (6.642e+10 bytes) *Memory available for all arrays:       63346 MB (6.642e+10 bytes) *Memory used by MATLAB:        2038 MB (2.137e+09 bytes)Physical Memory (RAM):       65427 MB (6.861e+10 bytes)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Ordering of loop and function call using matrix incorrectly switched in generated code
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a MATLAB function that meets all of the following conditions: The function contains two for -loops that execute the same number of iterations. There is a function call between the for -loops. The same matrix is read in either for -loop and is passed as an input to the function call. The function definition corresponding to the call writes to the input argument that passes the matrix.  In the incorrect generated code, the ordering of the access to the matrix in either of the loops and the function call are interchanged. In addition, the function call writes to the same matrix that the loop accesses. Here is an example: function [y1,y2,y3] = issue_1844671(inData)tempData = computeTemp(inData);y1 = zeros(size(tempData));y3 = zeros(200,1);for k = 1:numel(y1)    y1(k) = k .* tempData(k);endy2 = writeThroughInput(tempData);for k = 1:numel(y1)    y3(k) = y3(k) + y1(k);endendfunction out = computeTemp(in)    coder.inline('never');    out = in + 2.*in;endfunction out = writeThroughInput(in)    coder.inline('never');    in = in + 10;    out = zeros(size(in));    for k = 2:numel(out)        out(k) = in(k-1) + in(k);    endend The generated code for this example combines the two loops adjacent to the call to writeThroughInput  into a single-loop and moves the call to writeThroughInput  before the combined loops. This is incorrect because writeThroughInput  modifies the value of tempData  in the generated code before the loop accesses it.void issue_1844671(const double inData[100], double b_y1[100], double y2[100],               double y3[200]){  double tempData[100];  int k;  double d0;  computeTemp(inData, tempData);  memset(&y3[0], 0, 200U * sizeof(double));  writeThroughInput(tempData, y2);  for (k = 0; k < 100; k++) {    d0 = (1.0 + (double)k) * tempData[k];    b_y1[k] = d0;    y3[k] += d0;  }}? Below is the correct generated code. Note that the ordering of the read of tempData  by the loop and call to writeThroughInput  is correct. The function call occurs after the combined loops.?void issue_1844671(const double inData[100], double b_y1[100], double y2[100],               double y3[200]){  double tempData[100];  int k;  double d;  computeTemp(inData, tempData);  memset(&y3[0], 0, 200U * sizeof(double));  for (k = 0; k < 100; k++) {    d = (1.0 + (double)k) * tempData[k];    b_y1[k] = d;    y3[k] += d;  }  writeThroughInput(tempData, y2);}

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Cast that changes the value of its input might be incorrectly removed if used on a bitwise operator
Description: Incorrect code generation might occur for the following patterns in your MATLAB code: out = cast(bitwiseOperator (in1,in2),'type ') relationalOperator  someExpression ;?  out = cast(cast(bitwiseOperator (in1,in2),'type '),'logical');   This issue occurs when all of the following conditions?are true:? ? ? 1. bitwiseOperator   is either bitand , bitor , or bitxor . ? ? 2.? type   is any type that results in the cast changing the value of its input. For example, if in1  and in2  are int64  and type   is uint32 , then this is a value changing cast. ? ? 3.? relationalOperator   is either ==, ~=,?<, <=, >,  or ?>= .? ? ? 4. someExpression  ?is any MATLAB expression. For example, consider the following MATLAB code: function out = foo(in1,in2)out = cast(bitor(in1,in2),'uint32') ~= 0;end Here?in1  and in2  are of type int64 .?The generated code is: boolean_T xg1844659(long in1, long in2){  return (in1 | in2) != 0L;} The cast to 'uint32'  does not appear in the generated code. The correct code should be:? boolean_T foo(long in1, long in2){  long i0;  i0 = in1 | in2;  if (i0 < 0L) {    i0 = 0L;  } else {    if (i0 > 4294967295L) {      i0 = 4294967295L;    }  }  return (unsigned int)i0 != 0U;}

Summary: MATLAB Function Block with multiple instances of model references do not stop at breakpoints
Description: When you start a simulation from the top level of a model reference hierarchy with multiple instances of referenced models, the simulation incorrectly does not stop at breakpoints set in any MATLAB Function blocks in those referenced models.?

Summary: Security Issue: Running Polyspace or MATLAB with Polyspace installed for R2013b ¨C R2018a may allow remote code execution
Description: Security updates are available for R2013b¨CR2018a. Running MATLAB with Polyspace installed may allow remote code execution.   If you are using R2018b, no action is required. This release is not affected.   If you are using an older release,?upgrade to R2018b .   If you need to use older releases, download security updates linked in the table below.              Release       Solution                     R2018b       No action required                R2018a       Download R2018a Update 6                 R2017b       Download R2017b Update 9                 R2017a       Download R2017a Update 3                 R2016b       Download R2016b Update 6                 R2016a       Download R2016a Update 7                 R2015b       Download R2015b Update 1                 R2015a SP1       Download R2015a SP1 Update 3                 R2013b¨CR2015a       See the Workaround  section below
Workaround: For R2013b¨CR2015a, download the attachment and follow the instructions below.Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect value for element of multi-dimensional matrix that results from concatenation along the second dimension
Description: Simulating a model that contains a MATLAB Function block, a MATLAB System block, or a Stateflow chart might incorrectly represent the value of a matrix for the following conditions: The matrix has > 2 dimensions and less than 11 elements. The matrix is the result of concatenating multiple matrices along the second dimension.  For an example, see the model issue_example_1843368.slx  in the attached  issue_1843368.zip . This model contains a MATLAB Function block with this content. ? function out1 = issue_example_1787271_1(in1,in2)    in1 = [-(in2( 1:1:2, 3, 6:8:14 )), (in2( 1:1:2, 3, 2:4:6 )) - (in1( 1:1:2, 1, 1:1:2 ))];    out1 = in1( 1, 2, 1 );end A uint32  matrix of zeros at in1  and a?uint32  matrix of ones at in2 drives t he MATLAB Function block. The output should be?1  but instead simulation produces a value of?0 . The model contains a second MATLAB Function block that is driven with the same inputs. In this block, the concatenation result is the condition of an if  statement.  function out1 = issue_example_1787271_2(in1,in2)        in1 = [-(in2( 1:1:2, 3:2:3, 6:8:14 )), (in2( 1:1:2, 3:2:3, 2:4:6 )) - (in1( 1:1:2, 1:2:1, 1:1:2 ))];    out1 = -10;    if(in1( 1, 2, 1 ))        out1 = 10;    endend The simulation should result in a value of 10 because in1(1,2,1)  has a value of 1  and the body of the if  statement executes. Instead, the if statement body does not execute and simulation produces an incorrect value of -10.?

Summary: Pipe (TL) block does wrong check on number of initial liquid pressures
Description: In Pipe (TL) blocks with the Number of elements  parameter set to 2 , a check done on the size of the Initial liquid pressures  vector may give wrong results.  The check is meant to ensure that the number of initial liquid pressures agrees with the number of pipe segments. A pipe with two segments should have one initial liquid pressure (for a uniform pressure distribution) or two initial liquid pressures (for a linear pressure gradient between the pipe segments).  Instead, the check requires that there be one initial liquid pressure or two initial liquid temperatures ( instead of pressures). The number of initial liquid temperatures is checked separately and it is out of place in a check on initial liquid pressures. As a result, if the pipe has two initial liquid pressures and one initial liquid temperature, the check reports an error when there should be none. If, on the other hand, the pipe has more than two initial liquid pressures and two initial liquid temperatures, the check fails to report an error when in fact there should be one.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation might ignore property validation code for a class in a package
Description: MATLAB classes can contain code to validate the type, size, and some invariants of a property value. In certain cases, MATLAB Coder and MATLAB Function Blocks in Simulink might fail to apply the property validation rules when generating C code for an assignment to an object property. Specifically, validation code is ignored if the class containing the validated property is located inside a package. For example, suppose that the class VAClass  is in a folder named +package  and that the function xValidate  creates an object of class VAClass . classdef VAClass  properties    Prop (1, 1) double  endend function out = xValidate  c2 = package.VAClass;  c2.Prop = 'a';  out = c2.Prop;end The generated C code for XValidate  computes a different value than MATLAB computes. Because property Prop  is constrained to be of type double, the assignment c2.Prop = 'a'  is supposed to convert 'a'  into a double. In code generation, if VAClass  is located inside a package, this assignment writes a character to the property without converting it to a double.

Summary: Mismatch?in behavior with?MATLAB for conversion from NaN to integer type
Description: When converting the value NaN to an integer type, the simulation behavior of MATLAB Function blocks, MATLAB System blocks, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow?might not match the behavior of MATLAB. In C/C++, a cast of a floating point NaN to an integer type is undefined. In MATLAB, a conversion of NaN to any integer type is well-defined: it has the value 0.?A simulation?with?MATLAB Function blocks, MATLAB System blocks, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow might use a C-style cast to convert from floating point NaN to any integer type, and not return 0.? For an example model that shows this behavior,?see the file 'example_issue_1837085.slx' attached within 'issue_1837085.zip'.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Conditionally called function with persistent system object incorrectly called unconditionally in generated code
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for MATLAB code that meets these conditions: ?An if-else statement calls a function in both branches of the if-else statement. The called function contains the use of a persistent system object.  In the case of the incorrect generated code, the code contains calls to the function before the if-else statement. The result of the calls are stored in temporary variables and used within the generated if-else statement. This code is incorrect if the persistent system object updates some form of internal state when used.  For an example of this issue see the model issue_model_1836686 in the attached file issue_example_1836686.zip. This MATLAB Function block in this model contains an if-else statement calling calledFcn if(in2 > 0)  y = calledFcn(in1);else  y = calledFcn(-in1);end? The function calledFcn uses a persistent system object, dsp.FIRFilter .? In the generated code, the function calls occur before the if-else statement.  issue_model_1836686_calledFcn(issue_model_1836686_P.Constant_Value, tmp);  for (i = 0; i < 9; i++) {    tmp_0[i] = -issue_model_1836686_P.Constant_Value[i];  }  issue_model_1836686_calledFcn(tmp_0, tmp_1);  tmp_2 = (issue_model_1836686_U.In1 > 0.0);  if (tmp_2) {    memcpy(&issue_model_1836686_Y.Out1[0], &tmp[0], 9U * sizeof(real_T));  } else {    memcpy(&issue_model_1836686_Y.Out1[0], &tmp_1[0], 9U * sizeof(real_T));  } This is incorrect because the function issue_model_1836686_calledFcn updates the internal state of a dsp.FIRFilter. Therefore it should only be called depending on the branch taken by the if-else statement. The correct generated code can be seen below:?   if (issue_model_1836686_U.In1 > 0.0) {    issue_model_1836686_calledFcn(issue_model_1836686_P.Constant_Value,      issue_model_1836686_Y.Out1);  } else {    for (i = 0; i < 9; i++) {      tmp[i] = -issue_model_1836686_P.Constant_Value[i];    }    issue_model_1836686_calledFcn(tmp, issue_model_1836686_Y.Out1);  } This issue might affect the following code generation workflows:Code generation using MATLAB Coder only Code generation from a Simulink model containing MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks Code generation from Stateflow charts containing MATLAB Functions

Summary: imresize can give wrong answers for gpuArray images created by indexing a larger image array or stack of images.
Description: If you create an input to imresize  by indexing a larger gpuArray  image, it can give incorrect answers for certain sizes, usually appearing to have shifted the image several pixels. This will typically occur when indexing a single color channel of an image, or selecting a single image from a batch of images. In the following example, imresize  should not change the image because the scale factor is one, but instead it gives a wrong result for the bottom half of the array. ? N = 5;? im = [gpuArray.ones(N,N,3), gpuArray.zeros(N,N,3);? ? ? ? gpuArray.zeros(N,N,3), gpuArray.ones(N,N,3)]? im_3 = im(:,:,3)? im_large = imresize(im_3, 1)
Workaround: The problem is related to the sharing of data between the indexed part and the original image or image stack. You can avoid it by breaking this share. One way to do this is to delete the original.? im_3 = im(:,:,3)? im = [];? im_large = imresize(im_3, 1) % Answer now correctAlternatively, you can force the data to unshare by operating on it. Adding a scalar zero will have the desired effect.? im_3 = im(:,:,3)?+ 0? im_large = imresize(im_3, 1) % Answer now correct?

Summary: If a Requirement Set is opened from a?directory?that is not the current working directory, Update may fail to match the previously imported requirements
Description: If a Requirement Set is opened from a directory other than the current directory, Update may fail to match the previously imported requirements. As a result, Update will delete the requirements, and re-create new ones, which will invalidate any links to the previously imported requirements, since the re-created requirements are assigned different Sequence Identifiers (SIDs).
Workaround: To work around this issue, reset the location of the external document that is stored in the Requirement Set before invoking Update:Right-click on the top Requirement item corresponding to the imported document.? For example, "Import1";Select the menu item "Update source document name or location...";When prompted with "Select the new name or location for source requirements file", the file selection dialog is prefilled with the name of the previously imported document. For example, assume the file is named "SystemReqs.xlsx".? Select another Requirement Set (for example, "SystemReqs_1.xlsx") and click "Open". The second requirement set file has to exist and has to have a different name than the original document.? The second requirement set will not be affected by this step.? Invoke the "Update source document..." a second time. This time, select the correct file in its current location ("SystemReqs.xlsx"). Then invoke Update.

Summary: Compilation fails with message cannot open source file
Description: In the compilation phase Polyspace creates a preprocessed file for each file to be analyzed. If a preprocessed file size exceeds 2 GB, it might result in this error: Catastrophic error: cannot open source file "<temp>\Polyspace\temporary_file": not a regular file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator can?produce wrong answers for reusable export-function model that includes reusable subsystem that uses shared local data store
Description: The code generator can generate incorrect code for a model?when all of these conditions are true: The model is an export-function model. If the model is a referenced model, model configuration parameter?Total number of instances allowed per top model  ?parameter is set to?Multiple.  If the model is a top model, model configuration parameter  Code interface packaging   is set to  Reusable function.  The model includes multiple instances of an atomic subsystem with block parameter Function packaging  set to Auto  or?Reusable function .  The atomic subsystem includes?a Data Store Memory block?with block parameter Share across model instances   selected . The generated subsystem function is called from multiple call sites.  When these conditions exist, multiple calls to the generated subsystem function all access the same shared local data store instance instead of accessing unique instances of the data store, which can result in wrong answers.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model?with a Unit Delay block inside an Enable subsystem
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Unit Delay block inside an Enable subsystem. The Unit Delay block connects to the output port of the Enable subsystem. A Reusable  custom storage class is specified on the input, output or internal state of the Unit Delay block or the the model parameter Optimize global data access? is set to Use global to hold temporary results .?
Workaround: set_param(bdroot, 'GlobalVariableUsage', 'none') and/or remove the Reusable custom storage class from the Unit Delay block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with an in-place MATLAB Function block and customized code interface
Description: Incorrectly generated code is possible for a?model that meets these conditions:In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the System target file   is set to ert.tlc . The model contains a MATLAB Function block that connects to a pair of model-level root input and output ports. The MATLAB Function block input/output pair that connect to the root-level input and output ports contain an in-place specification (that is, they contain the same name.) In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Code interface packaging  parameter is set to Reusable function .? In the generated code, the root-level input/output pair that connect to the MATLAB Function block have different names.
Workaround: Remove the in-place specification from the MATLAB Function block or using function prototype control specify the same name for the root-level input and output ports that connect to the MATLAB Function block.

Summary: The Inherit sample rate from input?parameter on MIMO Fading Channel and SISO Fading Channel blocks does not work when checked
Description: When the Inherit sample rate from input  parameter is checked, the MIMO Fading Channel and SISO Fading Channel blocks erroneously get the signal sampling rate from the Sample rate (Hz)  parameter, instead of deriving it from the input signal. As the default value of the?Sample rate (Hz) ?parameter is 1, the channel may erroneously behave frequency-flat while it is expected to be frequency-selective for an input signal sampling rate of much larger than 1.?
Workaround: Uncheck the Inherit sample rate from input?parameter, calculate the signal sampling rate using the following script, and specify the?Sample rate (Hz)?parameter to the Rs variable value. To run the script, replace modelName by the name of the Simulink model where the channel block is and replace chanBlockName by the name of the channel block.?modelName([],[],[],'compile');inDim = getfield(get_param(chanBlockName, 'CompiledPortDimensions'), 'Inport');Ts = get_param(chanBlockName, 'CompiledSampleTime');Rs = inDim(2)/Ts(1);modelName([],[],[],'term');

Summary: readtable detects trailing empty fields on the first line of the file as text
Description: If the first line of a text file ends in a delimiter followed by an empty field, then readtable? returns the variable after the delimiter as text. For example, if a file text.csv  contains the following lines: Var1,Var2,Var3  A,1,  B,3,4  Then,?readtable  reads the data as follows.?  T = readtable('test.csv')  T =  ? 2ˇÁ3 table  ? ? Var1? ? Var2? ? Var3  ? ? ____? ? ____? ? ____  ? ? 'A'? ? ? 1? ? ? ''??  ? ? 'B'? ? ? 3? ? ? '4'?
Workaround: Use detectImportOptions with readtable:>> opts = detectImportOptions(filename)>> T = readtable(filename,opts)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table might produce incorrect answers for fixed-point?Table data with non-trivial slope and Linear Lagrange interpolation method
Description: Lookup table blocks might produce mismatched results between simulation and generated code when all of the following conditions are true:  Table data ?is of a fixed-point type? The fixed-point type has a slope value not equal to 1 Interpolation method is set to Linear Lagrange?   The following blocks are affected: n-D Lookup Table 1-D Lookup Table 2-D Lookup Table Interpolation using Prelookup?  You are more likely to encounter this problem when the table dimension is greater than?3 or?the slope is?much smaller or much larger than 1.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Write to handle class data incorrectly removed in the presence of copy referring to the same object
Description: When generating code for a Simulink model containing MATLAB Function blocks, MATLAB System blocks, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow, Simulink Coder might incorrectly omit code that writes to a handle class object. This can happen for MATLAB code when: Multiple statements write to the data of a handle class variable, with no reads of the handle class variable between writes. A shallow handle class copy referring to the same object is used between the writes.  For an example, see the model issue_model_1827492.slx ?within the attached file issue_example_1827492.zip . The MATLAB function block in this model contains the code: handle_C.value = handle_B.value;handle_D.value = handle_shallow_copy_C.value;handle_C.value = handle_shallow_copy_D.value; The writes to handle_C.value  are required because of the read of handle_shallow_copy_C.value ,?which is equivalent to reading handle_C.value . In the generated code, the first write is incorrectly omitted:   /* '<s1>:1:28' */  /* '<s1>:1:29' */  handle_D = *handle_shallow_copy_C;  /* '<s1>:1:30' */  handle_C = *handle_shallow_copy_D; The correct code looks like:   /* '<s1>:1:28' */  handle_C = handle_B;  /* '<s1>:1:29' */  handle_D = *handle_shallow_copy_C;  /* '<s1>:1:30' */  handle_C = *handle_shallow_copy_D;

Summary: Access violation sporadically happens on "locRegisterSampleTimeSpecAllBlk" in "slrootgraphtstable.cpp"
Description: Access violation sporadically happens related to bus expansion on a single rate block when sample time?to block information is cached. The problem could be reproduced by running?a test points on win64  about 100 times.

Summary: CI engine dynamometer?Resize Engine and Recalibrate Controller test incorrectly updates Mapped CI Engine block lookup tables
Description: The compression-ignition (CI) engine dynamometer reference application?Resize Engine and Recalibrate Controller  test incorrectly updates the Mapped CI Engine block lookup tables. After you run the test, the reference application does not use the correct fuel injection slope to update the lookup tables in the Mapped CI Engine block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Order of generated code is partially swapped for bus signal elements inheriting different variant conditions
Description: The order of the generated code may?be partially swapped for bus signal elements inheriting different variant conditions?when all these conditions are true: At least two elements of the bus share an inherited variant condition. At least one element of the bus inherits an additional variant condition that is not inherited by the other elements. The bus signal is passed through a block with a state (for example, Unit Delay) that feeds directly into an atomic subsystem. The model configuration parameter?Simulink block comments ?is selected.  This?swap in the order of generated code?can lead to incorrect results, such as a signal being read before being initialized.

Summary: Error reported when importing ReqIF file into Simulink Requirements
Description: When you attempt to import a ReqIF file into Simulink Requirements that does not contain "title" ?or "sourceToolId"  in the ReqIFHeader , you will encounter the following error message: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Sequences ofoperations of complex gpuArrays that create copies can give wrong answers
Description: Some sequences of operations on gpuArrays  of complex numbers can result in incorrect results when the operations require copies of the operands. Example 1:  Createa complex gpuArray  and perform a sequence of operations on it.  >> x = gpuArray(complex(ones(3), ones(3))) x =   1.0000 + 1.0000i   1.0000 + 1.0000i   1.0000 + 1.0000i   1.0000 + 1.0000i   1.0000 + 1.0000i   1.0000 + 1.0000i   1.0000 + 1.0000i   1.0000 + 1.0000i   1.0000 + 1.0000i>> x = uminus(x);  In particular,create a function handle as part of the sequence. Note that function handlesneed to copy its input arguments. For more information on this behavior, see Variablesin the Expression .  >> fh = @()sin(x);   If you evaluate the function handle, you get a wrong answer. >> r = fh()  r =?? 0.8415 + 0.0000i?? 0.8415 + 0.0000i?? 0.8415 + 0.0000i?? 0.8415 + 0.0000i?? 0.8415 + 0.0000i?? 0.8415 + 0.0000i?? 0.8415 + 0.0000i?? 0.8415 + 0.0000i?? 0.8415 + 0.0000i  Example 2:   Create a complex gpuArray  and call cellfun  on it. By default, cellfun  creates an intermediate copy of the output.  >>x = gpuArray(complex(ones(3), ones(3)));   >> z = cellfun(@(x) {sin(x)}, {x});The output contains a wrong answer: >> z{:}ans =   1.2985 + 0.0000i   1.2985 + 0.0000i   1.2985 + 0.0000i   1.2985 + 0.0000i   1.2985 + 0.0000i   1.2985 + 0.0000i   1.2985 + 0.0000i   1.2985 + 0.0000i   1.2985 + 0.0000i
Workaround: To work around thisissue, gather the data beforethe copy operation, or avoid the creation of intermediate copies. Example 1:To avoid a wronganswer, gather the gpuArray before MATLAB copies it. >> x = gpuArray(complex(ones(3), ones(3)));>> x = uminus(x); >> gather(x);>> fh = @()sin(x);>> r = fh()r =? ??? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i Example 2: To avoid a wronganswer, avoid the creation of an intermediate copy of the outputs, by setting UniformOutput to false.>> z = cellfun(@(x) sin(x), {x}, 'UniformOutput', false); >> z{:}?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i?? 1.2985 + 0.6350i

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: spline function might return wrong answers when there are only two data sites
Description: The spline  function might return wrong answers when the first input is a vector of length 2, the second input is a vector of length 4, and at least one of the two inputs is variable in length. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: MAT-files resaved by Root Inport Mapper are larger than original MAT-files
Description: The Root Inport Mapper?resaves MAT-files?in 7.3 format,?resulting?in MAT-file?sizes that are much larger than the original MAT-file.

Summary: SimulationStatus does not change during a callback from a .NET application
Description: In a callback from a .NET application, if you use ? ? ?set_param(modelName, 'SimulationCommand', 'Stop')  the SimulationStatus  does not change to 'stopped'  until the callback is exited.
Workaround: For releases R2016b through R2018a only, you can work around this issue by checking the SimulationStatus in a separate callback.

Summary: Compilation might fail with the error assertion failed at: "edg/?src/const_ints.c"
Description: When using 16-bit targets, the Polyspace compilation phase of a?file can fail with this error message:   Verifying my_file.c (X/Y)    Internal error: assertion failed at:  "b:\?matlab\?polyspace\?src\?shared\?cxx_front_end_kernel\?edg\?src\?const_ints.c",? line 399

Summary: matlab.io.fits.writeKey?returns an error when writing certain keys to a FITS file
Description: The  matlab.io.fits.writeKey  function returns an error when writing certain reserved keywords of form?PTYPEn, TDIMn, TDISPn, TFORMn, TTYPEn, TUNITn  to a FITS file .? For example, this write operation returns an error:? fits.writeKey(fptr, 'TTYPE4', 'abc');  Error message:?? Error using matlab.io.fits.writeKey (line 67)  Value for the 'TTYPE4' keyword should be a character vector or string scalar.

Summary: fmincon crashes when using some nondefault options
Description: The fmincon solver can   crash when all of these options are set: 'Algorithm' is 'sqp'    'ScaleProblem'  is 'obj-and-constr'   'OutputFcn' is a function that causes the solver to stop during iteration 0 of the algorithm
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can do any of the following:Switch the 'Algorithm' option to 'sqp-legacy'.?Remove the 'ScaleProblem' option or the 'OutputFcn' option.Ensure that the output function returns false?(so it does not cause the algorithm to stop) when the iteration number?is 0. For example:function stop = outfun(x,optimValues,state)if optimValues.iteration == 0? ? stop = false;else?? ? % Insert your normal output function logic hereendend

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using add_line to add a line between connection ports
Description: MATLAB might crash when you perform these steps: Load a model by using the?load_system ?function. Without opening the loaded model, add a line between two of its connection ports by using the add_line  function.
Workaround: Before using the?add_line?function,?use open_system to open the?specific subsystem?in which you want to add the line.

Summary: Parallel builds of model reference simulation targets can fail
Description: Simulation and code generation can fail when all of these conditions apply: The model being built references other models. The top model has the?Enable parallel model reference builds  check box selected.  When the model reference simulation target fails to build, you receive this error message: "The [...\slprj\sim\modelname\...] folder does not exist. The file might have been moved or deleted."

Summary: Code generation assertion for AUTOSAR model with error status port mapped to root inport with queued receive data access mode
Description: Building an AUTOSAR model fails to generate code if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root inport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to?QueuedExplicitReceive . The model contains a Stateflow chart that receives queued data messages from the root inport. The model contains an AUTOSAR error status port, which is mapped to the root inport.  Under these conditions, AUTOSAR code generation fails with an assertion.
Workaround: Before generating code, remove the error status port from the AUTOSAR model. Error status ports are not needed for root inports that receive queued data messages in Stateflow charts. You can use Stateflow functions to evaluate error status on the root inport.

Summary: Extra header file (multiword_types.h) included in the generated code
Description: The generated code may contain the directive #inlcude "multiword_types.h"  when it is not required. This may happen when the following conditions are met: Model uses configuration reference. Configuration parameter Maximum word length  (ERTMultiwordLength ) is set to a value other than its default (256). Model is saved in R2016b or before and is loaded in R2017a or later.
Workaround: Manually set configuration parameter Maximum word length in the referenced configuration set to a desired value and then save the changes to the MAT-file or the data dictionary.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results when there are multiple scoped-exported Simulink functions with the same name
Description: Simulation results from a call to a Simulink function may be incorrect when all of the following are true: There are multiple exported Simulink functions with the same name (for example, myFunc, scoped to an export-function model  ) at various levels of the model hierarchy. myFunc  is called? from the top-most level of the model hierarchy. The top-level model contains multiple instances of other levels of the model hierarchy that contain myFunc .

Summary: Models with Implicit ODE solvers may simulate slowly when the model Jacobian pattern is dense and 'Solver Jacobian Method' is set to 'auto'
Description: Simulink models that have specified an ODE solver that requires Jacobian matrix for integration may be impacted by a simulation slowdown compared to the previous releases when the model Jacobian matrix has a dense (full) pattern and the Solver Jacobian Method is set to 'auto '. The following solvers are affected by this issue: ode15s, ode23t, ode23tb, ode23s and ode14x .
Workaround: If you observe a slowdown, you can explicitly specify the Solver Jacobian Method as detailed below instead of using 'auto'.?In the Solver pane of the model Configuration Parameters, set the Solver Jacobian Method in the 'Solver details' section to 'Full perturbation'.Alternatively, you can also set Solver Jacobian Method parameter for a model named 'myModel' that is open or loaded using the set_param on the MATLAB command line as follows:set_param('myModel', 'SolverJacobianMethodControl', 'FullPerturbation');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when performing an element-wise operation on N-D arrays (N>2) of mismatched sizes
Description: In certain cases, generated code for element-wise operations where the two inputs have mismatching numbers of dimensions will produce an output of the smaller size.? This might happen even if the two inputs have different numbers of elements as long as the first two dimensions match.? Specifically, any element-wise operation on two arrays can produce an incorrect answer if: at least one of the arrays have more than two dimensions, the arrays have a different number of dimensions, and the trailing dimensions in the larger array are variable-size.  For example, consider the function?test1 : function out = test1(x,y)  out = x.*y;  end  If the size of?x ?is 512-by-1 and the size of?y ?is 512-by-1-by-2 at run-time, and the third dimension of?y ?is variable-size at compile time, then the generated code for?test1 ?produces a 2-dimensional output of size 512-by-1. MATLAB produces an output of size 512-by-1-by-2. To generate code and observe the difference in size, enter: ? ?codegen test1 -args {coder.typeof(ones(512,1)), coder.typeof(1,[512,1,Inf])}   ? ?size(test1(ones(512,1), ones(512,1,2))) ? ?size(test1_mex(ones(512,1), ones(512,1,2)))?  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: If the intended behavior is to expand thesmaller array to match the size of the larger one, use the?function bsxfun toperform the operation. If the intended behavior is to operate on identical size arrays only, add a size check prior to the operation.

On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.
