Summary: Incorrect diagnostic error message for Simulink.Variant objects having condition ('0==ARBITRARY_COND')
Description: Incorrect diagnostic error message may be generated for a simulink model that meets the below conditions:  Simulink Function block has a specified variant condition  A .  Function caller block gets a propagated variant condition  A .  Where,  A  is? Simulink.Variant object is defined as,  A = Simulink.Variant('0 ==? ARBITRARY_COND')   Example diagnostic error message: Propagated condition on block 'min_repro/Function Caller1' will lead in a build error during code generation if 'min_repro/Function Caller1' is calling an inactive Simulink Function block 'min_repro/Simulink Function1'.  Variant condition on blocks:  ? 'min_repro/Function Caller1': (0 == ARBITRARY_COND)  ? 'min_repro/Simulink Function1': (0 == ARBITRARY_COND) ?
Workaround: Specify the variant condition using Simulink.Variant object as:A.Condition = 'ARBITRARY_COND == 0' , instead ofA.Condition = '0 ==ARBITRARY_COND'

Summary: ImageDatastore? returns an error when the last file in the Files property is deleted
Description: If you delete the last file in the Files  property of ImageDatastore , this error occurs:? Error using audioDatastore/ subsasgn (line 364)    Index exceeds array bounds.   For example, this code will return an error: ds = imageDatastore({});?  ds.Files =??'corn.tif';  ds.Files(1) = [];?

Summary: PDF image mapping fails to map small areas and implement area styles
Description: The DOM API incorrectly positions ImageArea  objects whose height is less than 2 points.The DOM API also ignores the Style  property of ImageArea  objects. This make it impossible to draw borders around image hot spots.
Workaround: To enable approximately correct positioning of image areas, wrap the image to be mapped in a paragraph with a font size less than 2pt, for example,import mlreportgen.dom.*import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('imgmap','pdf');img = Image(which('b747.jpg'));map = ImageMap;append(map,ImageArea('https://google.com','',0,0,10,2));img.Map = map;p = Paragraph;p.FontSize = '1.5pt';append(p,img);append(d,p);close(d);rptview(d);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Failure to produce compilation error when you use an unassigned property of an object contained in a variable-size cell array
Description: When you define a variable-size cell array that contains objects, and one or more of the contained objects has an unassigned property, the code generator may not produce an error when that property is used and incorrectly generate code. You can create a variable-size cell array by using coder.varsize  or by passing a variable-size cell array to the codegen  function as a top-level input.

Summary: Error when running parsim on a non-local cluster for a model using Simulink Project
Description: Running parallel simulations using parsim on a non-local cluster for a model which is inside a Simulink Project but has file dependencies outside of the Simulink Project throws a java.lang.NullPointerException. Java exception occurred:?java.lang.NullPointerException?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.exportfilters.FileExportFilter.getAllParentFoldersUpToRoot(FileExportFilter.java:33)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.exportfilters.FileExportFilter.modify(FileExportFilter.java:47)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.ProjectExport.applyProjectFilters(ProjectExport.java:100)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.ProjectExport.getProjectArchiver(ProjectExport.java:52)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.ProjectExport$1.archive(ProjectExport.java:44)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.ProjectExport$1.archive(ProjectExport.java:39)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.matlab.api.MatlabAPIProjectManagerFacade.export(MatlabAPIProjectManagerFacade.java:469)?at? com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.matlab.api.MatlabAPIMatlabProjectManager.export(MatlabAPIMatlabProjectManager.java:235)??
Workaround: Add files?identified as a model dependency to the Simulink Project.?

Summary: Configuration Parameters dialog fails to load and displays an error message
Description: The Configuration Parameters dialog fails to load and displays the following error message: An unexpected error occurred while creating the Configuration Parameters Dialog.  This might be caused by functions in your MATLAB path that shadow built-in MATLAB functions.   Argument to dynamic structure reference must evaluate to a valid field name.  file: C:\matlab\toolbox\shared\sldv\+Sldv\+ParameterTuning\@Manager\getparamsFromConfig.m  name: getParamsFromConfig  line:31?
Workaround: Reset the parameters of the model by typing these statements at the command line:?set_param(model_name,'DVParameterNames',[]);save_system(model_name);close_system(model_name);open_system(model_name);?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Function-Call Subsystem driven by an exported Stateflow function may produce a mismatch in the numerical outputs of the simulation and the generated code
Description: The generated code may produce an output that varies from the simulation of that model if it meets the following conditions: The model contains a?Stateflow chart calling an exported Simulink function in an another Stateflow chart. This exported function is in turn connected to a Simulink Function-Call Subsystem. This Simulink Function-Call?subsystem is then connected directly to a root-level Outport?block. The execution order between the two Stateflow charts is inverted. That is the callee chart executes before the caller chart.  The simulation executes the root-level Outport?block before the Stateflow chart with the exportable Simulink function is called. The generated code executes the write to the Outport buffer when the Stateflow chart with the exportable Simulink function?is called. This causes a simulation and code generation mismatch with the simulation containing a one time step delay in its output value.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Function-Call Subsystem and the root-level Outport block with its Exclude this block from 'Block Reduction' Optimization parameter turned on. This ensures the generated code has the same behavior as the simulation.

Summary: Incorrect?error for data dependency violation with state ports
Description: When a state port forms a loop with?other graphical connections,?Simulink reports an error if the loop contains blocks that are not supported in an algebraic loop. Since a loop with a state port?cannot form an?algebraic loop, such a graphical loop should not lead to a data dependency violation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect memcpy call for some index expressions containing computations of the form x * (1 + y)
Description: Incorrect results are possible in rare situations where the MATLAB Code contains a?copy?from elements?of A to B. The subscript of B?contains an expression of the form x*(1+y ),  where x  and y  are not for-loop  index variables, and their values?are not known at compile time. The incorrect code contains an incorrect memcpy  call which leads to an answer mismtach between MATLAB and generated code.For example, the attached?MATLAB function contains this code : function B = example_1819918(A, x, y)  ?B = zeros(100,1);  for k = 1:50  ? ? B(k) = A(k +? x*(1 + y));? ? ? ?  end?  end?  The incorrect?generated code contains this incorrect memcpy  function call: void example_1819918(const double A[100], int x, int y, double B[100])  {  ? memset(&B[0], 0, 100U * sizeof(double));  ? memcpy(&B[0], &A[x] , 50U * sizeof(double));  }  The correct generated code should look like:? void example_1819918(const double A[100], int x, int y, double B[100])  {  ? memset(&B[0], 0, 100U * sizeof(double));  ? memcpy(&B[0], &A[x * y + x] , 50U * sizeof(double));  }  The attached zip file?Example_1819918.zip  contains the ?example_1819918.m ?and codegen_example_1819918.m  files for this example.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Output of disp may?be?different?between MATLAB?and generated MEX?when?disp is passed a property of an object that is conditionally defined as another object
Description: Generally, the code generator?produces an error?if your MATLAB code uses?a property that has not been previously defined along all potential execution paths.?However, the code generator?does not?produce an error if all of these conditions are true: An?object X had a property that is?defined along some, but not all, execution paths to be some other object?Y.? The?object X?is passed to a function?in which the?property Y?is directly used by disp .   In such case, the?behavior that occurs at the use of Y in the generated code is undefined. For example, consider this MATLAB function: function exampleFunction(in)obj = MyClass;if in > 0    obj.p1 = MyClass;endobj.displayIt();end Here the class MyClass  is defined as: classdef MyClass < handle    properties        p1    end    methods        function displayIt(obj)            disp(obj.p1);        end    endend Running exampleFunction ?in MATLAB produces?no output. However, if you run the generated?MEX?function, the disp  function displays this output: MyClass?with properties:  p1: []  This issue affects your code only if?disp ?directly uses the property that is an object rather than accessing one of its members.?So, for example, this issue?would not occur if disp  used?obj.p1.p1  in the above example code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Deep learning code generation for TensorRT generates incorrect results for non-square inputs
Description: When generating TensorRT code for a deep learning network containing a convolution  layer, the generated code returns incorrect results for non-square inputs. For example, consider a deep learning network (net)? with the following layers:? >> net.layers?  ? ? ?1? ?'Input'? ?Image Input? ? ? ? ?384x512x3 images?  ? ? ?2? ?'conv1'? ?Convolution? ? ? ? ?96 11x11x3 convolutions with stride [4? 4] and padding [0? 0? 0? 0]?  ? ? ?3? ?''? ? ? ? Regression Output? ?mean-squared-error?   The input fed to the imageInputLayer ?is non-square and as a result, the output from executing the?predict ?method on the network contains incorrect results. This issue does not affect networks with an?imageInputLayer ?that accepts square inputs.

Summary: Verification fails during compilation phase with error: assertion failed
Description: For some C++ applications, verification can fail during the compilation phase. The error message is similar to this:? pathto\myfile.cpp, line xx: internal error: (at end of source) assertion failed at: "b:\matlab\polyspace\src\shared\cxx_front_end_kernel\edg\src\trans_corresp.c", line 4253 ... Compilation aborted.

Summary: Very large CTF files when compiling applications that include features from Deep Learning Toolbox
Description: If you compile an application using MATLAB Compiler, and that application uses features from Deep Learning Toolbox, then the CTF file can be much larger than expected, e.g., it can be 300Mb rather than 50-70Mb.

Summary: Performance regression when large number of errors or warnings are reported in Simulation Manager when using parsim
Description: When running approximately greater than 500 simulations, if all or a large fraction of simulations complete with errors or warnings, additional time is spent updating the status of simulations in the Simulation Manager. This results in slowing down the task of executing simulations by up to three times slower than when the Simulation Manager is not used.? The status of the simulations as shown on the Simulation Manager is still accurate. The results and performance of the simulations themselves are not affected.?

Summary: Test harnesses for a model might not load correctly after the associated harnessInfo file is merged
Description: Test harnesses stored externally?might fail to load when all of these conditions occur: Multiple test harnesses are present in a model Two or more test harnesses are edited by different users and submitted to source control The <modelname>_harnessInfo.xml  file, which maintains metadata about the test harnesses, is manually merged?  Subsequent harness operations, including opening or updating metadata, can fail with warnings similar to the following: The test harness <test harness name> was saved externally but the owner block?<path to the block that owns the harness> was not found in <model name>. This  test harness will not be created. Re-save <model name> to update the harnessInfo metadata file <harness info file name>.

Summary: Calling close_system or bdclose from a callback can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: When you simulate a model hierarchy in normal mode, MATLAB can crash, stop responding, or simulate incorrectly when these conditions apply: The model hierarchy contains at least one Model block that has Simulation mode  set to Normal . A callback that runs when you simulate the model hierarchy uses close_system  or bdclose . These functions unload models from memory.

Summary: Generic clusters do not work in compiled applications
Description: MATLAB throws an error when you try to use a generic cluster in a compiled application, evenif you add the integration scripts location?under Files required foryour application to run  in the Application Compiler app or with the-a ?option to mcc . The output of a compiled application that usesgeneric clusters can contain errors of the following forms:  The file?  ? ? '/path/to/integration/scripts/location/communicatingSubmitFcn.m'?    is not in the application's expanded CTF archive at  ? ? '/home/username/.mcrCache9.4/appName0'.  This is typically caused by calls to ADDPATH in your startup.m or matlabrc.m files. Please see the compiler documentation and use the ISDEPLOYED function to ensure ADDPATH commands are not executed by deployed applications.  OR? Previously accessible file "/path/to/integration/scripts/location/deleteJobFcn.m" is now inaccessible.  OR? Error using parallel.Job/submit (line 327)?  ? ? Job submission failed because the user supplied CommunicatingSubmitFcn (communicatingSubmitFcn) errored.  ? ? ? ? Undefined function 'communicatingSubmitFcn' for input arguments of type 'parallel.cluster.Generic'.
Workaround: In all MATLAB releases:Touse generic clusters in compiled applications you must add the integration scripts location?under?Files required for your application to run?in the Application Compiler app or with the?-a?option to?mcc. ?In R2017a and R2017b:If you are using Generic cluster objects, you must use the following workaround to specify the?IntegrationScriptsLocation?to be under?ctfroot. ?Here GenericProfile?is the name of a generic cluster profile.c = parcluster('GenericProfile');if isdeployed    if startsWith(c.IntegrationScriptsLocation, matlabroot)        pathRelativeToCtfroot = erase(c.IntegrationScriptsLocation, matlabroot);    else        folderStructure = split(c.IntegrationScriptsLocation, filesep);        folderStructure = folderStructure(~cellfun(@isempty, folderStructure));       ?pathRelativeToCtfroot = folderStructure{end};    end    c.IntegrationScriptsLocation = fullfile(ctfroot, pathRelativeToCtfroot);endIf you are using cluster profile names in parallel functions, such as parpool('GenericProfile'), then?you?must convert your code to use Generic cluster objects and use the above workaround. ?For example:c = parcluster('GenericProfile');if isdeployed    if startsWith(c.IntegrationScriptsLocation, matlabroot)        pathRelativeToCtfroot = erase(c.IntegrationScriptsLocation, matlabroot);    else        folderStructure = split(c.IntegrationScriptsLocation, filesep);        folderStructure = folderStructure(~cellfun(@isempty, folderStructure));       ?pathRelativeToCtfroot = folderStructure{end};    end    c.IntegrationScriptsLocation = fullfile(ctfroot, pathRelativeToCtfroot);endparpool(c);

Summary: fitsread function returns an error when reading variable length character data from certain FITS files
Description: The?fitsread ?function returns an error when reading variable length character data from binary tables in FITS files. This error is observed when the FITS file has more than 10,000  rows. For example, this read operation returns an error:   data = fitsread('test.fits','binarytable'); Error message:? ? Unable to perform assignment because the size of the left side is 10000-by-25 and the size of the right side is 10000-by-23.  ? Error in matlab.io.fits.readCol (line 86)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? [coldata(k:index,:), nullval(:,k:index)] =? fitsiolib('read_col',fptr,colnum,k,rowsToBeRead);  ? Error in fitsread>read_bintable_hdu (line 578)  ? ? ? ?[coldata,nuldata] = fits.readCol(fptr,tableColumns(j));  ? Error in fitsread (line 144)  ? ? ? ? ? data = read_bintable_hdu(info,hdu_index,raw,tableColumns,tableRows);

Summary: Slow performance when running a model with a From File block loading large buses
Description: Models containing a From File block that reads a bus signal with a large number of elements (> 1000) take much longer to run than compared to previous releases.
Workaround: Instead of a From FIle block, use a?matlab.io.datastore.SimulationDatastore?object in a?Simulink.SimulationData.Dataset?object to stream data into a root?Inport?block.See the following documentation page for more details and examples:?Load Big Data for Simulations

Summary: Cosimulation waveform mismatch due to incorrect clock ratio computation
Description: When you run cosimulation on a model that consists of multiple clock rates, in some cases, the output signal belonging to a slow clock rate may mismatch with the original model.The mismatch occurs because the clock ratio so computed for that particular output signal is incorrect.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Submitting large jobs to a MATLAB job scheduler might fail due to a TimeoutException
Description: When a job is submitted to a MATLAB Job Scheduler, the input arguments of its tasks are transferred to the scheduler database. If the input arguments are large enough, the scheduler will stream the data in multiple calls using a buffer. Each of these calls to fill the buffer must complete within a specified time, otherwise submission of the job will fail due to a TimeoutException.? The maximum buffer size used is 50 MB and the call timeout is 30 seconds, so users with a network connection between the submission client and the MJS host of less than 1.67 MB/s might experience this problem.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when assigning a struct field that contains anonymous function handles
Description: Attempting to assign a struct field that already contains an anonymous function handle causes MATLAB to crash, rather than throw an error. The following examples cause MATLAB to crash. Example 1: A  is a struct with the field Thermal . The second assignment is attempting to convert the field into an array of anonymous function handles.   A.Thermal(1)=@(T) 8*298./T;  A.Thermal(2)=@(T) 8*298./T; Example 2: A  is a struct with the field Thermal . The second assignment into Thermal  tries to convert a double to function handle, which should generate an error.   A.Thermal(1)=@(T) 8*298./T;  A.Thermal(2)= 1;

Summary: Simulink logs incorrect time values to MATLAB workspace for discrete signals in Enabled Subsystems
Description: When a model logs discrete signals in Enabled Subsystems, workspace variables with logged data might contain incorrect time values. During first interval when the Enabled Subsystem is enabled, the time values logged to the MATLAB workspace increase at a faster rate than the true simulation time values. A discrepancy between simulation time values and logged time values happens only if the Enabled Subsystem remains enabled for more than 20,000 time steps during the first interval, and then is disabled at least once.
Workaround: To obtain a workspace variable with the correct time values, you can export the signal from the Simulation Data Inspector. Export the signal itself, not the run that contains the signal. Open the Simulation Data Inspector by entering?Simulink.sdi.view? in the Command Window. To export a signal, select the signal and click?Export?on the toolbar or from the signal's context menu.

Summary: Warning when configuration set is loaded from MAT-file and?CodeProfilingInstrumentation?is 'on'
Description: When you load a configuration set from a MAT-file that was saved by using an earlier MATLAB release, and CodeProfilingInstrumentation  is 'on' , you see this warning: The value 'on' is not supported for 'CodeProfilingInstrumentation'. 'on' is replaced by 'Detailed'. If you want to profile only atomic subsystems and referenced models, ?set 'CodeProfilingInstrumentation' to 'Coarse'.

Summary: Some parameter values are not?restored correctly for linked port blocks
Description: Simulink does not?restore the value of some parameters for linked port blocks. The problem occurs when the following conditions are true: There is a masked port block in the library. For the masked port blocks, some of the parameter values are set to something other than their default value. There is a link to this masked port block in the model.? Opening the model does not?correctly load the linked port block. The parameters do not get the value set on the library block. They instead get the default values.

Summary: Shift arithmetic block can generate incorrect HDL code when the shift value specified via dialog is unsigned
Description: Shift arithmetic block may generate incorrect HDL Code when all the conditions are true: Bits to shift: Direction  is set to Left??  Bits to shift: Source   is  Dialog  Bits to shift: Number is an unsigned constant

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: CAN Pack and J1939 Transmit blocks generate incorrect results in Rapid Accelerator or code generation mode
Description: The following block configurations generate incorrect results in Rapid Accelerator or code generation mode when the input signal type is not single  or double : CAN Pack block with a signal whose data type is set to signed  or unsigned , and factor is not 1 . J1939 Transmit block with a parameter whose data type is set to signed ?or unsigned , and factor is not 1 .

Summary: Models containing sink S-functions cannot be used as accelerated model references
Description: Models containing sink S-functions (S-functions with no outputs) cannot be used as accelerated model references. The Simulink build process fails when you try to build the model reference simulation target for such models. The build process encounters the following assertion:? Error using tlc_c>GetBuildModuleList. Assertion failed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Scan String parameter and input mismatch may cause inconsistent simulation and code generation answers
Description: When the Scan String block input string?does not match the block Format  parameter: In?simulation, all unmatched outputs are set to the output data type ground value and?the block?returns a?warning?indicating this behavior. In?code generation,?any unmatched output continues to hold its value from the previous time step.  This?difference in?behavior might cause inconsistent?answers for simulation and code generation.

Summary: Undamped solid axle suspension oscillation when driving vehicle over bumpy terrain
Description: Vehicle Dynamics Blockset incorrectly calculates the suspension motion for vehicles configured with a solid axle.? When you drive a vehicle configured with a solid axle suspension over bumpy terrain, the suspension undergoes undamped oscillation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator?might not?honor data alignment when an operation or function performed on a submatrix is replaced by a custom function
Description: The code generator does not honor the data alignment requirement, producing incorrect code, when?all of the following conditions?are true: The model or MATLAB function from which you are generating code contains at least one operation/function that accesses a contiguously allocated submatrix data. The code generator uses a code replacement library to replace an operation or function with a custom function. The function/operation that accesses the submatrix data is replaced with a custom function.  The corresponding?code replacement?entry specifies an alignment requirement with the AlignmentBoundary  property. In the generated code, the custom function accesses the submatrix data by taking the address of  a matrix element other than the first element.     If you are using a compiler on a target that usesinstructions which produce incorrect numeric results or a crash when dataalignment requirement is not satisfied, then the compiled generated code fromSimulink model (or Matlab function) may produce incorrect numeric results or a crash.

Summary: Memory sections no longer allow $ in pre/post pragma statements
Description: When you use pre/post pragma statements for memory sections, previously supported usage of $ in the statements now generates an error message. This message does not occur when using?$?as part of the? $N ?identifier.

Summary: getembl returns "Internal Server Error"
Description: When you call getembl  in MATLAB R2018a or earlier, you get an error message detailing an "Internal Server Error" from EBI. In June 2018, the European Bioinformatics Institute (EMBL-EBI) moved the location of its tool for retrieving EMBL records, from https://www.ebi.ac.uk/cgi-bin/dbfetch to http://www.ebi.ac.uk/Tools/dbfetch/dbfetch.

Summary: SIL simulation error with lcc compiler: 'cp' is not recognized as an internal or external command
Description: If lcc  is the only compiler installed on your computer,?you cannot run a SIL simulation. You see an error message. For example: 'cp' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file
Workaround: To inspect your current system path, at the Windows command prompt, enter path.Using an editor, in a folder that lies on your system path, create the file cp.bat.In cp.bat, insert this line of text:copy %1 %2

Summary: Model with Reusable?custom storage class, MATLAB Function block, or Model Reference block may crash if Conditional input branch execution?parameter is enabled
Description: MATLAB may crash when you simulate, perform an update diagram or generate code for a model that has the configuration parameter?Conditional input branch execution ?selected and? meets one of these conditions:The model contains a Reusable ?custom storage class. The model contains a MATLAB Function block that uses the same variable for a pair of input and output arguments. The model contains a Model Reference block. Using function prototype control, the model step function contains the same variable for a pair of input an output arguments.  ?
Workaround: Clear the Conditional input branch execution parameter.

Summary: Blank page follows last section in a report
Description: A blank page, created by Report API?mlreportgen.report.Section  reporter,?follows the last section in a Word report.
Fixed In: R2017b Update 8, R2018a Update 4, R2018b, R2019a

Summary: An ASCII to String block?contained in?an atomic subsystem may only return the first character?during accelerated mode simulation
Description: An ASCII to String block?contained in?an atomic subsystem may only return the first character?during accelerated mode simulation. For example, if the uint8  array input to the ASCII to String block contains [72 101 108 108 111] , then the output string may contain only?"H"  instead of the complete string "Hello" .

Summary: The Run Failure Analysis in Parallel shortcut in the asbhl20 example fails for certain parallel cluster configurations
Description: Running the Run Failure Analysis in Parallel  shortcut of the asbhl20  example?(NASA HL-20 with Optional FlightGear Interface) fails to complete when the MATLAB session default parallel cluster is set to local  and contains at least two workers.

Summary: Plotting a cfit object with an outlier index equal to 1 returns an error
Description: If you plot a cfit object with an outlier index equal to 1, then MATLAB returns an error.
Workaround: If the first point is the only outlier, then specify the outliers using logical indexing. That is, specify the outliers as a vector with number of elements equal to the number of points and with the first element equal to 1 and all other elements equal to 0.

Summary: Out of memory error when plotting test and simulation outputs in the generated report
Description: If you select a large number of simulation outputs to plot in a report, report generation can cause an out of memory error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized local variables in code are possible in Initialize Function subsystem
Description: The generated code might contain uninitialized local variables for a model that meets all of the following conditions:The Initialize Function subsystem?has a block that generates a local block output variable. The local block output variable is initialized with a constant. The local block output variable is not optimized away.  When the bug occurs, there may be a numerical mismatch between simulation and code generation values.
Workaround: Mark the uninitialized block output as global signal or test point. This might work with variants.

Summary: MATLAB System block may generate uninitialized variables for System objects inheriting from matlab.mixin.Nondirect
Description: MATLAB System block may generate uninitialized variables for System objects inheriting from matlab.mixin.Nondirect.  This happens when the System object uses nondirect feedthrough has no direct feed-through and both the OutputImpl? and UpdateImpl methods are implemented.? When this happens, the generated code may contain uninitialized variables and there is a warning message link at the bottom of the model. When you click the link, the message is?displayed on the Diagnostics Viewer. However, there are no visual warnings on the MATLAB command window.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Order of generated code is partially swapped for Function-Call Subsystem that share a common caller
Description: The order of the generated code may be partially swapped for Function-Call Subsystem blocks when all these conditions are true: At least two Function-Call Subsystem blocks share a common caller. The common caller uses a Demux block to split its output rather than a Function-Call Split block. At least one of the Function-Call Subsystem blocks has an additional caller that is not shared with the other Function-Call Subsystem blocks. Data is transfered between the Function-Call Subsystem blocks. The model configuration parameter?Simulink block comments  is selected.  This swap in the order of generated code can lead to incorrect results, such as a signal being read before being initialized.

Summary: Duplicate coverage results from Simulink test iterations run with MATLAB Unit Test
Description: If you have a test case in Simulink Test that:  Is configured with iterations, and Is set to collect coverage (using Simulink Coverage)  And you run the test case as part of a test suite in MATLAB Unit Test, duplicate coverage results are returned for the same model. The number of coverage results equals the number of test iterations for the parent test case. This behavior may also occur for results at the test suite or test file level in Simulink Test. The expected behavior is that coverage is aggregated across test iterations for previous results in the test hierarchy.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Math expressionswith gpuArrays thatinvolve multiple operations with implicit expansion along different dimensions can return incorrectresults.
Description: Implicit expansion was introduced into the language in R2016b. For more information, see the release notes.  From R2017b, MATLAB can give incorrectresults when math expressions on gpuArrays contain multiple operations on thesame line, and each ofthem requires expansion along different dimensions. Consider the following example: >> A = gpuArray.rand(1,2); >> B = gpuArray.rand(2,2); >> C = gpuArray.rand(1,1,2);  % Gives wrong answer >>? A + B + Cans(:,:,1) =?? NaN?? NaN?? NaN?? NaNans(:,:,2) =?? NaN?? NaN?? NaN?? NaN   Here MATLAB expands A  along the first dimension,then A+B  along the third dimension. Note that the resulting answer from these faulty expressions may not be NaN.  To check that the result is not correct, comparethe results in GPU and CPU using isequaln : >> isequaln( A + B + C, gather(A) + gather(B) + gather(C))   ans =   ? logical   ?? 0
Workaround: Break the expression into multiple lines, so that each line only contains expansion along one dimension.% The same expression, when split into two different lines, gives the right answer>> A = gpuArray.rand(1,2); >> B = gpuArray.rand(2,2); >> C = gpuArray.rand(1,1,2);% expansion along first dimension>> T = A + B; % expansion along third dimension>> C + T ans(:,:,1) =??? 1.7223??? 0.6798??? 1.8116??? 1.4767ans(:,:,2) =??? 1.9004??? 0.8579??? 1.9897??? 1.6549

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using Discrete State-Space block with state name defined as xnew can lead to incorrect answer during code generation
Description: If a model has Discrete State-Space block, incorrect code is generated when all these conditions are met: State name  is xnew .?The name xnew  conflicts with the TLC keyword. Code Generation storage class  is ExportedGlobal , ImportedExtern  or any Custom storage class.
Workaround: Change the State name.
Products: Embedded Coder, Simulink Coder, MATLAB Coder
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 7, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: dsp.FIRDecimator System object crashes MATLAB when cloning after input error
Description: For the ?dsp.FIRDecimator  System object, MATLAB crashes if you: Create the System object. Call step ?with incorrect inputs. Then,? clone   the system object.  For example: h = dsp.FIRDecimator?  step(h, 1)?  clone(h)   MATLAB crashes only if the first call to?step ?is? with incorrect inputs.? MATLAB does not crash for subsequent calls of the System object.
Workaround: If an error occurs after calling step?for the first time, do not?clone the System object. Instead,?create a new System object, and clone that new object. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation might call delete method on a System Object more than once
Description: In certain cases, the generated C code?might contain?a call to the delete  method of a System Object even if the variable containing that object has not been assigned and the object has not been created. This?might cause a crash when the generated code is executed. The issue only affects functions?that contain?a variable that is conditionally assigned from more than one object constructor. For example, consider this MATLAB function: function y = foo(n)if n > 0    o = dsp.FFT('FFTImplementation','FFTW');else    o = dsp.FFT('FFTImplementation','FFTW');endy = step(o,([1:128]'));end In this example, the generated C code might contain two calls to dsp.FFT.delete , even though only one object is created. Here are the System objects that?might be affected by this issue:? audiointerface.audioDeviceReader   audiointerface.audioDeviceWriter   vision.FFT   vision.VideoFileReader   dsp.AudioFileReader   dsp.AudioPlayer   dsp.AudioRecorder   dsp.FFT   dsp.IFFT   imaq.VideoDevice   This issue?affects MATLAB Coder and?MATLAB?Function blocks in Simulink.

Summary: Speedgoat target computer booted in Standalone mode does not load and run real-time application
Description: When you boot the Speedgoat target computer in Standalone mode, the target computer boots, but does not load and run the real-time application.

Summary: Unexpected license error can occur if local workers in a Parallel Computing Toolbox pool are idle for longer than FlexLM license timeout period
Description: When using a parallel pool, a worker can be idle, that is, notexecuting any MATLAB code. For example, a worker can be idle when the client is not executing anyparallel language constructs such as parfor, parfeval, or spmd, or when not allworkers are busy executing parallel constructs, such as the last few iterationsof a parfor loop.  If a worker is idle for longer than the FlexLM license timeoutperiod, then the license is returned. When the worker resumes executingMATLAB code, it does not correctly reacquire the license. This condition leads to thefollowing license error: ? License checkout failed.??License Manager Error -5??Cannot find a license for Distrib_Computing_Toolbox.????Troubleshoot this issue by visiting: ??https:/ / www.mathworks.com/ support/ lme/ R2018a/ 5 ????Diagnostic Information:??Feature: Distrib_Computing_Toolbox ??License path: 27001@ah-pmasoero ??Licensing error: -5, 0.??License checkout failed.??License Manager Error -5??Cannot find a license for Distrib_Computing_Toolbox.????Troubleshoot this issue by visiting: ??https:/ / www.mathworks.com/ support/ lme/ R2018a/ 5 ????Diagnostic Information:??Feature: Distrib_Computing_Toolbox ??License path: 27001@ah-pmasoero ??Licensing error: -5, 0.??Error using distcompserialize??Unable to checkout a license for the Parallel Computing Toolbox.?
Fixed In: R2017a Update 3, R2017b Update 8, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: Unexpected error occurs when atomic subchart data mapping results in size mismatch.
Description: When you map atomic subchart data to an expression in the main chart, and the mapping results in a size mismatch, Stateflow might throw the following error: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: b:\matlab\src\cgir_xform\dom_core\optimization\smartinliner.cpp line 600: Function inlining failed. ". Please report this to MathWorks if you can cause it to recur Component:Simulink | Category:Blockdiagram error
Workaround: Change the size of atomic subchart data to match the size of the expression in the main chart.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation might ignore an output of an extrinsic function that returns embedded.fimath or embedded.numerictype
Description: Code generation might ignore an assignment from an extrinsic function to a variable if all of the following conditions are met: The extrinsic function returns an instance of embedded.fimath  or embedded.numerictype.?  The return value of the extrinsic function is assigned to a variable.? The variable was previously assigned a different value of the same class (embedded.fimath  or embedded.numerictype ).  All MATLAB code generation products (MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function block and MATLAB System block in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow) have a limitation that a variable assigned a value of class ?embedded.numerictype  or of class embedded.fimath  cannot be reassigned. An attempt to assign a different value to the variable produces a compile-time error. However, when?the right-hand side of the assignment is a call to an extrinsic function, the check is not enforced.?Instead of producing an error, the code generator ignores the assignment and the variable retains its previous value. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Compilation warning shows I/O internal error on file c-coding-rules.dmp
Description: This warning message appears during the compilation phase of a Polyspace analysis: Verifying?myfile.c (x/y)Warning: rule :zzzz from example XML not found in the mapping (XML file).???????? Skipping the annotation.*** I/O internal error in getc, n=0 on file "<pathto>\c-coding-rules.dmp" at position 0Warning: Failed to load user comments from <pathto>\c-coding-rules.dmp.|??????? Comments will not appear in the viewer and the launcher  The analysis tries to take into account all Polyspace-specific code annotations in?myfile.c ?and import them to the current results. ? If ?the file has a large number of annotations, the warning appears and some comments in the code annotations might not appear in the results. These are comments associated with a justified status.

To reproduce this bug, see the example in the attached zip file 'example_1799708.zip'.??

Summary: Setting scalar variables withconstant variability?or calling the?fmi2EnterEevntMode function afterinitialization?returns errors and crashes Simulink
Description: Setting scalar variables with constant variability?or calling the?fmi2EnterEevntMode  function after initialization?returns errors and crashes Simulink.? In the first case, the FMU Import block sets values of scalar variables with constant variability, which is contrary to the FMI specification. For more information, see Functional Mock-up Interface for Model Exchange, version 1.0.1, pp 37 and Functional Mock-up Interface for Model Exchange and Co-Simulation, version 2.0, pp 48.? FMU Importblock calls fmi2EnterEventMode  after initialization. Althoughthis is not against the FMI specification, FMUs exported from some third-party?tools return errors.?For more information, see Related FMI spec page is Functional Mock-up Interface for Model Exchange and Co-Simulation, version 2.0, pp?85.  ?
Workaround: For scalar variables with constant variability,?remove all scalar variables with constant variability from the model description XML schema.For calls to fmi2EnterEventMode, there is no workaround.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect value during simulation of Stateflow chart containing enumeration data or outputs
Description: Stateflow might use the incorrect value for enumeration data or outputs during simulation of a model that meets all of the following conditions: The enumeration is of a built-in integer type other than int32 The enumeration is set to an Exported DataScope The enumeration is used in both a Stateflow Chart and a MATLAB Function Block Variables or signals utilizing the enumeration are NOT explicitly written in all states within the Stateflow Chart  For example, see issue_model_1799390.slx  in within the attached issue_example_1799390.zip . At both time step = 0 and time step = 1 the output of the Stateflow Chart should be SomeThing.ModeB . At time step = 1 there is an incorrect output value of SomeThing.ModeA .

Summary: Comparison of two duration operators produces wrong answer
Description: Stateflow might evaluate a relational operator (for example, <, <=, >, >=, ==) incorrectly if the operands on both sides use duration() . For example, the following comparison might return true instead of false.? duration(condition1) < duration(condition1)??  Similary, duration(condition1) < duration(condition2)? ?  might return the wrong answer.
Workaround: Assign both operands to local data variables and use those in the comparison. For example,?localData1 = duration(condition1)localData2 = duration(condition2)?localData1 < localData2?

Summary: Simstruct macros related to data-type properties do not work in Rapid Accelerator mode.
Description: Prior to R2018b, user-written S-Functions that contain calls to the following simstruct macros were not compatible with Rapid Accelerator mode: ssRegisterDataType ssGetDataTypeSize ssGetNumDataTypes ssSetDataTypeZero ssGetDataTypeZero ssGetDataTypeId ssGetDataTypeName  In releases prior to R2018b, use of the macros will cause build failures or run-time errors in Rapid Accelerator mode.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when a handle to a deleted object is appended to a DOM document
Description: If a handle that refers to a deleted object is appended to a?DOM document or document part, MATLAB crashes. For example: import mlreportgen.dom.*  % Set up reportd = Document('MyReport','docx');% Create a figurefigHandle = figure;plot(1:10);% Delete figure handle by closing figureclose(figHandle);% Add figure handle to document -- CRASH OCCURS HEREappend(d,figHandle); This example shows code that causes a crash. It is not intended to show how to add a MATLAB figure to a document. To add a figure to a document, you must save the figure as an image and add the image to the report. Alternatively, you can use the Report API Figure reporter. ?

Summary: Test harness compilation and simulation generates warning: "Matching "Goto" for "From" <block> not found"
Description: The test harness rebuild operation deletes Goto and From blocks from the Input Conversion Subsystem and Output Conversion Subsystem, which produces this warning message during compilation and simulation: Matching "Goto" for "From" <block> not found

Summary: Wrong linearization results for some linear Simscape models
Description: Linearization results are incorrect for some Simscape models. The bug only occurs for linear Simscape models (those for which all equations of the model are linear), and only for a subset of these models. Specifically, the bug can only occur for linear Simscape models in which dynamic state derivatives are redundant, in the sense that their local linear expressions in terms of other variables are linearly dependent. A typical example is the internal position variable of a mechanical spring (x.der == v) when there's also a motion sensor attached across the spring (x_sensor.der == v).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing initialization code for block I/O of Function-Call Subsystem within a For Each subsystem block
Description: Incorrect code is generated under these conditions: A Function-Call Subsystem is nested inside a For Each subsystem block. The Function-Call Subsystem?contains a block I/O (for example, a Gain block) that is an output argument for the Function-Call Subsystem?function in the generated code. The block I/O has a fixed-point data type which does not have a ground value 0. For example, a data type of?fixdt(1,16,2^-6,10)  will have a ground value of -320.?  The generated code does not initialize the block I/O before the model_intialize()  or model_start()  method. As a result the output of the Function-Call Subsystem?is not initialized to the correct ground value at t=0 . This leads to inconsistent answers between simulation output and output of the executable created by compiling the generated code at time t=0 .?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant Bus may propagate incorrect variant conditions
Description: Simulink models with Variant Bus signals may propagate incorrect variant condition when all of the following conditions are true:  Individual signals, including other bus signals are bundled using Bus Creator block.  A Bus Selector block with?Output as virtual bus? parameter enabled, creates two or more different hierarchies for the input signal.  Another Bus Selector selects the same input signal from two or more different hierarchies created by the Bus Selector.   This also results in incorrect variant conditions in the generated code

Summary: Incorrect results are possible for cascading Unit Delay blocks inside Switch Case Action Subsystem block
Description: The state for a Unit Delay block may incorrectly be a local variable in the generated code for a model that meets all of these conditions:  The Unit Delay blocks are inside a Switch Case Action Subsystem block. The output of one Unit Delay block feeds into another Unit Delay block. The Unit Delay block outputs are vectors or a matrices. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Enable local block outputs  parameter is selected.
Workaround: Unzip the attachment and save the workaroundForBugReport1796701.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run this script in each MATLAB session before opening your model.?
Fixed In: R2016b Update 7, R2017a Update 3, R2017b Update 8, R2018a Update 5, R2018b, R2019a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The C++ class generated from referenced models might incorrectly initialize tunable parameters to 0
Description: The code generator?might?produce C++ code?that incorrectly initializes tunable parameters to 0 when all these conditions are met:? The model from which you are generating code is a reference model. A tunable block parameter in the model is set to Inf  or Nan? from the block's mask.  The model is configured with these parameter settings: Language  is set  C++.   Code interface packaging  is set to C++ class.   Default parameter behavior ?i s set to??Tunable.     The code generator might incorrectly initialize a tunable parameter to 0, instead of Inf ?or?Nan, ?in the initialize  member function of the C++ class.?
Workaround: If your model meets all of the conditions specified in the Description of Problem section, and you have to use?Inf?or?Nan?as a parameter value, follow the steps below to avoid the issue:??Create a variable in the MATLAB base workspace.Set the value of that variable to?Inf or Nan. Specify the variable?in the mask for the block that uses the tunable parameter.

Summary: When working with a large data dictionary hierarchy, Model Explorer?might be slow and unresponsive
Description: When you open a large?data dictionary hierarchy consisting of multiple data dictionaries in Model Explorer, working with the data dictionaries?might be?slow and unresponsive.

Summary: RapidAcceleratorParameterSets?specified in?SimulationInput?objects is silently ignored with?RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck 'off'
Description: When?RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck? is set to?'off' ?for Rapid Accelerator simulations, parameter values can be tuned using model parameter?RapidAcceleratorParameterSets . When?parameters are tuned using?RapidAcceleratorParameterSets ?specified in?S imulink.SimulationInput ?objects, the new parameter values specified in?RapidAcceleratorParameterSets ?are silently ignored and the value of the parameter that the model was compiled with will be used instead.
Workaround: Use the following workflow to run Rapid Accelerator simulations:Build the rapid accelerator target before calling?sim/parsimSet?RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck?to?off?using?setModelParameterUse?setVariable?method on?SimulationInput?object to specify new values for parameters instead of using?setModelParameter?to specify a new value for?RapidAcceleratorParameterSetsSee attached file for an example code.?

Summary: Model update error when performing indexed assignment into a sparse matrix in a MATLAB-based Simulink block
Description: Suppose that you have an indexed assignment into a sparse matrix?s  in a MATLAB-based Simulink block. For example:s(I) = v;? s(I,J) = v;  This operation can cause an error like:? Undefined function 'getConfigProp' for input arguments of type 'double'.  This bug affects?MATLAB-based Simulink blocks?such as?MATLAB Function blocks, MATLAB System blocks, and?Stateflow MATLAB charts.

Summary: Error when a link whose destination is an external reference to a ReqIF item is selected in Links View
Description: Simulink throws an error when a link whose destination is an external reference to a ReqIF item is selected in the Links View of the Requirements Editor or the Requirements Perspective. This issue can be seen when you import requirements from a ReqIF file and create a link between requirements that reference to items of the ReqIF file.
Workaround: Access link properties from command-line APIs instead.For example: linkSet = slreq.find('Type','LinkSet','Name','SystemRequirements'); srcs =? linkSet.sources;link = slreq.outLinks(srcs(1))?

Summary: FTP mput operation returns incorrect error code 257
Description: In certain environments, such as low bandwidth networks, the  mput  function returns an incorrect error.   When uploading data,  the  mput  function? returns error code 257?which is a success code and not the correct?error code.  For example, this code can return? an error code 257:   ftpobj = ftp('ftp.example.com')  mput(ftpobj,'myNewScript.m');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assignments to global variables might be incorrectly moved outside of a for-loop
Description: The code generator might incorrectly move assignments to global variables outside of a for-loop. This issue can occur when all of the following conditions are true: The generated code contains a for-loop that assigns to and reads a global variable. In the for-loop, there are at least three assignments to the global variable before it is read.  One of the assignments to the global variable is inside of an if-else construct that cannot be moved outside of the for-loop.  If the assignments to the global variable are moved outside of the for-loop, then when the global variable is read inside of the loop, the value may differ depending on the if condition. Consider this MATLAB code: function out = example_1795739(x1)coder.inline('never');global G1;  for k = 1:10??? G1 = x1 + 1;??? if(x1 > 0)??????? G1 = x1 + 10;??????? sideEffectFcn();??? end??? G1 = 2;??? sideEffectFcn();??? G1 = 3;??? out = G1 + k;end  end  The incorrectly generated code where the assignments to the global variable are moved outside of the loop might look like:  /*?* Arguments??? : double x1?* Return Type? : double?*/double example_1795739(double x1){? double out;? int k;? G1 = x1 + 1.0;? G1 = 2.0;? G1 = 3.0;? for (k = 0; k < 10; k++) {??? if (x1 > 0.0) {?????
