Summary: Expected output values in report differ from those produced by harness simulation
Description: After Simulink Design Verifier analysis, the expected output values included in the report and sldvdata differ from the output values produced by simulating the harness model. This occurs when the model satisfies both the following conditions:  1. The parameter Randomize data that do not affect the outcome is set to 'On'. 2. The model contains an input signal whose value does not influence the satisfaction of a test or proof objective.
Workaround: Use sldvruntest API to calculate the model output.

Summary: Missing VHDL library name in Xilinx? Vivado??IP core generated using IP Core Generation?workflow
Description: When you run the IP Core Generation  workflow for your Simulink model with Xilinx? Vivado? as the Synthesis tool and the VHDL library name specified as a value other than the default value work, the IP core definition file component.xml  for the generated Vivado?IP core does not contain the top-level VHDL library name. The custom library name is missing because:  For example, if ledgroup  is the library name, the generated component.xml  file must have a line containing the tag logicalName  corresponding to the ? VHDL library name. <spirit:file><spirit:name>hdl/vhdl/led_count_ip_addr_decoder.vhd</spirit:name><spirit:fileType>vhdlSource</spirit:fileType><spirit:logicalName>ledgroup</spirit:logicalName></spirit:file> The custom library name is missing because the ?logicalName? is not included in the  component.xml  file of the generated IP core .

Summary: A MATLAB System block that accesses Data Store Memory with array of bus could produce incorrect results with non-double data type signals
Description: In a Simulink model where a MATLAB System Block is accessing Data Store Memory using array of bus, the model could produce incorrect results for non-double data types.?

Summary: Simscape frequency and time equation formulation incorrectly allows non-constant mass matrix, giving wrong simulation results
Description: Simscape frequency and time equation formulation does not support non-constant mass matrix. Using non-constant mass matrix with frequency and time equation formulation selected on the Solver Configuration block can lead to wrong simulation results.
Workaround: Do not include components with non-constant coefficients of differentiated variables in models, when models are simulated with frequency and time equation formulation. A custom component with an equation M(x) .* x.der == <right-hand-side> can be re-written as x.der == <right-hand-side>/M(x) to make the mass matrix contribution constant.

Summary: Error when resaving Ground Truth Labeler session containing pixel labels
Description: While labeling pixel label data using the Ground Truth Labeler, saving a previously saved labeling session causes an error when all of the following conditions are true: The next image frame contains pixel labels The next image frame has not been navigated to (using the scrubber, playback buttons, or time edit boxes) since the session was previously saved

Summary: Setting an FMU Import block to direct debug logs to file might crash Simulink in multithreaded simulation.
Description: Setting an FMU Import block to to direct debug logs to file?and running the simulation might crash Simulink if the simulation is multithreaded. The crash log may include the following:? MATLAB: management.cpp:723: find: Assertion `find: no active context for type 'matrix::ErrorMode'? [ 5] 0x00007fe62a267798 bin/glnxa64/libmx.so+00976792 _ZN6matrix12getErrorModeEv+00000024[ 6] 0x00007fe40e1ee0ed bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_services.so+02142445?[ 7] 0x00007fe40e1ef38a bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_services.so+02147210 _ZN14CMatlabCommand7executeEbN6matrix9ErrorModeE+00000058?[ 8] 0x00007fe40e1efa95 bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_services.so+02149013 _Z17slsvFEVAL_NoThrowiPP11mxArray_tagiS1_PKcPvbN6matrix9ErrorModeEPSt10unique_ptrIS_15TMxArrayDeleterE+00000181? ?[ 14] 0x00007fe25c606f87 bin/glnxa64/libmwfmubase.so+01019783 _ZN15FMUBasePrmDescs13getFMULogFileB5cxx11EPK7SLBlocki+00000087??
Workaround: Use one of the following workarounds:Turn off file logging for the FMU import block:>> set_param(gcb, 'FMUDebugLogging', 'off')?Turn off multithreaded simulation from the command line:>> set_param(gcs, 'MultiThreadCoSim', 'off')?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when an IF or WHILE condition uses the |?operator with an empty operand?and code coverage is enabled
Description: When using the |  operator in an IF  or WHILE  condition in MATLAB code, incorrect behavior may occur when all of the following conditions are true: The left-hand operand is an empty matrix such as [].   The right-hand operand is a scalar. Code coverage is performed during the Check for Run-Time Issues  step?of the MATLAB Coder app or during fixed-point conversion. In the MATLAB Coder app, code coverage is performed when the Collect MATLAB line execution counts ?check box is selected.   In this case, the condition should evaluate to false. However, the generated MEX code evaluates the condition as true because it treats the right-hand operand as?a logical value.?
Workaround: Clear?the?Collect MATLAB line execution counts?check box or rewrite the code from:if a | b ... end?to:cond = a | b;?if ~isempty(cond) && all(cond)?...?end?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when using element-wise multiplication (.*)?that mixes?double and int64 or uint64 inputs
Description: The generated code can produce a wrong answer if your MATLAB code contains an element-wise multiplication (.*) ?that mixes double  and int64  or uint64  inputs. This issue occurs?when all of the following conditions are true:? One input is double  and the other is?either int64  or uint64.  The double  input has absolute value less than 2^-12.   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow. See also Bug Report ID 1849460
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 7, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: Unexpected vehicle motion at stopped conditions?
Description: If you configure the vehicle with the Combined Slip Wheel 2DOF  block, the Magic Formula tire force calculations at low speeds can result in unexpected vehicle motion at stopped conditions.

Summary: Write to variable missing when variable is size vector argument of reshape function call within a loop
Description: The code generator might incorrectly omit writes to a variable when all of the following conditions occur:The variable is the size vector argument of a reshape  function call. The reshape  function call exists within a loop. There are writes to every element of the variable within the same loop  When this bug occurs, writes to the variable preceding the loop might be incorrectly missing in the generated code. For an example, see the function issue_example_1793805 below.function Y = issue_example_1793805(X,initSize,finalSize)    assert(isa(X,'double'));    assert(all(size(X) == [10,10,10]))    assert(isa(initSize,'int32'));    assert(all(size(initSize) == [1,3]));    assert(isa(finalSize,'int32'));    assert(all(size(finalSize) == [1,3]));    initSize(2) = initSize(2)*initSize(3);    initSize(3) = int32(1);    Y = zeros(numel(initSize),1);    for k = 1:numel(initSize)        if(k == 1 || k == numel(initSize))            Y(k) = locProcess(int32(reshape(X,initSize)));        end         initSize(k) = finalSize(4 - k);    endendfunction out = locProcess(in)    coder.inline('never');    out = size(in,2)*size(in,3) + size(in,1) * sum(in(:));end  The generated code for this function is as follows:void issue_example_1793805(const double X[1000], int initSize[3], const int? finalSize[3], double Y[3]){? int i;? emxArray_int32_T *b_X;? int i0;? int b_initSize[3];? int loop_ub;? double d0;? int i1;? (void)initSize;? for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {??? Y[i] = 0.0;? }? emxInit_int32_T(&b_X, 3);? for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {  ? ?? [SNIP]   The writes corresponding to the statements 'initSize(2) = initSize(2)*initSize(3)'  and 'initSize(3) = int32(1);'? are not in the generated code. These writes are necessary for the behavior of the generated code to match MATLAB. Instead the code generator should produce this code. Note the assignments 'initSize[1] = (int)i0;' and ' initSize[2] = 1;'. void issue_example_1793805(const double X[1000], int initSize[3], const int? finalSize[3], double Y[3]){? long i0;? int i;? emxArray_int32_T *b_X;? int i1;? int loop_ub;? double d0;? int i2;? i0 = (long)initSize[1] * initSize[2];? if (i0 > 2147483647L) {??? i0 = 2147483647L;? } else {??? if (i0 < -2147483648L) {????? i0 = -2147483648L;??? }? }? initSize[1] = (int)i0;? initSize[2] = 1;? for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {??? Y[i] = 0.0;? }? emxInit_int32_T(&b_X, 3); ? for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {  ? [SNIP]    This issue can happen for these code generation workflows:MATLAB Coder only workflow Simulink code generation of a model containing a MATLAB Function Block, Matlab System Block, or Stateflow Chart
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 7, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: Code generation error with LCT generated S-Function when convertNDarrayToRowMajor (or?convert2DMatrixToRowMajor)?is true
Description: When you configure the Legacy Code Tool to convert an ND array to row major, an error similar to the following is thrown during code generation: Row Major S-Function 'sfun_2darray' does not support Column Major code generation.??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible run-time crash for element-wise operators with sparse matrix inputs
Description: When you generate code for the following element-wise operations with sparse matrix inputs, the generated code might crash: +  -  .*  &  |  <  >  ~=  mod?   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: If possible, convert the sparse operands to full matrices?before the operation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: J1939 Transmit block with floating-point input might lose output precision
Description: If you use the J1939 Transmit block with a floating-point type input, there can occasionally be a loss of precision in the output value during simulation of the model.

Summary: Models using variable-step solvers and adaptive zero-crossing algorithm with multiple "chattering" zero-crossing signals might run slower
Description: In R2018a, Simulink uses different heuristics in the adaptive zero-crossing algorithm for detecting and handling the Zeno condition ("chattering" zero-crossing signals). For most models, this has no impact on the simulation behavior. However, in select cases, when the model has many zero-crossing signals that are "chattering" simultaneously, this issue might manifest as follows: Simulation of these models might slow down because a higher number of zero crossings are detected. In the rarest of cases, if the slowdown is severe, the simulation might seem to have stopped making progress.? Warning and/or error messages indicating the Zeno condition might not appear in such cases.??

Summary: DO Qualification Kit for Simulink Test might report failed qualification status for certain execution paths
Description: If the execution path of the DO Qualification Kit contains dots or the string 'MATLAB' (not case sensitive), the parser might not fully remove the path from the auto-comparison of Simulink Test reports. This can affect comparison and a failed qualification status can be reported.

Summary: Static Code Metrics Report?not generated for a model with custom code specified using a relative path
Description: When a model uses custom code that is specified by using a relative path, the Static Code Metrics Report  might not be generated.
Workaround: Specify custom code by using an absolute path.

Summary: Display of the psia and psig units is incorrect in the Simulink Editor
Description: Simulink displays Pa  instead of the correct unit in the Simulink Editor when these conditions are true: The model Display  > Signals & Ports  > Port Units  option is enabled. The Unit  parameter contains psia  or psig .

Summary: Source control sometimes causes MATLAB to crash
Description: When working with files under source control in the MATLAB?Current Folder browser,?MATLAB might crash when you view or commit a change.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong initial value or data copy for nested structure arrays
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible when using nested structure arrays in your MATLAB code. The incorrect code is missing a call to memset  or memcpy , resulting in wrong initial values. The issue is more likely to?occur when you are either initializing to the same value or doing a data copy between similar structures.For example, in the below MATLAB function memset_example_1791965 , the function input is a nested structure with fields f1  and f2  which are array of structures as follows: S = struct('f1',repmat(struct('nested1',1,'nested2',2),[100,1]),... ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? 'f2',repmat(struct('nested1',3,'nested2',4),[100,1]));  When trying to initialize these fields (f1  and f2 ), the generated code initializes only one field?(f1 )?using memset  and skips the other field, leading to an answer mismatch between simulation and the generated code. For example, for this MATLAB code: function [S] = memset_example_1791965(S)  for k = 1:100  ? ? S.f1(k).nested1 = 0;  ? ? S.f1(k).nested2 = 0;  ? ? S.f2(k).nested1 = 0;  ? ? S.f2(k).nested2 = 0;  end  end  Here is the incorrect generated C code:? void memset_example_1791965(struct0_T *S)  {  ? memset(&S->f1[0], 0, 100U * sizeof(struct1_T));  }  For a memcpy example in which the bug occurs, see ebr_memcpy_example_1791965.m  and codegen_memcpy_example.m  in the attached zip file Example_1791965.zip .
Workaround: Set?Use memcpy for vector assignment to Off, or, set the?Memcpy threshold (bytes)?parameter to a very high value such as int32(intmax).

Summary: Subsystems open off center when using a high-DPI display in Windows
Description: On Windows computers with a high-DPI display, when you open a subsystem, the diagram might be centered incorrectly and the scrollbar appears.

Summary: The simulation of Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block outputs wrong result for some 1-D tables
Description: The simulation?of Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block outputs wrong?results when all of these conditions are met: Table is from input port.  Table is a 1-D vector with 2-D dimension (for example, N  x 1 or 1 x N ), but is not interpreted as a 1-D vector. The index port input value is greater than the table dimension size. The Inputs select this object from table  parameter of the block is set as Element .  In R2013b and earlier releases, both simulation and code generation output wrong results?for these?conditions.? Starting in?R2014a, the code generation output is correct for these conditions, but the simulation output is wrong.
Workaround: If the table is input from a Constant block,?select the Interpret vector parameter as 1-D check box for the?Constant block.Otherwise:Insert a Reshape block, with the Output dimensionality parameter set to?1-D array,?between the Table input port (T)?of the Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block and the?block that feeds the table port T.For an example of this workaround, see the attached model.
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 7, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong results for model containing a root-level Outport block with Ensure outport is virtual parameter selected
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for a model that meets all of these  conditions: The model contains a root-level Outport block. On the?root-level?outport block parameters dialog box, the Ensure outport is virtual  parameter is selected. The model contains at least one other signal with the same signal attributes?(e.g., datatype)?as that of the signal driving the root-level Outport block.  The block that drives the root-level Outport block drives it directly (not from within a subsystem)?or is within an unconditionally executed subsystem?with the Function packaging  parameter  set to Auto or ?Inlined.   Reuse local block outputs ?configuration parameter is enabled.
Workaround: Clear the Ensure outport is virtual parameter.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may report incorrect number of objectives in the Results Summary window
Description: The Results Summary  window may display incorrect count for decided objectives when Configuration Parameters > Design Verifier > Advanced parameters > Run additional analysis to reduce instances of rational approximation  is selected.  In such a case, Property Proving and Design Error Detection analysis may report less objective count than actual for "Valid" or "Falsified" status in the Results Summary  window. Similarly, Test Generation analysis may report less objective count for "Satisfied" or "Unsatisfiable" status.

Summary: Incorrect red overflow on bitwise NOT operator
Description: Polyspace incorrectly detects a red?Overflow  (OVFL) check on casts to unsigned char  after the bitwise NOT operator (~) is applied.  For instance, Polyspace detects an incorrect red?OVFL check on the following cast to unsigned char  when you use these options: -compiler tasking  -target ?tricore  -scalar-overflows-checks signed-and-unsigned   The following code demonstrates the issue: {    unsigned char ret = 0U;    const unsigned char CSTR = 1U;    if (value == (unsigned char)(~CSTR)) {      ret = 1U;    } else {      ret = 0U;   }   return ret;}

Summary: Incorrect red Non-Terminating Call on a function with cross recursive calls
Description: Polyspace incorrectly displays a red Non-Terminating Call  (NTC) check on a function call, when the code being verified involves a chain of complex cross recursion calls. For instance, when foo()  calls bar(),  and then bar()  calls foo() . If the red check persists after upgrading, contact Technical Support and, if possible, provide the offending code and the corresponding verification log.

Summary: Assertion during HDL code generation with non-virtual buses and Bus Assignment block
Description: You will get an assertion:?Assertion failed: b:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\dom_pir_core\bustransforms.cpp:98:t && t->isRecordType()? ?during HDL Code Generation from your model?if the following conditions are true: You are assigning an element to a non-virtual block The name of one or more of the elements is alphanumeric characters appended to an already existing element name, e.g. a and a1 or sub and sub_a etc.??
Workaround: You can try one of the following:Convert the bus to a virtual busUse bus element names which are not subsets of other elements e.g. use a1 and a2 or subs and sub_1

Summary: MATLAB may crash in the presence of for-loops under some circumstances while generating GPU code
Description: MATLAB may crash while generating GPU code in the presence of some variable bound for-loops (either user-written or implicit functions) , containing the text "GpuLoopBoundsShrinkImpl::expressionFolding"? in the stack trace.? ?The stack trace may look like this: [? 5] 0x000000000f3cbe40 EMC::assertion_func at b:\matlab\src\emlcoder\emccoder\context.cpp:56 (in matlab\bin\win64\emlcoder.dll)  [? 6] 0x0000000005d61e5e CG::Expr::swap at b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\expr\expr.cpp:62 (in matlab\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll)?  [? 7] 0x0000000010578c5e `anonymous namespace'::GpuLoopBoundsShrinkImpl::expressionFolding at b:\matlab\src\cgir_gpu\xform\gpuloopboundsshrink.cpp:233 (in matlab\bin\win64\cgir_gpu.dll)?  [? 8] 0x0000000010578e9e `anonymous namespace'::GpuLoopBoundsShrinkImpl::exprsFolding at b:\matlab\src\cgir_gpu\xform\gpuloopboundsshrink.cpp:149 (in matlab\bin\win64\cgir_gpu.dll)?  [? 9] 0x000000001057985c CG::transform::GpuLoopBoundsShrink::processFcn at b:\matlab\src\cgir_gpu\xform\gpuloopboundsshrink.cpp:333 (in matlab\bin\win64\cgir_gpu.dll)?

Summary: hdl.RAM System object cannot break algebraic loops when used in MATLAB System block
Description: hdl.RAM?System Object?cannot break algebraic loops when used in a MATLAB System block which is inside a feedback loop.? You may get an error message: ?Algebraic loop error with "block_name"  hdl.RAM System object operates in direct feedthrough mode.
Workaround: Use delay blocks in the feedback loop to break algebraic loop that includes a MATLAB System block when?using hdl.RAM System Object.

Summary: Incorrect size of structures with Tasking compiler and Tricore target
Description: The Tasking compiler uses a specific packing for structures: halfword (2 bytes) packing unless the structure uses doubles, circular buffers, __laccum, or pointers.  Polyspace does not emulate this packing correctly. For example: struct {char c1, c2, c3; } => Tasking: 4 bytes; Polyspace: 3 bytes struct {char c1, c2, c3, c4, c5;}  => Tasking: 6 bytes; Polyspace: 5 bytes This incorrect size computation can lead to wrong checks, especially out of bounds array index and illegally dereferenced pointer.?

Summary: Simulation of SIL or PIL block containing Enabled Subsystem or Atomic Subsystem produces error?when execution-time profiling is enabled
Description: When you run a SIL or PIL block simulation with execution-time profiling enabled, you see an error at the end of the simulation if the SIL or PIL block is created from a subsystem that contains: An Enabled Subsystem block. The error message is: Insufficient number of outputs from right hand side of equal sign to satisfy assignment   Atomic Subsystem blocks with TreatAsAtomicUnit  set to 'on'  and RTWSystemCode  set to 'Reusable function' . The error message is: Undefined function 'getCallSiteSids' for input arguments of type  'double'
Workaround: Convert the subsystem to a referenced model and run a Model block SIL or PIL simulation. For more information, see:Convert a Subsystem to a Referenced ModelConfigure a Model Block for a SIL or PIL Simulation

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Same Localizable storage class may be incorrectly applied to two different model input ports
Description: If you specify the same signal on two different root input ports and the signal has?a Localizable  storage class, the generated code may contain the same variable name for both inputs making it impossible for the root input ports to have different values. ?If you specify other custom storage classes on multiple input ports, you get an error which is the correct behavior. As another aspect of this bug, the code generator may apply a?signal specification that is downstream of?an input port?to the input port?when all blocks?in between?are virtual or are removed by block reduction. For example, if the input port connects to a Gain block, which has a value of 1  and its output has a signal specification, that signal specification may end up applying to the input port. For storage classes other than Localizable , if another?input port?has the same signal name, you get an error which is the correct behavior. For the?Localizable  storage class, the conflict may not be detected.

Summary: Days selection in borrow UI cannot be changed
Description: If you are using a concurrent, perpetual license and you try to borrowa product using the borrow UI, you cannot move the days selectionpast the default value of 1 day.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: for?loops with at least two dependent array accesses and more than one level of nesting might be incorrect in generated code
Description: The code generator contains an optimization that identifies invariant nested?for ?loops?and moves the inner?for ?loop before the outer?for ?loop as long as?the move preserves program semantics.??The code generator might incorrectly apply this optimization to nested?for ?loops which?meet the?following two conditions:? The?for ?loops are such that the index of an array being written to in the current iteration is used in a future iteration. The read access?occurs after the write access.  For example, please see the attached model issue_1789011 . The MATLAB Function block in this model example_1789011  defines the function ?errorFcn , in which variable?OUT ?contains different values when the?for ?loop indexed with?index_j ?is moved before the?for ?loop indexed with?index_i . function [OUT, P] = errorFcn(IN, OUT, P)??? for index_i = 1 : 7??????? for index_j = 2 : 15??????????? IN(index_j - 1) = 3;??????????? OUT(index_j) = IN(index_j)??????? end??????? P(index_i) = P(index_i) + 1;??? endend    The incorrectly optimized generated C code is as follows: /* Function for MATLAB Function: '<Root>/example_1789011' */static void issue_1789011_errorFcn(real_T IN[100], real_T OUT[100], real_T P[100]){  ? int32_T index_j;? for (index_j = 0; index_j < 14; index_j++) {??? IN[index_j] = 3.0;??? OUT[index_j + 1] = IN[index_j + 1];? }? for (index_j = 0; index_j < 7; index_j++) {??? P[index_j]++;? }}

Summary: UDP Receive block error: Too many input arguments
Description: When using a UDP Receive block configured to "Get Latest Data" the model might generate the following error at run time: Too many input arguments

Summary: Signal strength?calculations?are incorrect for antenna sites with elevation angle
Description: If a transmitter or receiver site has a non-zero elevation angle defined in the AntennaAngle  property, then subsequent path loss and signal strength calculations will be incorrect. An example of a transmitter site with an elevation angle of -15 degrees?is: tx = txsite('AntennaAngle',[0; -15])  The amount of?error?increases as the magnitude of the elevation angle increases. This issue affects the results of the following?antenna site functions: sigstrength , link , coverage , and sinr .
Workaround: You can apply an elevation angle? by setting a tilt on the antenna object using its Tilt and TiltAxis properties.?Set the TiltAxis to 'y' and the Tilt to the opposite of the elevation angle. For example, to apply a -15 degree elevation angle to a dipole antenna, execute:d = dipole;d.TiltAxis = 'y';d.Tilt = 15;?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer when the output of a function call is assigned to a variable that is passed as input
Description: In rare situations, the generated code may produce a wrong answer when all of the following conditions are true: ?1. The MATLAB code contains a function of the form function A = bar(B)  that writes to indices of A that are subsequently read from B. ?2. The MATLAB code contains a function call of the form A = bar(...) where '...' is some  expression that involves  A.  ?For example: function A = foo(A)   A = bar( reshape(A(1:1:6, 1:1:19),[6,19]) + reshape(?A(5:1:10, 1:1:19),[6,19]) );endfunction A = bar(B)   A(2:21) = B(1:20);end See 'example_1787643.m' in the attached zip file 'Example_1787643.zip'.

Summary: Maneuver reference applications that use the PassVeh7DOF?bicycle model variant incorrectly calculate tire pressure?
Description: Double Lane Change, Increasing Steer, and Swept SineSteer reference applications configured with PassVeh7DOF  bicycle model variant incorrectlycalculate the tire pressure.? Reference applications that use the?PassVeh7DOF ?dual model variant are not affected.
Workaround: Openthe reference application.Navigateto the Passenger Vehicle > Body, Suspension, Wheels > PassVeh7DOF > LongitudinalWheels 4DOF ¨C Single track (bicycle) > Tires subsystemDisconnectinput port Press(7) from the Longitudinal Wheel block?Gnd?input ports.Disconnectinput port Gnd(10) from the Longitudinal Wheel block TirePrs input ports.Connectinput port?Press(7)?to the Longitudinal Wheel block TirePrs input ports.Connectinput port Gnd(10) to the Longitudinal Wheel block Gnd?input ports.Forthe Double Lane Change reference application, use this command to change the PredictiveDriver block parameter Error filter time constant, tauerr.set_param('DLCReferenceApplication/PredictiveDriver/Linear Predictive Driver','tauerr','0.1')?For the Swept Sine referenceapplication, use this command to change the Longitudinal Driver block parameterError filter time constant, tauerr.set_param('SSSReferenceApplication/LongitudinalDriver/Longitudinal Driver','tauerr','0.1')?

Summary: Drive Cycle Source block does not retain the Drive cycle source setting after you save and reopen the model
Description: In the Drive Cycle Source block, after you Install additional drive cycles ?from the support package, the block does not retain the?Drive cycle source ?setting. Instead, the block incorrectly reverts to the default FTP75  drive cycle after you save and reopen the model.?
Workaround: After you install the Powertrain Blockset Drive Cycle Data support package, start MATLAB as an administrator and execute these commands:libDir = which('autolibshared.slx');fileattrib(libDir,'+w'); open_system('autolibshared')save_system('autolibshared')close_system('autolibshared')fileattrib(libDir,'-w');clear libDirThere are no release restrictions or limitations associated with the workaround.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch?in behavior with?MATLAB for conversion from NaN to integer type
Description: MATLAB Coder might produce floating point to integer conversion code that does not match the behavior of MATLAB. This mismatch happens only?when the conversion's floating point input has?a runtime value of NaN .? For example, given this MATLAB function:?? function out = reproEx(out)  assert(isscalar(out));  assert(isa(out,'int32'));  temp = double(out < 0);  out = int32(rem(13,temp));end  MATLAB Coder generates the following incorrect code:  void reproEx(int32_T *out){  *out = (int32_T)muDoubleScalarRem(13.0, *out < 0);} The function muDoubleScalarRem  is produced by the code generator to implement the remainder computation. When the second argument to this function has a value of  0.0,? muDoubleScalarRem  returns a value of NaN . Under these conditions, the generated C function produces an output that does not match MATLAB because it uses a C-style cast to convert from floating point NaN  to integer. ? I n C/C++, a cast of a floating point NaN  to an integer type is undefined. In MATLAB, a conversion of NaN  to any integer type is well-defined: it has the value 0.?  The expected C code would be similar to:   void reproEx(int32_T *out){  real_T d0;  d0 = muDoubleScalarRem(13.0, *out < 0);  if (d0 < 2.147483648E+9) {    *out = (int32_T)d0;  } else {    *out = 0;  }} This function?assigns  0  to  *out  when ? muDoubleScalarRem  returns  NaN .  This?issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function?blocks and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, MATLAB functions in?Stateflow, and Fixed-Point Designer code.

Summary: Check "mathworks.maab.himl_0003" does not report on cyclomatic complexity for Japanese MATLAB versions
Description: The check "mathworks.maab.himl_0003" does not report values for cyclomatic complexity of the code in a MATLAB Function block on a Japanese MATLAB versions.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect value for element of multidimensional matrix that results from concatenation along the second dimension
Description: The code generator might incorrectly represent the value of a matrix under the following conditions: The matrix has > 2 dimensions and less than 11 elements. The matrix is the result of concatenating multiple matrices along the 2nd dimension.  For example, the function issue_example_1787271_1  contains this code.  function out1 = issue_example_1787271_1(in1,in2)    assert(isa(in1,'uint32'));    assert(all(size(in1) == [2,2,2]));        assert(isa(in2,'uint32'))    assert(all(size(in2) == [2,4,15]));    in1 = [-(in2( 1:1:2, 3, 6:8:14 )), (in2( 1:1:2, 3, 2:4:6 )) - (in1( 1:1:2, 1, 1:1:2 ))];    out1 = in1( 1, 2, 1 );end The generated code incorrectly represents?in1(1,2,1) ?as 0 .? ? uint32_T issue_example_1787271_1(const emlrtStack *sp, const uint32_T in1[8],  const uint32_T in2[120]){  (void)sp;  (void)in1;  (void)in2;  return 0U;} The value of??in1(1,2,1)  depends on the values of in1  and in2  at runtime. Therefore, to determine the value of element?in1(1,2,1),? the generated code should contain the necessary runtime computations as shown below. uint32_T issue_example_1787271_1(const emlrtStack *sp, uint32_T in1[8], const  uint32_T in2[120]){  [SNIP]  for (i0 = 0; i0 < 2; i0++) {    for (i1 = 0; i1 < 2; i1++) {      for (i2 = 0; i2 < 2; i2++) {        in1[(i2 + (i1 << 1)) + (i0 << 2)] = b_in2[(i2 + (i1 << 1)) + (i0 << 2)];      }    }  }  return in1[2];} As another example, the?issue_example_1787271_2  function contains this code:  function out1 = issue_example_1787271_2(in1,in2)    assert(isa(in1,'uint32'));    assert(all(size(in1) == [2,2,2]));        assert(isa(in2,'uint32'))    assert(all(size(in2) == [2,4,15]));        in1 = [-(in2( 1:1:2, 3:2:3, 6:8:14 )), (in2( 1:1:2, 3:2:3, 2:4:6 )) - (in1( 1:1:2, 1:2:1, 1:1:2 ))];    out1 = -10;    if(in1( 1, 2, 1 ))        out1 = 10;    endend In the generated code,?in1(1,2,1) ?has an incorrect value of 0. As a result, an internal optimization incorrectly removes the if  statement from the generated code as shown below. real_T issue_example_1787271_2(const emlrtStack *sp, const uint32_T in1[8],  const uint32_T in2[120]){  (void)sp;  (void)in1;  (void)in2;  return -10.0;} The value of??in1(1,2,1)  depends on the values of in1  and in2  at runtime.?Therefore, to determine the value of element in1(1,2,1), the generated code should contain the necessary runtime computations and the if  statement as shown below. real_T issue_example_1787271_2(const emlrtStack *sp, uint32_T in1[8], const  uint32_T in2[120]){[SNIP]  for (i0 = 0; i0 < 2; i0++) {    for (i1 = 0; i1 < 2; i1++) {      for (i2 = 0; i2 < 2; i2++) {        in1[(i2 + (i1 << 1)) + (i0 << 2)] = b_in2[(i2 + (i1 << 1)) + (i0 << 2)];      }    }  }  out1 = -10.0;  if (in1[2] != 0U) {    out1 = 10.0;  }  return out1;

Summary: Model Advisor check "Check transition orientations in flow charts"?for MAAB guideline db_0132?does not flag transition actions used in flow charts
Description: MAAB guideline db_0132 states "Transition actions are not used in flow charts. Transition actions are only valid when used in transitions between states in a state machine, otherwise they are not activated because of the inherent dependency on a valid state to state transition to activate.". However, corresponding Model Advisor check "Check  transition orientations in flow charts"(check ID mathworks.maab.db_0132 )  does not flag transition actions?that are used in flow charts. ?

Summary: SI and CI dynamometer?reference application Generate Mapped Engine from Spreadsheet produces incorrect torque lookup tables?
Description: The SI andCI dynamometer?reference application?GenerateMapped Engine from Spreadsheet? feature produces incorrect torquelookup tables. The?Generate Mapped Enginefrom Spreadsheet ?feature does not use the imported data to set thetorque and speed limits. Instead, the reference application incorrectly usesthe default torque and speed data.??

Summary: Model reference blocks show model arguments that are no longer in the model workspace?
Description: In a referenced model, the model reference block might display model arguments that are no longer in the model workspace. For example:? MATLAB code defines paramA  and paramB  as arguments. The model reference block dialog for the top model displays paramA  and paramB . The MATLAB code is changed to define paramC  and paramD  as arguments.?? The model workspace is reinitialized. The model reference block dialog for the top model displays paramC  and paramD , but also still displays?paramA  and paramB .
Workaround: In the referenced model, after change the MATLAB code that model workspace refers to and reinitialize the model workspace, use command line API set_param(model, 'ParameterArgumentNames', '...') to mark model workspace parameters as arguments instead of using UI.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answers can occur when a Model block referencing a multi-instance export-function model is in SIL mode
Description: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) mode simulation of a Model block?can result in wrong answers if all of the following conditions are true: The Model block references an export-function model whose?Total number of instances allowed per top model ?parameter is set to ?Multiple  or whose   Code interface packaging    parameter is set to  Reusable function . The Model block has a model initialize port that receives an initialize function call. The referenced model contains at least one block whose initial condition is zero.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink assumes that an in-place operation with a MATLAB Function Block does not change the size of a variable-size signal
Description: If an in-place optimization that operates on a variable-size Simulink signal changes the size of the signal, the signal dimensions are incorrect. You specify such an in-place operation as?y=foo(y)  at the MATLAB Function block. The resulting generated code does not include the necessary code to update variable-size signal.
Workaround: Do not specify the same variable-size signal name on the input and output of a MATLAB Function block.

Summary: Model Advisor Check "Check folder names" (ID:mathworks.maab.ar_0002) does not analyze individual folders in model file path
Description: The Check currently looks at the model file path as one complete string (for example, "C:\My Projects\new_project_") and flags the entire string for violations.  The Check should look at individual directory names in the path (such as "My Projects", "new_project_") and flag each of them.

Summary: When a local pool of Parallel Computing Toolbox workers uses Simulink logging, the logged data can be incomplete
Description: When a local pool of Parallel Computing Toolbox workers is used to run simulations that use Simulink logging, the logged data can contain incomplete results for one or more workers. The logged data can omit data for one or more signals from a worker or all the data from a worker. One or more crashed workers is a common cause of missing data.
Workaround: To ensure complete results in your logged data, use?manual?parallel worker support mode and send results to the client after the simulations complete. For example:% Configure PCT support for manual mode before running any simulations.Simulink.sdi.enablePCTSupport('manual')% Use spmd to load the model?you want to simulate.spmd? ? load_system('model');end% Start simulations on local workers.Do not send data from the workers to the client in the parfor loop.parpool('local',5)parfor index = 1:100? ? sim('model');end% After the parfor simulations finish, use Simulink.sdi.sendWorkerRunToClient to access logged simulation data.spmd? ? runIDs = Simulink.sdi.getAllRunIDs;? ? for idx = 1:length(runIDs)? ? ? ? Simulink.sdi.sendWorkerRunToClient(runIDs(idx))? ? endend

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multi-instance export-function referenced models can produce wrong answers when?a parent model is in accelerator simulation mode with fast restart enabled
Description: Accelerator simulation results?can be incorrect for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a referenced export-function model whose Total number of instances allowed per top model   parameter is set to Multiple . The model is configured to simulate in the accelerator mode with fast restart enabled. You run multiple?accelerator simulations.

Summary: Error when resolving a configuration reference in the base workspace through a data dictionary with base workspace access
Description: When you select?Allow access to base workspace  for a data dictionary, a model that uses this dictionary is unable to resolve a configuration reference in the base workspace during update diagram and reports this error: The configuration set 'Reference' referred to by the active configuration reference 'Configuration' was out of date. The configuration set has been updated,  so model compilation was stopped to avoid inconsistencies. Restart the operation from the beginning.

Summary: Shared header file does not include necessary?Simulink.AliasType?header files
Description: During model reference build, the header file generated for?Simulink.AliasType ?object or?Simulink.Bus ?object might not include all the necessary header files, leading to compilation errors. For example, an exported bus type generates a header file myBus.h. ?The bus object depends on?Simulink.AliasType ?object A ?which is defined in A.h  and?Simulink.AliasType ?object B ?which is defined in B.h. ?The header file?myBus.h ?includes the header file A.h  but not the header file?B.h.?  This bug might also occur when two Simulink.AliasType  objects satisfy all these conditions: Exported to the same header file. Referenced by different models in the model hierarchy.? Both specify a?Simulink.AliasType ?object as their base type.
Workaround: If you consistently see this issue during model reference build, kindly contact the MathWorks Technical support team for assistance.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a For Iterator Subsystem block
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model containing a For Iterator Subsystem block. Inside this subsystem, a?For Iterator block connects to a Unit Delay block. In the For Iterator Block Parameters dialog box, the?Show iteration variable ?is selected.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, set the Optimize Global Data Access parameter to None or at the MATLAB command prompt enter this command:set_param(bdroot, 'GlobalVariableUsage', 'none')

Summary: Syntax error when generating code from CAN Pack block with certain offset and factor values
Description: When generating code from a CAN Pack block, if any signal in the packed message has an offset equal to 0 and a factor not equal to 1, then the generated code might fail to compile due to a missing close parenthesis.

Summary: Incorrect shear strain values in the structural mechanics workflow
Description: The following values?are incorrect: shear strain values in StaticStructuralResults  shear strain values returned by evaluateStrain? for TransientStructuralResults  interpolated shear strains values returned by?interpolateStrain  for StaticStructuralResults  or TransientStructuralResults

Summary: polyspace-configure?does not find all header files when compiler is used in Cygwin environment
Description: An options file created with?polyspace-configure ?does not contain paths to some compiler headers. You see this issue when running?polyspace-configure ?on a build system that uses a Cygwin environment. As a result, running Polyspace Bug Finder with the options file causes compilation errors. The issue happens when the cygwin1.dll  binary is not located in the same folder as the compiler binary.?

Summary: IEC Certification Kit for Simulink Test might report failed validation status for certain execution paths
Description: If the execution path of the IEC Certification Kit contains dots or the string 'MATLAB' (not case sensitive), the parser might not fully remove the path from the auto-comparison of Simulink Test reports. This can affect?comparison and a failed validation status can be reported.

Summary: Inconsistent name between variable definition and declaration for bus objects with ground value
Description: In the generated code, there can be a mismatch between the names of the external ground variable declaration and ground variable definition under these conditions: Model has root inports that have bus data type. Bus variable length exceeds the value specified in the configuration parameter Maximum identifier length .  For example,?you could get the following code with the bug: /* External data declarations for dependent source files */extern const BusObject1 modelWithVeryLongName_rtZBusObject1;/* BusObject1 ground */  /* External outputs (root outports fed by signals with default storage) */ExtY_modelWithVeryLongName_T modelWithVeryLongName_Y;const BusObject1 modelWithVeryLongName_rtZBusObj = { 0.0,/* a */  0.0 /* a1 */  };   In the above code, the ground variable declaration contains a 1 appended to modelWithVeryLongName_rtZBusObject  causing it to not match the variable definition. With the bug fix, the variable name in the declaration matches the name in the definition.

Summary: Folder access error during PDF report generation
Description: PDF report generation randomly fails with a folder access error. This generally occurs when another process, such as Microsoft OneDrive or a virus scanner, attempts to access the intermediate files that are used to generate a PDF report. The error message is similar to:? Error closing document package: Could not commit changes: Could not rename the temporary package to final location: Permission denied

Summary: cd command fails to change to UNC network directory containing non-ASCII characters
Description: On Windows, MATLAB fails to execute the cd  command to UNC network paths containing non-ASCII characters. For example,?? >> cd \\192.168.0.1\??? Error using cd  Cannot CD to \\192.168.0.1\??? (Failed to change current directory).   >> cd \\servername\??? Error using cd  Cannot CD to \\servername\??? (Failed to change current directory).
Workaround: Map the UNC network path directory containing non-ASCII characters to a drive letter and cd to the drive letter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Interpolation using Prelookup block extracts wrong elements when selection port (sm) has negative values
Description: Incorrect code is generated from the Interpolation using Prelookup block under these conditions:  The Specification  parameter is set to Lookup table object  The Lookup table object?has Breakpoints Specification  set to?'Reference'  Each Simulink.Breakpoint  object has the Support tunable size  check box selected The Number of sub-table selection dimensions ?is greater than 0 Input to the selection port is a negative value   These conditions cause?incorrect elements to be output?from the table data?of the Simulink.LookupTable  object.
Workaround: Between the input source and the selection port (sm) of the Interpolation Using Prelookup block, add a Data Type Conversion block, whose Output data type is unsigned int,? with the following settings:Saturate on integer overflow check box is selectedOutput Minimum is set?to 0Output data type is set to the corresponding unsigned int:If input is?int8 - output?uint8If input is?int16 - output?uint16If input is?int32 - output?uint32

Summary: Requirement annotation resizing in Stateflow subchart crashes MATLAB
Description: MATLAB errors or crashes when the following conditions occur: The model is in the Requirements perspective. Requirement annotation(s) are displayed in a Stateflow subchart. The requirement annotation is resized using the mouse.  If you are using R2017b, MATLAB crashes.?? In R2018a, the resize operation does not work and the following error is displayed in the MATLAB command window: Assertion failed: Cannot access NULL ref. Use isValid() before calling methods atb:\ matlab\ src\ glue2_datamodel\ export\ include\ glue2_datamodel\ glue2\ diagramelement_h.hpp:93:

Summary: Simulink dependency analysis does not return results for files with a corresponding SLMX file that is not on the MATLAB path
Description: Simulink dependency analysis will not return any results for a file with a corresponding Simulink Requirements link set (SLMX) file that is not on the MATLAB path. The Simulink Project dependency graph will not display any dependencies for the file. This occurs for any file type with a corresponding SLMX file, such as M, MLX, SLDD, MDL, and SLX files.
Workaround: Add the folder containing the SLMX file to the MATLAB path and rerun the dependency analysis.

Summary: During Design Error Detection and Property Proving analysis incremental results are not updated in Results Inspector window and model highlighting
Description: During analysis, Simulink Design Verifier updates incremental results on the model by highlighting model blocks for which the objective status changes. The change in the results is also updated in the Results Summary and Results Inspector window. For Design Error Detection and Property Proving analysis these incremental results are updated in the Results Summary window but the changes are not updated in the Results Inspector window and model highlighting.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: ASCII to String block outputs incorrect string when input comes from Mux block
Description: The ASCII to String block accepts a uint8 vector and treats each vector element as the ASCII value of a character. It then outputs a string that consists of those characters.?? When the input vector comes from a Mux block, the ASCII to String output characters?are incorrect.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, insert a Signal Conversion block between the Mux block output and the ASCII to String block input.

Summary: Creating a Test Manager report with verify signal can cause error
Description: Creating a Test Manager report can cause an error. The error occurs when there is one data point to plot for a verify signal. Test Manager reports this error: The length of X must match the number of rows of Y.

Summary: Incorrectly reported run-time error for coding pattern involving persistent loop induction variable and coder.unroll
Description: The code generator might produce code that incorrectly reports a run-time error during execution. This issue can happen for a MATLAB function containing a loop with A persistent induction variable The use of coder.unroll ?  For example, consider the following function.?? function out = reproEx(IA5)assert(all(size(IA5) == [50,50]));assert(isa(IA5,'double'));persistent k2for k2 = 1:23    for k3 = 1:1    endendfor k1 = 1:7    for k2 = 1:1    endendpersistent P6for k1 = 4:5    for k2 = coder.unroll(11:30)        P6 = IA5( k2 - 7, 1 );    endendout = P6;end A MEX?function generated for this function should run to completion when called with a real, double 50 by 50 input argument. Instead, a run-time error is reported related to the statement within the loop??for k2 = coder.unroll(11:30). ? ? ? ? ? ? >> reproEx_mex(zeros(50,50));?  Index exceeds array dimensions. ?Index value -6 exceeds valid range [1-50] of array IA5.  Error in reproEx (line 16)  ? ? ? ? ? ? P6 = IA5( k2 - 7, 1 );?

Summary: Building a model hierarchy that includes a protected model results in a missing file error
Description: If your build process involves using the RTW.BuildInfo  object from a protected model to: Obtain the list of files. Copy the files to another location. Compile code.  You might see an error from your compiler indicating that a file whose name begins with '__cf_ ' is missing similar to the following: Error executing command "touch -c / home/ pi/ slprj/ ert/ QGroundControlInterface/ *.*;make??-f QGroundControlInterface.mk all -C / home/ pi/ slprj/ ert/ QGroundControlInterface". Details:?STDERR: modelName.c:1:42: fatal error: __cf_modelName.h: No such file or directory ?

Summary: Customized edit-time checks might flag all blocks if any of the naming checks are in the configuration
Description: If you create a default model advisor configuration to customize the edit-time check behavior, and the new configuration has any naming check present and selected, customized or not, the check will flag all blocks in the model.

Summary: Temporal logic conditions evaluated incorrectly for certain sample times
Description: If the sample time of your Stateflow chart is such that?int(sampleTime * 1000^n) != round (sampleTime * 1000^n) ?for some integer n,?  according to C++ semantics, Stateflow might incorrectly evaluate absolute time temporal logic in your chart, and transitions will not be taken at the expected time step. For example, the following is a list of sample times between 0 and 2 seconds (incrementing by .001 seconds) that are known to be affected: 0.201 0.203 0.402 0.406 0.803 0.804 0.811 0.812 0.819 1.001 1.003 1.005 1.007 1.009 1.011 1.013 1.015 1.017 1.019 1.021 1.023

Summary: Generated code for Shift Arithmetic block might contain an unused DWork variable *_HasIssuedWarning
Description: Generated code might contain an unused DWork variable for a model that meets all of the following conditions:? In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the System target file ?parameter is set to grt.tlc.  The model contains a Shift Arithmetic block.   The unused variable has the name *_HasIssuedWarning .?

Summary: MATLAB might crash at startup or error when using graphics on Linux machines with nouveau or VMware graphics drivers
Description: There are two MATLAB issues that might occur on Linux machines with nouveau or VMware graphics drivers: Crash at startup. Error messages and incorrect rendering when using graphics.
Workaround: Start MATLAB with the -softwareopengl startup option.

Summary: The Generate Run Script block parameters Install FlightGear scenery during simulation and Disable FlightGear shader options are ignored
Description: The Generate Run Script block contains these check boxes: Install FlightGear scenery during simulation  Disable FlightGear shader options?    The block ignores the settings of these check boxes and treats them as always 'on' (selected) in the generated run script file.

Summary: Report.getReportLayout returns wrong layout
Description: The getReportLayout  method of an mlreportgen.report.Report  object returns a report layout object. The returned object is the correct report layout only if a reporter invokes?the method. Otherwise, the method returns a layout object that is not the current report layout. For example, in the following script, the getReportLayout  method should return the layout of the current chapter. However, it returns the layout that was in effect before the chapter was added to the report.    import mlreportgen.report.*  rpt = Report('myreport',?'docx');  add(rpt, Chapter('Title', 'Introduction'));  plo = getReportLayout(rpt);
Workaround: Use the following function to get the current report layout.function?layout = getLayout(rpt)    c = rpt.Document.Children;    layouts = c(arrayfun(@(child) isa(child, 'mlreportgen.dom.PageLayout'), c));    if ~isempty(layouts)        layout = layouts(end);    else        layout = rpt.Document.CurrentPageLayout;    endend

Summary: Check usage of Logic and Bit Operations blocks?in Model Advisor flags inactive blocks in the model
Description: When a model uses Variant Sink or Variant Source blocks, certain blocks in the model are inactive based on the variant conditions. Model Advisor check  Check usage of Logic and Bit Operations blocks ?looks at models with variants and flags inactive relational blocks as violations.  IDs for  Check usage of Logic and Bit Operations blocks  are:  For DO-178C/DO-331:?mathworks.do178.LogicBlockUsage  For IEC 61508, IEC 62304, EN 50128, and ISO 26262:?mathworks.iec61508.LogicBlockUsage   The warning reported for the inactive relational block is as follows: The input data types are not known for these blocks.
Workaround: Please note that the below Model Advisor checks would be replacing?Check usage of Logic and Bit Operations blocks from R2018bCheck for Relational Operator blocks that equate floating-point types (ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0016)Check usage of Relational Operator blocks (ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0017)?Check usage of Logical Operator blocks (ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0018)

Summary: The Label Definitions button in the Audio Dataset Recorder example app generates an error
Description: Attempting to edit Audio File Label definitions by clicking Label Definitions   in audioexample.AudioDatasetRecorder  fails and generates the following error in the MATLAB command window:   Error using audioexample.dsrecorder.controller.LabelDefinitionsDialogController/openDialog (line 73)   Undefined variable "audioexample" or class "audioexample.dsrecorder.view.LabelDefinitionsDialog".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Referenced model without state might not execute if input is connected to block with inf sample time
Description: If all of the following conditions are true, a Model block outputs might not change with time because the model is not executed. ? The referenced model uses only pure mathematics blocks. The model doesn't have any block that has state, nor use time. The Model block referencing this model is placed in a Triggered Subsystem, Function Call Subsystem,? Enable and Triggered Subsystem, or For-Iterator Subsystem.? The Model block(s) are the only run-time non-virtual blocks in this subsystem. All other blocks are virtual or constants.? At least one input port of the Subsystem is driven by a Constant block, or other block whose sample time is [inf, 0].
Workaround: Add a block that has state (e.g. unit delay block) or a block that uses time (e.g. Scope) to either the Subsystem or the referenced model.?

Summary: uidatepicker is positioned out of view in tab and panel
Description: The datepicker   component is out of view when parented to a panel or tab because the default position is [20,300,150,22].?
Workaround: Manually adjust the position by setting the Position property of the component.

Summary: slwebview_cov? produces errors when generating model Web view
Description: Using?slwebview_cov ?produces the following errors:? Error using slreportgen.utils.getSlSfHandle?  Unexpected type "slreportgen.webview.SlProxyObject".?    Error in slreportgen.utils.getModelHandle?    Error in slwebview_cov

Summary: Incoming links are not shown in Property Inspector when a block is selected in Requirements Perspective
Description: When you select a block in the Requirements Perspective, links information for the selected block is shown in the Info tab of the Property Inspector. However, only the outgoing links of the selected block are displayed; incoming links are not shown.

Summary: Simulink crashes?in rapid accelerator mode when logging signals inside a Terminate Function block that is referenced from?a Model block
Description: Simulink crashes when you simulate?a top model in rapid accelerator mode?that references a model?with the following criteria:? The top?model contains a Model block with a terminate port. The Model block is referencing another model with a Terminate Function block. The Terminate Function block contains signals that are configured for logging. The top model is?configured?for rapid accelerator mode.

Summary: Code inspection might return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for models that require the CodeWarrior compiler
Description: Code inspection returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for models that contain custom storage classes and that require the CodeWarrior  compiler for a FreeScale PowerPC target.

Summary: Code and Simulation mismatch when Simulink Function is in an algebraic loop
Description: The behavior of the code generated for a model may not match the behavior of a simulation of the model?when all of following conditions are true:? The model contains a Simulink Function block An output port of the Simulink Function block is connected directly to an input port of that Simulink Function block Inside the Simulink Function, the Outport block corresponding to that Simulink Function?block output port is connected directly to an ArgIn  port
Workaround: Place a Signal Conversion block after the ArgIn port and select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization checkbox.
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 7, R2018a Update 3, R2018b

Summary: String data type on the output port of FMU Import Block may cause Simulink to crash.
Description: If you have a FMU with an output signal of string data type and import it in Simulink, the output port of the FMU Import Block?has string data type. This may cause Simulink to crash.

Summary: Generating Polyspace report with?a template?customized??for code metrics with MATLAB Report Generator can cause an error
Description: Using MATLAB Report Generator to create a customized Polyspace report template can lead to an error if one of these filters is used in the Report Customization (Filtering)  component: Include project metrics  Include file metrics  Include function metrics   This error occurs when you generate a report from Polyspace results using the customized template: Conversion to cell from struct is not possible.Error in CXMLMetricsReader/BuildReviewedFilterIndices (line 702)
Workaround: To work around this issue, apply the required code metrics filters in the Polyspace UI and then generate a report interactively using the option Only include currently displayed results.

Summary: Memory leak in Java engine when a string is returned from MATLAB
Description: There is memory leak in the Java engine when MATLAB returns a a string to Java.? Eventually, theJava process will crash with an out-of-memory condition.

Summary: Report API DiagramElementFinder fails to find elements in Stateflow.SLFunction object
Description: The Report API slreportgen.finder.DiagramElementFinder  produces these errors when attempting to find elements in a Stateflow.SLFunction  object. Error using slreportgen.utils.getSlSfHandle>resolveHierarchyId (line 53) No object found backing hierarchical ID. Error in slreportgen.utils.getSlSfHandle (line 42)         h = resolveHierarchyId(in);     Error in slreportgen.finder.DiagramElementFinder/findImpl (line 348)             dobj = slreportgen.utils.getSlSfHandle(dhid); Error in slreportgen.finder.DiagramElementFinder/find (line 241)                 results = findImpl(h, h.Container);

Summary: Diagram reporter does not report Stateflow.SLFunction?subsytem
Description: The  Diagram  reporter does not produce a snapshot of a Stateflow.SLFunction ?subsystem. An error occurs indicating that the object is an invalid source.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Product block might generate wrong?answer for complex integer?0 divided by 0
Description: The Product block might generate wrong answers when these?conditions are all true: There?are one or more division operations, as specified by the Number of Inputs  parameter, for example,?/*/.  The Multiplication  parameter is set?to Element-wise(.*).  The output of the Product block is complex.?  For division operationsAll inputs have the same built-in integer type, such?as int8 , uint8 , int16 , uint16 , int32 , and so forth. The denominator is complex and has the value 0 for its real and imaginary parts.    This issue affects both simulation and code generation.

Summary: Slow model compilation with model reference hierarchies containing Stateflow charts
Description: Simulink takes much longer to compile models with a large number (>500) of Model blocks that contain Stateflow charts than compared to previous releases.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: validatestring may return an incorrect class when passed mixed class inputs in code generation
Description: When generating code from the function validatestring  with MATLAB Coder, the result may have the incorrect class in the following cases:? The first input is a string and the second input is a cell array of character vectors. In this case, the generated code returns a string but MATLAB returns a character vector. The first input is a character vector and the second input is a string.?In this case, the generated code returns a character vector but MATLAB returns a string.
Workaround: If you encounter either of the situations described above, use the functions char and string to convert the output of validatestring to the desired class:str = char(validatestring("a",{'a','b'},'functionName'));str = string(validatestring('a',"a",'functionName'));?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Function blocks?that specify shared input/output argument might reuse wrong input in generated code
Description: If a Simulink Function block with input/output reuse has any another input with the same datatype, complexity, and size as the reused input, the generated code might call the?function?with incorrect arguments. Specifically, the generated code might reuse the wrong variable at the Function Caller block?and produce a wrong answer. This occurs even if the other inputs are also specified to reuse with another output. In the below example, the?Simulink Function?f  has a 2 inputs u ?and x , and two outputs y ?and x .? The input/output?have the same?name, so?a single argument?is used in the function for the input/output pair. The generated code for the function definition looks similar to the following code: /* Output and update for Simulink Function: '<Root>/Simulink Function' */  void f(real_T rtu_u, real_T *rty_y, real_T *rtuy_x)  {  ? ...  ?The problem occurs at the function call site.? Here is an example of incorrect code: ? ...  ? /* FunctionCaller: '<Root>/Function Caller' */  ? rtb_inoutarg = rtb_input2;  ? f(rtb_input2, &rtb_output1, &rtb_inoutarg);  ? ... ? The first statement occurs because Simulink cannot reuse the same variable on the shared input/output -- a copy of the input is introduced.? The problem is that the two copies of input x  are passed to the Simulink function as inputs x  and u . ?The correct code uses a different variable for input u  and appears as follows: ? ...  ? /* FunctionCaller: '<Root>/Function Caller'? */  ??rtb_inoutarg?=?rtb_input2;  ? f(rtb_input1, &rtb_output1, &rtb_inoutarg);  ? ...
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 6, R2018a Update 2, R2018b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Interpolation Using Prelookup block inlines symbolic dimensions for table data when specified via the Table data port
Description: The Interpolation Using Prelookup block inlines the symbolic dimensions for table data when the table data is specified via the Table data port (Source  set to port ), rather than with the block parameter (Source  set to Dialog ).
Workaround: To specify symbolic dimensions for table data:In the MATLAB workspace, define a Simulink.Parameter object that contains your symbolic dimensions.In the Interpolation using Prelookup block,?set the Source?parameter to Dialog and specify the Simulink.Parameter object in the Value parameter.?For more information, see?https://www.mathworks.com/help/ecoder/ug/implement-dimension-variants-for-array-sizes-in-generated-code.html.

Summary: Test case extension may cause the analysis to time-out before the Maximum analysis time
Description: Extension of test cases causes the test case?loading time to be added to the analysis time.This may cause Simulink Design Verifier to time-out before the specified Maximum analysis time.   For example: If the test case loading takes 10 mins and the?Maximum analysis time ?is specified as 5 mins then the?Simulink Design Verifier?would always time-out without performing any analysis.

Summary: MATLAB file operations such as cd, path, and addapth?fail on local or network directory paths with mixed permissions
Description: MATLAB file operations such as  cd,  path,  and  addpath?  fail on?local or network directory paths with mixed permissions.?For example,?consider the following paths: local path?C:\public1\private1\private2\private3\public2  network path?\\hostname\public1\private1\private2\private3\public2   If permission is denied for a particular user for?any reason to the directory?private1\private2\private3, then  cd,  addpath,  and  path   show the following?behavior:  >> cd '\\hostname\public1\private1\private2\private3\public2'  Error using cd  Cannot CD to?\\hostname\public1\private1\private2\private3\public2'? (Directory permission denied).  In addition to the?above error, cd  sometimes also displays the following error:? fl::except::IInternalException  >> addpath '\\hostname\public1\private1\private2\private3\public2'  Warning: Directory permission denied:?\\hostname\public1\private1\private2\private3\public2  >> path '\\hostname\public1\private1\private2\private3\public2'  Warning: Directory permission denied:?\\hostname\public1\private1\private2\private3\public2  Similar behavior is shown for local drive paths as well.
Workaround: For local drive paths, there is no workaround.For network paths, map the path to a drive letter and?then cd?to the drive letter.?

Summary: Update does not modify "Modified on" of a requirement when only its custom attribute value is changed, thus failing to trigger the change information
Description: If you update a requirement set that uses custom attributes, and only the custom attribute value of a requirement is changed since the previous update, the update process does not change the "Modified On" timestamp for the corresponding requirement. As a result, the change information is not automatically triggered for the updated requirement. The requirement is updated, and the summary of the changes (in Comments) includes those updates.
Workaround: One possible workaround that involves some manual work is as follows: Look at the summary of the changes produced by the Update process.Note the IDs of the updated requirements.Go back to the external document, and locate the requirements with the above IDs.Manually modify at least one of the built-in attributes for those requirements, for instance by adding a period to the Summary or the Description attribute. Please note, whitespace-only differences are ignored by the Update process, so the modification must not include only whitespace.Rerun the Update process.The Update process will now re-update the requirements, and update the "Modified On" timestamp on those requirements, which will trigger the change information.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Silent wrong answer might occur due to sporadic data corruption on the target computer
Description: Sporadic data corruption on the target computer might result in intermittent incorrect results, without a warning or error. This data corruption occurs due to interrupt service activity on the target computer. Known conditions that increase the frequency of incorrect results include:? More periodic sample times in the application than cores on the target computer Leaving the SLRT Explorer open and connected to the target computer High levels of network traffic involving the target computer Running the model in external mode
