Description: MATLAB can crash during HDL code generation with the following stack trace if Clock Rate Pipelining is turned on. [ 0] 0x000000011b1c6e00 bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+09989632 pir::OptimizeArrayIncDecOneIndexing::scope+00302784 [ 1] 0x000000011b1c6a50 bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+09988688 pir::OptimizeArrayIncDecOneIndexing::scope+00301840 [ 2] 0x000000011b1c532c bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+09982764 pir::OptimizeArrayIncDecOneIndexing::scope+00295916 [ 3] 0x000000011b29a147 bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+10854727 pir::SAX2PirParse+00141927 [ 4] 0x000000011b29f267 bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+10875495 pir::SAX2PirParse+00162695 [ 5] 0x000000011b29f31e bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+10875678 pir::SAX2PirParse+00162878 [ 6] 0x000000011b29caaa bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+10865322 pir::SAX2PirParse+00152522 [ 7] 0x000000011aca461e bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+04605470 pir::Network::optimizationsRequested+00000158 [ 8] 0x000000011ae6fd69 bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+06487401 cleanupUddPir+00075337 [ 9] 0x000000011a9d3b6a bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+01653610 cleanupDnnUddPir+00114826
Workaround: hdlset_param(bdroot, 'ClockRatePipelining', 'off')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Composite Bus ports with different variant conditions may generate uninitialized variables
Description: Variant Bus signals with composite ports may result in incorrect variant conditions and may cause uninitialized/unknown variables in generated code.  This incorrect behavior happens when all of the following conditions are true  ?Both parent and a child from the same bus signal hierarchy are selected using composite ports within a Subsystem  ?Different variant conditions are propagated on both the selected parent and child bus signals using Variant Sink or Variant Source block  ?Both the parent and child signals are driving separate output ports at the root level

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discontiguous demuxed bus with Inline Variants may not generate error during code generation
Description: The following? erroneous? modeling pattern generates error?during simulation but not?during code generation:  Discontiguous demuxed bus signals feeding to Inline Variants with GeneratePreprocessorConditionals  turned on The Inline Variants block is connected to a Mux block The output of the Mux has a signal object which is defined in the global workspace

Summary: Generating report for requirements set from default template checks out MATLAB Report Generator license
Description: On Microsoft Windows platforms, generating a report for a requirements set checks out a MATLAB Report Generator license, even when using the default report templates.?A MATLAB Report Generator license should be checked out only when explicitly using the MATLAB Report Generator functionality.

Summary: Unused local variables in model initialization function
Description: Simulink Coder can generate unused local variables in the model_initialize  function under these following conditions: The model contains a user written S-function that generates C/C++ code utilizing a TLC implementation. The user written S-function implements TLC based expression folding. The user written S-function TLC implementation has a block initialization function.
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 6, R2017a Update 3, R2018a Update 4, R2018b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The bus elements of a variant virtual bus?are unconditionally generated in the code
Description: Variant bus signals with? GeneratePreprocessorConditionals? selected ?are unconditionally generated in the code when all of the below conditions are true: A subsystem containing a bus selector block has a? different variant condition than the incoming virtual bus. The option  Treat as grouped when propagating variant conditions ? or?Treat As Atomic Unit ? is selected for the subsystem.  Both the parent bus and the child signals get selected using the bus selector within the subsystem.  The child bus signals have an output port connection. The parent bus is left unconnected.
Fixed In: R2016b Update 6, R2017b Update 8, R2018a Update 4, R2018b

Summary: mlreportgen.reporter.Figure reporter always uses default font size when creating figure snapshot
Description: If you use the mlreportgen.reporter.Figure  reporter to create a snapshot of a figure, the figure's default font size is always used.  For example, if you create a figure and change the font size of its title, the figure snapshot taken by the mlreportgen.reporter.Figure  reporter will not reflect this change in font size.
Workaround: Use the PRINT function to print the figure as an image file. Then, create an?mlreportgen.dom.Image object with that image file as the input. Set the image height and width properties to fit the page, and add the image object to the report. After the report is closed, delete the image file.

Summary: Code generation results in CGIR assertion error for models containing ASCIIToString blocks when Number of bits:int configuration parameter is 16
Description: When generating code for a model where?the following are true: Model contains at?least one ASCIIToString block The Number of bits: int  (TargetBitPerInt )   configuration parameter (Hardware Implementation  > Number of bits:  > int ) is set to 16?   The following error is produced: CGIR assertion '!MatrixType::isa(exprType)' failed in 'b:\matlab\src\cgir_xform\dom_c\lowermatrixtypesforbackend.cpp:929'[...]??Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: b:\matlab\src\cgir_xform\dom_c\lowermatrixtypesforbackend.cpp line 929: !MatrixType::isa(exprType)".? Please report this to MathWorks if you can cause it to recur

Summary: Incorrect logging for signals inside a Terminate Function block referenced from?a Model block
Description: Under these conditions, Simulink logs the signals inside the Terminate Function block at the end of a simulation. Signals should instead be logged whenever the terminate port of the Model block is called during a simulation.? Your model is subject to this bug when it meets the following criteria:? The top?model contains a Model block with a terminate port. The Model block is referencing another model with a Terminate Function block. The Terminate Function block contains signals that are configured for logging. The top model is simulated with accelerator mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect value?from Merge block?when driven by export-function Model block.
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when it meets the following conditions. Your model contains a Merge block. The Merge block is driven by?an outport from a?Model block referencing an export-function model. The initial value of the Merge block is different from the driving Outport block inside the export-function model.  Under the above conditions, the initial value from inside the export-function model overrides the initial value of the Merge block, resulting in a wrong answer. If the Merge block is driven by more than one export-function Model block, one of?the blocks?will override the initial value of the Merge block.
Workaround: Ensure initial values of the Merge block and Export Function Model block sources are consistent.

Summary: MATLAB? does not correctly load complex numbers from certain MAT files
Description: Some MAT-files containing complex number variables do notload correctly in R2018a, and the loaded data might be corrupted.?  This behavior is observed when theMAT-files are written in the V4 format by: MATLAB release R12 or older, or  some third-party tools
Workaround: To work around this issue, load and re-save affected MAT files using any MATLAB? release after R12, up to and including R2017b.

Summary: bitshift with a vector second input containing large values of type int64 or uint64 may give incorrect results
Description: bitshift  may give incorrect results when both of these conditions are met: The second input is a vector or array (not a scalar). The shift values in the second input are of type int64  or uint64  and have absolute value larger than 231 .  Specifically, in a function call bitshift(a,b)  meeting the above conditions, incorrect results occur for values of b  where abs(mod(b,2^31)) <= 64 . For example: >> a = [int64(1) int64(100)];>> b = [int64(1) int64(5629499534213120)];>> bitshift(a,b)ans =                    2                  100 The correct result is the vector [2 0] . However, because mod(int64(5629499534213120), int64(2^31)) == 0 , the incorrectly returned result [2 100]  is equivalent to bitshift(int64(100), int64(0)) .
Workaround: Casting the second input to double produces the correct behavior:>> a = [int64(1) int64(100)];>> b = [int64(1) int64(5629499534213120)];>> bitshift(a,double(b))ans =                    2                    0

Summary: Models containing Variant blocks may take long time to update.
Description: Models containing Variant blocks takes longer time to simulate/update as compared to a model without Variant blocks.This time difference is more profound in larger models with more number of blocks.

Summary: Contour plot does not properly update to reflect changes in?ZData values
Description: If you change the ZData ?property on an existing contour plot, and if the?LevelList ?property is automatically calculated, then the contour plot might not update to reflect the new data. This bug only occurs if: You change the?ZData ?property without modifying the?XData ?or?YData ?property The?LevelListMode ?property is set to?'auto'  The list of levels calculated based on the new data shares elements with the list of levels calculated based on the old data?  For example, this code generates a contour plot and then changes the ZData   values. The contour plot does not update properly. [~,c] = contour(peaks(31)/10);drawnowc.ZData = membrane+0.2;
Workaround: To work around this bug, use one of these options:Call the contour command to create a new Contour object with the new ZData, rather than changing the ZData?property on the existing?Contour?object.contour(peaks(31)/10);drawnowcontour(membrane+0.2);Set the LevelListMode?property to 'manual' before setting the?ZData property.?To change the?LevelListMode?property,?either set the?LevelListMode?property directly or set the LevelList property to a hard-coded list of levels.?If you want an automatically calculated LevelList?property, you can set the LevelListMode?property back to 'auto' after changing the ZData property.[~,c] = contour(peaks(31)/10);drawnowc.LevelListMode = 'manual';c.ZData = membrane+0.2;drawnowc.LevelListMode = 'auto';Modify the XData or YData?property after modifying the ZData.[~,c] = contour(peaks(31)/10);drawnowc.ZData = membrane+0.2;c.XData = c.XData + 1;

Summary: MATLAB might crash while creating a subsystem from a selection
Description: MATLAB might crash while creating a subsystem from a selection if the selection contains a block linked to a parent library block that modifies itself.
Workaround: Insert a Subsystem block and move the blocks into this subsystem using cut and paste.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer between simulation and generated code for Interpolation using Prelookup block?when Ground block is connected to any index port (kn)
Description: Interpolation using Prelookup block returns a possible wrong answer?for simulation under all these conditions: Ground block is connected to any Index Port(kn)?  Interpolation using Prelookup?block?fraction port (fn )?corresponding to the index port (kn ) is a vector or matrix? Interpolation using Prelookup?block parameter Interpolation method  is Nearest , Linear point-slope , or  Linear Lagrange .
Workaround: Replace the?Ground block?connected to the index port (kn)?with?a Constant block?that has?Constant parameter value zeros(M,N),?where M,N is a dimension of the?corresponding fraction port (fn).

Summary: Error when importing .reqif or .reqifz?files
Description: An error might occur while importing .reqif or .reqifz files under these conditions: slreq.import()  API is called with the path to a .reqif or .reqifz file.?? .reqif  file is selected from the drop-down list in the Source Document selector when using the Document Import Wizard. The .reqif?file does not contain any SpecRelations information.  The error?states?either that ?Dot indexing not supported for variables of this type   or Invalid .reqif file .?
Workaround: In cases (1) and (2) please unzip the reqifz file, and import the .reqif file found inside. Ensure the file extension is in lower case: .reqif,? not .REQIF.In case (3),?you can try to go back to the 3rd party tool that created the .reqif file, and ensure?that links?between objects are exported, and not just the objects. If there are no links between objects, you may need to create a dummy link before exporting to ReqIF.?

Summary: svd with two or three output arguments does not converge for some rare matrices
Description: When the svd  function is called with two or three output arguments and operates on certain rare matrices, it can produce the error: Error using svd. SVD did not converge.  In R2018a , this bug affects some input matrices that were working in R2017b .
Workaround: To safeguard against matrix A being one of the rare matrices that causes svd to fail to converge, wrap the svd call in a try/catch block and compute the SVD of a slightly perturbed matrix if svd fails to converge. try    [U,S,V] = svd(A);catch err??? if err.identifier == "MATLAB:svd:svdNoConvergence"??????? [U,S,V] = svd(A + 1e-14*norm(A)*randn(RandStream("mt19937ar"), size(A)));??? else??????? rethrow(err)??? endend

Summary: FMU Import block ignores user-specified initial state values during simulation
Description: The FMU Import block ignores the user-specified initial state values and uses the initial values specified by the FMU, if all of the following hold: The model contains an FMU Import block that loads an FMU v1.0 or v2.0 file in Model Exchange mode. The FMU contains continuous states and specifies initial values for these states. User specifies 'Initial state' values in the 'Configuration Parameters' panel.  In this case, Simulink does not use the initial state values specified by user and instead uses initialized state values from FMU.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Transition path with multiple sources is taken at unexpected time when using temporal operators
Description: The temporal operator on a transition segment might be evaluated as true at the incorrect time when all of the following conditions are true: The transition segment is reachable from different source states Those source states can be active at the same time (for example, one state is a parent of the other)  The incorrect behavior occurs in both simulation and the generated code.

Summary: Suggested fix fails for model variable evaluation error when the variable cannot access a variable in another data dictionary or sub data dictionary
Description: The suggested fix for a model variable evaluation error fails when all  of these conditions exist: A Simulink model links to a Simulink data dictionary with a referenced data dictionary. The model uses a variable that refers to another variable. For example, variable A  refers to variable B . Variable A  cannot access variable B  for one of these reasons:? A is defined in the base workspace and B is defined in a data dictionary. A is defined in a data dictionary and B is not defined within the same data dictionary or any of its referenced dictionaries.   Compiling this model results in an error similar to: Undefined variable or function 'B'.  In this case, if you choose to fix the issue by clicking Create a new variable  In the Diagnostic Viewer, Simulink reports a message similar to: No action taken because variable B already exists.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized local variables may be generated with variant bus.
Description: Model containing variant blocks and buses may cause uninitialized local variables when all the below conditions are met:  A virtual bus containing variant elements is?fed to a block which is unconditional.  A Bus Creator/Out Bus Element creates a variant bus with  GeneratePreprocessorConditionals  on.   Example code below depicts that the variable? rtb_a remains uninitialized when A=1 and V=2 #if V == 1 || A == 1 real_T?rtb_a; #endif? #if V == 1    ? rtb_a = 2;    #endif? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ??    #if A != 1 && V != 1    ? rtb_a = 0.0;    #endif? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ??   model_Y.Out4 = rtb_a;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect variable reuse when a Model block or Probe block connects to a Unit Delay block
Description: When a Model block or Probe block connects to a Unit Delay block, the state variable may be incorrectly reused if the following conditions are true: In the case of the Model block, inside the Model block, the root outport block that drives the Unit Delay block has non-zero initial value (for example, it is driven by a Constant block) that is different from the initial value of the state variable.  In the case of the Probe block, the Probe block connects to a Unit Delay block, and the initial value is not equal to the signal size that the Probe block acts on.  Optimize global data access  configuration parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results or Minimize global data access ; or Reuse global block outputs  configuration parameter is enabled.  Reuse local block outputs ?configuration parameter is enabled.  When these conditions are true, the model block output or Probe block output and Unit Delay state may get merged, resulting in an incorrect answer because the mismatched initial conditions were merged.?
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 6, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect row-major code for a model containing State Writer block that writes states to Delay block
Description: Embedded Coder can generate incorrect code?when all these conditions are true for a model: Model configuration parameter Array layout  set to 'Row-Major'. ? Model contains a State Writer block which writes states to a Delay block with Delay length  parameter greater than 1. N-Delay block has a nonscalar state value.

Summary: Running Simulink Design Verifier check from Model Advisor may incorrectly display "Passed" result for models that have Undecided objectives
Description: When you run a Simulink Design Verifier check, the Model Advisor might incorrectly display the result status as "Passed" if the analysis completes within the specified maximum analysis time and reports some objectives as "Undecided".

Summary: binfo_mdlref.mat changes after simulation or code generation
Description: The?binfo_mdlref.mat ?file in the simulation cache or code generation folder changes even though all model reference simulation and code generation targets are up to date. The change occurs under these conditions: You simulate or generate code for the model hierarchy. The top model?Rebuild ?configuration parameter is set to?If any changes detected .

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier report generation fails for some Stateflow models
Description: After design error detection analysis, Simulink Design Verifier fails to generate a report for some models that include Stateflow. This may occur in models that include truth tables in the S-Function block.  Report generation fails with these errors: Error using Simulink.ID.getHandle (line 79)  Invalid SID: 'mReportError_17a:1:4'. No object corresponds to ':4'.  Error in Sldv.ReportUtils/prepareProperties>prepareProperty (line 55)  Error in Sldv.ReportUtils/prepareProperties (line 17)  Error in Sldv.ReportUtils.generate (line 196)  Error in sldvreport (line 61)   For test generation and property proving analysis, the report is generated, but the highlighting of the model elements from the report fails with the same errors.

Summary: Automatic Orange Tester error: Unable to copy pathto\file.bat to pathto\?.relaunch\?post_preprocessing_command.txt
Description: When you launch a Polyspace analysis with the -automatic-orange-tester  option, and also specify  the option -post-preprocessing-command pathto\file.bat , the following error might occur during the compilation phase: Instrumenting myfile.c (1/N)*** I/?O error on BinIo.openOut (file=pathto\?.relaunch\?post_preprocessing_command.txt). Retrying...(errno=2) *** I/?O error on BinIo.openOut (file=pathto\?.relaunch\?post_preprocessing_command.txt). Exiting.*** flags=0x200System error No such file or directory (noent: No such file or directory)Error: Unable to copy pathto\file.bat to pathto\?.relaunch\?post_preprocessing_command.txt
Workaround: Instead of:-post-preprocessing-command?pathto\file.batuse:-post-preprocessing-command call pathto\file.bat

Summary: Accessing structure fields can lead to a false red check when the structure is initialized by a stubbed function
Description: In some code-dependent situations, if a structure is initialized by a stubbed function, a red Non-initialized variable  check might be observed on a field of the structure. The access to the first field is orange, but accessing the other fields can lead to a red check. Because the function potentially initializes all structure fields, the accesses should all be orange.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Symbolic dimension specified for Output Size of Selector block is ignored in the generated code
Description: When generating code for a model containing a Selector block whose Output Size is specified using a symbolic dimension, Embedded Coder omits the symbolic specification and instead inlines the numeric value.?
Fixed In: R2016b Update 7, R2017b Update 8, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: Editing items in ModelExplorer or in editor when ModelExplorer is open crashes Simulink
Description: While editing items in ModelExplorer, simulink:SLPropInfo::getValue()  can cause Simulink to crash. A crash also can occur when ModelExplorer is visible and contents of the editor are modified or deleted. Editing items in ModelExplorer also can cause Simulink to crash.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may take longer time than the specified Maximum analysis time
Description: Simulink Design Verifier shows analysis in progress for long time even after the analysis time has exceeded the Maximum analysis time  specified for a model when any of the following is true: Simulink Design Verifier is launched from the UI menu  Using  sldvrun  API with  showUI  flag set to true   For such models, stop button may not respond for long time.
Workaround: Perform Simulink Design Verifier analysis by using sldvrun with showUI option set to false.

Summary: Misleading error message for coder dictionaries that are incompatible with current release?
Description: When you open a coder dictionary that was created in a newer release than the release you are currently running, you might see a misleading error message that does not indicate the incompatibility issue.? For example, if you open an R2018b coder dictionary in R2018a, instead of an incompatibility error message that indicates how you can export your coder dictionary from the new release to the older release, you might see the following error message:? Invalid value of Access Methods ?

Summary: Some MATLAB tools might fail to open on Ubuntu 18.04
Description: These MATLAB and Simulink tools might fail to open on Ubuntu 18.04: App Designer Simulink Data Inspector Root Inport Mapping Simulink Template Gallery Export Model to Template Add-Ons Explorer Simulink Start Page  If a tool fails to open, follow these steps: Open MATLAB. Set the current folder to the  matlabroot .  Type? !./bin/glnxa64/MATLABWindow  in the Command Window.?   If MATLAB displays this error in the Command Window, then the issue is caused by a problem with the MATLABWindow application: MATLABWindow: symbol lookup error: /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libatk-1.0.so.0: undefined symbol: g_log_structured_standard

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for root-level outport driven by reusable subsystem with union of rates
Description: Incorrect code is possible for a model that meets all of the following conditions: A top-level model operates at multiple rates. A root-level output port in the top-level model operates at a union of rates. The root-level output port is driven by a subsystem with the?Function packaging ?parameter set to?Reusable . The subsystem might also have Auto ?function packaging and the code generator generates a reusable subsystem.??

Summary: Error when you export variables from Econometric Modeler that overwrite existing variables in the MATLAB Workspace
Description: Assume the following conditions apply: Database Toolbox is not installed on your machine. You export a variable from the Econometric Modeler app that overwrites at least one variable in the MATLAB Workspace.  A dialog appears requesting whether you want to overwrite existing variables. If you select any option, then MATLAB issues the following error: Error using message/getString?  Unable to load a message catalog 'database:dbgui'. Please check the file location and format.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect removal of a function or method that modifies a handle object
Description: MATLAB Coder might incorrectly remove a function or method when all of the following conditions are true: The function has no outputs or the outputs are not used The function takes a handle object as an input. The handle object contains another handle object as one of its properties. The other handle object is written to by the function or another function called from the current function.  The following example function meets the conditions. The function sets different properties of obj.Solver ?where both the obj  and the Solver  properties are handle objects. The function has no output. The code generator might incorrectly remove the?body of the function. function set.SolverParameters(obj, solverparams)%Set solver parametersparams = obj.Solver.getSolverParams;  % An alias is created to the solver params inside obj.Solver.getSolverParams...if isfield(solverparams, 'MaxIterations')... params.MaxNumIteration = solverparams.MaxIterations;... end...obj.Solver.setSolverParams(params);end

Summary: estimateStereoBaseline function returns incorrect results
Description: The estimateStereoBaseline  function returns incorrect results. The errors are exaggerated when you use a checkerboard that is significantly rotated with respect to the camera. The Stereo Calibration  app also returns incorrect results when you use the estimateStereoBaseline  function with fixed camera intrinsics.

Summary: Opening plot tools causes MATLAB to hang when running with no desktop
Description: If you run MATLAB with the -nodesktop  startup option, and then open the plot tools, MATLAB can stop responding.  For example, if you create a figure with a plot and select Edit > Figure Properties, a dialog appears that says "opening plot tools." However, this dialog hangs and the Command Window stops responding.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when a Simulink tooltip is opening
Description: A tooltip appears on many objects in the Simulink editor (depending on user preferences) after the cursor has hovered over them for a short time. If at that instant you delete the object or rapidly move the mouse cursor to another location, MATLAB might crash. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to: [10] 0x0000000116c08679 MG2::RolloverTarget::effectiveHierarchicalTarget at b:\matlab\src\mg\widget\rollovertarget.cpp:35 (in \\mathworks\devel\bat\archive\R2016b\GR\perfect\matlab\bin\win64\mg.dll)[ 11] 0x0000000116c0870f MG2::RolloverTarget::sameEffectiveTargets at b:\matlab\src\mg\widget\rollovertarget.cpp:61 (in \\mathworks\devel\bat\archive\R2016b\GR\perfect\matlab\bin\win64\mg.dll)[ 12] 0x0000000116c07285 MG2::RolloverManager::destroyRolloverForTarget at b:\matlab\src\mg\widget\rollovermanager.cpp:225 (in \\mathworks\devel\bat\archive\R2016b\GR\perfect\matlab\bin\win64\mg.dll)
Workaround: When hovering over an object in the Simulink editor, wait for the tooltip to fully appear before taking any subsequent action.?

Summary: Referenced protected models do not honor top model Generate Code Only configuration parameter or generateCodeOnly option for rtwbuild
Description: When a top model that references a protected model has the?Generate Code Only ?configuration parameter selected, the protected model does not honor the top model setting and compiles its code. This error also occurs when you use the generateCodeOnly? option with rtwbuild .

Summary: Error or exception occurs when MATLAB release version and Simulink Real-Time kernel version do not match
Description: An error or exception occurs in MATLAB when the MATLAB release version and Simulink Real-Time kernel version do not match. For example, when the host computer is running MATLAB release version R2018a and the target computer is running Simulink Real-Time release version R2017b, the versions do not match. This condition causes these and other errors and exceptions: When a real-time application is loaded on the target computer, MATLAB crashes. When a file is copied to the target computer by using the SimulinkRealTime.copyFileToTarget  function, MATLAB displays this error message:  >> In SimulinkRealTime.copyFileToTarget (line 34)? ?Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback?for event Connected defined for class? ?MathWorks.xPCTarget.FrameWork.xPCTargetPC:? ?Error using slrtFileExists (line 37)? ?NaN's cannot be converted to logicals.  >>?In xpctarget.AutoConnect (line 6) ? ?In SimulinkRealTime.fileSystem/fopen (line 51) ? ?In SimulinkRealTime.copyFileToTarget (line 68)? ?In SimulinkRealTime.fileSystem/fopen (line 51)?? In SimulinkRealTime.copyFileToTarget (line 68)

Summary: Job Monitor causes java exceptions if it fails to retrieve the list of jobs
Description: If the Job Monitor fails to retrieve the list of jobs, you see the following error in MATLAB: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java/lang.UnsupportedOperationException at java.util.AbstractList.add(Unknown Source)  This problem is caused when one or more of the cluster's job files become corrupted.
Workaround: You can fix the problem by deleting the corrupted job or jobs. To identify which jobs are corrupted, display each job individually:cluster = parcluster();arrayfun(@disp, cluster.Jobs);?This shows an error when trying to display the corrupted job. Delete this job's corresponding job folder from the clusters job storage location, cluster.JobStorageLocation. For example, if the job with ID 3 failed to display, delete the Job3 folder. Repeat this method to delete any additional corrupted jobs.

Summary: MATLAB incorrectly throws "Not enough input arguments."? when the JobStorageLocation of a cluster is corrupt
Description: When the JobStorageLocation  of a cluster is corrupt and you use your cluster, MATLABthrows an incorrect error message: Not enough input arguments.  Instead, MATLAB should throw the following warning: Warning: Unable to read the required metadata file '/path/to/jobstoragelocation/matlab_metadata.mat'?  This issue affects all cluster types whose JobStorageLocation  is a file system folder, including the 'Local' and 'Generic' cluster types. Cluster types based on the MATLAB Job Scheduler, including 'MJS' , 'Cloud', and 'MJSComputeCloud', are not affected.
Workaround: The underlying problem of this issue is a corrupt?JobStorageLocation.? For assistance recovering job data from a corrupt JobStorageLocation, contact MathWorks Support Team. Otherwise, delete all files in the JobStorageLocation and try again. Check the JobStorageLocation in the properties of your cluster profile.

Summary: Incorrect display of?MISRA C:2012 rule 12.3 when the rule violation occurs in a macro expansion
Description: If a macro expansion violates MISRA C:2012 rule 12.3, Polyspace highlights the violation on every use of the macro instead of showing it once on the macro definition. For instance, a macro defined like this violates rule 12.3: #? define MyMacro(data) (MyVar = (data), ((int)ok))?  Polyspace shows the violation on every use of??MyMacro()  instead of the macro definition.  For all other rules, Polyspace shows a rule violation only once on the macro definition. In addition, the result has events that point to uses of the macro.

Summary: Simscape model throws an error that 'SimscapeLogToSDI' parameter is not present in the model
Description: If the focus is not on the Simscape model during simulation, you get?the following error message: block_diagram does not have a parameter named 'SimscapeLogToSDI'
Workaround: During the simulation, make sure the focus is on the model.

Summary: Saving objects to MAT files causes memory leak
Description: Memory leaks might occur while saving objects to MAT files if the objects contain nondependent properties with default values in the classdefinition. Memory leaks are more likely to occur when using string arrays for default values.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier analysis might appear to be frozen for models with many bus inputs
Description: Simulink Design Verifier analysis?may take long time to show any progress and may appear to be frozen at the start. This is predominant for models that consists of large number of bus inputs.

Summary: ReqIF file import fails to import images?when?embedded as?OLE objects
Description: When importing requirements content from a ReqIF file, images that are embedded as OLE objects are?not preserved. Instead?the?imported?content shows?the text "OLE Object".

Summary: Missing explicit link for makefile when generating code for Intel MKL-DNN targets using cnncodegen command
Description: When using cnncodegen  command to generate code for Intel MKL-DNN targets: >> cnncodegen(net, 'targetlib', 'mkldnn ')? the generated makefile does not explicitly link against the Intel library libmklml_intel.so . This library is needed for successful linking and loading of the generated library. The library must be found in the path pointed to by the LD_LIBRARY_PATH  environment variable in your machine.?

Summary: Default transition, history junction, and row type icons do not appear In the State Transition Table UI
Description: In the State Transition Table UI, the following icons do not appear: default transition, history junction, and row type?icons. This makes it difficult to determine when syntax types are present and difficult to verify when they have been modified.

Summary: Nested tables overflow the page
Description: Nested tables incorrectly inherit column width information from the parent table. For example, if the first column of the parent table has a width of 75%, then a nested table in that column inherits 75% width for all its columns. This causes the nested table to overflow.
Workaround: Nested tables must specify fixed table layout and specify column width.? For example:d = mlreportgen.dom.Document('myreport', 'pdf');nestedTable = mlreportgen.dom.Table({ ...    '1111111', '222222', '333333';    '1111111', '222222', '333333';    '1111111', '222222', '333333';    });% Specify column width (required)nestedTableCSG = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpecGroup();nestedTableCS1 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();nestedTableCS1.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('33%')};nestedTableCS2 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();nestedTableCS2.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('33%')};nestedTableCS3 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();nestedTableCS3.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('33%')};nestedTableCSG.ColSpecs = [nestedTableCS1, nestedTableCS2, nestedTableCS3];nestedTable.ColSpecGroups = nestedTableCSG;% Specify fixed table layout (required)nestedTable.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.ResizeToFitContents(false)};% Specify nested table width (required)nestedTable.Width = '100%';layoutTable = mlreportgen.dom.Table({...    'a', ...    'b', ...    nestedTable});layoutTable.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.ResizeToFitContents(false)};layoutTableCSG = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpecGroup();layoutTableCS1 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();layoutTableCS1.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('20%')};layoutTableCS2 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();layoutTableCS2.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('20%')};layoutTableCS3 = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpec();layoutTableCS3.Style = {mlreportgen.dom.Width('60%')};layoutTableCSG.ColSpecs = [layoutTableCS1, layoutTableCS1, layoutTableCS3];layoutTable.ColSpecGroups = layoutTableCSG;layoutTable.Width = '100%';append(d,layoutTable);close(d);

Summary: imwarp?fails for 'cubic' interpolation of image with data type logical
Description: When you specify cubic interpolation, imwarp  calls the MATLAB function?interp3 , which checks that the fillValue  is numeric. When the input is logical , the fillValue ?is also logical, and so the check fails and throws the following error:  Error using interp3 (line 62)?  EXTRAPVAL must be a scalar.?    Error in images.internal.interp3d (line 73)?  ? ? outputImage = interp3(inputImage,X,Y,Z,method,fillValue);?    Error in imwarp>remapAndResampleInvertible3d (line 511)?  ? ? outputImage = images.internal.interp3d(inputImage,srcXIntrinsic,srcYIntrinsic,srcZIntrinsic,method,fillValues, SmoothEdges);?    Error in imwarp>remapPointsAndResample (line 247)?  ? ? ? ? outputImage = remapAndResampleInvertible3d(inputImage,R_A,tform,outputRef,method,fillValues, SmoothEdges);?    Error in imwarp (line 226)?  outputImage = remapPointsAndResample(parsedInputs.InputImage,R_A,tform,outputRef,method,fillValues, SmoothEdges);
Workaround: Cast fillValue to?double before calling imwarp with cubic interpolation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initial value in generated code when Variant Subsystem contains referenced export-function model
Description: Initial value is incorrect in generated code, when a Variant Subsystem has Model blocks referencing export-function models as variant choices.

Summary: bwmorph3 crashes with large inputs
Description: When you pass into bwmorph3  an input volume of size greater than intmax('int32') , MATLAB?crashes.
Workaround: To work around this issue, divide input volume into multiple chunks and process each independently, and then combine the results to get the final output.

Summary: Invalid MEX-file error using Fortran MEX files built in R2006a and earlier
Description: If a Fortran MEX file uses specific Fortran API functions and is compiled in a MATLAB release R2006a or earlier, MATLAB displays the following error message when loading the MEX file: Invalid MEX-file '<Path to MEX-file>':?  Missing symbol '<Fortran API function name>' in '<Path to the dynamic library libmx>' required by '<Path to MEX-file>'.
Workaround: Rebuild the affected MEX-file in R2009b and later.

Summary: The MATLAB function tcpclient fails to execute in a deployed application and throws an error.
Description: The MATLAB function?tcpclient   fails to execute in a deployed application and throws the following error message: Error using myFun (line 3)?  The specified superclass 'matlabshared.transportlib.internal.ITransport' contains a parse error, cannot be found on MATLAB's search path, or is shadowed by another file with the same name.?  This error occurs because the matlabshared.transportlib.internal.ITransport  function is missing from the MATLAB Runtime.?
Workaround: Include the?matlabshared.transportlib.internal.ITransport?function in the compiled application to address this issue. You can:?Add the following line to the source code that calls tcpclient:%#function matlabshared.transportlib.internal.ITransport?or?Pass the function to mcc?command using the -a flag. For example:mcc <myFun.m> -a matlabshared.transportlib.internal.ITransport

Summary: Model default storage class selected from Model Parameter Configuration dialog for tunable parameters causes compilation error
Description: In R2018a, the storage class SimulinkGlobal (auto) ?was renamed to Model default. If, in the Model Parameter Configuration dialog box, you set the storage class for a model workspace?parameter to?Model default ?to declare the parameter as tunable, Simulink returns this error when compiling the model:   Block diagram 'model ' parameter 'paramName' m ust be a comma-separated list of Auto,  ExportedGlobal,  ImportedExtern,  or ImportedExternPointer

Summary: DataValidity diagnostics related to signal overflow remain inactive when set to 'Error' and when the model contains the Discrete Filter or Discrete FIR Filterblock.
Description: If a Simulink model contains theDiscrete Filter or Discrete FIR Filter block, and the following conditionsare met in the 'Data Validity' Diagnostics pane of the Configuration Parameterswindow:  'Wrap on overflow' set     to 'Warning'  'Saturate on overflow' set to 'Warning'  and the signal wraps orsaturates on overflow, the model throws a warning message. Under the sameconditions, if the diagnostics are set to 'Error', they remain in 'None' (inactive) state and throw no error message for these blocks.
Workaround: If a Simulink modelcontains the Discrete Filter or the Discrete FIR Filter block, set the ˇ®Wrap onoverflowˇŻ and ˇ®Saturate on overflowˇŻ diagnostics to ˇ®Warning' or 'None'.Alternatively,use?the Biquad Filter block from the DSP System Toolbox library, in placeof the Discrete Filter block for fixed-point IIR filter design and implementation.?

Summary: Signal properties are not saved?correctly when a model contains?multiple Signal Editor blocks
Description: When?a model contains multiple?Signal Editor blocks, one of the blocks loses signal property settings. For example, if?a model contains two Signal Editor blocks and you?select the Interpolate data ?check box?for both blocks and save the model, one of the Signal Editor blocks will have the?Interpolate data ?check box?cleared when you reopen the model.

Summary: slreportgen.finder.DiagramElementResult getReporter returns empty reporter for?Simulink.Port?or?Simulink.Segment?object
Description: The?getReporter ? method of?slreportgen.finder.DiagramElementResult ?returns an empty reporter for a?Simulink.Port ?or? Simulink.Segment ?object.?It should return an?slreportgen.report.SimulinkObjectpProperties  reporter for each of these objects.
Workaround: Instead of calling the?getReporter method for Simulink.Port or Simulink.Segment objects, use slreportgen.report.SimulinkObjectProperties directly for those objects to get their properties.For Example?% get all the necessary headers?import slreportgen.report.*?import slreportgen.finder.*?import mlreportgen.report.*?model = "sf_car";?load_system(model)?rpt = slreportgen.report.Report('output','pdf');?chapter = Chapter();?chapter.Title = 'Diagram Element Reporter Example';?% Find Simulink.Port and Simulink.Segment objects from the model sf_car?elemFinder = DiagramElementFinder(model);?elemFinder.Types = ["Simulink.Port", "Simulink.Segment"];?elems = find(elemFinder);?for elem = elems?? ? section = Section("Title", ...?? ? ? ? strrep(elem.Type, newline,' '));?? ? ? ? % Calling SimulinkObjectProperties directly for Simulink.Port and Simulink.Segment objects to fetch the proerties? ? rptr = SimulinkObjectProperties(elem.Object);?? ? add(section,rptr);?? ? add(chapter,section);?end?add(rpt,chapter);?close(rpt);?rptview(rpt);?

Summary: External component has thrown an exception error when accessing nested abstract .NET classes
Description: If an object is an instance of a class derived from a nested abstract class, then accessing a property or calling a function that returns such an object fails with the error message: Message: External component has thrown an exception.  Source: dotnetcli  HelpLink:?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Custom model step and initialize function prototypes ignored when generating code
Description: Code generation omits model function interface modifications if both of the following conditions exist: Model parameter?RTWFcnClass ?is set to?RTW.FcnDefault , as shown in the output of?get_param(bdroot, 'RTWFcnClass') .? This condition can occur in an existing model, or if you open a new model and use a code generation template, such as Code Generation System  on the Simulink Start Page. You modify the function prototypes of model step and initialize functions using?Configure Model Functions ?on the the?Code Generation > Interface ?pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.?  Even if you configure step function arguments along with function names, the generated code does not reflect the modified interface. Generating an executable displays an error.
Workaround: Use this command to set RTWFcnClass to null:>> set_param(bdroot, ˇ®RTWFcnClassˇŻ, [])Then delete code generation folders and rebuild the model.You can set the RTWFcnClass value to null before or after configuring the model function prototypes.

Summary: Degradation-based RUL estimators generate incorrect result for remaining useful life (RUL)
Description: Predictive Maintenance Toolbox provides two degradation-based models for estimating remaining useful life (RUL):?linearDegradationModel  and exponentialDegradationModel . The RUL estimate returned when you call?predictRUL  on these models is incorrect.

Summary: IF block and its action subsystems incorrectly inherit continuous sample time
Description: If you have an IF block in a model with action subsystems that drive a Simscape network, the IF block might inherit continuous sample time from a Simscape block. This produces an incorrect simulation result.? ?

Summary: bwskel with 'MinBranchLength' option removes unbranched skeleton segments
Description: Computing pruned skeletons using the bwskel  function with the 'MinBranchLength'  option generates incomplete results. The 'MinBranchLength'  option removes all unbranched skeletons regardless of their lengths.

Summary: Reference to non-existent field 'ColumnHeaderHeight' error in Set Target Interface task of IP Core Generation workflow
Description: Sometimes, when running the IP Core Generation  workflow in the HDL Workflow Advisor, selecting the Set Target Interface  task pops up a window with this error message: Reference to non-existent field 'ColumnHeaderHeight' .? This behavior is observed only on Windows.?
Workaround: This issue can be avoided by exporting the HDL Workflow Advisor session to a script and then running the workflow at the command line. However, the target platform interfaces cannot be specified in the HDL Workflow Advisor UI.

Summary: MATLAB crashes on simulation of a model containing a commented out Discrete-Time Integrator block having an initial condition port
Description: A segmentation violation is seen on simulation of a Simulink model when the following conditions are true: The model contains a Discrete-Time Integrator block which is commented out The commented out Discrete-Time Integrator block has its initial condition source   parameter specified as external   The user opens, or navigates to, the subsystem containing the commented out Discrete-Time Integrator block before simulation of the model

Summary: Error when training dnCNNLayers using denoisingImageDatastore with Shuffle enabled in trainingOptions
Description: You can use denoisingImageDatastore  to generate noisy images to train dnCNNLayers . When training this combination, you get an error message if Shuffle  is enabled in trainingOptions . The error message is 'Index exceeds array bounds' . The error also occurs if readByIndex  method of denoisingImageDatastore  is called with index(1) > index(end) . Also, the method returns a wrong answer if the indices are not in consecutive order (equivalent to Shuffle  enabled in trainNetwork ).

Summary: CAN Receive block receives no messages from Kvaser devices when the Extended IDs Filter is set to Allow all
Description: On CAN Receive blocks configured for Kvaser devices, when the Extended IDs Filter  is set to Allow all , and the Standard IDs Filter  is set to Block all , no messages are received during simulation, even if the messages contain extended IDs.

Summary: Model block coder target error when generating code for model hierarchy with String Constant blocks
Description: An incorrect error is produced when generating code under the following circumstances: The top model contains a Model block, referencing a submodel? The submodel contains a String Constant block which connects directly to an output port The output port has constant sample time  The generated error output looks like:  Output port 1 of Model block 'top_model/Model' has a constant sample time, but the value of the output in the Model Reference Coder target differs from the value obtained during simulation.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when running models in External mode
Description: A MATLAB crash may occur when running models in External mode. The crash is more likely to happen for models configured with smaller time steps or that run for long time. The issue occurs only on the Windows operating system with certain newer CPU types, and is related to an incompatibility between the real-time binary code generated for these CPUs and the real-time kernel.
Workaround: Follow these steps to work around this issue:Open the compiler configuration file? ? ?edit(fullfile(toolboxdir('sldrt'),'rtw','sldrtclang.mk'))Navigate to line 34, which starts with?LLC :=.?Add the compiler option -mattr=-avx to the end of the line.Save the file. You need administrator privileges to save the file.Rebuild all models affected by this issue before running them.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect parentheses and inconsistent relational/logical conditions with Inline Variant blocks
Description: Incorrect parentheses may be generated in the code with? variant conditions containing equal precedence relational operators.? For example, Variant condition expression (A == B) != C gets generated in code as "A == B != C". This is because operators "==" and "!=" are defined to be left-to-right associativity in C. However, "A == (B != C)" is printed with the parentheses preserved. Additionally, inconsistent simulation behavior may occur with Variant condition expression containing relational/logical operations with integer values. For example, Variant condition expressions "A == 2" and "(A == 2) != 0" might result in different simulation behavior, even though both are semantically equivalent. Furthermore, the variant condition expression "~~A(or !(!A))" is simplified to "A" in the generated code even if A is not a Boolean.

Summary: Curve Fitting Tool (CFTOOL) is not visible with MATLAB Online
Description: When starting Curve Fitting Tool (CFTOOL) with MATLAB Online, either from the Apps Gallery or the command-line, the app does not appear. It shows the first time you open it, but does not show in subsequent session of MATLAB Online.

Summary: Code generated for?Ideal Airspeed Correction block not optimized to exclude unused lookup table data
Description: The Ideal Airspeed Correction block generates code that always contains lookup table data for all airspeeds less than Mach 5, resulting in unnecessarily large compiled executables when one of the following is true: * The block is used for subsonic applications* Method  is set to Equation?

Summary: MATLAB crashes after variable scope change in eval or script and then builtin function call
Description: If a function contains a call to eval  or a script that changes the scope of a variable to an existing global or persistent variable, then a subsequent call to a builtin function with the variable as input might cause MATLAB to crash. For example, consider the following function. function exampleFunc    for i = 1:10        eval('global x'); %changes scope of variable        y = isempty(x);    endend The following code at the Command Prompt might cause MATLAB to crash. global xx = struct;x.b = 'a';exampleFunc
Workaround: Copy the global or persistent variable into a local variable. Thenuse the local variable to call the builtin function. In the example function,assign x to a local variable.function exampleFunc    for i = 1:10        eval('global x');        z = x;        y = isempty(z);      end  end

Summary: Single-input, single-output Inline Variant within Atomic Subsystem fails model simulation
Description: An Atomic Subsystem with Bus output directly connected to Bus input through a?single-Input, single-output Inline Variant block may fail simulation with the following error: The inport block 'sample/Subsystem/In1' is directly connected to outport'sample/Subsystem/Bus Creator' and therefore cannot accept a signal with?elements of differing data types. This can be usually fixed by inserting a?Signal Conversion block with the 'Signal copy' option selected, at the?output of the inport block 'sample/Subsystem/In1'
Workaround: Insert a non-virtual block on the signal which has a?single-Input, single-output Inline Variant block.

Summary: Symbolic Math Toolbox hangs or issues an error after installing Windows 10 Spring Creator Update 2018
Description: After you install Windows 10 Spring Creator Update 2018, the symbolic engine does not respond to any input or reports this error: Internal error with symbolic engine. Quit and restart MATLAB.?  For example,?syms x ?does not resolve.

Summary: Stray processes after pop-up message:?Failed to connect to the license server. Polyspace will exit now.
Description: After being disconnected from the network, the following message pops up in the Polyspace user interface:   Failed to connect to the license server. Polyspace will exit now. After clicking OK , the Polyspace user interface closes. However, some processes associated with Polyspace, such as polyspace-code-prover.exe *32  or MATLAB.exe , continue to run in the Task Manager.
Workaround: Installation instructions using Polyspace R2016b  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.?  Exit MATLAB and Polyspace UI.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. On?Windows Systems,?you may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: imread function returns incorrect data for TIFF images that have half-precision floating-point format
Description: When reading TIFF image with half-precision floating-point sample format, the imread  function returns incorrect data. Instead of reading the half-precision floating-point data with the data type single ,  imread   returns intensity values as uint16 .

Summary: A block with only discrete states may convert from Continuous Sample Time to Fixed-In-Minor-Step
Description: If a block has only discrete states, Simulink may convert the Continuous Sample Time, either specified in the block or inherited from its source signals, to Fixed-In-Minor-Step.
Workaround: If you are authoring your own S-function, add a placeholder continuous state to avoid the issue.

Summary: Incorrect parameter values for MATLAB Function block inside Initialize Function block
Description: When a MATLAB Function block is placed inside an Initialize Function block and the MATLAB Function block has parameters that are based on variables in the MATLAB workspace, the parameter values are set to zero regardless of the values of the workspace variables.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code is possible for scalar Data Store Memory block value that is read inside reusable subsystem
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions: A Data Store Memory block?is read inside a reusable subsystem. The reusable subsystem has a single output. The Data Store Memory block is not written in the parent model of the subsystem. The Data Store Memory block variable is a scalar.  In the incorrect?code, the Data Store Memory block value may be incorrectly defined as a local variable which is not initialized.?
Workaround: Unzip the attached P-file,?workaroundForBugReport1755403.p.Insert the P-file on the MATLAB file path.For every new MATLAB session, in the Command Window, enter the following command:>> workaroundForBugReport1755403
Fixed In: R2017a Update 3, R2017b Update 7, R2018a Update 2, R2018b

Summary: Mismatch between ECG Classification example documentation and MATLAB script
Description: Clicking the Open Script  button on the Signal Classification Using Wavelet-Based Features and Support Vector Machines   example documentation page opens the MATLAB script XpwWaveletMLExample.m . There is a mismatch between the script and documentation. The example uses ECG data publicly available from PhysioNet. The documentation and script differ with regards to obtaining the data. The documentation explains in the Download Data  section how to obtain preprocessed ECG data from the GitHub repository as a single zip file. Executing?XpwWaveletMLExample.m  downloads raw ECG data from PhysioNet. After downloading, the data is resampled at 128 Hz. Downloading and resampling the raw data are time-consuming operations.  After the script downloads and resamples the data, the script and documentation match. Downloading the preprocessed data from GitHub is the recommended method.
Workaround: Go to the Signal Classification Using Wavelet-Based Features and Support Vector Machines example page.Click the?Open Script?button on the page. This action copies XpwWaveletMLExample.m and necessary helper functions to a folder in your personal MATLAB directory. The Editor opens to show XpwWaveletMLExample.m.Follow the instructions given in the Download Data?section on the example page to download the preprocessed ECG data from GitHub.Execute the first two blocks of MATLAB commands listed in the Download Data section:unzip(fullfile(tempdir,'physionet_ECG_data-master.zip'),tempdir)unzip(fullfile(tempdir,'physionet_ECG_data-master','ECGData.zip'),...? ? ? fullfile(tempdir,'ECGData'))andload(fullfile(tempdir,'ECGData','ECGData.mat'))You can now proceed one of two ways. You can eitherContinue to copy and paste the remaining commands from the example page into the command window and execute them, orExecute the commands in XpwWaveletMLExample.m, starting at line 102:percent_train = 70;[trainData,testData,trainLabels,testLabels] = ...? ? helperRandomSplit(percent_train,ECGData);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant bus?signals?along with composite ports may generate?uninitialized local variables
Description: Composite signals when used to extract bus signals may cause uninitialized?local variables when all of the below conditions are true:  The nested bus is an input or an output of a subsystem with TreatAsGroupedWhenPropagatingVariantConditions  or TreatAsAtomicUnit  selected The variant child bus signal has an active condition extracted from the composite port The variant parent bus signal has an inactive condition extracted from the composite port

Summary: Executing the exec function on an Oracle database connection many times eventually leads to a maximum open cursors exceeded error
Description: An error occurs when you execute the exec  function many (on the order of hundreds) times using a single Oracle database connection. Eventually, the following error occurs: ?SQL error: ORA-01000: maximum open cursors exceeded  This error occurs even when you execute the close  function to close each new?cursor  object created by the exec  function.??

Summary: MATLAB crashes when XCP external mode?simulation with execution-time profiling fails
Description: MATLAB crashes if you run an XCP external mode simulation with execution-time profiling enabled and? the target application fails.

Summary: Stateflow charts in a multi-instance export-function model can generate uncompilable code
Description: The code generator produces code that does not compile for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model is an export-function model. If the model is a top model, model configuration parameter Code interface packaging  is set to Reusable function . If the model is a referenced model, model configuration parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model  is set to Multiple . The model has Stateflow charts that have inputs or outputs not directly connected to root inports or outports. The Stateflow charts in the model generate functions that are called by functions generated by the same Stateflow chart.  When these conditions exist, a function generated by a Stateflow chart may use arguments that are not defined in it, which can result in generated code that does not compile.

Summary: Changing the MATLAB path can cause errors when printing or exporting figures or Simulink models
Description: Changing the MATLAB path by removing Simulink  after printing or exporting a figure or model can result in errors during subsequent printing or exporting operations. When the error occurs, the error stack includes the following:?? Undefined function or variable 'is_simulink_loaded'   Changing the MATLAB path by?adding?Simulink  after printing or exporting a figure may result in errors during subsequent printing or exporting operations.?  When the error occurs, the error stack includes the following:?? Error using checkArgsForHandleToPrint>LocalSimName2Handle
Workaround: Use any one of these approaches to temporarily work around the issue:? ?Restore the path to its original state. For more information on the path, use the command:?doc pathManually clear the private function isslhandle?using the command:?clear private/isslhandleRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Graphics might not display in figure windows on Linux machines with certain NVIDIA drivers
Description: Plotting data on Linux machines with certain NVIDIA drivers displays a figure window, but the graphics contents of the figure window might be missing.
Workaround: Start MATLAB with the -softwareopengl startup option. Note, however, that this option disables MATLAB graphics hardware acceleration.

Summary: Figure caption styles for Simulink Report API reporters not applied correctly
Description: Changing the FormalImage reporter caption style also changes the figure caption styles for these Simulink Report API reporters:  Figure reporter Diagram reporter LookupTable reporter    For example, if you update the style of the FormalImage reporter caption, add that FormalImage reporter to the report, and then, add one of the above reporters, the caption style of that reporter is incorrectly changed to match the FormalImage reporter caption style.
Workaround: To specify the style of a FormalImage Caption, create a DOM Text object with the desired styles and assign it to the Caption of the FormalImage reporter. For example,?formalImage = mlreportgen.report.FormalImage(which('ngc6543a.jpg'));text = mlreportgen.dom.Text("Cat''s Eye Nebula or NGC 6543");?text.Color = "blue";?text.Italic = true;?formalImage.Caption = text;?

Summary: Mismatch between page boundaries in the output and the Simulink Editor when using tiled printing
Description: When tiled printing is enabled, PDF and paper prints might?use different page boundaries than displayed in the Simulink Editor. The contents of one tile in the Simulink Editor might be split across multiple pages in the print.

Summary: Output from a model containing a String Constant block can be overwritten by output from?other string blocks during simulation
Description: Output from a model containing a String Constant block can be overwritten by output from other string blocks during simulation if the following conditions exist at the same time:? A String Constant block directly connects to a Display block.? ? One or more Constant blocks connect to any block that can output a string and the string output is connected only to a Display block.?

Summary: During?accelerator mode simulation, long string outputs exceeding specified maximum lengths are not truncated
Description: You?can specify the?maximum length of a string output?for blocks. For example,?specifying stringtype(10) ?for a block output data type means the output string signal can have at most 10 characters. If the length of the string signal exceeds 10 characters, the string signal?is truncated to 10 characters during normal mode simulation. However, during?accelerator mode simulation, the string signal is not truncated.?

Summary: Error when selecting a *.reqifz file from the list in Document Import options dialog
Description: When importing an a *.reqifz document into Simulink Requirements, and when there are both *.reqif and *.reqifz files present in the current directory, the Document Import options dialog will list both types of files in the drop-down control for the Source Document field. You will get an error if one of the *.reqifz files is selected: Search term must be a string array, character vector, or cell array of character vectors. ?

Summary: Simulink may crash when MATLAB System block?uses MATLAB string as?parameter value
Description: When MATLAB System block?uses any?MATLAB string as parameter value?and simulate using?code?generation,?Simulink?may crash?if Update?diagram or Simulate model is selected.
Workaround: Define parameters using char instead of string.

Summary: Links for support package documentation missing or broken after installation
Description: After installing any support package, opening the documentation browser by clicking Help  in the MATLAB desktop, or by typing doc  at the MATLAB command line, might not show the support package documentation. If the documentation is visible, the link that appears under Supplemental Software  might be broken.
Workaround: To work around this issue, create a startup.m file in your userpath folder. (If you already have a startup.m file, add the lines to it described in step 4.)Ensure you have a userpath defined by typing userpath?in the command window.NOTE: If not defined, you must issue a userpath('reset')?or userpath('<custom_folder>')?where <custom_folder> is a folder in which you have read, write and execute permissions.Navigate to your userpath folder:>> cd(userpath);?Create a startup.m file, if it does not already exist:>> edit startup.mAdd the following lines of code to the startup.m file:fprintf('startup.m invoked from %s\n', which(mfilename));spRoot = matlabshared.supportpkg.internal.getSupportPackageRootNoCreate();matlabshared.supportpkg.internal.SingleRootHandler.updateSprootValueForDoc(spRoot);matlabshared.supportpkg.internal.util.refreshDocCenter();??Close MATLAB:>> exit?Start MATLAB. You should be able to see installed support package documentation by typing doc in the command window or by clicking the Help button on the toolstrip.

Summary: Simulation does not start when running a model in real time
Description: Real-time simulation of a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model might fail to start, freeze?at Time = 0, or report a timeout when trying to run the model in real time on specific CPU hardware configurations with Windows OS.

Summary: MATLAB can crash during shutdown when data dictionary reference is removed
Description: MATLAB can crash during shutdown when all of the following conditions are true: A Simulink model links to a data dictionary that contains referenced dictionaries. The model uses a variable that is defined in one of the referenced dictionaries. The model is opened from Model Explorer or by using?open_system() . The dictionary reference that contains the variable definition is removed from the parent dictionary. After the dictionary reference is removed, MATLAB is shut down without compiling the model, simulating the model, generating code from the model, or closing the model.

Summary: J1939 Transmit block does not honor the parameter group data page bit
Description: When transmitting parameter groups with the J1939 Transmit block in Simulink, the data page bit of the PGN is always sent as 0. For a parameter group where it should be defined as 1, this results in an improper PGN.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect HDL code generated when using clock-rate pipelining optimization
Description: If you have a multirate model, that is, if your Simulink? model contains multiple sample rates or is single-rate and uses?an oversampling factor ?greater than one, by default, the clock-rate pipelining optimization is triggered. In certain cases, the generated HDL code for this model can be incorrect and results in an assertion when simulating the validation model, which indicates numeric differences.
Workaround: If you get incorrect HDL code when using the clock-rate pipelining optimization, avoid this issue by using the 'CRPWithoutFlattening' option:hdlset_param(model, 'CRPWithoutFlattening', 'off'); This option is available only in R2017b. To ensure optimization correctness, it is recommended to simulate the generated validation model. For more information, see the Generated Model and Validation Model topic in the HDL Coder documentation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for??inpolygon may return wrong answers for nonvector inputs
Description: The code generated for the??inpolygon  function allows the first two inputs to have any shape, but it may return incorrect results when the inputs are not vectors. This issue can also occur in a MATLAB Function block or Stateflow chart.?
Workaround: Convert data inputs to vectors and reshape the outputs of the function.? ? [in1,on1] = inpolygon(x(:),y(:),xv,yv); ? ? in = reshape(in1,size(x));?? ? on = reshape(on1,size(x));?

Summary: Simulink model window opens without menus and toolbars
Description: The Simulink model window sometimes opens without menus and toolbars.
Workaround: To work around this issue, run sl_refresh_customizations at the MATLAB command line.

Summary: Libraries exported to previous version can have broken links
Description: When a library has a subsystem block that contains a link to another block in the same library,  the export from R2018a to R2017b does not open correctly in R2017b. You see broken links and a "failed to load" warning.

Summary: The summary qualification report for Simulink Check includes incorrect references to Model Advisor reports
Description: Executing the DO Qualification Kit test procedure for Simulink Check generates a summary qualification report (qualkitdo_slchk_qualificationreport.html ), which includes references to the actual results for each executed test case. The report incorrectly references the same Model Advisor report instead of the results for each test case.
Workaround: Do not take into account the incorrect references to the Model Advisor report. Instead, use the Model Advisor reports provided after each test case in the summary qualification report as the actual results.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subtraction can provide incorrect results in generated HDL code
Description: In extremely rare circumstances, the HDL code generated for a fixed-point subtraction can product an incorrect result. The result is off by no more than one bit. The conditions that may produce an incorrect result require: A subtraction block where the Output data type is smaller than the input data types The subtraction block's Accumulator type indicates a type larger than the output type  Even with both of these conditions met, HDL Coder rarely generates the incorrect result.
Workaround: Set the Accumulator type of the affected Subtraction block to the same type as the Output type of the block.

Summary: The Polyspace Code Prover Tool Qualification Package artifact provides an incorrect Tool Error Detection Class for use case PCP_E6
Description: Section 2.5.3 ˇ°Tool Classification Summaryˇ± in the Polyspace Code Prover Tool Qualification Package artifact (certkitiec_codeprover_t qp ) provides an incorrect Tool Error Detection Class (TD) for use case PCP_E6 ˇ°Global variable usage analysis ¨C incorrect resultsˇ±.   TD justification claims that "The likelihood of detecting incorrectly identified usage of global variables by testing is considered to be low". However, per ISO 26262-6 TD definition, TD2 shall be selected if there is a medium degree of confidence that a malfunction and its corresponding erroneous output will be prevented or detected. Therefore, the Tool Error Detection Class for this use case should be TD3.   In summary, use case PCP_E6 has a Tool Error Detection Class of TD3 and a Tool Confidence Level of TCL3.
Workaround: Disregard the incorrect Tool Error Detection Class value of?TD2 that is?defined for use case PCP_E6 "Global variable usage analysis ¨C incorrect results". The correct Tool Error Detection Class is TD3.?

Summary: Every chapter in a Word report starts on page 1
Description: Every chapter in a Word report generated by the Report API starts on page 1. The correct default behavior is for only the first chapter to start on page 1. Succeeding chapters should continue numbering from the last page of the preceding chapter.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code can?be generated when a model contains a root-level Outport block with the parameter Ensure outport is virtual?set to on.
Description: Incorrect code?can be generated when all the following conditions are true: The model contains a root-level Outport block which has the parameter Ensure outport is virtual  set to On .? This root-level Outport block is connected to a Constant block or a block with Inf  sample-time. When the model is updated after turning on Display  > Sample time  > All , the Constant block or the above block with Inf  sample-time?displays with a pink color.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when renaming, adding or deleting files in a Simulink Project, or running dependency analysis
Description: When you run dependency analysis on a Simulink project, MATLAB can crash. Dependency analysis runs whenever you add, rename or delete project files, or you can choose to run dependency analysis. You see this crash under the following conditions: Desktop platform is Windows. The current working directory path contains a non-ASCII character. The option "Analyze C/C++ Code" is selected in the dependency analysis options menu. It is enabled by default.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, either;Avoid running Simulink Project dependency analysis when the current working directory contains non-ASCII characters. This means the project path and project sub-directories must only consist of ASCII characters.Clear the option "Analyze C/C++ Code" in the dependency analysis options menu. This prevents MATLAB from crashing, but C/C++ code analysis does not run.
