Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a MATLAB Function block that calls the?bitand ?function . A message and stack containing lines similar to those below is displayed when this issue is hit:? Assertion at b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\const\consttools.cpp line 219:  aType != nullptr    [ snip ]    Stack Trace (captured):  [snip]? [? 6] 0x0000000007ed78f7 CG::ConstTools::fitsInTypePrecisely at b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\const\consttools.cpp:221 (in F:\..\matlab\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll)  [? 7] 0x0000000007ed79e0 CG::ConstTools::fitsInTypePrecisely at b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\const\consttools.cpp:229 (in F:\..\matlab\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll)  [? 8] 0x000000000fcc1ab2 CG::transform::peephole::RemoveUnnecessaryCastsOnBitwiseExpr::matches at b:\matlab\src\cgir_xform\peephole\removeunnecessarycastsonbitwiseexpr.cpp:44 (in F:\..\matlab\bin\win64\cgir_xform.dll)  [? 9] 0x000000000fc41de4 remove_unnecessary_casts_bitwise_expr at b:\matlab\src\cgir_xform\dom_core\optimization\cg_peephole_core.cpp:2830 (in F:\..\matlab\bin\win64\cgir_xform.dll)  This issue is more likely to occur when the bitand  function is called with unsigned integer inputs.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Pulse Generator block simulation inactive level?is not zero for fixed-point data type with non-zero bias
Description: During simulation, the Pulse Generator block outputs wrong results when the value of the?Amplitude ?parameter is specified via a fixed-point data type with non-zero bias. The inactive level of the output signal will be the minimum value of the fixed-point type, rather than the value zero.? For releases?prior to?R2007b, the active level of the output signal might also be wrong when the bias of the fixed-point data type is non-zero.? Code generation outputs the correct result.?
Workaround: Set the bias of?the?Amplitude value data type?to 0 and then add a Data type conversion block after the Pulse generator block. The output data type of the data type conversion block is set as the desired fixed-point data type with non-zero bias. For?an?example of the workaround, see the?example model (saved in R2011b release).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: for?loops with at least two dependent array accesses and more than one level of nesting might be incorrect in generated code
Description: The code generator might incorrectly move a nested inner?for ?loop before the outer?for ?loop?when all of the following conditions are true:? Thefor  loops are such that the index of an array being written to in thecurrent iteration is used in a future iteration. The read access?occurs after the write access.  For example, in the below MATLAB function errorFcn , the OUT  variable contains different values when the for  loop indexed with index_j  is moved before the for  loop indexed with index_i .  MATLAB Example: ?function [OUT, P] = errorFcn(IN, OUT, P)??? for index_i = 1 : 7??????? for index_j = 2 : 15??????????? IN(index_j - 1) = 3;??????????? OUT(index_j) = IN(index_j)??????? end??????? P(index_i) = P(index_i) + 1;??? endend  The incorrectly optimized generated C code is as follows: ?/*?* Arguments??? : double IN[19]?*?????????????????????????? double OUT[325]?*?????????????????????????? double P[29]?* Return Type? : void?*/void errorFcn(double IN[19], double OUT[325], double P[29]){? int index_j;? for (index_j = 0; index_j < 14; index_j++) {??? IN[index_j] = 3.0;??? OUT[index_j + 1] = IN[index_j + 1];? }? for (index_j = 0; index_j < 7; index_j++) {??? P[index_j]++;? }}

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: If compiler options enable fast math, generated code may produce incorrect handling of non-finite floating point values
Description: Many compilers have the option to enable fast math. Examples include /fp:fast ?for Visual Studio and -ffast-math  for GCC and Clang.?  If compiler options enable fast math, these options trigger compiler optimizations that are valid only in the absence of non-finite values, such as infinity or NaN.? Enabling fast math options when non-finite values occur during execution of the the generated code produces unexpected numerical results. When selecting compiler options for Simulink Coder and other code generation products, consider the potential for your generated code to encounter non-finite floating-point values before enabling fast math options. Because fast math options improve performance of controller code, Simulink Real-Time enables compiler fast math options by default.
Workaround: For Simulink Coder and other code generation products, avoid this issue by not enabling any compiler options for fast math in your code generation configuration.For Simulink Real-Time, avoid this issue by disabling compiler options for fast math:Open the Code Generation tab in the Configuration Parameters dialog box.Select Custom?for the Compiler optimization level.Append /fp:precise to Custom compiler optimization flags.

Summary: Cannot create a new Simulink project using the Simulink start page
Description: If you try to create a new Simulink project using the Simulink start page, you can see an error. This problem occurs when all of the following conditions are true: The user machine is a Mac.? You use the "Browse Folder" button on the New Project dialog to select the location of the new project.  ?When you click OK in the New Project dialog box, you see an error dialog with the message: The first input to exist must be a string scalar or character vector.

Summary: Incorrect recognition of #pragma inline using iar-ew compiler
Description: If a #pragma inline  has been put in front of a C function in an header file, Polyspace ignores it, leading to many duplicate functions and possibly missing defects.? This can happen when using system headers of a iar-ew compiler.? For instance, it could be common in a system header to encounter such syntax:  #pragma inline=no_bodyint __my_function(double x, double y) { ... } The log file might contain many?warning messages like this: c:\path\to\my\header\file.h, line xyz: Warning: procedure '__my_function' multiply defined:| previously defined at c:\path\to\my\header\file.h, line xyz| warning when linking the 2 translation units:| c:\path\to\my\first\firstfile.c| c:\path\to\my\second\secondfile.c

Summary: Code generation from deep learning networks for TensorRT INT8 target errors out.
Description: An error is thrown when generating code for TensorRT INT8 targets. For example, when executing the command, ?cnncodegen(net,'targetlib','tensorrt','targetparams',tensorrtParams);  where tensorrtParams  is a struct containing the fields: ?DataType : INT8 ? ?DataPath : [calibration data path] ?NumCalibrationBatches : [Number of batches]?  The following error is thrown. ?Error using fprintf ?Invalid file identifier. Use fopen to generate a valid file identifier.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for a Stateflow chart?containing a?Simulink function
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that contains a mux or virtual bus signal feeding into the input port of a Simulink function that is?inside of a?Stateflow chart. In the incorrect generated code, an uninitialized local variable?may be used.
Workaround: Insert?a Signal Conversion block?directly after the creation of mux or virtual bus signal. In the Signal Conversion?block parameter dialog box, select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer?between the simulation and the generated code when string literals with C trigraphs are not escaped for the Compose String and Scan String blocks
Description: When the generated code contains a trigraph inside of a string literal, the C/C++ compiler may interpret it as the equivalent symbol, resulting in different behavior between?the simulation and the generated code?. For example, C/C++ compiler interprets "abc??=def " as "abc#def ".  This issue can occur when the toolchain settings specify language?standard options such as -std=c++11 or -std=c99 with gcc. The following list shows the trigraphs and their equivalent?symbols:?   ??=#?  ??/\?  ??'^?  ??([?  ??)]?  ??!|?  ??<{?  ??>}?  ??-~

Summary: Error when generating?Model Advisor reports in PDF and Word format
Description: When generating a Model Advisor report in PDF or Word format,?an error can occur with message: Error occurred during report generation HTML Error: expected end of tag 'p'

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer when generated code contains a trigraph inside of a string literal
Description: When the generated code contains a trigraph inside of a string literal, the C/C++ compiler may interpret it as the equivalent symbol, resulting in different behavior between?MATLAB and the generated code?. For example, C/C++ compiler interprets "abc??=def " as "abc#def ". This issue can occur when the toolchain settings specify language?standard options such as -std=c++11 or -std=c99 with gcc. The following list shows the trigraphs and their equivalent?symbols:   ??=#?  ??/\?  ??'^?  ??([?  ??)]?  ??!|?  ??<{?  ??>}?  ??-~

Summary: Bloomberg TimeOut property value is not used,?dateFormat variable error when executing the Bloomberg timeseries function
Description: Bloomberg data requests do not timeout after the time in seconds specified in the TimeOut  property of the Bloomberg connection object. Also, users receive this error message sometimes?when executing the Bloomberg timeseries  function: Undefined function or variable 'dateFormat'.??  Error in blp/timeseries>eventHandler (line 524)  ? d =  ? convertoutput(d(:,[2,1,3:end]),{'OPEN','DATE','HIGH','LOW','CLOSE','VOLUME','NUMBER_OF_TICKS','TOTAL_VALUE'},returnTable,returnTimeTable,returnDateTime,dateFormat);?

Summary: Table title styles for Report API Simulink reporters not applied correctly
Description: If the?BaseTable ?reporter?Title ?style is changed, the table Title  styles for these Simulink Report API reporters are also updated: SimulinkObjectProperties  reporter  StateflowObjectProperties  reporter  LookupTable  reporter  MATLABFunction  reporter   For example, if you update the style of the?BaseTable   reporter?Title , add that?BaseTable   reporter to the report, and then, add one of the above Report API Simulink reporters, the?Title ?style of that reporter is incorrectly changed to match the BaseTable   reporter title style.
Workaround: To specify the style of a?BaseTable?Title, create a DOM Text?object with the desired styles and assign it to the?Title?of the BaseTable reporter. For example,?bt = mlreportgen.report.BaseTable(magic(5));text = mlreportgen.dom.Text("Title for Magic Square");?text.Color = "blue";?text.Italic = true;?bt.Title = text;?

Summary: TLC error when generating code for a modelthat defines the default storage class for local parameters
Description: MATLAB might throw a TLC error when generating code for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model defines the default storage class for local parameters as an Unstructured  storage type.? The?model contains a global or shared local data store, or is the top model of a model hierarchy in which another model contains a global or shared local data store. The Initial value of the data store is either a MATLAB variable, or a Simulink.Parameter  object whose storage class is either Auto  or Model default . The configuration parameter Code Ceneration > Optimization > Default parameter  behavior is set to Tunable .  The TLC error that occurs might appear similar to the following:? Error: File: I:\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\datagrouplib.tlc Line: 464 Column: 28?The specified index (5) was out of the range 0 - 4?Main program:?==> [00] I:\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\datagrouplib.tlc:SLibCoderDataGroupElementInit(464)?????[01] I:\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\datagrouplib.tlc:SLibEmitStaticInitCoderDataGroup(939)?????[02] I:\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\datagrouplib.tlc:<NONE>(464)?????[03] I:\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\datagrouplib.tlc:SLibDeclareCoderDataGroupInstances(1190)?????[04] I:\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\formatparam.tlc:SLibFormatParam(219)? ? ??[05] I:\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\formatwide.tlc:<NONE>(309)??
Workaround: Set the Initial value of the global or shared local data store to be a Simulink.Parameter object whose storage class is neither Auto or Model default.?

Summary: Incomplete verify signal plots in Test Manager
Description: If you run a test case in Test Manager on a model that contains a Test Sequence block that uses verify , the ?test case result might show? incomplete? verify ?signal plots if there are a large number of points. ?
Workaround: If you see this issue on a?verify?signal in Test Manager, right-click and export the verify signal to a workspace variable and import it into Data Inspector. Data Inspector should be able to plot the entire verify signal. Note that you have to export the?verify?signal one at a time, e.g., export a single?verify?signal instead of the "Verify Statements" group.?

Summary: Complex fixed-point numeric objects display incorrectly in the Variables editor
Description: Complex fixed-point numeric objects display with the real and imaginary parts interspersed incorrectly.? The imaginary part of one row displays in the real part of the next row, and so on.

Summary: Incorrect variant condition propagation for models with switch blocks and variant bus signals
Description: Models with switch blocks and bus signals may cause incorrect variant condition propagation when all the below conditions are true:  The bus hierarchy at the inputs of the switch block is partially different  The bus signals are variant

Summary: Simulink.VariantManager.convertToVariant?might fail for a block with?Index exceeds array bounds?error
Description: Simulink.VariantManager.convertToVariant ?fails to convert a subsystem or a model to a variant subsystem if its connected to a block with? ShowStatePort  selected.  For example:? Consider an Integrator block having ShowStatePort  selected.If the output port of the integrator for the block's state is connected to a subsystem, then the subsystem fails convert to variant.

Summary: Error when generating HDL code for Subsystem containing Matrix Gain block with 1-D vector input
Description: If you have a design that contains a 1-D vector input to a Matrix Gain block, then generating HDL code for the design may result in a compilation error: ?Inner matrix dimensions must agree. Internal Error: This error occurred inside a MathWorks function.? For example, this error occurs if you generate HDL code for a design that contains a Gain block with?Multiplication? block parameter set to?Matrix(K*u)? or?Matrix(u*K) and gets its input from a  ?Constant block with?Interpret vector parameters as 1-D ˇŞ Treat vectors as 1-D? block parameter enabled.?
Workaround: Based on the type of multiplication being performed, change the input vector to a row or column vector by adding a Reshape block with the Output dimensionality set appropriately.

Summary: null is not an object error when?opening the Test Sequence editor
Description: If you have a model that contains a Test Sequence block, and the model start time is a variable (for example, START_TIME), opening the Test Sequence editor leads to a long error in the MATLAB console that begins with: Error using reactive>_setSimTimeAttr (line 315)TypeError: null is not an object (evaluating 'a.toExponential')  ...
Workaround: Set the model start time to a literal number (for example, 0.0).?

Summary: Using hdf5info or hdf5read on some HDF5 files crashes MATLAB
Description: Using hdf5read  and hdf5info  functions to read variable-length string type attributes from certain HDF5 files, crashes MATLAB?.  For example, these code snippets crash MATLAB: info = hdf5info('fileWithVLStringAttribs.h5');  OR? attrVal = hdf5read('fileWithVLStringAttribs.h5', '/group1/group2', 'vlStrAttribs');
Workaround: To query information about the HDF5 file, use the h5info function:?info = h5info('fileWithVLStringAttribs.h5');?To read the value of the attribute and use the h5readatt function:?attrVal = h5readatt('fileWithVLStringAttribs.h5', '/group1/group2', 'vlStrAttribs');?

Summary: Constant?value leaks out through virtual root Outport block?in?referenced model before Model block initialize port is called
Description: Under certain conditions, the Model block outputs the value of?a Constant block in a referenced model at the beginning?of a simulation?even though the initialize port of the Model block has not been called. The output of the Model block should remain zero until the initialize port of the Model block is called for the first time. This issue occurs when all of the following criteria are true: The top model contains a Model block with an?initialize port. The Model block is referencing a model that contains a root Outport block with the option?Ensure outport is virtual  selected. The root Outport block?is driven by?the output port of a conditionally executed subsystem block, for example Enabled Subsystem. The corresponding Outport block of the conditionally executed subsystem?inherits its initial value from a Constant block within the subsystem. To confirm this condition, update the block diagram for the referenced model and look for the label IC  at the input port of the Outport block in question. The presence of the Iabel indicates that the Outport block is inheriting the initial value from the Constant block. Simulate with normal or accelerator mode.

Summary: Importing groundTruth labels containing line and rectangle labels results in error.
Description: In the Ground Truth Labeler app, if you follow these steps: Export a mix of line and rectangle label types as a groundTruth object. Import the groundTruth object back into the app.  You get the following error: Error using images.internal.drawingTools.Rectangle/ set.Position (line 716)? Rectangle position must be set as [x, ?y, ?w, ?h]??

Summary: Invalid error reported when two models define scoped Simulink functions at the root level with the same name?
Description: Embedded Code can report an invalid error under these conditions:? Two Simulink models define scoped Simulink functions at the root level with the same name. The models are configured to generate C++ classes.  However, the scoped Simulink functions become class member functions, so they do not conflict in the generated code. The invalid error may appear similar to the following:? ### Build procedure for model: 'mModel2' aborted due to an error.?  Error using tlc_new?  Error: File: C:\Sandbox\Sync\R2017b\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\lib\utillib.tlc Line: 1358 Column: 8?  %exit directive:? Simulink Coder Fatal: The Simulink Function block 'getA' is being defined in both the model 'mModel1' and the?model 'mModel2'.? Remove the Simulink Function block from one of the two models, remove the slprj folder, and regenerate code for?the model hierarchy.The Simulink Function block 'setA' is being defined in both the model 'mModel1' and the model 'mModel2'. Remove the Simulink Function block from one of the two models, remove the slprj folder, and regenerate code for the model?hierarchy.
Workaround: Rename one of the two scoped Simulink functions.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initialization code?can be generated for a root-level Outport block with the parameter Ensure outport is virtual set to on
Description: Incorrect initialization code is generated when all the following conditions are true: The model contains an Outport block at the root-level?with the parameter Ensure outport is virtual  set to on . The Outport block is directly connected to a Constant block with a literal value (For example,?not specified through a MATLAB Variable).

Summary: Simulink crashes when running a model with CAN Pack blocks in accelerator mode
Description: When a Simulink model containing CAN Pack blocks runs in accelerator mode, Simulink sporadically crashes. The crash might be more likely when the model contains a large number of CAN Pack blocks.

Summary: Embedded Coder Dictionary contents missing from data dictionary when opened in new MATLAB session
Description: ?The code generator removes?code definitions defined in the Embedded Coder Dictionary part of?a data dictionary or hierarchy of data dictionaries when this sequence of tasks occurs: Open a MATLAB session. Create a data dictionary. From the Model Explorer, open the Embedded Coder Dictionary part of the new data dictionary. In the Embedded Coder Dictionary, create code definitions (storage classes, function customization templates, and memory sections). Save the data dictionary, which saves the code definitions in the Embedded Coder Dictionary. Exit the MATLAB session. Open?a new MATLAB session. Open the data dictionary saved in the previous MATLAB session. Modify contents of the data dictionary (for example, add, change, or delete design data or configurations)?and do not open the Embedded Coder Dictionary. Save the data dictionary. Exit the MATLAB session. Open a new MATLAB session. Open the Embedded Coder Dictionary of the saved data dictionary.

Summary: Execution of real-time models that contain interrupt triggered function-call subsystems call the hardware start function twice
Description: Real-time model execution calls thehardware start function twice when these conditions are both true:  The model contains a     function-call subsystem triggered by the device.   The device driver has a     hardware start function.

Summary: Cluster profile validation fails when using a proxy
Description: If you are using a proxy server, then cluster profile validation can fail with the following error: This cluster requires that your MathWorks account is associated with a MATLAB Distributed Computing Server license managed online.
Workaround: To work around this problem:    Add the following MATLAB code in your startup.m file. For more information about startup.m, see?https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/matlab_env/startup-options.html#brlkmbe-1.    % Fix proxy issues created by the authentication WS clientif verLessThan('distcomp', '6.12')    ac = parallel.internal.webclients.currentAuthenticationClient();else    ac = parallel.internal.mwa.currentAuthenticationClient();endfield = getDeclaredField(getClass(ac), 'fClient');field.setAccessible(true);awsc = field.get(ac);ep = awsc.getEndpoint();dmwscc = com.mathworks.mlwebservices.ws_client_core.DefaultMathWorksWebServiceClientConfigurator(ep);dmwscc.configureClient(awsc);Restart MATLAB and then run code on your cluster.

Summary: System Design Description report for models with requirements might produce Java exception
Description: Simulink Design Description reports for models with requirements may generate nested tables.  These nested tables cause the PDF engine to produce Java stack overflow error exception.Java exception occurred:?java.lang.StackOverflowError?at org.apache.fop.complexscripts.util.CharScript.dominantScript(CharScript.java:730)?at org.apache.fop.fonts.GlyphMapping.processWordMapping(GlyphMapping.java:121)?at org.apache.fop.fonts.GlyphMapping.doGlyphMapping(GlyphMapping.java:89)?at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.TextLayoutManager.processWord(TextLayoutManager.java:973)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.TextLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(TextLayoutManager.java:809)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.InlineLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(InlineLayoutManager.java:329)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.InlineLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(InlineLayoutManager.java:329)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.LineLayoutManager.collectInlineKnuthElements(LineLayoutManager.java:708)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.LineLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(LineLayoutManager.java:634)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.BlockLayoutManager.getNextChildElements(BlockLayoutManager.java:145)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.BlockStackingLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(BlockStackingLayoutManager.java:292)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.BlockLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(BlockLayoutManager.java:115)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.BlockLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(BlockLayoutManager.java:107)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.table.TableCellLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(TableCellLayoutManager.java:224)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.table.RowGroupLayoutManager.createElementsForRowGroup(RowGroupLayoutManager.java:119)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.table.RowGroupLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(RowGroupLayoutManager.java:65)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.table.TableContentLayoutManager.getKnuthElementsForRowIterator(TableContentLayoutManager.java:580)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.table.TableContentLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(TableContentLayoutManager.java:504)
Workaround: If you are using the Report Explorer, generate the System Design Description report using "Acrobat (PDF)" file format instead of "Direct PDF (from template)".If you are using the MATLAB Report Generator DOM API, do not add width style for ColSpec for nested tables.

Summary: Parameter Estimation tool and Sensitivity Analysis tool do not save sessions with multiple experiments
Description: When you try to save a session with multiple experiments in the Parameter Estimation tool, or multiple signal matching requirements in the Sensitivity Analysis tool, the following error is thrown in the command line: Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event ListItemSelected defined for class toolpack.component.TSDropDownPopup:  Subscripted assignment between dissimilar structures. Error in sldodialogs.data.DesignData/saveSession (line 58) ? ? ? ? ? ? LE(ct) = rmfield(le, 'OperatingPointSetup');   ...  Error in javaaddlistener>@(o,e)cbBridge(o,e,response) (line 47) ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? @(o,e) cbBridge(o,e,response));? > In toolpack.component.TSDropDownPopup>LocalListItemSelected (line 111) ? In hgfeval (line 62) ? In javaaddlistener>cbBridge (line 52) ? In javaaddlistener>@(o,e)cbBridge(o,e,response) (line 47)??

Summary: VideoReader returns distorted frames from videos recorded using mobile devices
Description: On systems with Microsoft? Windows 10, reading frames from videos recorded using mobile devices, such as an iPhone, results in a distorted output.  For example, the following code returns distorted frames: vidObj = VideoReader('iphone_portrait_orientation_video.mov');vidFrame = readFrame(vidObj);figure, imshow(vidFrame); % Distorted output?

Summary: Error when generating code that incorrectly says there are unsaved changes
Description: When you generate code, you can see an error saying that the model has unsaved changes, even when you have not made any changes.? This can happen when the model contains library links to a library which is saved in an MDL file and contains both Model blocks and Stateflow blocks. You see an error like the following: Can not update the model reference target ... because it has unsaved changes.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, save the library in an SLX file and then generate code.

Summary: regions?can return wrong output for a polyshape with multiple regions and holes
Description: For  an input?polyshape ?object containing multiple solid regions and holes, the?regions ?function might return a polyshape  with holes different from the input polyshape .

Summary: HDL Code Generation Report displays Japanese comments incorrectly in the generated code
Description: If you use Japanese comments in your model and select? Generate Traceability Report ?with? Line Level  as the? Traceability Style  in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the japanese comments are annotated incorrectly in the generated code.?
Workaround: Use?Comment Based as the??Traceability Style when you select?Generate Traceability Report and generate HDL code for your model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inactive Variant Bus signals with composite ports may generate uninitialized variables
Description: Simulink model with variant bus signals using composite ports may fail build with uninitialized variables in generated code when all of the below conditions are true: A non-virtual bus feeds the composite ports in a subsystem Some of the composite ports which select signals from buses are inactive Composite ports which select other remaining signals from the same bus are active

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized local variables generated with Variant Subsystem when CombineOutputUpdateFcns is selected in Configuration Parameters
Description: For ERT-based targets, uninitialized local variables may be generated in code for Simulink models with Variant Subsystem when all the following conditions are met:  The variant subsystem has the GeneratePreprocessorConditionals  parameter selected.   Configuration Parameters> Code Generation >Interface ?pane>Single output/update function  is selected. A direct feedback loop exists from an output port of the variant subsystem to the input port.  A variant subsystem choice involved in the direct feedback loop consistently breaks the loop from within the choice itself.  A variant subsystem choice not involved in the direct feedback?loop has a state update block (example,Unit Delay) connected to a block capable of creating local variable.
Workaround: Any of following workarounds help avoiding the incorrect behavior:In the Code Generation > Interface pane, disable Single output/update function.Ensure that the algebraic loop is not broken from within the variant subsystem choice.Break the algebraic loop by connecting the Unit Delay block between the output and input of the variant subsystem.

Summary: invalid unordered_map<K,?T> key error during code generation
Description: An error may occur during code generation for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a For Each subsystem. The For Each subsystem input signal has a Reusable custom storage class or is a partitioned input signal from a Data Store Read block, an in-place MATLAB Function block, or an in-place MATLAB System block.  The error message states invalid unordered map <K, ?T> key .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Block output signal memory reuse in generated code when multiple String Find blocks drive multiple virtual root Outport blocks
Description: The generated code might incorrectly reuse the signal memory for a String Find block under the following conditions: A model contains multiple root Outport blocks with parameter Ensure outport is virtual  selected. Each of the root Outport blocks is driven by a distinct String Find block.

Summary: regionprops3 returns incorrect results for 'ConvexImage'
Description: The?'ConvexImage'  property of regionprops3  returns incorrect results for non-square sized regions in a binary volume.

Summary: Using hdf5read on some HDF5 files crashes MATLAB
Description: Using the hdf5read  function to read an empty dataset of type variable-length string from an HDF5 file crashes MATLAB. For example, the following code snippet crashes MATLAB:   dset = hdf5read('fileWithVLStringEmptyDataset.h5','/group1/group2/vlStrEmptyDataset');
Workaround: To read the dataset, use the h5read function:  dset = h5read('fileWithVLStringEmptyDataset.h5','/group1/group2/vlStrEmptyDataset');

Summary: No indication MATLAB is busy when using the?Run Section button in the MATLAB Editor
Description: When running code using the Run Section ?button in the MATLAB Editor, MATLAB does not show any indication that it is busy. The code runs, but there are no changes to the display to indicate that the code has run.

Summary: Live Editor does not open on Windows Server 2016
Description: The Live Editor fails to open on Windows Server 2016. This failure might be accompanied by the following error message: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NullPointerException?  at com.mathworks.mde.liveeditor.LiveEditorApplication.openAsLiveCode(LiveEditorApplication.java:524)?  at com.mathworks.mde.liveeditor.OpenAsLiveCodeActionProvider$1.run(OpenAsLiveCodeActionProvider.java:71)?  at com.mathworks.mde.liveeditor.OpenAsLiveCodeActionProvider$1.run(OpenAsLiveCodeActionProvider.java:62)?  ... ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation might fail to produce an error for an unitialized property in a variable-size structure array
Description: ?Code generation might fail to produce an error when all of the following conditions are true: A?structure array?has a variable size. A field of the structure array contains a class. The class has an uninitialized property.  When this happens, the generated code produces a different answer than MATLAB. ?For example, consider the following class classdef val    properties        contents    end    methods        function this = val(contents)            if nargin >= 1                this.contents = contents;            end        end    endend Code generation for the following function does not ?produce an error. The generated code does not match MATLAB. function result = foocoder.varsize('local', [1 3], [0 1]);local = struct('field', {val(0) val});local(1).field.contents = 0;result = local(2);

Summary: Unable to install support packages to the default directory on MAC and Linux
Description: When trying to install a support package on Mac or Linux, the installation might fail with the installation logs indicating an invalid support package root directory:  No such file or directory?? This can occur if the userpath had been cleared with the command userpath('clear') .
Workaround: To work around this issue, execute a userpath('reset') command in MATLAB and then try installing again.

Summary: Loading model with Simulink function crashes MATLAB
Description: Loading a model with a Simulink Function block can crash MATLAB when the following conditions are satisfied: The Simulink function has an argument whose name is the same as the function. The Simulink function is either global or scoped and placed at the root-level of the model.

Summary: Lookup Table Editor cannot make corresponding changes?to the?Lookup Table object if the table data dimension is modified
Description: Lookup Table editor does not update the Table data  and Breakpoints N  parameter of the Lookup Table block when saving data back to the original Lookup Table block if these conditions are true: Lookup Table data (table and breakpoint data) is     specified?for a Lookup Table object. For example, the Data specification  parameter is set to Lookup table object . Adding or deleting a row or column in the Lookup Table Editor changed?the dimensions of the?table and/or breakpoints.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results when simulating a Reset Function block containing conditionally executed subsystems
Description: When a Reset Function block contains conditionally executed subsystems, such as Enabled Subsystem, If-Action Subsystem, Switch Case Action Subsystem, or Function-Call Subsystem blocks, the conditions?that activate the subsystems are ignored during simulation, and the contents inside these subsystems always execute, regardless of the condition values. The code generated for the Reset Function block is not affected by this issue. Therefore, a mismatch between the results from simulation and code generated can be observed.

Summary: VideoReader fails to read MP4 files with no audio stream
Description: On the Windows? platform, the VideoReader  function fails to read MP4 files that do not contain an audio stream and returns this error message: Error using VideoReader/init (line 601) Unexpected exception in plug-in: 'Unable to determine the number of channels in the audio stream Reason: Unknown error 0xC00D36E6' Error in VideoReader (line 171) obj.init(fileName);

Summary: rmidocrename produces broken URLs when applied to links where destination documents are URLs
Description: Simulink Requirements product on Microsoft Windows makes a mistake?of replacing?"/" with "\"? in URLs.?? ?Details: When using rmidocrename to update parts of URLs, the result is a broken URL For example, if your links point to  "https://application.server.com/proj1/config1/component1/..."?? and you now want to target the same document in the same project and the same component but with a different configuration config2, running the following command will produce invalid links: >> rmidocrename(MODEL, '/config1/', '/config2/')?  The modified URLs will look like "https:\\application.server.com\proj1\config2\component1",? which is incorrect.

Summary: Extrinsic function error when running a model-referenced Fuzzy Logic Controller block in Accelerator, SIL, or PIL simulation mode
Description: Simulink throws an error when you run a model in which both of the following conditions are true: A Fuzzy Logic Controller block is inside of a referenced model You simulate the model in Accelerator, SIL, or PIL mode  Under these conditions, the software attempts to compile an extrinsic function, which causes the following error: Error:The extrinsic function 'feval' is not available for standalone code generation. It must be eliminated for stand-alone code to be generated. It could not be eliminated because its outputs appear to influence the calling function. Fix this error by not using 'feval' or by ensuring that its outputs are unused.?
Workaround: Open the fuzblkobsolete block library. At the MATLAB command line, type?fuzblkobsolete.In your model, replace the Fuzzy Logic Controller block with the FIS Wizard block from the fuzblkobsolete library, and Specify the?FIS structure parameter of the new block.

Summary: Misleading "must be a valid scope identifier" message from incomplete real-time interrupt configuration
Description: If the real-time interrupt configuration for the model selects the Real-time interrupt source ?as Auto (PCI only) ?and does not select a specific I/O board generating the interrupt , the model build fails with an error message similar to:? "The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier"   The real-time interrupt configuration for the model must indicate the board from which the interrupt comes. To resolve the issue, use Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Simulink Real-Time Options > Real-time interrupt source  to select a board as the interrupt source.

Summary: Accessing compound HDF5?datasets containing long member names crashes MATLAB?
Description: Attempting to read or query information from HDF5 files about compound datasets, that have member names longer than 63 characters, crashes MATLAB?. For example, any of the following code snippets will result in a crash: dsetInfo = h5info('myfile.h5', '/dsetWithLongFieldNames');  OR  data = h5read('myfile.h5', '/dsetWithLongFieldNames');  OR  fileID = H5F.open('myfile.h5');dsetID = H5D.open(fileID, '/dsetWithLongFieldNames');data = H5D.read(dsetID);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model with variant subsystem might generate uninitialized local variable with build warnings
Description: Models with variant subsystem generates uninitialized?local variables when all the following conditions are true:  Function call subsystem as variant choices  Function call signal routed through data inputs of variant subsystem  Bus signal routed through the output of a variant subsystem Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals ?is selected   The following warning is displayed during the build process when?Code Generation>Build process>Generate code only  is not selected in Configuration Parameters  : warning: 'rtb_FcnCallSubsysAtOut1_0' is used uninitialized in this function

Summary: MATLAB crashes when a parallel for-loop has variable bounds and requires large local memory exceeding the threshold
Description: For a parallel for-loop requiring large local memory, GPU Coder maps the for-loop into a kernel and allocates the local variables on the GPU's global memory. In previous versions, GPU coder only supported loops with known maximum bounds, so the number of threads launched for the kernel is assumed to be a compilation time constant. However, with support for loops with variable bounds, this assumption is no longer valid and parallel for-loops with variable bounds and large local memory requirements may cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Propagated signal labels might be incorrect when a referenced model has virtual bus outputs
Description: When a referenced model outputs a virtual bus, the propagated signal labels from the Model block might be incorrect.  These incorrect signal labels might result in errors. For example, if those signals are inputs to a Bus Creator block that requires specific signal names, the mismatch produces an error.?
Workaround: Insert Signal Conversion blocks after the Model block and rename the output signal copies.?

Summary: Simulink linking option missing in RMI dialogs and menus
Description: Some Simulink options and shortcuts might not be in RMI dialogs and menus if you?complete either of these actions:?? Open the?Requirements context menu for a selection in an?M-file before loading Simulink. Launch the Requirements Editor (for a non-Simulink source) before loading Simulink.  The options missing are:? Link to Selection in Simulink  option in the Requirements context menu. Simulink option in the Document Type  drop-down selector when you create a new link in the Link Editor dialog box.
Workaround: Execute the following command to refresh context menus:>> rmiml.selectionLink([])?

Summary: Out-of-memory error when running the Semantic Segmentation Using Deep Learning example.
Description: The SemanticSegmentation Using Deep Learning  example runs out of memory whenprocessing too many batched images. It also can run out of memory if the images are too large. If you do not change the MiniBatchSize  property to a smaller value, then the example tries to process too many images at one time. The example displays the following error when trying to evaluate the trained network: >> pxdsResults = semanticseg(imdsTest,net,'WriteLocation',tempdir,'Verbose',false);?  Error using nnet.internal.cnngpu.poolingMaxBackward2D  Out of memory on device. To view more detail about available memory on the GPU, use 'gpuDevice()'. If the problem persists, reset the GPU by calling 'gpuDevice(1)ˇŻ.    Error in nnet.internal.cnn.layer.util.MaxPooling2DWithOutputIndicesGPUStrategy/forward (line 22)  ? ? ? ? ? ? argmax = nnet.internal.cnngpu.poolingMaxBackward2D(...    Error in nnet.internal.cnn.layer.MaxPooling2D/predict (line 100)  ? ? ? ? ? ? Z = this.ExecutionStrategy.forward( ...    Error in nnet.internal.cnn.DAGNetwork/activations (line 331)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? outputActivations = thisLayer.predict(XForThisLayer);    Error in DAGNetwork/activations (line 849)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? YBatch = this.PrivateNetwork.activations(X, layerIndex, layerOutputIndex);    Error in semanticseg>iClassifyImagePixels (line 384)  ? ? allScores = activations(net, X, name, ...? ?    Error in semanticseg>iProcessImageDatastoreInParallel (line 616)  ? ? ? ? L = iClassifyImagePixels(X, net, params);    Error in semanticseg (line 183)  ? ? ? ? filenames = iProcessImageDatastoreInParallel(imds, net, params);
Workaround: To fix this error, set the MiniBatchSize property in the semanticseg function to a value smaller than the default value of 128, for example 4:pxdsResults = semanticseg(imdsTest, net, 'MiniBatchSize', 4, 'WriteLocation', tempdir, 'Verbose', false);?You many need an even smaller value for? MiniBatchSize, depending on the amount of GPU memory on your system.??

Summary: Quick Insert does not show list of recommended blocks
Description: Quick Insert fails to list recommended blocks when invoked from the end of an unconnected signal line. Normally, Quick Insert displays a list of recommendations based on the upstream block in the signal path.

Summary: GPU coder does not recognize custom files with the .hpp or .cu extension when using the codegen command
Description: When using the codegen  command, you can specify custom files to include in the build.?However, GPU Coder throws an error when you include the following types of files: CUDA file (.cu) Header file (.hpp)

Summary: MATLAB can crash when a submodel?contains a constant signal?feeding a string block and runs in accelerator mode
Description: MATLAB can crash when simulating a model reference hierarchy in which?the following conditions are met: A submodel of the hierarchy runs in accelerator mode The submodel?contains a constant?signal feeding a string block The constant string signal is not an output signal from a String Constant block  For example,?a?model reference?hierarchy?submodel that contains?a Constant block feeding a To String block?running in accelerator mode can cause a crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MinimizeClockEnables incorrectly removes clock enables for Model Reference blocks during HDL code generation
Description: In a model containing a referenced model, if the?Minimize clock enables ?check box in the HDL Code Generation > Global settings  pane is selected, the generated HDL code for the referenced model might be incorrect.

Summary: Java NullPointerException when closing a Simulink Project with a Simulink model open
Description: If you close a Simulink Project while Simulink models are loaded, you can sometimes see a Java NullPointerException in the command window.
Workaround: You can ignore the Java exception and continue working. To prevent the exception appearing, close all Simulink models before closing the Simulink Project.

Summary: Variant Subsystems with?export function models as choices might cause build failure
Description: Models containing variant subsystems fails build process when all of the conditions are true :  Variant subsystem has export function models as choices  Variant subsystem has default variant control specified   Example error message:  CGIR assertion 'ifNode != nullptr && mergeNode != nullptr' failed in 'b:\matlab\src\rtwcg\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_variant_transform.cpp:267'

Summary: Synchronous machine Canay Inductance causes decrease in fault current and affects the speed of the machine in the example model power_machines
Description: Synchronous Machine parameter mapping algorithm (from standard to fundamental) was updated in R2018a to include the affect of Canay inductance. With this change, the machines fault current has decreased significantly and the speed of the machine in the example model ?'power_machines'?decreases incorrectly.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU Coder creates a kernel with uninitialized variables under certain conditions.
Description: Under certain conditions, GPU Coder? may create a kernel without including the necessary parameter in the parameter lists. Instead, it creates uninitialized local variables that results in generating a warning during code compilation and incorrect results at run-time. For example, if the MATLAB code contains imperfectly nested parallel loop that is transformed to a perfectly nested loop, compilation of the generated code results in the "Variable <name> is used before its value is set" warning and incorrect results at run-time.

Summary: ?Code generator triggers software assertion for models that include a Simulink function?that calls?another?Simulink function
Description: The code generator can trigger a software assertion when generating C or C++ code for an Export-Function model that contains a Simulink function that calls another Simulink function: CGIR assertion 'isa(aConst)' failed in 'export/include/cg_ir/const/Fcn.hpp:539'?  This can occur for valid combinations of Simulink functions and Simulink function callers, where the caller (Function Caller block, Stateflow chart, or MATLAB function block) is in a global Simulink function (Simulink Function block, exported Stateflow graphical function, or exported Stateflow MATLAB function) or in a root-level scoped Simulink Function block.

Summary: Margin, border, and padding formats corrupt PDF style sheet
Description: Specifying a border, margin, or padding format in a PDF style sheet corrupts the style sheet. The first use of a style containing one of these formats overrides their occurrence in any other style. For example, consider the following style sheet: p.GreenStyle {??? color: green;??? font-size: 12pt;??? font-style: italic;??? top-margin: 16pt;??? bottom-margin: 0pt;?}?  p {??? color:blue;??? font-size: 11pt;??? font-style: normal;??? top-margin: 0pt;??? bottom-margin: 10;}?  If a report?applies?GreenStyle  to?the first paragraph in a report, all subsequent paragraphs?have a 16pt  top margin and a 0pt  bottom?margin, including paragraphs that specify another style or?do not specify a style.?
Workaround: Specify CSS?border, margin (represented by the DOM OuterMargin format), and padding (InnerMargin) formats programmatically, for example,import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('myreport', 'pdf', 'mytemplate');pStyle = {OuterMargin('0pt', '0pt', '10pt', '0pt')};append(d, Paragraph('Green Style', 'GreenStyle'));para = append(d, Paragraph('Base Style'));para.Style = [para.Style pStyle];para = append(d, Paragraph('Base Style'));para.Style = [para.Style pStyle];close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);?

Summary: DOM API HTML and HTMLFile objects incorrectly convert?vertical-align?format
Description: The DOM API HTML  and HTMLFile  objects may incorrectly convert the? vertical-align:super ?or?vertical-align:sub ? format. These values can also corrupt a DOM-generated Word document.
Workaround:
Instead of using vertical-align:super or vertical-align:sub?format in?HTML and HTMLFile objects, use the HTML or tag, respectively. For example, instead of using

This text contains superscript text.

use

This text contains superscript text.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Running the code generated from a SegNet network produces incorrect results
Description: For the code generated from a SegNet deep learning network, successive runs of the predict  method returns incorrect results. For example, if predict? was? called on successive frames of an input video, it returns incorrect outputs for the successive frames.

Summary: Unnecessary include of Rte_Type.h in model_types.h
Description: For AUTOSAR model builds, the header file Rte_Type.h  is included in model _types.h  file, even if model _types.h  file does not depend on contents of Rte_Type.h  file.
Workaround: Download the attached .zip file.Unzip the attachment and save the workaroundForBugReport1740170.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path.?Run this script in each MATLAB session before opening your model.

Summary: Running generated code from deep learning networks fails when the executable is generated inside codegen directory
Description: With Deep learning code generation workflow, users can automatically generate a static library and link it with their custom main file to generate an executable. If the executable is generated within the codegen  directory that contains the generated static library and the network parameter files, code generation may throw an assertion in the function MWBatchNormalizationLayerImpl::iLoadParamOntoGPU ?
Workaround: As a workaround, generate and run the executable from the parent directory of codegen directory that contains the generated static library and the network parameter files.

Summary: Use of tall arrays and mapreduce with MATLAB Parallel Cloud produces an error
Description: If you use features such as tall arrays and mapreduce  that use the isequal  method with MATLAB Parallel Cloud clusters, you see the following error:   Error using containers.Map/subsref  The specified key is not present in this container.     Features that use this functionality throw this error when using a parallel pool running on MATLAB Parallel Cloud as their execution environment. For example:   pool = parpool('MATLAB Parallel Cloud');   ds = datastore('airlinesmall.csv');   T = tall(ds);

Summary: MATLAB? Production Server? dashboard consumes a large percentage of CPU resources.
Description: When the MATLAB? Production Server? dashboard has been runningfor a few months you will notice a large consumption (20-40%) of CPU resourcesby the postgres  process. Opening additional instances of the dashboard furtherincreases CPU consumption and significantly slows down the processing ofrequests by the server.
Workaround: 1.??????Download the attached .zip file and unzip it. 2.??????Remove the currently running dashboard. Forinstructions, see RemoveMATLAB Production Server Dashboard.3.??????Navigate to the following location: ? ? ?<matlab_production_server_install_location>\<release>\dashboard\main\config? ? ? ? ? Example: ? ? ?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\MATLAB ProductionServer\R2018a\dashboard\main\config4.??????Replace the file named db-postgres.sql with theunzipped file .sql file.5.??????Set up the dashboard one more time. For instructions,see SetUp and Log In to MATLAB Production Server Dashboard.

Summary: Test harnesses and their file dependencies do not appear in the Simulink Project dependency analysis
Description: If the Simulink model contains a test harness but no file dependencies, the test harness does not appear in the Simulink Project dependency analysis. Any file dependencies of the test harness do not appear either.?

Summary: Simulating a model with signal logging enabled in more than one instance of MATLAB results in crash or corrupt data logs
Description: When two or more MATLAB instances simulate any Simulink model with signal logging enabled, MATLAB might crash. Any logged data might have missing or incorrect values.
Workaround: Run the attached disablePersistentDMR p-file in each instance of MATLAB before starting the simulations.?

Summary: XCP Data Acquisition block does not let you specify the DAQ List Priority?
Description: The XCP Data Acquisition block does not provide a mask parameter to specify the DAQ List Priority value.?  The priority value defaults to 0. This value could cause?an unexpected accumulation of?XCP packets before transmission to the master.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow absolute time temporal logic incorrectly evaluated for small integer types
Description: Stateflow transitions guarded by absolute time temporal logic might be evaluated as true early if all of the following conditions are true: The chart uses MATLAB as the action language. The first argument P  to the temporal logic operator is a Stateflow data of small integer type (for example, uint8). The time unit used in the absolute time temporal logic operator is greater than the sample time Ts  of the chart (for example, using after(P, msec)  with Ts  < 0.001).  Instead of staying P / Ts  number of chart wakeups in that state, the transition will be taken on the value of P / Ts  wrapped to the data type of P .
Workaround: In the temporal logic operator, cast the first argument?P?to uint32, for example:after(uint32(P),msec)?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assignments to a handle class object in a MATLAB Function block might be incorrectly missing when there is an alias to the?object
Description: The code generator contains an optimization that removes redundant assignments to a handle class object. The code generator might incorrectly remove an assignment when all of these conditions are true:? There are at least two consecutive writes to the handle class object. There is at least one read from an alias to the handle class object.  For example, consider this MATLAB function in a MATLAB Function block:   function A? = model_1738576(A)??? handle_A = handleClassObj;??? handle_A.value = A;??? handle_alias_A = createMCOSAlias( handle_A );??? handle_A.value(3,4) = handle_alias_A.value(1,4);??? handle_A.value = handle_alias_A.value;??? A = handle_alias_A.value;end?    The incorrectly generated code, where the write to handle_A.value(3,4)  is removed, might look like:  static void model_1738576_output(void){? uint32_T rtb_A[48];? handleClassObj_model_1738576_T handle_A;? handleClassObj_model_1738576_T *handle_alias_A;? memcpy(&rtb_A[0], &model_1738576_P.IA2_2_1[0], 48U * sizeof(uint32_T));? memcpy(&handle_A.value[0], &rtb_A[0], 48U * sizeof(uint32_T));? handle_alias_A = model_1738576_createMCOSAlias(&handle_A);? handle_A = *handle_alias_A;? memcpy(&rtb_A[0], &handle_alias_A->value[0], 48U * sizeof(uint32_T));? .........}  The correctly generated code looks like: static void model_1738576_output(void){? uint32_T rtb_A[48];? handleClassObj_model_1738576_T handle_A;? handleClassObj_model_1738576_T *handle_alias_A;? int32_T i;? memcpy(&rtb_A[0], &model_1738576_P.IA2_2_1[0], 48U * sizeof(uint32_T));? memcpy(&handle_A.value[0], &rtb_A[0], 48U * sizeof(uint32_T));? handle_alias_A = model_1738576_createMCOSAlias(&handle_A);? handle_A.value[38] = handle_alias_A->value[36];? handle_A = *handle_alias_A;? ......... }?  To reproduce this issue, see the example in the attached zip file example_1738576.zip

Summary: Error : "Could not reserve enough space for object heap" with a large amount of RAM when launching the Polyspace UI
Description: On systems with a large amount of RAM, the Polyspace user interface can crash at launch time with this message: Error occurred during initialization of VM  Could not reserve enough space for object heap  Error: Could not create the Java Virtual Machine.  Error: A fatal exception has occurred. Program will exit.  A problem occurred while executing polyspace-bug-finder !?
Workaround: On Linux, the Polyspace user interface is launched using a script (polyspace-code-prover andpolyspace-bug-finder). You can modify these scripts to limit the amount of RAM allocated to the Java virtual machine.?Look around the line 300. You will see something like:?# and the maximum ofmmax and 256if [ $mmax -lt 256 ]; then mmax=256fi[ $opt_mx -eq 0 ]&& mem_max="$mmax"mThe workaround is to add these lines:?if [ $mmax -gt 4000] ; then mmax=4000fi ?after the first if?block, so the modified code is:?# and the maximum ofmmax and 256if [ $mmax -lt 256 ]; then mmax=256fiif [ $mmax -gt 4000] ; then mmax=4000fi [ $opt_mx -eq 0 ]&& mem_max="$mmax"mThis will limit the maximum amount? of RAM to 4Gb.

Summary: MATLAB crash?when running MEX generated with integrity checks disabled
Description: MATLAB might crash when you?run MEX that was generated with integrity checks?disabled. The crash can occur even when the code does not have run-time errors.?
Workaround: Turn on the IntegrityChecks MATLAB Coder configuration setting and regenerate the MEX file. The equivalent setting in the MATLAB Coder app is Ensure memory integrity.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you simultaneously plot multiple channels of a logged constant matrix signal in the Simulation Data Inspector
Description: MATLAB crashes when you follow these steps to plot multiple channels of a logged constant matrix signal: ? ? 1. Simulate a model that logs a constant matrix signal.? ? ? 2. Open the Simulation Data Inspector and hold Ctrl ?or Shift ?to select?two channels from the matrix.?? ? ? 3. Press the space bar to plot the selected channels in the Simulation Data Inspector.? ?The stack trace for the crash includes this line: 0x000000015b47cbb4? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? bin\win64\shared_sdi_mcos.dll+03591092 sdi::cacheDeinterleavedData+00003588

Summary: MATLABdoes not apply property validation to Abstract properties if their SetAccessis private or immutable
Description: A bug in MATLAB prevents the validation ofproperty default values when a superclass declares a property asAbstract with a SetAccess of private or immutable. The followingexample demonstrates this bug. SuperclassAbstractProp  defines an Abstract property Foo  that is restricted to adouble scalar?value by?its property validation. Subclass MyClass  implements this property and assigns a 1x3 double value as the default value. This default valueshould result in an error when creating an instance of MyClass ?because thevalue is not a scalar. However, MATLAB does not issue an error. Instead the Foo  property in MyClass  is assigned the nonscalar default value. classdef (Abstract) AbstractProp?  ? ? properties (Abstract, SetAccess = immutable)?  ? ? ? ? Foo (1,1) double;?  ? ? end?  end  classdef MyClass < AbstractProp?  ? ? properties (SetAccess = immutable)?  ? ? ? ? Foo = 1:3;?  ? ? end?  end  >> b = MyClass?  b =?  ? MyClass with properties:?  ? ? Foo: [1 2 3]

Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code using ?MATLAB Coder with an assertion error referencing "LocalAnalysisImpl.cpp line 1138" (Linux, Mac) or?"localanalysisimpl.cpp line 1138" (Windows).
