Summary: Analysis on the replacement model may report objectives as?Undecided with testcase?
Description: If the replacement model consists of a?For Iterator  or a While Iterator  block,Simulink Design Verifier may report the decision objectives for these blocks as  Undecided with testcase.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect behavior for the code generated for a Probe block whose input has a constant sample time.
Description: When a Probe block connects to the output of a block with constant sample time, ?and the Probe block outputs the sample time of its input signal, the expected value of that Probe block output is [Inf 0] . Normal and Accelerated simulation modes provide the expected result.?However, ?in the generated code the value of that Probe block output will be [Inf -1].
Workaround: To check if the input to the Probe block has a constant sample time, consider checking only whether the first element of the output array?of the Probe block is Inf, since the constant sample time is the only sample time that uses Inf as the value of the first element.

Summary: Initialize block or Terminate block in model produces build error
Description: If a Simulink Real-Time model contains an Initialize block or a Terminate block, builds of the model fail.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for the n-D Lookup table block with Interpolation method?Nearest?and Remove protection against out-of-range input in generated code?check box selected
Description: Incorrect?code for the n-D Lookup table block can generate incorrect output when all of the following conditions are true:? The Interpolation method ?is set to Nearest  The Index search method ?is set to Linear   search  The Remove protection against out-of-range input in generated code ?check box is selected The value of any input is nearest?to the last index value ofits breakpoints
Workaround: Do one of the following:Set the?Index search method?to be?Binary?searchClear?the?Remove protection against out-of-range input in generated code?check boxReplace the n-D Lookup Table block with one or more Prelookup blocks connected to?an Interpolation Using Prelookup block?and match the corresponding settings
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 6, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: Configuration sets stored in the data dictionary might be reset to the default
Description: A configuration set stored in a data dictionary might be reset to the default if the data dictionary is created on an installation of MATLAB with add-on products and then opened on an installation without add-on products.  For example, if a configuration set is stored in a data dictionary on a machine in which Simscape is installed and is opened on a machine in which Simscape is not installed, the configuration set is reset to the default.

Summary: Incorrect code may be generated for fraction output of Prelookup block?in models containing multiple similar Prelookup blocks
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for the?fraction output of Prelookup blocks?under these conditions Model contains at least two Prelookup blocks Index search method  of each Prelookup block is set to Evenly spaced points  Data type of input signal and Breakpoints  data type?are not the same Breakpoints  data type of each block is a fixed-point data type The word length of?one or more?fixed-point data type differs from the word length of the other Breakpoints  data types The word length of each of the fixed-point data types must have the same container word length
Workaround: Set the word length of?all Prelookup Breakpoints data types to the same?length.
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 7, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: High CPU usage is seen when MATLAB is at debug prompt.
Description: For a MATLAB function foo, set a breakpoint in foo, and break inside debugger in foo as shown below. ? ? ?>> dbstop in foo; foo;? ? ? ?k>>?? Now, check the CPU usage. It will be high.?

Summary: Error when packaging a deployable archive with a function signature file using the Production Server Compiler app
Description: Sometimes, when you package a deployable archive with a function signature file using the Production Server Compiler app, you get the following error message without additional information:  Failed to build CTF file. ?  There are two scenarios when the error details are omitted: The function signature file contains a signature for a MATLAB function that was not added to the "EXPORTED FUNCTIONS" list. The number of input or output parameters specified in a function signature does not match the actual number of parameters in the function implementation.?
Workaround: Generate a new function signature file template with correct signatures by following these steps:Note the file name of the existing signature file.Click "Remove File" to remove the signature file from the project.??Click "Create File" to generate a new signature file.?Use a diff tool to compare the new file and the old file, and identify any extra functions or incorrect numbers of input or output parameters.

Summary: audioread?returns empty matrix when reading certain MP4 files?on Windows?
Description: The  audioread  function returns an empty matrix when reading certain MP4 files on the Windows? platform.   S pecifically, on Windows 10 systems, if the file contains a mid-stream format change, then audioread  returns an empty matrix.

Summary: MATLAB crashes sporadically when using AMD graphics hardware
Description: On Windows systems, MATLAB might crash sporadically when using an AMD graphics card with driver version 8 or 9.
Workaround: To work around the issue, update the AMD graphics card driver to latest available version. Alternatively, you can switch to using software OpenGL by specifying the -softwareopengl option on MATLAB startup or by running the following command in your MATLAB session:>> opengl software

Summary: MATLAB might crash while displaying or printing graphics
Description: MATLAB might crash while displaying graphics on-screen, or printing them to a file or printer. These crashes are more likely to occur when you are displaying many figures, creating large image files, or have a large numbers of graphics objects.? When the error occurs, a crash report dialog is shown that contains a message with the following form: ------------------------------------------------------------------------  Assertion detected at <date and time>   ------------------------------------------------------------------------  ??  ? <Computer configuration information>   ?  Fault Count: 1?  Assertion in foundation::usm::Detail<struct foundation::usm::scope::Mvm>::find at b:\matlab\foundation\usm\management.cpp line 559:  find: no active context for type 'struct `anonymous namespace'::JavaClassList'
Workaround: In some cases you might be able to avoid the issue by increasing the Java heap memory that MATLAB uses:Open the MATLAB Preferences dialog.Select MATLAB > General > Java Heap Memory in the list of preferences.Drag the slider to the right to increase the memory available in Java.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: VideoReader causes MATLAB? to crash when reading certain Motion JPEG AVI files on Windows?
Description: VideoReader  crashes MATLAB when reading certain MotionJPEG AVI files on Windows platforms. S pecifically, MATLAB might crash on Windows 10 systems running the Intel Media SDK.
Workaround: Turn off? the Hardware Acceleration option using>>matlab.video.read.UseHardwareAcceleration('off')?;

Summary: ischange can fail to converge or return an unexpected error if the input data contains NaN or Inf
Description: When the input data contains?NaN ?or?Inf , ?the? ischange ? function might fail to converge or might return an unexpected error. The following example is code that fails to converge:  n = 100000; a = [zeros(1, n) ones(1, n) -ones(1, n) ones(1, n)];? ?% Data with no NaN b = [NaN a];? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?% Data with NaN ind_a = ischange(a, 'mean');? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? % Compute changes in mean ind_b = ischange(b, 'mean');? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? % Compute changes in mean The first call to  ischange   correctly finds three changes in the mean of the input data a . However, the second call to  ischange   fails to converge because the input data b  contains NaN .  The following example is code that causes an unexpected error:?  c = [1:5?51.3378?56.8528?59.0945?NaN?58.9998];ind_c = ischange(c, 'linear', 'MaxNumChanges', 1)  Error using builtinUnknown command option.?  The  ischange   output should indicate the sixth element of  ind_c   as a change point.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for variant subsystem with default variant and specific optimization model parameter setting
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for a model that meets these conditions:  Optimize block operation order in the generated code    is set to  Improved Execution Speed.  Starting In R2018a, setting?Optimization Level ? to  Maximum ?will ?automatically set? Optimize block operation order in the generated code  to Improved Execution Speed.  These settings are default for new models created in R2018a.  The model contains a variant subsystem?that meets all of these conditions:  The Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  parameter is selected.  One of the variants uses  (default)  variant condition.  One of the non-default variants has less outputs than the default variant.    When the above conditions are true, preprocessor conditions are generated and the default variant?is not the last variant.??The generated code may look like: model_step() {?  ...?#if Condition == 1?   ? ? variant1(&out1, &out2);  #else  ? ? variant3(&out1, &out2);  #endif?  #if Condition == 2?  ? ? variant2(&out1);  #endif?  ?...  }??  In the above code, when Condition  is equal to 2 , both variant3()  and variant2()  are active. ?The correct code is:? model_step() {?  ...?  #if Condition == 1?  ? ? variant1(&out1, &out2);  #elif Condition == 2?  ? ? variant2(&out1);  #else  ? ? variant3(&out1, &out2);  #endif?  ?...  }??
Workaround: To avoid this issue, perform one of the following actions:Set the Optimize block operation order in the generated code parameter to?Off.Set block priority of the default variant to a larger number than that of the other variants.On the variant subsystem block parameters dialog box, clear?the?Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals?parameter on the variant subsystem block.Replace default variant with negation of variant conditions. For example, ~(Condition==1 || Condition==2)).

Summary: Report generation fails with message: Input #2 expected to be a cell array
Description: When you download a pre-R2017b result from the Polyspace Metrics web repository to the Polyspace user interface, and generate a report, you see this error message: ...Processing Summary?In component Code Verification Summary : Input #2 expected to be a cell array, was struct instead.?In component Chapter/Subsection : Input #2 expected to be a cell array, was struct instead.?? "chapter" contains no content and will not be inserted into document?Error using cellfun?Input #2 expected to be a cell array, was struct instead.?Error in CStudioData/GetProjectSummary (line 51)?Error in PolySpaceResultInterface/GetSummary/GetSQOSummary (line 237)?
Workaround: Installation instructions for Polyspace R2015aSP1This workaround works only with Polyspace R2015aSP1 installed on a desktop.  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the appropriate attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: ? cd <MATLAB>? unzip <.zip file>        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: fitsread returns an error when reading character data from certain FITS files
Description: The?fitsread ?function returns an error when reading character data from a binary table. This error is observed when the FITS file has more than 10,000 rows. For example, the following read operation returns an error:   data = fitsread('test.fits','binarytable'); Error message:   Error using zeros  Input following 'like' is not a numeric or logical array.  Error in matlab.io.fits.readCol (line 63)      coldata = zeros(repeat, numrows, 'like', coldata);  Error in fitsread>read_bintable_hdu (line 567)      [coldata,nuldata] = fits.readCol(fptr,tableColumns(j));  Error in fitsread (line 133)      data = read_bintable_hdu(info,hdu_index,raw,tableColumns,tableRows);

Summary: Input command prompt not displayed when invoked from graphics, uimenu, or App designer callbacks.
Description: When running the input  command from graphics, uimenu, and App designer callbacks, the prompt is not displayed. The input  command runs successfully and waits for user input, but without the prompt it appears that the?input  command failed. function test    f = figure;    f.ButtonDownFcn = @func;endfunction func(~,~,~)    disp('button was down')    b = input('input prompt here :', 's');    disp(['the input was: ' b])end

Summary: Test cases might fail to achieve intended model coverage for objectives impacted by 'mod' operator
Description: If your model consists of 'mod' operator computations, test generation analysis incorrectly analyzes the objectives impacted by the 'mod' operator. Simulink Design Verifier reports the status of such objectives as 'Undecided with testcases' , and the test cases that the software generates might fail to achieve the intended model coverage.

Summary: Code generated from Stateflow charts might contain literal double constants which can cause compilation errors with 32 bit compilers
Description: If you have the following assignment in Stateflow: data = 1.0; ? where data  is specified as having a single precision floating-point type (single ), then the generated code will have the following assignment:? data = (real32_T)1.0;?  The literal double precision floating-point constant 1.0 in the generated code can cause problems with compilers which strictly support single precision only. In previous releases, the generated code was:? data = 1.0F;?  Note that it is expected behavior for floating point literals used in any context other than the above (that is, in direct assignment) to be emitted without the 'F' suffix in the generated code unless you specifically use the 'F' suffix in the diagram.?
Workaround: Force the generated code to use a literal single precision floating point constant by entering the following assignment in the Stateflow chart:data = 1.0F;?Or, to work around the issue in R2017a only, you can follow the instructions below to install the attachment.??Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.   Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.  Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model containing Enabled Synchronous Subsystem or Resettable Synchronous Subsystem and?Single output/update function parameter set can generate incorrect C code
Description: Embedded Coder can generate numerically incorrect code when a model meets all the following conditions: Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface >?Single output/update function  parameter is selected.  ANDThe model contains an Enabled Synchronous Subsystem, OR? The model contains a Resettable Synchronous Subsystem,?  The results obtained from running this C code can be inaccurate and differ from simulation results.??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer?in code generated for certain?assignments from load?
Description: The code generated for?load ?might load incorrect data when all of the following conditions are true: The output?from load  is assigned directly to?a function output argument. The assignment?executes on all executions paths. The function is not an entry-point function.  For example, the following code might not load the correct data for myFile.mat : function out = entryPointFcn?? ? out = subFcn()end?  function out = subFcn?? ? out = load('myFile.mat');end??  The issue can also occur for functions that are nested. For example:? function out = entryPointFcn? ? out = nestedFcn();  ? ? function out2 = nestedFcn? ? ? ? ?out2= load('myFile.mat');? ? end? ?end
Workaround: Assign the?result of load to a temporary variable. Then, assign the temporary variable to the function output argument.?For example:function out = subFcn? ? t = load('myFile.mat');? ? out = t;?end??

Summary: Model Advisor check "Check transition orientations in flow charts" flags Stateflow Truth Table blocks that have actions not drawn on the vertical
Description: When you execute?Model Advisor check "Check transition orientations in flow charts" (check ID: mathworks.maab.db_0132) on Simulink models that include Stateflow Truth Table blocks, the check?incorrectly produces warnings that actions are not drawn on the vertical.

Summary: Code generation produces an assertion error in the presence of direction connection between Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks
Description: Code generation may fail with an assertion error for a model that meets all of these conditions:There is a direct connection between a Data Store Read?block and a Data Store Write block. The data store read is of a bus element of one Data Store Memory block (for example, v1.e1 ) The data store write is of another Data Store Memory block (for example, v2 )? There is another Data Store Read?block that is reading from the second Data Store Memory block (v2 ). The model configuration parameter Reuse buffers for Data Store Read and Data Store Write  blocks is selected.  The assertion message may appear close to the following: ?CGIR assertion 'T::isa((const Type*)aType)' failed in '../ ../ derived/ glnxa64/ src/ include/ cg_ir/ type/ ../ ../ ../ ../ ../ ../ src/ cg_ir/ export/ include/ cg_ir/ type/ Type.hpp:515'
Workaround: Here are two possible workarounds:Clear the?Reuse buffers for Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks parameter. This change has a wider effect on code efficiency.Introduce a Signal Conversion block between the directly connected Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks. Check that Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter is selected.To minimize the impact on code efficiency, use the second workaround.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for AUTOSAR IRV mapped to Bus Creator block signal in a virtual subsystem
Description: Incorrect C code is generated for an AUTOSAR inter-runnable variable (IRV) if these conditions are true: In an AUTOSAR model, an IRV is mapped to one of the signals of a Bus Creator block. The Bus Creator block is located inside a virtual subsystem. The bus feeds into a root-level outport block.  Under these conditions, the generated C code contains?Rte_IrvWrite_SrcRunnable_IRVName(value)  instead of value = Rte_IrvRead_DstRunnable_IrvRead() .
Workaround: Add a Signal Conversion block on the IRV signal that feeds into the Bus Creator block. Then open the Signal Conversion block parameters?and select the option Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: Slow performance when selecting the root node of a Simulink Data Dictionary in Model Explorer
Description: In Model Explorer, when you select the root node of a Simulink Data Dictionary to display the dictionary reference hierarchy tree, you might experience performance degradation if the dictionary includes a large number of references to other dictionaries.? Reference dictionaries can reference other dictionaries, so the slowdown can occur for both of these circumstances: A hierarchy with many referenced dictionaries at the top level. A deep hierarchy of dictionaries referring to other dictionaries.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change log:March 23, 2018: attachment_1732613_15a_2018-02-23.zip, attachment_1732613_15b_2018-02-23.zip, attachment_1732613_16a_2018-02-23.zip, attachment_1732613_16b_2018-02-23.zip, attachment_1732613_17a_2018-02-23.zip: initial version?

Summary: normalizeWords("alize") crashes MATLAB
Description: Calling normalizeWords  on the word "alize"  (or other capitalizations, such as "Alize" ) crashes MATLAB. This happens for both string and tokenizedDocument input. The problem is limited to this particular input.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using temporal operators with different data types
Description: In a model with a Stateflow chart that contains multiple uses of temporal operators with arguments of different data types, MATLAB can crash when you Run  or Update Diagram .

Summary: Error when using uigetdir in a deployed application
Description: This error occurs when using uigetdir   in a deployed application: Error using matlab.ui.internal.dialog.FolderChooser/ doShowDialog (line 70)?Java exception occurred:?java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: eval requires a valid MCR id when running in standalone mode.?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.PostMatlabMessage(Native Method)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.postMatlabMessage(NativeMatlab.java:224)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.postMatlabMessage(NativeMatlab.java:240)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.MatlabLooper.postMatlabMessage(MatlabLooper.java:150)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.MatlabMCR.postAsync(MatlabMCR.java:1546)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.MatlabMCR.eval(MatlabMCR.java:518)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.Matlab.eval(Matlab.java:1197)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.MatlabPath$PathCallback.completed(MatlabPath.java:1449)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.MatlabMCR$InterestHandler.messageReceived(MatlabMCR.java:1667)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.ProcessPendingEvents(Native Method)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.processPendingEvents(NativeMatlab.java:508)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$MatlabLatch.safeWait(AWTUtilities.java:892)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$MatlabLatch.waitFcn(AWTUtilities.java:907)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$Latch.acquire(AWTUtilities.java:770)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$Invoker.invoke(AWTUtilities.java:500)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities.invokeAndWaitInternal(AWTUtilities.java:250)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities.invokeAndWait(AWTUtilities.java:111)?Error in matlab.ui.internal.dialog.FolderChooser/ show (line 48)?Error in uigetdir_helper (line 32)?Error in uigetdir (line 61)?Error in myuigetdir (line 2)?MATLAB:Java:GenericException??
Workaround: Create and use the function?uigetdir_workaround instead of the?uigetdir?function.??uigetdir_workaround uses the Java Swing JFileChooser?object directly to?open a folder selection dialog box.?To create?uigetdir_workaround?, copy this code into a file called?uigetdir_workaround.m:function [pathname] = uigetdir_workaround(start_path, dialog_title)% Pick a directory with the Java widgets instead of uigetdirimport javax.swing.JFileChooser;if nargin == 0 || isempty(start_path)? ? start_path = pwd;endjchooser = javaObjectEDT('javax.swing.JFileChooser', start_path);jchooser.setFileSelectionMode(JFileChooser.DIRECTORIES_ONLY);if nargin > 1? ? jchooser.setDialogTitle(dialog_title);endjchooser.setMultiSelectionEnabled(false);status = jchooser.showOpenDialog([]);if status == JFileChooser.APPROVE_OPTION? ? pathname = jchooser.getSelectedFile();? ? pathname = char(pathname);elseif status == JFileChooser.CANCEL_OPTION? ? pathname = 0;else? ? error('An error occurred while choosing a directory.');end?

Summary: Mixed scalar and empty inputs to graph constructor, digraph constructor, and addedge function return wrong results
Description: When you create a new graph or add edges to an existing graph using graph , digraph , or addedge , the edges are specified by two inputs, s  and t , specifying the source and target nodes of each edge. If one of s  and t  specifies exactly one node, and the other is empty, then this should  specify an empty list of edges. Instead, the aforementioned functions return incorrect results: graph(s,t) ?and digraph(s,t)  return a graph with a self-loop on node 1. addedge(g,s,t)  returns a graph with a self-loop on node 1 added to g . Additionally, in the special case where?s ?is empty and?t ?is a node name,?addedge ?produces the error:?Index exceeds array bounds .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Prefix missing in RTE compatible C code with Variant Subsystem's default option
Description: In the RTE compatible C code, names of preprocessor symbols used in the variant condition logic are generated as below :    #if Rte_SysCon_Mode  #elif Rte_SysCon_Mode  #endif  However, with default option selected in Variant Subsystem, the code generated misses the? Rte_SysCon_ prefix.

Summary: Simscape causes error for invalid but unused Simulink.Parameter objects
Description: When a referenced model contains Simscape blocks and the model or base workspaces contain invalid Simulink.Parameter objects, an error is issued even if the model does not use the Simulink.Parameter object. An example of an invalid Simulink.Parameter object is one that is set to a fixed-point type but has?a strictly floating point value, like inf  or nan .?
Workaround: Delete the invalid Simulink.Parameter object from the model or base workspace.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when two actions open user interface elements at the same time
Description: MATLAB might crash when two actions open user interface elements at the same time. For example, if you pause on a block with tooltips enabled, the tooltip appears after a brief interval. If you double-click the block during this interval, the Block Parameters dialog box and the tooltip might open, with one or the other being unresponsive. When this occurs, MATLAB might crash.?
Workaround: Pause on an item for at least one second before clicking it.

Summary: Assertion error during code generation?when vector is transposed?in?a concatenation
Description: MATLAB might throw the following assertion error when you try to generate code for a transpose operation in a concatenation that occurs inside of a loop: Type checker is unable to infer the type of Node 'matrix_val'. Please provide the type explicitly.  Here is an example of a transpose operation in a concatenation that is inside of a loop: A = [];for i=1:n??? A = [A;(i:-1:1)'];endout = A;  This issue can also occur?when generating code for a?MATLAB?Function block or Stateflow chart.
Workaround: Remove the transpose operation from the concatenation in one of these ways:Replace the transpose operation with a call to a function, such as permute, reshape, or shiftdim,?that produces equivalent results. For example, replace A = [A;(i:-1:1)']; with A = [A; permute(i:-1:1,[2,1])];Perform the transpose operation?after the end of the loop. For example:A = [];for i=1:n??? A = [A i:-1:1];endout = A';

Summary: When importing requirements from ReqIF file, attributes of Enum type are not imported correctly
Description: Enum type attributes imported from ReqIF files will have a?Plain Text?String type?in the resulting SLREQX file.  Additionally,?because some?ReqIF files?define?separate unique alphanumeric keys?that map to?user-configured enumerated?values in the source Requirements Management?application, when?Simulink Requirements incorrectly treats?imported values as?basic text attributes, the?UI will display the bare?alphanumeric keys instead of the actual enumerated?values.

Summary: Possible errors at link time when code is generated for referenced models with default C-mappings
Description: Code that cannot be linked may be generated for models when the following criterias are met: The model is a referenced model The default C-Mapping for Internal Data  is set to something other than Default.  The Enable local block outputs  configuration parameter is not enabled. The Remove internal data zero initialization  configuration parameter is not enabled.  In this case, variables may be initialized in the referenced model's model_initialize  function that are never declared or defined in the model reference hierarchy. This will result in errors at link time.
Workaround: To work around the issue, you can enable the Enable local block outputs configuration parameter.

Summary: Code generation fails?for models?which use symbolic dimension with leading or trailing 1-s in the specification
Description: MATLAB may crash during code generation for Simulink models using a combination of symbolic dimensions with and without trailing 1-s (for example: [1,SymbolA] and SymbolA or [SymbolB,1] and SymbolB). This is because of an?incompatibility between the specified symbolic expression and the?corresponding computed numeric dimension.

Summary: Root Outport block cannot accept Mux block output with number of dimensions greater than 1
Description: Simulink incorrectly assumes the root Outport to be a 1-D signal when your model meets the?following criteria: One of the following is true: Model contains a?root Outport block with option?Ensure outport is virtual ?selected.  The root Outport block is?connected to an?Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block.  And one of the following?is true: Original source of the signal to the Outport block is a Ground block. Outport block is unconnected and the option?Port?dimensions  is specified as 2?or greater. The source signal goes through a Mux block with at least two input signals?that are combined into a 2-D output signal.

Summary: Incorrect results on the second run of Fast Restart for some Stateflow charts
Description: On the second (and subsequent) runs while using Fast Restart, Stateflow transitions guarded by absolute time temporal logic might be taken at the wrong time if all of the following conditions are true: Execute (enter)?Chart?At?Initialization? is enabled The chart uses absolute time temporal logic The simulation start time is non-zero

Summary: LDPC tone mapping and de-mapping is incorrect for VHT and HE processing.
Description: Low-density parity-check (LDPC) tone mapping and de-mapping is incorrect for VHT and HE processing. A WiFi device will not be able to successfully decode an LDPC waveform generated with WLAN Toolbox. Similarly, WLAN Toolobox will not be able to decode an?LDPC waveform generated by a WiFi device.

Summary: Computer shuts down or does not respond when simulating a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model
Description: Real-time simulation of a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model might fail to start, report a timeout, or cause the computer to shutdown or restart?after updates are applied to the Windows operating system, or after the system has been upgraded to a newer release of Windows 10.
Fixed In: R2016b Update 7, R2017a Update 4, R2017b Update 7, R2018a Update 3, R2018b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when assigning an object to a property with size validation
Description: In generated code, assigning a object to a property with a size specification does not expand the scalar to the specified size.? Suppose that the size of a property in your MATLAB code?is given as a non-scalar by using a validator. If you assign an object to this property,?MATLAB?applies scalar expansion to the right-hand side. On the other hand, the generated code does not apply scalar expansion to the right-hand side. It does not produce an error message either.??  For example, define the class: classdef Obj    properties        property (1,2)    endend In the generated code for these lines, the code generator does not scalar expand y ?to match the size of x.property . x = Obj;y = Obj;x.property = y;

Summary: Lines with a width greater than 0.5 draw slowly on computers with AMD graphics hardware
Description: If using a computer with AMD graphics hardware, lines with a width greater than 0.5? might exhibit a degradation in performance in comparison to lines drawn at 0.5 line width.?

Summary: Objects in table entry hole corrupt a DOM-generated Word document
Description: Adding any of the following objects in a table entry hole can corrupt a DOM-generated Word document: An empty list object as the only content of a table entry hole A table object as the only content or as the last content in a table entry hole A DocumentPart   object that contains a layout A Group  object, that contains a DocumentPart  with a layout
Workaround: If the content to be added to a table entry hole contains an empty list or a table, add an empty Paragraph?as the final content of the hole. If a?Group with a DocumentPart or a DocumentPart?has a layout object, remove the layout object before the Group or DocumentPart?is added to a table entry hole.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Coding error in user written Level 2 C-Mex S-function not detected via error during update diagram
Description: The user written Level-2 C-Mex S-Function is missing the SS_OPTION_ALLOW_CONSTANT_PORT_SAMPLE_TIME  specification. Simulink fails to report an error during the update diagram when that S-Function is used in a model.

Summary: False positive in MISRA C++:2008 checker for rule 9-3-3 when calling a non-const function of base class from a derived class
Description: MISRA C++: 2008 rule 9-3-3 states, "If a member function can be made static then it shall be made static, otherwise if it can be made const then it shall be made const." The rule checker can sometimes show a violation on a non-const  member function that cannot be declared as const. The function cannot be declared as const because it belongs to a derived class and calls a non-const  member function of the base class. namespace test {  class Base {     public: Base();       void start();  };   class Derived: public Base {    public:       Derived(): Base() {}       void foo() {  // should not raise a 9-3-3 violation here         start();       }   }; };

Summary: Generating code for Stateflow Chart might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: In rare cases, MATLAB might crash when generating code for a model containing a Stateflow Chart.  The generated stack trace might contain lines similar to: Abnormal termination:? Segmentation violation  [snip]?  Stack Trace (from fault):?
