Summary: Compiler warnings due to a missing cast from pointer-to-const to pointer
Description: In rare cases, the code generator may produce code like the following static void example_1722468(const creal_T x_data[], const int32_T *x_size, creal_T y_data[], int32_T *y_size)?  {? emxArray_creal_T_myStruct_T locStruct;?  [SNIP]?  locStruct.size = x_size;   The assignment locStruct.size = x_size? discards the const qualifier from  x_size ?as  locStruct.size ?is not const. This may lead to a C or C++ compiler warning similar to the following:  warning: assignment discards ˇ®constˇŻ qualifier from pointer target type?  In this situation the generated code is missing a needed cast for const-correctness: locStruct.size = (int32_T *)x_size; ?

Summary: Simulation errors when the MATLAB Drive Connector and 3D Engine are both enabled?
Description: If you run a simulation with the MATLAB Drive Connector and 3D Engine both enabled, the simulation can error with this message:   MATLAB System Block '.../Simulation 3D Engine' error occurred when invoking 'setupImpl' method of 'Simulation3DEngine'
Workaround: Exit MATLAB Drive Connector.Restart MATLAB.Run this MATLAB script:dcpsInfoRepoProcess = System.Diagnostics.Process.GetProcessesByName('DCPSInfoRepo');if dcpsInfoRepoProcess.Length > int32(0)    dcpsInfoRepoProcess = dcpsInfoRepoProcess(1);    dcpsInfoRepoProcess.Kill();    dcpsInfoRepoProcess.WaitForExit(10000);endRun the desired?Simulink model with the 3D Engine enabled.

Summary: blastlocal fails with corrupted report error for some FASTA input files
Description: The blastall  command line tool, used by blastlocal , issues a warning when it encounters sequences containing Selenocysteine. These warnings result in errors when parsing the output of blastall. ?As a result,?blastlocal ?might error unexpectedly with the message "Report appears to be corrupted".

Summary: Running Test Manager on model referencing multiple instances with coverage enabled throws error
Description: Running Test Manager on a model that references multiple instances of another model with coverage enabled might result in an error: "Error while initializing parameters"

Summary: Subsystems containing only "No HDL" architecture blocks cause synthesis errors
Description: Some?Simulink blocks do not generate HDL code. These blocks such asDisplay, Scope etc. are used to observe the designˇŻs behavior in Simulink. The architecture of these?blocks is  "NO HDL" , and can be?viewed?in HDL Block Properties.??  Building a subsystem with only these  "NO HDL"  blocks will generate HDL Code having??an empty module in Verilog, or empty architecture body in VHDL.   When this generated code is synthesized using downstream EDA tools like Xilinx Vivado, following error may be thrown during?synthesis/implementation:?  [DRC 23-20] Rule violation (INBB-3) Black Box Instances - Cell 'u_Subsystem' of type 'u_Subsystem/? Subsystem' has undefined contents and is considered a black box.??The contents of this cell must be defined for opt_design to complete successfully.
Workaround: Ensure?that each Simulink?subsystem?has at least one?active, connected?output.

Summary: Model contains objects that Simulink Design Verifier does not support?error on a model that uses bus arrays
Description: If you define a function in a Stateflow chart or MATLAB Function block which uses bus arrays as arguments, Simulink Design Verifier might throw the following error: Model contains objects that Simulink Design Verifier does not support
Workaround: If the expression involving the bus array is an input argument of a function, assign it to a temporary variable and then use this variable as the input argument of the function.If the expression involving the bus array is an output argument of a function, use a temporary variable as the output argument of the function and then assign it to the bus array.

Summary: Missing JointToParentTransform &?ChildToJointTransform properties in?robotics.Joint?class
Description: The JointToParentTransform ?and ChildToJointTransform ?read-only properties of the robotics.Joint  class are not accessible. These properties are not displayed in the object display, and accessing them returns the following error: No public property 'JointToParentTransform' for class 'Joint'.  No public property 'ChildToJointTransform' for class 'Joint'.?  You can still set the properties using the robotics.Joint.setFixedTransform  method, but the properties are not accessible in the object.

Summary: Generating code for figures containing legends with titles can error
Description: If you have a figure with a titled legend and attempt to generate code using the File > Generate menu option, the generation errors out.
Workaround: To avoid the error, interactively remove the title from the legend before generating code. To remove the title, right-click the legend and select the 'Edit title' option. Then, delete the title text.?

Summary: The?int?function returns incorrect results for definite integrals of rational trigonometric functions over certain intervals
Description: For definite integrals of certain rational combinations of trigonometric terms, over an interval of width pi, 3*pi, 5*pi, ˇ­, symbolic integration (int ) returns incorrect results. >> syms x??  >> int(sin(x)/(sin(x)+3),x,-pi,0)  ans =  -(pi*(3*2^(1/2) - 4))/8  >> double(ans)  ans =  ?? -0.0953  >> integral(@(x) sin(x)./(sin(x)+3),-pi,0)  ans =  ? ?-0.9115
Workaround: Shift the interval to start at 0 by replacing:All instances of?x?with?x?+?a?in the integrandThe right endpoint with b - aThe left endpoint with 0

Summary: Standardization of edge case behavior for?uminus
Description: In R2018b there is consistent handling for real values stored in a complex array for uminus . In previous releases, the answer could change depending on whether you use a loop or a vectorized method. For example, calculating values in a loop produces?y1 : >> x = [1i; 1]x =   0.0000e+00 + 1.0000e+00i   1.0000e+00 + 0.0000e+00i>> y1 = zeros(2,1);>> for i = 1:numel(x);y1(i) = -x(i);end>> y1y1 =   0.0000e+00 - 1.0000e+00i  -1.0000e+00 + 0.0000e+00i>> angle(y1)ans =  -1.5708e+00   3.1416e+00 For element-wise functions, vectorization of a for -loop should produce identical answers. However, in releases R2018a and earlier this is not true for?uminus , as -0 imaginary part propagated in the vectorized method, which can be highlighted by calling angle . >> y2 = -xy2 =   0.0000e+00 - 1.0000e+00i  -1.0000e+00 + 0.0000e+00i>> angle(y2)ans =  -1.5708e+00  -3.1416e+00  In R2018b, the values in y2   match those of y1 .?

Summary: Simulink may hang when compiling a model with a Model block whose referenced model has Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Functions and the referenced model inherits its sample time from the parent model.
Description: Given a model?with?a Model block,?if the referenced model satisfies the following conditions:? 1. It has Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Functions 2. It has inherited its sample time from the parent model Simulink may hang when compiling this model.
Workaround: This issue can be fixed by modifying the Periodic sample time constraint for the referenced model.?In the referenced model, open the?Configuration Parameters. In?Solver details >> Tasking and sample time options, change the?Periodic sample time constraint?parameter from "Ensure sample time independent" to "Unconstrained".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer between the simulation and generated code for?n-D Lookup Table and Interpolation using Prelookup blocks when?floating and fixed-point data types are specified
Description: n-D Lookup Table and Interpolation using Prelookup blocks might?return different answers?between simulation and code generation?when these?conditions are true: Table data  type is set to fixed point with non-trivial slope and bias Intermediate results  type is set to double  or single  For the n-D Lookup Table?block, Fraction  type is set to double  or single   Differences between simulation and code generation are caused by:  At?simulation, table data is cast to a float (double  or single ) type before?the interpolation is calculated,?causing?the?slope and bias?to be?applied before interpolation computation.  At?code generation, stored integers of table data are used to compute the interpolation, and the slope and bias corrections are applied afterwards.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong size for varargout that is conditionally assigned from cell arrays of different sizes?
Description: If an entry-point function?uses varargout , and the varargout  output is conditionally assigned from cell arrays of different sizes, then the generated code might produce an incorrect answer. Specifically, the generated code might return varargout? as a cell array with a?greater size than expected and fill the extra elements with undefined values.? For example, consider this code: function varargout = fcn(in)varargout{1}= 1;if in > 1    varargout{2} = 2;else    varargout = {};endend The expected answer for fcn(0 ) is an empty cell array. However, suppose that you generate code using codegen as follows: ?  codegen fcn -nargout 1 -args {1}?  fcn_mex(0)   produces a cell array of size one. This issue is specific to MATLAB Coder. It does?not affect other code generation products.?It affects only entry-point functions that have varargout  output.

Summary: Subsystem context menu contains Identify Modelling Clones item?without Simulink Check license
Description: When you right click on a?subsystem,?if you do not have a Simulink Check license, you may see the?Identify Modeling Clones  menu item. When you click on it, you get this message in the MATLAB command window: 'Warning: Children generator function returned an empty cell array'?  The Identify Modeling Clones tool requires a Simulink Check license.?

Summary: Invoking the Update functionality on a Requirement Set that uses custom attributes may lead to the corruption of the Requirement Set file
Description: After invoking the update functionality on a Requirement Set that contains external requirements that use custom attributes, the update process can corrupt the Requirement Set (.slreqx) file.?  The file corruption might not be apparent until a user restarts MATLAB and tries to reopen the Requirement Set?(.slreqx)?file. Opening the Requirement Set file can result in the following error in the MATLAB command window:  Warning: MF0 parse error in <<fileName.slreqx>>:?  Cannot establish association to a dangling reference to xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx.?   where x is some digit or lower-case letter.  This corruption is known to happen under the following conditions:  The external requirements are imported from an external data source and use custom attributes. For example, when importing from Microsoft Excel, users typically map some columns to custom attributes in the Requirement Set, or when using the command-line utilities to import from DOORS, users may also create custom attributes. Before Update, some rows were deleted in the external document. As a result, Update deleted from the Requirement Set some of the previously imported external requirements.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Signal object initial value is not applied to output of a Model block in generated code
Description: When generating code from a model containing a Model block with a Simulink.Signal  object specified on its output port, where:The object's field CoderInfo.StorageClass ?has a value other than 'Auto ' specified; The object's field InitialValue ?has a nonzero value specified; and The object's field DataType  is not 'double '.  In such a case, in the generated code, the initial value of the output of the Model block is initialized to zero instead of the specified value.

Summary: Some custom library blocks don't appear in Library Browser
Description: Subsystems containing MATLAB Function blocks do not appear in the Library Browser tree. This issue occurs when these subsystems belong to libraries that are created or saved in R2017b or R2018a or libraries exported from a different release to R2017b or R2018a. ?

Summary: Error in the C++ linking phase:cast to type is not allowed?
Description: For C++ applications, in some situations, the verification can fail during the linking phase. The error message is similar to this:? Linking pst_file_glob0.c (xx/ yy)?  Linking pst_fileX.c (zz/ yy)?pst_fileX.c, ?line xxxx: error: cast to type "struct __intern_type_xxx" is not allowed?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystem outputs are?incorrectly cached in local variables across subsystem calls in generated code
Description: Incorrect code may be generated when the?Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Structure reference  and Optimize global data access  parameter is set to Minimize global data access .  When these two parameters are enabled, global variables corresponding to subsystem outputs may be?incorrectly cached in local variables across calls to subsystems. As a result, a global variable in the function caller (for example, the model_step  function) may not get the changes that the function callee makes to cached variable, producing silent wrong answers.    For example, in this incorrect code, the model_B.subystem1.subsystem2.UnitDelay variable is incorrectly cached to the local variable UnitDelay  in the model_step  function. As a result, the variable model_Y.OutData  gets an older,incorrect value from the UnitDelay  variable.    /* Blocksignals (default storage) */    B_model_Tmodel_B;    /* setslocalB->UnitDelay to 18*/   void model_subsystem2(B_subsystem2_modelType_T *localB)    {    ? localB->UnitDelay = 18;    }    /* setslocalB->subsystem2.UnitDelay to 18 */   void model_subsystem1(B_subsystem1_modelType_T *localB)    {    ? model_subsystem2(&localB->subsystem2);    }    /* Modelstep function */   void model_step(void)    {    ? // cachesthe? value of model_B.subsystem1.subsystem2.UnitDelay    ? // in a localvariable    ? UnitDelay =model_B.subsystem1.subsystem2.UnitDelay;    ? // setsmodel_B.subsystem1.subsystem2.UnitDelay = 18   ? model_subsystem1(&model_B.subsystem1);    ? // makes use ofthe old value of model_B.subsystem1.subsystem2.UnitDelay   ? // rather thanthe value from model_subsystem1    ? model_Y.OutData = UnitDelay;    }

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with an error for Variant Subsystem containing Model blocks as choices
Description: If a Simulink model uses a Variant Subsystem containing Model blocks as choices, Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with the below error:?  Error: Variant Subsystem block 'mModel/variantSubsys' contains no active variant controls.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model uses P-code functionality even after P-code files are removed
Description: Suppose?a model containing a MATLAB Function block calls a function foo . This function has two associated files, foo.m and foo.p, where foo.p is the P-code file of foo.m. If foo.p is deleted or removed from the MATLAB path after performing an Update Diagram or running the model, the model keeps using the functionality given in that P-code file on subsequent runs, even if the M-code is different.?

Summary: Redefinition of typedef 'int_T' in MessageServiceLayer.h causes build failure
Description: On some Linux platforms, Simulink throws a build error when you try to run a model containing a Stateflow chart or MATLAB Function block: ...MessageServiceLayer.h: error: redefinition of typedef? 'int_T'? ?

Summary: Fast mode analysis fails with Error: Can't find graph file for /path/to/myfile.cpp
Description: Polyspace fast mode analysis using the option -fast-analysis  can fail with the following error message in the?Run Log ?pane:   Error: The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11.  /?path/to/myfile.cpp: warning: Failed compilation.  ...  Error: Can't find graph file for /?path/to/myfile.cpp.  Error: Fast Analysis: ps_shadows KO

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Write to matrix type variable with loop invariant index missing in generated code
Description: Incorrect results are possible in rare situations for a model design that results in the following generated code:The generated code contains a nested for  loop. Inside the nested for  loop body, a matrix type variable with loop invariant index is a temporary variable. The code contains a write to the temporary matrix type variable.?  The code generator contains an optimization that replacesuses of a matrix element with a scalar temporary variable inside of a for  loop.In the incorrect generated code, this optimization occurs, so within the nested?for ?loop, a scalartemporary a_0 ?replaces?a[i] . This replacement causes incorrect code if there is a read to the elements of the matrix?a  outside of the loopbecause instead of a write to the matrix  a , the code contains a?write  to the scalartemporary a_0. As a result, a  contains the wrong values. For example, the correct code contains this nested for  loop: ?for(i=0,i<3;i++) {??? for(j=0, j<2; j++) { ?????? a[i] = b    ?????? y = a[i];?? } }       In the incorrect code, the nested?for  loop statements contain this code:  ?for(i=0,i<3;i++)? {??? ?for(j=0, j<2; j++)? {?????????? a_0 = a[i];  ????????? a_0 = b;  ????????? y = a_0;??} }

Summary: Simulink Function or Function Caller block with configured C/C++ function interface in a referenced model might generate incorrect code
Description: If a Simulink Function or Function Caller block with a configured C/C++ function interface is in a referenced model, the generated code might be incorrect when both of the following occur: Another Simulink Function or Function Caller block (defining or invoking the same function)?is in another model in the same model hierarchy The block C/C++ function interface?is not explicitly configured, or it?is configured with an inconsistent function interface.
Workaround: Configure all of the Simulink Function and Function Caller blocks in the model hierarchy with consistent C/C++ function interfaces.

Summary: Copying to the clipboard stops working in the Live Editor in Windows 10
Description: Whencopying data to the clipboard from the Live Editor (for example, if you select code or text and type CTRL +C ),MATLAB might throw this warning: WARNING: Error in subscription handler java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: C:\?Users\?<username>\?AppData\?Local\Temp\ConnectorClipboard ... does not exist? After the warning occurs, copying and pasting no longer works in the Live Editor.? The problem occurs only in Windows 10, versions 1709 and above. In these versions, Windows 10 runs a SilentCleanup task?when the system enters an idle state.?SilentCleanup?tries to? free up disk space by removing empty temporary folders, which causes the problem.?
Workaround: To avoid this issue, stopSilentCleanup:1. Open a Command Prompt window as anadministrator.2. Runthis command to disable the?SilentCleanup task:schtasks.exe /change /TN "\Microsoft\Windows\DiskCleanup\SilentCleanup"/Disable3. Restart MATLAB.Note: When you disable the SilentCleanup task,unnecessary files such as temporary files are not automatically deleted. If you disabled the SilentCleanup task, make sure to regulary check for unnecessary files and delete them manually to avoid storing unused temporary files and running out of disk space.?To restart?SilentCleanup:1. Open a Command Prompt window as an administrator.2. Run this command to enable the?SilentCleanup?task:schtasks.exe /change /TN "\Microsoft\Windows\DiskCleanup\SilentCleanup"/Enable

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect  behavior or MATLAB crash when generating code with dynamically allocated  variable-size arrays?
Description: In some situations, MATLAB Coder attempts to reuse?the size vector of a dynamically allocated variable-size array that has been freed. This can cause the generated code to crash or, in the case of MEX-functions or simulations, report misleading errors about size mismatches. This bug is associated with changing the shape of a variable-size array (typically through single-dimensional indexing, eg, A(i)), and the lifetime of the reshaped array exceeding the lifetime of the original array.?  This bug requires variable-size signals and dynamic memory allocation in matlab functions to be enabled. These parameters are enabled by default in the MATLAB Coder App, in the codegen command,?and in Simulink Coder GRT target.?These features are disabled by default in ERT target with Embedded Coder. Prior to R2017a, this bug does not affect MATLAB code used inside Simulink models.?Since R2017a, this?bug affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks?and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier report generation may fail with unexpected error
Description: For some models, Simulink Design Verifier may fail to generate a detailed analysis report and displays the following error message: Expected one output from a curly brace or dot indexing expression, ?but there were 7 results.?  Error in Sldv.ReportUtils.getSidFromModelObjectData (line 16)?????????????replacementRuleName = replacementInfoOfModel.RepRuleInfo.RuleName;?Error in Sldv.ReportUtils/ prepareObjectives>buildObjectives (line 88)?????????[sid, ?modelname] = Sldv.ReportUtils.getSidFromModelObjectData(data, ?mo);Error in Sldv.ReportUtils/ prepareObjectives (line 9)?????objectives = buildObjectives(data, ?titles, ?createLink);?Error in Sldv.ReportUtils.generate?Error in sldvreport (line 53)????????[status, ?ActualreportFilePath] = Sldv.ReportUtils.generate(sldvData, options, ?showUI, ?reportFilePath);?Error in urlcall (line 168)?????????[~, ?reportFilePath] = sldvreport(sldvData, ?[], resultPaths.ReportFileName, ?true);

Summary: Update diagram with linked FMU block may fail due to invalid logging filter error
Description: Simulink model containing FMU block may fail to update diagram if the following conditions are true: FMU block is linked from a Simulink library Simulink model is loaded, but not opened  An example error message: ? ? Invalid logging filter for 'model/FMU'. Logging filters must be in a cell array format. For example: {'Warning', 'Error', 'Fatal'}.?

Summary: weboptions does not support non-ASCII characters in Username and Password properties
Description: A?non-ASCII character in Username  or Password  properties?might throw?an error when used with webread , webwrite  or websave .  For example, if?Password ?has the € symbol: options = weboptions('Username', 'someusername', 'Password', 'my€password', 'MediaType', 'application/json'); webwrite('http://example.com',struct(), options);   Invalid character code sequence detected.
Workaround: Manually format the authorization header using the HeaderFields property of weboptions.?The precise workaround depends on the character encoding that the server accepts for username and password. In most cases, a single-byte encoding such as windows-1252 or ISO-8859-1 works if the characters can be represented by those encodings.? For the example above using windows-1252:encoded = matlab.net.base64encode(unicode2native('someusername:my€password','windows-1252'));options = weboptions('HeaderFields',{'Authorization',['Basic ' encoded]});

Summary: Scope x-axis limits are incorrect after zooming
Description: On the Scope block or System object, the x-axis limits may be incorrect after zooming in this order: Autoscale the Y-axis. Autoscale the X & Y axes Manually zoom to an area larger than the y-axis autoscale in step 1.

Summary: Data tips display incorrect information on time series plots with events
Description: If you have a time series plot with events, then the data tips might not show the correct information.  For example, this code generates a time series plot with two events. The data tip for the first event appears correctly. The data tip for the second event does not display correctly.? data = [0:10:360]';?time = now + data;?ts = timeseries(data, time);?ts.TimeInfo.Units = 'days';?ts.TimeInfo.StartDate = datestr(0);?% Add events?tsout = addevent(ts,{'event1' 'event2'}, {ts.Time(1) ts.Time(27)});?plot(tsout, '.-')datacursormode on? Select the events. The first event displays correctly. The second event incorrectly shows the same event name and time as the first event.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer might occur for a reusable export function model that includes a reusable subsystem
Description: The code generator can generate incorrect code for a model?when all of the following conditions are true: The model is an export-function model. If the model is a referenced model, the model configuration parameter?Total number of instances allowed per top model  ?parameter is set to?Multiple .  If the model is a top model, the model configuration parameter? Code interface packaging?  is set to ?Reusable function .  The model includes at least one atomic subsystem with block parameter?Function packaging? set to?Auto ,  Nonreusable function ?or ?Reusable function .  In the generated function interface for the subsystem, the second argument is a pointer to the?real-time model (RT_MODEL ) data structure .  The subsystem function calls a function that belongs to the same subsystem (for example, the subsystem's reset function).   When these conditions exist, the pointer to RT_MODEL  can be incorrectly passed to the?calling function where a pointer to a different?struct  type is expected. Use of the incorrect pointer to a structure type can result in wrong answers.

Summary: Point markers do not render when using software OpenGL on Windows
Description: Point markers may fail to appear on screen when using software OpenGL on a Windows platform.
Workaround: To create small, point-like markers when using software OpenGL on a normal DPI Windows platform, try one of these approaches:?Use point?markers ('.')?that have a size of at least 16 points.?Use circle markers ('o') instead of point markers ('.'). To mimic the size of point markers, use one-third the desired size for the circle markers. For example, to mimic a point marker with a size of 15 points, create a circle marker with a size of 5 points.

Summary: Coverage analysis fails for large cascade of Logical Operator blocks
Description: For MCDC analysis, a cascade of Logical Operator blocks is treated as a single, complex logic expression. (This behavior occurs starting with release R2016b.) However, if the expression is too large, the following warning is shown, followed by an error: Warning: MCDC coverage not recorded for "LogicOp_Root", because a logic expression for this model object was too complex.   Depending on the context in which the coverage analysis was initiated, different error messages are shown.?
Workaround: To work around this issue, avoid analyzing a large Logical Operator cascade for MCDC.??One workaround is to simplify the expression represented by the Logical Operator blocks, reducing the size of the cascade (that is, the number of blocks, number of inputs, etc.).If you cannot simplify the expression, consider breaking up the cascade. For example, assume you have two Logical Operator blocks cascaded together. If the output signal of the upstream block is branched and connected to another block (for example, an Outport), then the two logic blocks are not treated as a single expression. Instead, they are analyzed individually for MCDC. See the Logical Operator Cascade Patterns?example for?the criteria of how these cascades are identified.Another workaround is to force Simulink Coverage to analyze all Logical Operator blocks individually for MCDC. The tool does not analyze logic cascades when logic blocks are treated as non-short-circuiting and the Unique-Cause definition of MCDC is being used. (Note that prior to R2017a, all MCDC analyses used Unique-Cause.) Use the following commands to change these settings:set_param(model,'CovLogicBlockShortCircuit','off');set_param(model,'CovMcdcMode','UniqueCause');? ?% Not applicable before R2017aThese settings may affect the MCDC results for other elements in your model. To understand the effects of short-circuiting, see the example?Analyzing MCDC for Cascaded Logic Blocks. For a description of the effects of setting CovMcdcMode, see?Modified Condition and Decision Coverage (MCDC) Definitions in Simulink Coverage.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for multitasking mode
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a?model that meets these conditions: In Solver  pane, under Additional parameters , the?Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  parameter is on. In the same pane, the?Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is off. In the?Diagnostics >Sample Time  pane, the?Multitask rate transition  parameter is set to warning . In the model, at least one root outport block has a different discrete sample time?than the block output port driving it.
Workaround: In the?Solver?pane,?under?Additional parameters,?select the?Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer parameter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when calling?ismethod?with the name of a protected method
Description: In generated code, ismethod  returns true  for protected methods, while MATLAB returns false . The issue can arise in this example, where the top-level function uses ismethod  to query the protected method onemethod :   function out = fcn    obj = myclass;    out = ismethod(obj,'onemethod');  end where myclass  is a protected method:   classdef myclass  methods (Access = protected)    function onemethod(obj)    end  end This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: In the Volume Viewer app on Lenovo laptops with Intel Integrated Graphics, the volume rendering pane of the app appears all black and does not properly render the volume
Description: In the Volume Viewer app on Lenovo laptops with Intel Integrated Graphics, the volume rendering pane of the app appears all black and does not properly render the volume.  The cause of this problem is a graphics driver bug in the version of Intel Integrated Graphics drivers that ship with certain Lenovo laptops.
Workaround: To resolve this problem, update the graphics drivers to the latest version with the following steps. On Windows, follow the "Update device drivers" menu under Control Panel, and select your Intel HD Graphics adapter under "Display Adapters".Go the Driver tab and select "Update Driver". When you have the most recent version of the graphics driver installed, power off your machine and restart.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when you?pass certain anonymous and nested functions to isequal
Description: In the generated code, isequal  returns true  when you pass it anonymous functions and nested functions created from the same syntactic anonymous or nested function with equal captured variables. Captured variables are the variables from outside the function scope that are used to define the result of the function. MATLAB considers anonymous and nested functions equal only if they are identical. For example: function result = foox = make;y = make;result = isequal(x, y);function result = makeresult = @() {}; The function foo  returns true  in generated code and false  in MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from the bitsra function gives a wrong answer
Description: Code generated from the bitsra  function,?including MEX code and code inside a MATLAB Function block,?gives a wrong answer when the first input is a floating-point value and the second input is an unsigned integer. For example:?  n = uint16(1); codegen bitsra -args {10,n} bitsra_mex(10,n)? returns 10 , and it should return 5 .?
Workaround: When the first input is floating-point, cast the second argument to any data type other than an unsigned integer.?For example:? n = int16(1); codegen bitsra -args {10,n} bitsra_mex(10,n)?correctly returns 5

Summary: Compiling the generated code may result in warnings or errors regarding the constness of pointer variables
Description: In rare cases, when you compile the generated C or C++ code, you may encounter one or both of the following issues: 1. The generated code dereferences a pointer-to-const and writes to the result. For example: ? ? ? ?? ? ?const int *a;  ? ? ? ? ?[SNIP]  ? ? ? ? ?a[i] = a[j];  ? ? ?The C or C++ compiler will output an error similar to:? ? ? ?Error: assignment of read-only location ? 2. The generated code assigns a pointer-to-const to a pointer. For example: ? ?? ? ? ? int *a;  ? ? ? ? ? const int *b;  ? ? ? ? ? [SNIP]?  ? ? ? ? ? a = b;  ? ? The C or C++ compiler will output an error or warning similar to: ? ? Error: invalid conversion from 'const int*' to `int*`  ? ? or? ? ? Warning: assignment discards ˇ®constˇŻ qualifier from pointer target type ?

Summary: Modifying a model may unexpectedly disable?zero-crossing detections.
Description: In 2017b, for some models that rely on the heuristic algorithm to compute sample times, modifying the models may change the Inport?blocks' compiled?sample times from Continuous to Fixed-In-Minor, and thus unexpectedly disable zero-crossing detections in the model.
Workaround: Specify Continuous sample time in the Inport block. Open the Block Parameters dialog of the Inport block and set Block Parameters >> Signal Attributes >> Sample time (-1 for inherited) to "0".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB for isequal with a function handle of a static method accessed from a property
Description: The code generated for isequal?does not match MATLAB when all of these conditions are true:   An input to isequal  is a function handle of a static method. The static method is accessed from a property of an object.  For example, suppose that?x  and y are objects   with property property  and the property is an object with static method staticMethod .  The following code causes the issue: disp(isequal(@x.property.staticMethod, @y.property.staticMethod)); If you ac ce ss the static method directly from an object, the issue does not occur. For example, suppose that? x and y are objects with?a static method staticMethod.? The following code does not cause the issue. disp(isequal(@x.staticMethod, @y.staticMethod));

Summary: Model Advisor issues a warning when using?"Display configurationmanagement data" check with the?Advisor.Application API
Description: When you use the "Displayconfiguration management data" check (ID: mathworks.iec61508.MdlVersionInfo)? with the Advisor.Application API to run checks onModel Hierarchy, the following warning appears in the generated report: Model Checksum Error - could notretrieve CheckSum?
Workaround: Run?this check in the?Model Advisor?user interface?or using modeladvisor.run().

Summary: Incorrect status for objectives associated with Enabled and Triggered Subsystems
Description: The Simulink Design Verifier analysis shows incorrect status for objectives associated with Enabled  and Triggered  Subsystems. For example, in Test Case Generation, the analysis might report an objective to be unsatisfiable.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when custom storage class is on a signal with a constant sample time
Description: Signals with custom storage classes that have constant sample time?are not correctly initialized in?these cases: Signal is the output of a Merge block Signal is the output of a Concatenate block?and?has one?dimension or concatenates columns Custom storage class is the output of a block (excluding Model Reference or Atomic Subsystem blocks)?that has constant sample time and non-constant sample time outputs.  When the bug occurs, the?generated code does not initialize the custom storage class resulting in the wrong value?for the signal with the custom?storage class, as well as incorrect values for signals downstream of the custom storage class. For example, a model contains a custom storage class SC  that is the output of a?Concatenate block and all inputs?are constant. The output of the?Concatenate block?is an input to an Add block. The code for this model might look as follows, where SC is   in the step function but never written to in either the model_step  or the model_initialize  functions: void model_step(void) {...?? /* Add: '<Root>/Add' */???rtb_add = SC + rtb_product;... } The?model_initialize  function should have the following code, but does not when the bug occurs void model_initialize(void) {?...? /* ?custom signals */? SC = 3;...?}
Workaround: These are possible workarounds:Remove the custom storage class.Insert another block before the signal with the custom storage class, such as a Signal Conversion block?in which?the Exclude Block Reduction parameter?is set.

Summary: Signal names propagated through subsystems might not update
Description: The signal name propagated through a subsystem might be wrong when all of the following conditions apply: The signal name being propagated is changed. For example, SignalA  becomes SignalB . The new name matches the name specified for a downstream signal. The corresponding signal label becomes?SignalB <SignalA> .  The propagated signal name (SignalA ) does not update to match the new name of the signal (SignalB ).

Summary: Web-based workflows fail when MATLAB Web Preferences specify a web proxy that uses Basic authentication
Description: When you configure MATLAB Web Preferences to use a proxy with Basic authentication, then workflows that connect to web servers via HTTPS will fail. If you use workflows that connect to web servers, such as using urlread  and urlwrite , you see an error like the following: ? ??Unable to tunnel through proxy. Proxy returns "HTTP/1.1 407 Proxy Authentication Required".  The Request Support  button in the MATLAB desktop displays a dialog box that reports "Unable to Contact MathWorks". This issue is caused by a change in behavior in Oracle's JRE 8 - described here:?https://www.oracle.com/java/technologies/javase/8u111-relnotes.html  See the section: Disable Basic authentication for HTTPS tunneling.
Workaround: Thefollowing workaround restores the less secure proxy authentication behaviorpresent in earlier versions of Java. ?Before applying this workaround, pleaseconsult the person(s) responsible for your web proxy to determine if thefollowing workaround is appropriate.After you have confirmed that you want to use a proxy that uses Basic authentication, then follow these steps:1. Close MATLAB.2. Create or edit a java.opts file as described in the documentation here:https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/matlab_env/java-opts-file.html3. Add this code on a separate line:-Djdk.http.auth.tunneling.disabledSchemes=4. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Floating point datatypes error can occur when a virtual bus with multiple data types drives a subsystem Outport block
Description: A misleading floating point datatypes error, The parameter 'InitialOutput' of block 'model/subsystem/Out1' is a floating point type ,  occurs when the following conditions are true: A conditionally executed subsystem's Outport block has an initial value of '[]'. The Outport block is driven by at least one non-virtual block not in this list , meaning the initial value is not inherited, and an initial value of zero is used. The Outport block is driven by a virtual bus signal with multiple datatypes.
Workaround: Either of these workarounds solves the issue:Use a MATLAB Structure as an initial value for the Outport block, as explained?here.Use a non-virtual bus signal to drive the subsystem Outport block.

Summary: Incorrect call sites generated with recursively calling Stateflow charts
Description: The code generator may generate incorrect call sites for Stateflow charts in a model that meets all of these conditions:? A?model has two Stateflow charts (for example, C1 and C2). The charts contain transitions with send? broadcast event between each other. You build for a model reference target  As the charts call each other, a recursive code structure (with C1 calling C2 in its body and vice versa) is expected. The code generator may not include all arguments for either chart when producing call sites for chart functions. This results in compilation errors.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code?generator does not honor data alignment?requirement when model uses continuous time
Description: The code generator does not honor a data alignment requirement when all of the following conditions?are true: Model contains blocks that use or generate continuous time, such as an integrator block. Model configuration parameter Support continuous time  is selected. Model has a?data alignment requirement specified by a Simulink signal property?or a custom code replacement library (CRL).  If the compiled code from your compiler for the target processor would result in different numeric results or crash when using an instruction?that requires specific data alignments but the data is not aligned properly,?the generated code can produce numeric results that differ from simulation results or result in crashes when all of the conditions above are met.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a?Unit Delay block?connecting to a model's root Outport block
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a?Unit Delay block. The Unit Delay block is inside a nonreusable subsystem. An async function call drives the nonreusable subsystem or the System target file  parameter is set to autosar.tlc .  In the incorrectly generated code, the input variable for the Unit Delay block is incorrectly reused with the variable for the Unit Delay block state.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model step function with reusable packaging may lead to incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code may be generated if all of these conditions are met: The configuration parameter Code interface packing  is set as Reusable function  for the model step function. The model step function is configured to be passed by reference using Configure Model Functions  on the the Code Generation > Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. In the generated code for model step function, a model output is accessed through a data copy where the variable read is only conditionally written.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when starting on some Linux systems, particularly Fedora
Description: MATLAB might crash when starting up on some Linux systems. Fedora is particularly prone to this issue. This might be due to an incompatibility between resident libraries and the freetype library that ships with MATLAB.
Workaround: To work around this issue, try the following commands in a Linux shell, where matlabdir is the MATLAB installation folder.  cd matlabdir/bin/glnxa64  mkdir unused  mv libfreetype.so.6* unused/If matlabdir is a public folder such as /usr/local/MATLAB/R2018a, you might need root privileges to perform these steps.

Summary: Inconsistent Simulink Design Verifier objectives for triggered MATLAB Function block
Description: Simulink Design Verifier test objectives for triggered MATLAB Function are inconsistent as below :  Objectives for the?MATLAB Function block?are duplicated and one set of duplicated objectives are reported as unsatisfiable/deadlogic. The label of the decision objectives related to the trigger port of the?MATLAB Function block?are incorrect. Some of the objectives of the MATLAB Function block?are incorrectly reported as "Undecided with testcase".

Description: On Windows? platforms, using fitsread  and fitsinfo  to read data or query information from a FITS file greater than 2GB results in an error.  The following code snippet will result in an error: info = fitsinfo('largeFITSfile.fits');?  Error using fitsiolib CFITSIO library error (116): error seeking file position Error in matlab.io.fits.openFile (line 63) fptr = fitsiolib('open_file',filename,mode); Error in fitsinfo>openFile (line 747) fptr = fits.openFile(filename); Error in fitsinfo (line 207) fid = openFile(filename);??

Summary: Exporting a clock signal from certain National Instruments devices might generate an error
Description: When using NI-DAQ devices, exporting a clock using addClockConnection ?and then starting a clocked operation with either startForeground  or startBackground , might generate the following error message: NI Error -89120: Source terminal to be routed could not be found on the device. Summary: Slow performance for imregister when used in a deployed application
Description: Performance of the imregister  function degrades when you use it?in a deployed application.

Summary: Working with source control can sometimes cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you use source control integration through the MATLAB?Current Folder Browser, MATLAB might crash when you view or commit changes. For more information, including in which releases and updates this bug is fixed, see Bug Report 1792111 .

Summary: A referenced model with an initialize port on does not support signal logging when simulated in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Signal logging (logsout ), i.e, the logging of intermediate signals, is not supported within a referenced model when both of these conditions are true: The top model is being simulated in rapid accelerator mode. The referenced model has the Initialize port enabled.
Workaround: Use a simulation mode other than rapid accelerator (either normal or accelerator).

Summary: Sample time independent model in a Function-Call Subsystem?may cause a strong error message
Description: When a model block referencing a sample time independent model is placed in a Function-Call Subsystem whose Trigger Port's sample time type is set to be periodic  and sample time to be -1 ,?updating?the model diagram may cause the following error: The S-function 'XX_Model/XX_Function/XX_REF' has improperly set the sample time of input port?YY when placed in the function call subsystem 'XX_Model/XX_Function'.
Workaround: To work around the error message,?change the Trigger Port's sample time to?be equal to the triggering signal's sample time. For example, if the Function-Call Subsystem is controlled by a signal with sample time 0.2, set the Trigger Port's sample time to 0.2?by opening the block parameter dialog of the Trigger Port block in the Function-Call Subsystem and change the Sample time?in the tab Main to 0.2.

Summary: When right-clicking using a trackpad on Mac, MATLAB might scroll or zoom unintentionally
Description: Right-clicking using a trackpad on Mac might cause MATLAB to scroll or zoom unintentionally. For example, when right-clicking: The document or window scrolls up or down a small amount. The context menu opens on a different item than was clicked. The context menu opens, but immediately closes. The zoom level increases or decreases (Live Editor).
Workaround: If this bug zooms the Live Editor:Use Command +?+?or Command + - to restore the desired zoom level. ??To avoid the bug, consider one of these workarounds:Use Control +?Click instead of a two finger click or tap.Use a mouse instead of the trackpad.Reconfigure the trackpad's secondary click to use "Click in bottom left/right corner" instead of "Click or tap with two fingers". To do this:Go to Apple menu >?System Preferences...?Click TrackpadUnder Secondary click, choose an option other than "Click or tap with two fingers"

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect behavior from generated code using memcpy?when compiled with Microsoft? Visual Studio? 2013
Description: If you compile generated C or C++ code using Microsoft? Visual Studio? 2013, the behavior of the executable code may be incorrect for the following conditions: ?The generated code uses memcpy ?in a specific pattern.  The  /O2 ?optimization flag is used when compiling the generated code.   The ?pattern has multiple calls to memcpy ?where the source pointer in a prior call is used as the destination pointer in a subsequent call. For example:  memcpy(&dv0[0], &fifo[0], 31U * sizeof(real_T));memcpy(&fifo[1],  &dv0[0], 31U * sizeof(real_T)); In the first call, &fifo[0]?is used as the source pointer. In the second call, &fifo[0] is used as the?destination pointer.? This issue is not reproducible when using Visual Studio 2015 (sometimes called Visual Studio 14).
Workaround: There are a few possible workarounds:Use a different version of Visual Studio to compile the generated code.Disable the?MATLAB Coder optimization?which can replace assignments with calls to?memcpy?by doing one of the following to prevent generation of memcpy?calls:At the command line, set the code configuration object property EnableMemcpy to false.In the MATLAB? Coder? app, set Use memcpy for vector assignment to No.?For standalone code generation, disable compiler optimization when using Visual Studio 2013 by setting the code configuration object property?BuildConfiguration?to 'Faster Builds'. Doing this may decrease the performance of the compiled binary because it disables many optimizations.

Summary: GraphicEQ can output signal with wrong gains if SampleRate is tuned after the object was locked
Description: GraphicEQ uses a gain correction matrix to improve the frequency response of the output when the EQ order is the default value of 2. This matrix is not being recomputed when the sampling rate is tuned after the object is locked. For example, generated audio plugins might lock the object with the default sampling rate (44100), so their subsequent attempts to tune the SampleRate  property will not recompute the gain correction matrix.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in simulation and code generation results when?multiword fixed-point data with large word lengths are input?to the Sqrt?block configured for the rSqrt function
Description: Mismatch occurs in simulation and code generation results when?multiword fixed-point data with large word lengths are input to a Sqrt block under these conditions: Function  parameter is set to rSqrt .  Method  parameteris set to  Newton-Raphson .  Intermediate results  datatype parameter is set to  Inherit: Inherit from input .  Output  datatype parameter is set to  single .

Summary: The RowNames, RowTimes,?and VariableNames properties of a table or timetable are not copied when assigning to a class property which has size validation
Description: If?a class has a property defined to be a table or timetable, and the property?uses class and size validation, then the RowNames , RowTimes ,?and VariableNames  properties are not copied from a value that is assigned to that property. Also,?RowNames , RowTimes ,?and VariableNames  are not copied from the property's default value defined by the class. For example, this class defines a property named Data  with class and size validation--a value for Data  must be an N-by-2 table. The default value for?Data  is defined as a 3-by-2 table whose variables are named X  and Y . >> type myClass.m  classdef myClass  ? ? properties  ? ? ? ? Data(:,2) table = table([0;0;0],[0;0;0],'VariableNames',{'X' 'Y'})  ? ? end? ? ?  end  The Data  property in an instance of this class is incorrectly initialized with a table that has variables?with the default names?Var1  and Var2, ?instead of the table variable names X  and Y  from the property's default value.?     >> obj = myClass obj =???myClass with properties: ? ? Data: [3ˇÁ2 table]  ?  >> obj.Data  ans =  ? 3ˇÁ2 table  ? ? Var1? ? Var2  ? ? ____? ? ____  ? ? ? 0? ? ? ?0  ? ? ? 0? ? ? ?0  ? ? ? 0? ? ? ?0  ?Also, assignment to the  Data  property does not copy the variable names from the value being assigned.  >> obj.Data = table([1;2;3;4],[5;6;7;8],'VariableNames',{'U' 'V'})  obj =?  ? myClass with properties:    ? ? Data: [4ˇÁ2 table]  >> obj.Data  ans =  ? 4ˇÁ2 table  ? ? Var1? ? Var2  ? ? ____? ? ____  ? ? ? 1? ? ? ?5  ? ? ? 2? ? ? ?6  ? ? ? 3? ? ? ?7  ? ? ? 4? ? ? ?8  ?Similarly, row names (for a table) or row times (for a timetable) are not copied on assignment to a property with class and size validation.
Workaround: Instead of using size validation for the property, use a property validation function to enforce the size restriction. For example, this class uses class validation for its Data property, but uses a size validation function to enforce the restriction to exactly two table variables.>> type myClass2.mclassdef myClass2? ? properties? ? ? ? Data table {requireNTableVars(Data,2)} = table([0;0;0],[0;0;0],'VariableNames',{'X' 'Y'})? ?end? ? ?endfunction requireNTableVars(t,N)if width(t) ~= N? ? error(sprintf('The Data property of a myClass2 object must be a table with %d variables.',N));endend

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect index used when a Bus array is selected before and after Atomic Subsystem boundary
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code for this modeling pattern: An array of bus signals connects to a Selector block The Selector block selects part of the array, which directly feeds into an Atomic Subsystem When entering the subsystem, that part of the array of bus signals immediately feeds into another Selector block or a Bus Selector block, and a subsequent selection occurs.  Under these conditions, the array index in the generated code for the bus signal selected in the subsystem might be incorrect.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Selector block output and the reusable subsystem input.

Summary: MATLAB? crashes when there is a significant change in display configuration
Description: MATLAB might crash when the display configuration switches between single and multimonitor modes. Other situations that might cause a crash include: Using Microsoft? Remote Desktop to access a Windows? system that is running MATLAB Docking or undocking a laptop that is running MATLAB and the Windows operating system Putting a Windows system to sleep or waking it while MATLAB is running

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation:?Simulink.DualScaledParameter?only works if defined in base workspace with explicitly specified data type
Description: Simulink.DualScaledParameter  has an option to specify a separate computation method that converts the parameter Value  to a CalibrationValue  with the desired scaling. This computation method is combined with the scaling of the parameter DataType  to determine the final computation method used by external calibration tools. You can specify the DataType  of this parameter object as: Context-sensitive, for example, auto  One of the built-in data types, for example,?double , single , int8 , ... Anonymous fixed point data type, for example, fixdt(...)  User-defined named data type, for example, name of data type object  For the first option, the parameter gets the actual data type from the model. Unless this data type is?double ?the COMPU-METHOD  written out in the ASAP2/AUTOSAR files is incorrect. For the last option, if you define the data type object in a data dictionary, the DualScaledParameter  is unable to find the data type object and an error occurs during code generation. If the parameter is defined in the model workspace, Simulink is unable to find the parameter object and an error occurs during code generation.
Workaround: To specify?DataType,?use one of these options:Specify DataType?explicitly as a built-in data type (double, single, int8, ...) or as an anonymous fixed-point data type (fixdt(...)).Define data type objects in the base workspace and configure the?data dictionary to Allow access to base workspace.In either case, define Simulink.DualScaledParameter objects in the base workspace or data dictionary, not in the model workspace.

Summary: MATLAB? crashes when using audioread for certain multi-channel WAV files
Description: MATLAB? crashes when using audioread  for certain multi-channel WAV files. Specifically, certain special purpose multi-channel WAV files, contain speaker position information, which leads to a crash. For example, the following command crashes MATLAB: [y, Fs] = audioread('my16channelfile.wav');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The functions strcmp and strcmpi?handle certain size mismatches differently between MATLAB execution and code generation
Description: The functions strcmp  and strcmpi ?handle certain size mismatches differently between MATLAB execution and code generation.  The behavior in MATLAB R2015a and previous releases is as follows:? strcmp   In code generation,?strcmp(a,b)  returns false if the sizes of a  and b  are not identical. Identical means that isequal(size(a),size(b))  is true. In MATLAB, strcmp(a,b)  applies a more permissive rule about the sizes of a  and b . strcmpi   In code generation,?strcmpi(a,b) ?ignores the sizes of a  and b . It ensures numel(a) == numel(b)  and that the corresponding elements match in a case-insensitive manner as if strcmp(lower(a(:)),lower(b(:)))  were called. In MATLAB,?strcmpi(a,b) ?applies a more restrictive rule about the sizes of?a ?and?b . Starting in MATLAB R2015b, in the generated code, strcmp  and strcmpi ?are strict about input size mismatches. This behavior differs from?MATLAB which applies a more permissive rule than the generated code about the sizes of the inputs.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: ppval can return incorrect results with degenerate spline interpolants calculated from variable-size inputs
Description: The ppval  function can misinterpret the output of the spline  function when the spline  interpolant is produced using a variable number of points and the number of points used is less than four. spline   produces a one-piece linear or quadratic interpolant. However, ppval  interprets the piecewise polynomial as piecewise constant.  This bug can occur when spline  is called with three inputs. This bug can occur when  interp1 , interp2 , or interp3  is used with the 'spline'  interpolation method. The bug does not occur with more than four points (the interpolant is cubic or piecewise cubic). It also does not occur with fixed-size inputs, regardless of the number of points.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: zip and unzip functions do not support large (>4GB) files
Description: R2017a and earlier releases:   The zip  function does not support creating a zip file that is greater than 4 GB in size, nor a zip file that contains more than 65535 files. If either of these conditions is true,  the zip  function might create a zip file that is invalid, without returning any warning or error. The unzip  function does not support unpacking zip files that are greater than 4 GB in size, or that contain more than 65535 files, or that contain individual files that are greater than 4 GB in size. R2017b and later releases:   The zip  function supports creating zip files that are greater than 4 GB in size if there are not more than 65535 files specified and no individual files are greater than 4 GB in size. The unzip  function supports unpacking all valid zip files, including zip files that are greater than 4 GB in size or that contain individual files that are greater than 4 GB in size.
Workaround: Create multiple zip files when the total size of the files to be compressed exceeds 4 GB, or when the total number of files exceeds 65535.
